name: Windstream 2019 Sarmento Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Cranberry and dark cherry aromas meet with charred wood, hibiscus and strawberry sorbet on the nose of this bottling. It's brisk on the palate, where the cranberry core meets with gravelly minerality, dried hibiscus, a hint of rust and a caramel finish. Strong acidity balances the relatively high alcohol.

name: Willamette Valley Vineyards 2019 Whole Cluster Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: The winery consistently hits a home run with this young, bright and delicious wine. It's a perfect light red for picnics or garden parties, with effusive plum and cherry fruit lightly touched with milk chocolate.

name: Westmount 2019 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: With the sure hand of veteran Laurent Montalieu helming this value brand, the overall quality and balance is excellent. This textured and detailed wine has an interesting fruit-herb mix of wild red berries, lemon verbena, some bitter herbs and good, grippy tannins. Another year in the bottle should help to smooth any remaining rough edges.

name: Weibel Family 2019 Archgate Reserve Bottling Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: Despite its low alcohol, this wine feels a bit heavy on the palate and offers plum and prune flavors accented by a hint of wood smoke.

name: Valle de Inez 2019 Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: This vintage of Doug Margerum's more affordable label is a complex and delicious stunner, beginning with opulent aromas of black cherry, wet loam and light mint on the nose. There's a tight line of stony, loamy minerality on the palate, balancing the wild raspberry, mulberry and black-pepper spice flavors.

name: Tree Fort 2019 Lush & Juicy Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)
review: This is a value brand to watch, originating out of August Sebastiani's 3 Badge Beverage Corporation. Aromas of dried berries, dried flowers, sandalwood and roasted plums lead into a palate with chalky tension and flavors of cranberry, hibiscus, wild mint and more floral touches. It's mellow and tightly woven, which is uncommon at this price point.

name: Tree Fort 2019 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: This is a fairly amazing wine for the price, competing with bottlings that are four times the cost. Deep and dark aromas of black cherry, caramel, cocoa, espresso bean and oregano lead into a grippy palate, where pinpoint, sandy tannins frame dense flavors of black currant and coffee grinds.

name: Three Thieves 2019 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This firm-textured and lightly tannic wine is a great value. It offers deep black-cherry and dried plum flavors on a full body. Sweet baking spices and wood smoke in the aroma give way to a tighter, more chewy palate.

name: The Pinot Project 2019 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Pure and expressive, this wine is more than just fruity, adding enticing black-tea and forest-floor notes to its tasty cherry and rhubarb core. Medium to full bodied, it offers fine-grained tannins to firm up the texture. This is the third excellent vintage in a row for this remarkable value wine.

name: Thacher 2019 Nouveau Shell Creek Vineyards Valdiguié (Paso Robles)
review: This nouveau-style Valdiguié is as unique as wine comes these days, starting with crisp aromas of raspberry, strawberry, dark melon and wild herbs. The vibrant palate, which is great chilled, is framed with an edgy texture, delivering boysenberry-jam and black-raspberry flavors.

name: Stolpman 2019 La Cuadrilla Red (Ballard Canyon)
review: This blend of 70% Syrah, 15% Grenache and 15% Sangiovese, which is directed by and directly benefits the winery's full-time vineyard crew, begins with aromas of roasted yet crisp red fruit, rose petals and grilled meats, with an underlying carbonic tone. The palate is bright and zesty, with fresh fruits, white-pepper and charred meat flavors.

name: Stolpman 2019 Pliocene Mourvèdre (Ballard Canyon)
review: From a densely planted block of own-rooted, head-pruned, “pre-clonal” vines, this still-young bottling begins with aromas of black fruit, wet stone and crumpled violet. There's fascinating tension and grip to the palate, with hints of white pepper, crushed chalk, rose hips and purple-flower flavors. Drink 2022–2032.

name: Stolpman 2019 Estate Syrah (Ballard Canyon)
review: Pure and lively aromas of purple flowers, wet rocks and damp soil show on the nose of this bottling, which will benefit from a bit of patience. Brisk flavors of raspberry, cola, chalk and lavender show much promise on the palate, which is tightly wrapped in staff tannins at this young age. Drink 2022–2034.

name: Stasis 2019 Murmur Vineyard Gamay Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: The early attempts at Gamay Noir on the Central Coast trend toward fresher styles, but this bottling is reminiscent of a serious Cru Beaujolais. Aromas of dark berry, crumpled flowers, crushed rock and white pepper lead into a palate of boysenberry, plum skin, pepper and purple flowers. The texture is tight, the acidity is bright and yet there is compelling density.

name: Stasis 2019 Carbonic Zinfandel (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: The carbonic movement has found a new candidate. This Zinfandel shows purple berry, lavender, violet and light elderberry on the nose. The palate features a tannic bite, with black pepper and peppercorn spicing up the grape-bubblegum flavor.

name: Solminer 2019 Carbonic Coquelicot Vineyard Syrah (Los Olivos District)
review: Quite transparent in the glass, this carbonic showcase of organic fruit sizzles with tight red-plum and light-licorice aromas on the nose. The bright red-currant flavors of the palate aren't much like Syrah, yet there is spicy pepper and familiar dried herbs, as well as an ashy streak: refreshing and zesty.

name: So Fresh 2019 Own-Rooted Field Blend So Fresh Red (Ballard Canyon)
review: There's a strong carbonic kick on the nose of this bottling, with lots of raspberry, light bubblegum and crushed granite elements. The palate is bouncy with fruit, offering cassis and cola-spice flavors.

name: So Fresh 2019 GDG Gamay Noir (Ballard Canyon)
review: Crunchy fruit aromas of red currant are enhanced with smashed up cement and cracked peppercorn on the nose. Exuberant acidity enlivens the palate, where dark-berry, chiseled stone and white-pepper flavors align.

name: So Fresh 2019 Crunchy Roastie Syrah (Ballard Canyon)
review: Crumpled rose and violet aromas meet with Dr Pepper spices and grape bubblegum on the nose of this bottling, which includes 5% Viognier. It's very zippy on the sip, blending purple-fruit and flower flavors with white-pepper spice and a lightly toasted finish.

name: So Fresh 2019 So Hot Syrah (Ballard Canyon)
review: Part of the winery's So Fresh series of carbonic, sulfur-free wines, this Syrah is an intense expression, with fresh boysenberry, white-pepper and crushed lava rock aromas. The palate is loaded with sticky texture and popping acidity, which uphold the smashed fruit flavors.

name: So Fresh 2019 Love You Bunches Sangiovese (Santa Barbara County)
review: The carbonic style is powerful and refreshing on the nose of this bottling, which offers raspberry and crushed stone aromas. There is immense amounts of zip to the sip, where crunchy strawberry flavors meet with light herbs.

name: Sean Minor 2019 Point North Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is light and gently pretty, but insubstantial. Slightly dilute fruit flavors touch on cranberry and raspberry, but lack any depth. The impression is of unsweetened soda pop.

name: Sean Minor 2019 4B Pinot Noir (California)
review: This attractive medium-bodied wine is smoky and brooding, offering forest floor aromas, hints of charred oak and solid-quality black-fruit flavors accented by black tea.

name: Scar of the Sea 2019 Hofer Vineyard Grenache (Cucamonga Valley)
review: From a dry-farmed vineyard that was planted in the 1930s, this bottling reminds of a walk through the late summer chaparral, full of sage, baked earth and crumpled laurel. There are also aromas of olive and elderberry on the nose, and flavors of raspberry and red currant on the palate, but it's the gray sage that coats everything.

name: San Simeon 2019 Estate Reserve Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Fresh out of the gates, this bottling is singing in opulent aromas of Bing cherry pie, clove, mace and rosewater. There is a wealth of baking spice on the palate, from nutmeg to star anise, while the silky, smooth mouthfeel presents flavors of cherry and mulberry.

name: Samuel Robert 2019 Vintner's Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Most reserves at this price point fail to justify the distinction, but given that Samuel Robert's regular Pinot is four dollars cheaper, and given that this has seriously good varietal flavors of blackberries, savory herbs, cola, a touch of bitter thistle and even some earthy minerality, what's not to like? Complexity and authenticity, along with Willamette Valley credentials, make this a peerless value.

name: Samuel Robert 2019 Conscious Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Don't let the lowball price fool you; this is a fine expression of authentic Willamette Valley Pinot Noir. Sweet black-cherry fruit is laced with cola scents and pretty highlights of mocha—all in a faultless wine of modest proportions. The tannins leave just a hint of bitterness as the wine trails away.

name: Saint K 2019 Like the Snows of Yesteryear White Hawk Vineyard Sangiovese (Santa Barbara County)
designation: Like the Snows of Yesteryear White Hawk Vineyard
review: Sporting a gun on the label, this is a powerful Sangiovese, starting with ripe aromas of black currant, blackberry, lavender and toast on the nose. It's thick on the palate, where baked and fresh dark-berry flavors meet with dark cocoa and violet.

name: Rickshaw 2019 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This smooth, medium-bodied wine is lavishly spiced with vanilla, cinnamon and toasted oak flavors, while a modest vein of red cherry remains in the background.

name: Rascal 2019 Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: Pretty aromas of berry and cherry open into a clean and fresh wine. It's fruit-driven, showing cranberry, raspberry and a touch of Bing cherry, with some skin phenolics framing the finish.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2019 Glenrose Vineyard Counoise (Adelaida District)
review: It's bizarre that Counoise is not more popular, as this bottling reveals its beautiful aromas of raspberry, rose petals and more red flowers. The palate is pleasingly complex, combining red currant and boysenberry with fennel pollen and white pepper.

name: Pacific Redwood 2019 Organic Cabernet Sauvignon (North Coast)
review: Fresh blueberry and grape aromas lead to a rather light texture and lean herb and berry flavors in this full-bodied wine.

name: Pacific Redwood 2019 Organic Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: Deep black-fruit flavors and a firmly tannic texture give this full-bodied wine a lot of power on the palate. Black cherries and blackberries are shaded by some assertive wood char notes.

name: Pacific Redwood 2019 Organic Syrah (California)
review: This wine is intense, peppery and tannic, offering tightly wound flavors of blackberry and dark chocolate in a firm texture. It has no added sulfites.

name: Odonata 2019 Cote du Denis Circle B Vineyard Carbonic Carignan (Paso Robles)
designation: Cote du Denis Circle B Vineyard Carbonic
review: Winemaker Denis Hoey hops on the carbonic train with this elegantly labeled bottling, and it hits all the marks. Bright raspberry, hibiscus and rainy cement aromas lead into a zesty palate of plum, strawberry and more wet rock flavors.

name: NSO By Dusty Nabor 2019 NSO Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This is a tart and lively take on Pinot Noir, done at an amazing price point. Underripe aromas of tangy red plum, pine needle and eucalyptus lead into a sagebrush-loaded palate of menthol, cranberry and tart plum.

name: Municipal Winemakers 2019 Hofer Cucamonga Grenache (Cucamonga Valley)
review: Very light and pretty in the glass, this wine from a vineyard planted in the 1930s 45-miles east of Los Angeles, now surrounded by runways and boxcars, starts with soft and plush aromas of cherry, strawberry, sagebrush and pepper. The sip sizzles with acidity, showing red currant, raspberry, hibiscus and white-pepper flavors.

name: McManis 2019 Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)
review: This full-bodied wine offers obvious wood-char aromas and oaky, cedary, peppery flavors atop bright, fresh cherry-berry fruit.

name: McManis 2019 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Pinot Noir (Lodi)
review: Effusive fruit flavors and light accents of baking spices and wood smoke make a very good combination in this medium-bodied, smooth and enjoyable wine.

name: McClean 2019 Love Red (Paso Robles)
review: This blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Zinfandel and Petite Sirah shows roasted black plum, Concord grape and rustic tones of earth and pepper on the nose. The wine is still quite young on the palate, so the tannins are resolving, but the fresh flavors of woody blackberries and toasted oak are promising.

name: Massalto 2019 Extravaganza Cinsault (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is a unicorn wine—100% Cinsault from Thunderstone Vineyard on the Royal Slope, barrel fermented with carbonic maceration. The aromas pop, with notes of plum, pomegranate, raspberry and lilac with a whiff of chocolate. It's light on its feet with a very slight spritz, dancing with juicy fruit flavors. It's an end-to-end, full-on charmer.

name: Margerum 2019 Margerum Estate Vineyard Cinsault (Los Olivos District)
review: Done right, Cinsault is a glou-glou grape at its finest. This bottling shows rose, bubblegum, red plum and lavender on the broad nose. The palate offers strawberry and more bubblegum flavors, with just enough spice and herb to keep it intellectual.

name: Kwaya 2019 Organic Sangiovese (Mendocino)
review: A smoky, rubber-boot aroma leads to flavors of graphite, charcoal and sour cherry in this rather raw-tasting and full-bodied wine. No added sulfites.

name: Kwaya 2019 Organic Merlot (North Coast)
review: Berry and herb aromas and earthy, meaty flavors make this full-bodied wine appealing. It turns tannic on the palate and has a smoky finish.

name: Kirkland Signature 2019 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Aromas of grilled meat, salt and pepper give this full-bodied wine a big, brash personality. It is moderately tannic and adequately filled in by cherry and black plum flavors.

name: Holly's Hill 2019 Poco Loco Estate Mourvèdre (El Dorado)
review: Smooth as silk and overflowing with perfectly ripe fruit flavors, this full-bodied but elegant wine is hard to resist. Light tannins and fruit acidity balance the gorgeous red- and black-cherry flavors and linger through the finish.

name: Gondak Cellars 2019 Campbell McKinney Vineyard Grenache (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a tiny production, light-handed and delicately crafted wine, from a cool-climate, hillside site. Tight-grained tannin support a lively, persistent acidity that gives lift to the flavors of cranberry, wild strawberry and rhubarb, finishing bright in citrus.

name: Girasole 2019 Estate Grown Zinfandel (Mendocino)
review: Superfruity and fresh, this is a very friendly, quaffable version of Zin, offering grapey, jammy flavors on a full body that doesn't feel too tough, due to light tannins.

name: Girasole 2019 Estate Grown Sangiovese (Mendocino)
review: An aroma like smoked blackberries leads to rich, plummy, black-fruit flavors and hints of milk chocolate in this full-bodied but non-tannic wine. It's fresh, mouthfilling and easy to swallow.

name: Girasole 2019 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Juicy and fresh-tasting, this wine offers plenty of cherry, plum and strawberry flavors that seem to gush out of the glass. It's full bodied, but fairly soft in texture and easy to gulp.

name: Frey 2019 Organic Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: Effusive, fresh cherry and blackberry aromas lead to a rather grippy, lively texture that's almost effervescent. This gulpable wine packs plenty of fruit flavors and has no added sulfites.

name: Frey 2019 Organic Cabernet Sauvignon (North Coast)
review: Black-pepper and veggie flavors accompany firm tannins and a full body in this rustic but enjoyable wine.

name: Frey 2019 Biodynamic Field Blend Red (Mendocino)
review: Jammy, fresh aromas lead to darker, richer black-fruit flavors in this full-bodied and firmly tannic wine. It shows a deep, dark color, tasty black-cherry and dark-chocolate flavors and has a lingering finish.

name: Frey 2019 Chateau Frey Red (Mendocino)
review: Dry and full bodied, this wine offers potent blackberry, earth and char flavors bolstered by light tannins.

name: Frey 2019 Organic Syrah (California)
review: Very ripe-tasting and robust, this full-bodied wine is one of the best no-sulfites-added wines on the market. It layers subtle baking spices over jammy blackberry flavors that run deep in the rich, moderately tannic texture.

name: Folktale 2019 Carbonic Sangiovese (Monterey County)
review: Sangiovese made in a carbonic fashion shows much promise. Clean lines of red currant and raspberry meet with potting soil, white pepper and a hint of game on the nose of this bottling. The palate is impressively layered, offering strong pepper spices and a taut texture against the dark-berry backdrop.

name: Folded Hills 2019 Cluster Grenache (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: This whole-cluster, carbonic bottling manages to capture the fresh factor of such a regime while retaining plenty of unique savory and spice qualities. Transparent in the glass, this begins with aromas of pomegranate, Ruby Red grapefruit, green sage and eucalyptus. The palate is snappy and grippy with raspberry, tarragon and dewy thyme flavors.

name: Folded Hills 2019 Cluster Nouveau Syrah (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: This brand is deeply exploring the whole-cluster and carbonic treatments to present fresh, lively and quaffable wines. Aromas of black raspberry bubblegum, smoked sage and thyme kick off the nose, while a mix of savory and fruity tones consume the palate, from red plum and roasted strawberry to peppercorns, herbs and a leather-kissed finish.

name: Firesteed 2019 Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: One of a growing number of mass-market Oregon Pinots under $20, this offers acceptable varietal character in a simple, blocky, straight-ahead style. Cherry fruit and slightly bitter herbs tell the tale, with no new oak. There's a hint of burnt rubber and a final impression of vanilla as it trails away.

name: Easton 2019 Zinster Lot 1852 Zinfandel (Fiddletown)
review: Aromas of dried herbs, rose petals, salt and pepper meet lively raspberry and cranberry flavors in this medium- to full-bodied wine. Made by fermenting whole clusters, it has an appetizing nip of tannin to firm up the texture.

name: Dusty Nabor 2019 NSO Grenache (Paso Robles)
review: Tart red-plum aromas play against richer strawberry and purple-flower tones on the nose of this very fairly priced bottling. The palate packs fresh fruit-punch and strawberry flavors with pleasantly sour acidity, making for a lively sip.

name: Delta 2019 Cabernet Franc (California)
review: This wine offers outstanding value for the quality, a mix of herbal, perfumed oak atop soft, approachable layers of raspberry and cassis. Bright and unfussy, it delivers nuanced flavor and a lasting hint of char.

name: Covenant 2019 Mensch Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: Jammy fruit flavors are coated with baking spices, vanilla and chocolate in this full-bodied, rich and indulgent wine.

name: Cobble Creek 2019 Pinot Noir (Paso Robles)
review: There's a tinge of sour cherry, light earth and crushed rocks on the nose of this bottling, which is billed as organic, gluten-free and vegan-friendly. There are bright flavors of raspberry on the palate as well as hints of smoke and chiseled stone.

name: Charlie & Echo 2019 Witherfall Tempest Red (San Diego County)
review: What happens when a Los Angeles metal band teams up with a natural winemaker in San Diego? This edgy blend of 32% Petite Sirah, 32% Syrah, 25% Nebbiolo and 11% Zinfandel, which with spicy aromas of cracked pepper, rosebuds and fruit punch. The palate sizzles with acidity, offering fun, almost fizzy flavors of green peppercorn, strawberry and dried herb.

name: Castle Rock 2019 California Cuvée Pinot Noir (California)
review: A reduced, flinty aroma gives way to pleasant fruit flavors and earthy overtones in this medium-bodied wine.

name: Castle Rock 2019 Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Fruity and savory flavors mingle well in this full-bodied, smooth-textured wine. It offers ample black-cherry and black-plum notes along with hints of tasty mushroom and beef jus.

name: Buttonwood Farm 2019 Carbonique Cabernet Franc (Los Olivos District)
review: Light in the glass, this carbonic style of Cab Franc pops with strawberry, white pepper, damp earth and dank herbs on the nose. The palate is deeply herbal and intriguing, offering both fresh and dried pops of marjoram, thyme and even cannabis, all playing against a strawberry and red-plum backdrop.

name: Bryn Mawr Vineyards 2019 Tinto Joven Tempranillo (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This young red, done in a Beaujolais Nouveau style, is principally Tempranillo, with 9% Tinta Cao. Scents of freshly baked bread rise from the glass, followed by fresh and lightly fruity flavors of tangy cranberries. This lovely wine is a perfect fit for a Thanksgiving feast, or anytime a light and lively red wine is wanted.

name: Böen 2019 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County-Sonoma County-Monterey County)
review: This bold and full-bodied wine offers an unusually deep, dark color, plenty of superripe cherry and berry flavors and lavish baking-spice accents. It's concentrated and mouthfilling.

name: Block Nine 2019 Caiden's Vineyard Pinot Noir (California)
review: Generous oak spices of cinnamon and clove accent moderate fruit flavors in this medium-bodied, smooth, soft and easy-drinking wine.

name: Birichino 2019 Mr. Natural No. 3 Enz Vineyard Old Vine Mourvèdre (Lime Kiln Valley)
designation: Mr. Natural No. 3 Enz Vineyard Old Vine
review: Featuring a label collaboration with illustrations by cartoonist Robert Crumb, this is a natural wine done well, starting with lively and peppery aromas of black raspberry, wet rocks and purple-flower blossoms. There's a brilliant sizzle of acidity on the palate, carrying crisp raspberry and pluot flavors as well as hints of white pepper and beef.

name: Birichino 2019 Enz Vineyard Old Vines Mourvèdre (Lime Kiln Valley)
review: This old-vine bottling packs freshness together with constant intrigue, calling for repeated tastes over the course of multiple days. It begins with aromas of freshly plucked plum, just-ripe cherries, light herbs, rose petals and a streak of stone. The palate follows suit with tense, grippy tannins, a fresh and light body and refreshing flavors of plum and crushed stone.

name: Belle Glos 2019 Balade Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: A dark, purple-red color in the glass, this single-block study begins with spicy aromas of cola and root beer as well as intense but not overripe berry and cherry aspects, a touch of toasty oak and an earthy, tar-like scent. The palate is rich with baked boysenberry-pie flavors but also deep and complex with loamy soil and more cola spice. The structure is tightly woven. Drink now through 2034.

name: 75 Wine Co. 2019 The Sum Red (California)
review: This full-bodied wine seems softened with a touch of sweetness, and offers lots of graphite, herb, olive and oak flavors for more of a savory expression than an outright fruity one.

name: 75 Wine Co. 2019 Feliz Creek Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendocino County)
review: Bay-leaf, clove and juicy plum flavors give this smooth, medium-bodied wine a savory profile.

name: ZO Wines 2018 Syrah (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Cherry vanilla and raspberry flavors brighten this light, crisp expression of subdued, nuanced red wine, simply constructed to showcase the fruit.

name: ZO Wines 2018 Oat Valley Carignane (Alexander Valley)
review: Meaty in texture with a soft, approachable style of moderate ripeness and oak, this red is rich and bright, with lasting flavors of cherry, plum and black pepper. It shows off a rustic variety that's rare in the appellation but great for the table.

name: Zinfandelic 2018 Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: This wine shows dark berry and bramble aromas, rich and complex fruit, black pepper and sage flavors and a smooth, lightly tannic texture. It offers wonderful aromas, nice concentration and a lingering finish.

name: Zaca Mesa 2018 Bien Nacido Vineyard Homage Collection Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: An elegant lavender aroma meets with raspberry, crushed rock and a hint of game on the nose of this bottling. The tannins are expertly taut, carrying more floral tones of lavender and rose alongside subdued plum, making for a delicate yet complex display.

name: Z. Alexander Brown 2018 Proprietary Red (California)
review: A smoky, beefy aroma and rich, ripe plum and dried herb flavors give this full-bodied wine a lot to like and a smooth texture wraps it all up.

name: Z. Alexander Brown 2018 Uncaged Pinot Noir (Monterey County-Napa County-Sonoma County)
review: Richly flavored with baking spices, this mouthfilling and full-bodied wine pours on the clove, cinnamon and ground ginger, heavily coating the modest fruit flavors.

name: Z. Alexander Brown 2018 Uncaged Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This is a serious, full-bodied wine from musician Zac Brown that will need some time to grow into its best self. Tight fruit and spice flavors are wrapped in firm tannins. While delicious now, it could wait until 2023 to be best enjoyed.

name: Wy'East Vineyards 2018 Blue Chip Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (OR))
review: The light cherry fruit has a leathery character, both in the nose and also when tasted. The net effect is to attenuate the fruit flavors and turn the finish astringent and drying. The wine spent 10 months in 15% new oak, adding a dash of sweet spice. Only one bottle was submitted.

name: Wrath 2018 Ex Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Dried cherry, shiitake, rusty earth and soy aromas show on the nose of this umami-rich bottling. There's a hint of oak on the sip, which is intriguing for a wine that did not see strong oak aging, but it works well with the dried cherry flavors.

name: Wrath 2018 Swan/828 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Quite dark in the glass, this clonal blend packs intense boysenberry, damson plum and dark-strawberry atoms, with vanilla pie crust, light violet and tea on the nose. It's lush and thick on the palate, with flavors of blackberry, nutmeg and French toast, proving ripe and hedonistic.

name: Wrath 2018 Pommard 4/777 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Baked red cherry, mace and hints of French toast spice show on the nose of this clonal combination. Toasty oak spice leads the sip, where baked red-fruit, sagebrush and a touch of eucalyptus flavors show.

name: Wrath 2018 115/667 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Pleasantly rustic in approach, this clonal combo showcase begins with earthy aromas of sagebrush, bloody rust, iodine and dried cherries on the nose. It lands on the palate with ample smoke as well as more blood and iodine, with mulberry at the core and a penetrating bay-leaf kick into the finish.

name: Wrath 2018 Ex Vite Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Zesty aromas of thyme, tarragon, pine needle and peppercorn reveal the whole-cluster style of this bottling, which rests on a fresh black-raspberry frame. The palate is peppery and herbal as well, tossing wild sagebrush and marjoram in with toasted tomato and lightly dried cherry flavors.

name: Wolff 2018 Teroldego (Edna Valley)
review: Lush and jammy while expertly ripe aromas of black cherry and boysenberry meet with vanilla and baking spice on the nose of this fresh and fun wine. The grape hails from the cooler spots of northeastern Italy, which fits the Edna Valley, producing a palate of smooth tannins, blackberry-pie flavors and spice accents of star anise and mace.

name: Wolff 2018 Syrah (Edna Valley)
review: Perfectly packaged aromas of roasted meats, dark berry fruits, purple flowers and ample baking spice satisfy all one needs in a Syrah. It's plush and generous on the palate, where hearty baked berry flavors are lifted by mace and star anise, cut by light sage and bay leaf and laid atop a soft yet penetrating texture.

name: Wolff 2018 Petite Sirah (Edna Valley)
review: Grown on a 125-acre vineyard that's just four miles from the coast, this may be the coolest spot for Petite Sirah on the planet. Aromas of baked boysenberry, lilacs and cola spice lead into a cherry-cola kick on the palate, with touches of pie crust and vanilla.

name: Wolff 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of rust, sweet cherry, fennel and pencil shavings show on the nose of this bottling. There is a fruity quality to the sip, where hints of oak and dried herbs meet with tangy black-plum-skin flavors.

name: Wise Villa 2018 Barbera (Alta Mesa)
review: Light herb and dusty earth aromas are backed by tangy red-fruit flavors and light tannins. This medium-bodied wine is lighter than many big reds.

name: Wise Villa 2018 Pinot Noir (Sierra Foothills)
review: Aromas of sweet oak and flavors of ripe plums and cherries melt into a rather soft, broad texture in this technically light-bodied but rich-feeling wine.

name: Wise Villa 2018 Sangiovese (California)
review: This earthy, savory, light-bodied wine has a rustic appeal, offering aromas of dried herbs, leather and sun-dried tomatoes followed by tangy, somewhat tannic flavors of cranberries and raspberries. It will be a great palate cleanser with rich food, especially Italian!

name: Wise Villa 2018 Wisdom of Wise Red (California)
review: Full bodied, ripe tasting and rich in mouthfeel, this blend offers tasty plum, strawberry and spice flavors. It's a fruit-forward, easy sipping wine, with moderate alcohol content.

name: WineShop At Home 2018 SunFish Petite Sirah (California)
review: A rather sleek version of the varietal, this concentrated wine offers focused blackberry and blueberry flavors wrapped in fine-grained tannins and accented by maple and wood-smoke nuances. Best through 2025.

name: WineShop At Home 2018 SunFish Pinot Noir (California)
review: This a sturdy wine, crisp and a bit tannic on the palate, offering smoky, meaty aromas and tight cherry flavors.

name: Winemakers Selection 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: A light smoky aroma and salt-and-pepper flavors give this wine a good, meaty expression. It is medium bodied, light in tannin and easy to sip.

name: Winemaker's Folly 2018 Chapter 1 Vino Tinto Red (Sonoma County)
review: Jake Bilbro's ode to simpler times growing up a winemaker's son in Sonoma County, this red combines 76% Syrah, 19% Zinfandel, 4% Grenache and 1% Petite Sirah. Meant to be a table wine without fuss, it overdelivers on that premise with bold, approachable flavors of blackberry, blueberry, wild game and a bit of garrigue. Soft and easy to enjoy, it's a great, full-bodied wine.

name: Wine Spots 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick tannin and toasted oak wrap around a full-bodied core of baked raspberry and strawberry in this approachably soft, rich wine.

name: Wine Guerrilla 2018 Paradise Lane Vineyard Old Vine Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)
review: Black cherry, blackberry and black pepper combine forcefully around broad, dry tannins and over-the-top ripeness in this big-boned wine. A touch of dried herb appears on the finish.

name: Windstream 2018 Sarmento Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: A dried cherry-skin note meets with leather and dried herbs on the rather mellow nose of this young bottling. The palate combines roasted meats in plum sauce with Christmas spices, offering bright fruit alongside a hint of vanilla on the finish.

name: WIndstorm 2018 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: Effusive fruit aromas and rich juicy berry and roast-beef flavors dominate this full-bodied and lightly tannic wine. It's an enjoyable pour that shows how extroverted this varietal can be.

name: Windrun 2018 No. 16 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Opulent black-cherry, boysenberry and concentrated hibiscus aromas meet with star anise on the nose of this bottling. The palate combines rich and dark strawberry flavors with a red-plum tang, enhanced further by pinches of oregano, thyme and pine needle.

name: Windchaser 2018 Grenache Noir (El Dorado)
review: This maverick wine made with 30% whole clusters in the fermenter offers a light color, subtle flavors and lean texture that are totally non-commercial but quite appealing. Light strawberry and tea aromas meet plummy, earthy flavors that break the usual mold.

name: Windchaser 2018 Syrah (Mendocino Ridge)
review: This is not your ordinary Syrah. Intense in flavor and rather tight in texture at this stage, this interesting and compelling wine offers a range of compelling aromas and flavors, from violets and cooked blackberries, to smoked meat and black pepper. Best through 2026.

name: Wilson Foreigner 2018 Rancho Chimiles Valdiguié (Napa Valley)
review: This is a beautifully complex, beguiling and lifted red wine, tasting of plum and dark cherry dappled in white pepper and dried herb. Fermented in concrete tank and aged in neutral oak, the grapes are grown on the ranch owned and farmed by the Wilson family in the far reaches of the eastern Napa Valley.

name: Wilson Creek 2018 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Temecula Valley)
review: Dried berry aromas meet with crushed rocks, dusty soil and hints of sagebrush on the nose of this reserve bottling. It's lighter bodied in style, offering muddled strawberry and red-plum flavors with a mineral-driven, rocky-flavored core, leading into an oregano-dusted finish.

name: Wilson Creek 2018 Bourbon Barrel Aged Zinfandel (California)
review: Deep fruity aromas of baked berries and damp mint make for a very extracted nose on this Bourbon-barrel-aged wine. It's quite sweet and treacly on the palate, where berry-candy and brown-sugar flavors lead to a caramel-sauce finish.

name: Wilson Creek 2018 Variant Series Cabernet-Zinfandel Red (California)
review: Light in the glass, this bottling starts with tangy red plum on the nose alongside plenty of wild herb and chaparral aromas. The palate hits with a bit of sweet strawberry, red currant and raspberry jam, lifted with a touch of licorice dust.

name: Willowbrook 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Gravelly, grippy texture supports a full-bodied expression of plum, strawberry and black tea, the core of the wine dusted in dried herb and rose petal.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Papera Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)
review: Tones of orange peel, cranberry and blackberry carry the nose, offering all the brambly elements of the variety. Bold, ripe and cohesive, it retains bright acidity and a nervy feel that lend balance.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Fanucchi-Wood Road Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)
review: Brawny and aromatic in blackberry, dark cherry and dried herb, this old-vine wine is thick and well integrated. The flavors veer from rich and broad to savory and spicy, with a lasting element of crushed rock

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Westside Road Neighbors Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: A perennial favorite, this wine brings together the grapes of many sites into one, delighting the senses in its inviting red-fruit flavors, hint of allspice and undeniably velvety texture. Complex, seductive and lengthy, it is a simply beautiful wine that remains on the mind long after it's gone.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Olivet Lane Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a site planted to a selection of older vines, this wine is carried by rose petal on the nose, with strawberry, rhubarb and tangerine flavors wrapped around the layered palate. Fresh, crisp acidity contrasts well against a backdrop of cola and nutmeg.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Terra De Promissio Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: The Petaluma Gap site shows its innate earthiness in droves here, opening in a rose petal aroma before evolving slowly to reveal dense, delicate layers of black tea, orange peel and allspice. The savory tones lead to a midpalate that bursts of rich berries, with soft, supple tannins framing it all.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: Light and delicately woven in flavors of clove, nutmeg and blue fruit, this approachable wine is sourced from vineyards in the Russian River Valley and Sonoma Coast appellations. It brings together lush richness and cool-climate acidity with ease.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Lush and earthy in all the right ways, this appellation wine is effusive and floral in a lovely bouquet of wild rose. Bright red fruit persists through the structured and well-integrated body, with fresh acidity and accents of black tea and cardamom lifting it up.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A beautiful expression of the cool-climate appellation, this wine shows enduring flavors of blackberry and cherry, with deft touches of dried herb and forest. It holds its oak and weight with ease, offering balance within a complex structure and hint of grip.

name: Williams Selyem 2018 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: A brilliant raspberry color, this annual bottling from San Benito County is as fresh and wild as usual, starting with aromas of raspberry sorbet, strawberry, lavender and herbs. The palate is immensely fresh, hitting with flavors of black raspberry, mace, clove and toast that ride a complex texture.

name: Willamette Valley Vineyards 2018 Estate Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: The fruit has a dusty quality, with rather thin mixed red-berry flavors. Sharp acids and a sprinkling of fresh herbs add texture and bite. The wine was aged 10 months in 17% new French oak.

name: Willamette Valley Vineyards 2018 Founders' Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: There are strong components here, and it's a jostle among them with no clear direction. Tart berry fruits, racy acids and a mix of herbs come up against earthy astringent tannins and then the wine seems to lose focus and fade.

name: Willamette Valley Vineyards 2018 Whole Cluster Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Irresistibly delicious, this perennial winner rocks bright, freshly picked raspberry and marionberry fruit, set against a core of minerality. A mix of Pommard and Wädenswil clones, all fermented with the stems, it sidesteps any herbal excess and finishes with a palate-cleansing burst of acidity.

name: Whitehall Lane 2018 Rassi Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma Valley)
review: Blended with 15% Merlot, this affordable red is nutty and herbal in cedar, with strong elements of toasted oak. Dark fruit presents as full bodied and deeply concentrated, with moderate structure giving it a supple foundation of power.

name: Whitehall Lane 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: A small addition of Petit Verdot adds to the full-bodied power of this wine, approachable in its well-integrated tannin and oak. Brawny blackberry and currant ride alongside mocha, vanilla and woody toasted notes.

name: White Rose 2018 White Rose Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: As with the winery's other Pinots, there's a gentle hand at work, using just 10% new French oak, though this is the top wine from the estate. The low alcohol suggests it was picked early, and the flavors offer a subtle mix of blood orange, plum, blueberry and blackberry fruit. Though delicate, it's not lacking for depth or complexity, and shows medium concentration through a long finish.

name: White Rose 2018 Guillen Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Bright, spicy plum and cherry fruit is underscored with skin and stem phenolics. It's been entirely whole-cluster fermented, and aged just under a year in 12% new French oak. There's an elegance to the frame and a deft balance throughout, which suggests tucking a few bottles away for a few years longer.

name: White Rose 2018 The Neo-Classical Objective Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Old vines (age unspecified) make up this cuvée, a weave of mashed berries and baking spices. Scents of cherry tobacco lift the aroma, and the whole cluster ferment plus minimal new French oak keeps all components in measured balance.

name: White Rose 2018 The Convivial Feast Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is an aromatic wine with blue- and red-berry fruits, highlighted with milk chocolate. The palate is satiny and full, and expands to include plums and cherries. New barrel baking-spice accents contribute a dollop of plum pudding, gingerbread and more. It's a beautifully integrated wine, even at this young age.

name: Whitcraft 2018 Stolpman Vineyard Grenache (Ballard Canyon)
review: This is not your daddy's Grenache. It's as light as a dark rosé, yet not shy on anything else. Aromas of bay leaf, green peppercorn, raspberry and strawberry lead into a sip that's laden with herbs of thyme and marjoram. Cranberry, watermelon and grapefruit-rind flavors complete the zesty if atypical palate.

name: Whitcraft 2018 Mt. Eden Clone Pence Ranch Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Loaded with aromas of fresh cherries and raspberries as well as damp earth and baking spice, this clonal study is extremely inviting on the nose. The vibrant sip starts with grippy flavors of cranberry skins and then moves into warmer tones of strawberry and hibiscus, with dusty sage herbs on the finish.

name: Whitcraft 2018 La Rinconada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Extremely fresh and zippy on the nose, this single-vineyard expression zings with aromas of raspberry, hibiscus and green-herb-inflected strawberry tops, with a pinch of white pepper. The palate packs taut red fruit together with sage sticks, riding a tightly woven texture into the finish.

name: Whitcraft 2018 Chene Vineyard Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Dark hibiscus and fresh cranberry aromas meet with earthy elements of wet adobe brick and crushed slate on the nose of this bottling. The sip zips into flavors of strawberry sorbet, wet gravel, plum skins and li hing mui spice, proving quite delicious and gluggable.

name: Whitcraft 2018 My Friend Matts Vineyard Syrah (Los Olivos District)
review: Buoyant aromas of fresh cherries, crumpled violets and dark currants make for a very floral nose on this bottling. It's zippy and alive on the palate, where ripe berry and cherry flavors meet with a granite-driven minerality.

name: Whitcraft 2018 Pence Ranch Clone 459 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Very light in the glass, this wine explodes on the nose with aromas of freshly plucked raspberry, strawberry and cranberry along with wet sidewalk minerality and hints of sagebrush and pepper dust. The fruity qualities are more reserved on the palate, where fresh hibiscus and white-pepper flavors ride a tense structure, leading toward a cherry paste and sumac finish.

name: Whipsmart Wine Company 2018 One Fine Morning Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)
review: Made from 100% whole-cluster grapes via carbonic maceration, the vineyard planted in 1885, this is an indeed fresh and fruity take on the variety that benefits from its processing. Light, crunchy red fruit presents as textured, exotic, herbal and full of bubbly acidity.

name: Westmount 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Winemaker Laurent Montalieu is quickly ramping up production for this top-notch, value-priced line. This new vintage is supple and layered with tart purple berries and light, sweet spices. It's a spot-on representation of very good Willamette Valley Pinot Noir, young and fresh and finishing with a seam of graphite.

name: Wentworth 2018 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This is a satisfying and well-structured wine made with 30% whole-cluster fruit. It tastes of pure red- and black-fruit flavors, with firm tannins and refreshing acidity lending to the mouthcleansing texture. Best through 2027.

name: Wentworth 2018 Nash Mill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: This wine is lively, charming, complex and vibrant, starting with a spearmint aroma that segues to mouthfilling red cherry and rhubarb before a tangy raspberry finish kicks in. It's an elegant wine that balances restraint with seduction.

name: Wente Vineyards 2018 Riva Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)
review: Ripe aromas of blistered cherries, clove, star anise and dusty earth show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. There's a pleasant herbal spice to the palate, where taut red-plum, pomegranate, hibiscus and cinnamon flavors lead into a structurally significant finish.

name: Wente Vineyards 2018 Wetmore Vineyard Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Livermore Valley)
review: Concentrated and deep, this velvet-textured wine soothes the palate and brings out rich black-cherry and black-currant flavors. Although well packed with fruit and subtle spices, it's not too heavy or tannic.

name: Wente Vineyards 2018 Southern Hills Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Livermore Valley)
review: This full-bodied but gentle wine wraps ripe, delicious black-cherry and dark-chocolate flavors in a creamy texture. A silky mouthfeel reveals little tannin influence and carries the rich flavors through a lingering finish.

name: Wente Vineyards 2018 Sandstone Estate Grown Merlot (Livermore Valley)
review: This medium-bodied, lightly tannic wine from the iconic Livermore winery layers savory aromas over plush red and black cherry flavors. It is well-balanced and very smooth in mouth-feel.

name: Waxwing 2018 Coastview Vineyard Syrah (Monterey County)
review: There's a density on the nose of this bottling that combines packed violets, tight black currant, loamy soil and roasted meats. The palate is also very tight at he moment, with hints of blackberry and black-plum paste and dark cocoa. But it's the structure that's most impressive. Drink 2022–2038.

name: Waxwing 2018 Deerheart Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Baked plum, brioche and a pinch of lavender make for a compelling aromatic entry to this bottling. Dark strawberry and exotic mulberry flavors show on the palate, where clove and cinnamon spice lead into a finish. The higher alcohol is not apparent at all.

name: Waxwing 2018 Lester Family Vineyard Syrah (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: The cooler climate of this vineyard presents dynamic aromas of peppercorn, bay leaf and charcoal alongside fresh plum and violet on the nose of this bottling. The high-acid palate falls in line accordingly, offering peppery spices against the tightly woven black-raspberry core.

name: Waxwing 2018 Lester Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Quite light in the glass, this bottling offers delicious, fresh and lively aromas of strawberry sorbet, forest floor and peppery spice on the nose. The sip snaps with fresh cranberry and raspberry, sprinkled with thyme and sagebrush, proving both easy-to-drink and thought-provoking at once.

name: Waters 2018 Waterstone Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: All of this wine comes from fan trained, third leaf fruit, and it's both promise and delivery. The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of blueberry, whole orange, wet stone and smoked meat, very pretty and fruit forward. Full feeling, voluptuous smoked meat, berry and savory flavors follow. Many wines from the Rocks sub-appellation are all savory. This one brings plenty of that but also an abundance of fruit.

name: Waters 2018 Waterstone Cabernet Franc (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Fruit for this wine comes from third leaf, fan trained vines—a long held dream realized for the winery. The aromas are arresting, with notes of plum, ash, sliced green pepper and satsuma. It coats the palate with notes of ember, cherry and herb, with the velvety, high pH feel of the Rocks District sub-appellation of the valley. Smoke notes linger on the finish. It's a unique offering of the variety for the state.

name: Waterbrook 2018 Mélange Founder's Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Subdued aromas lead to flavors that seem a bit muddled. It's somewhat off-step with the vintage.

name: Waterbrook 2018 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of dark cherry and barrel spice lead to a full, fleshy palate. The oak is more primary than desired for the variety, but it still delivers a lot of enjoyment. A light medicinal note emerges with some time open.

name: Waterbrook 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of plum, black currant, pencil lead, whole green pepper and dried herb are followed by rich, ripe flavors. It's chock-full of fleshy fruit. A sneaky layer of firm tannins backs it all up. It delivers big-time value. Drink now.

name: Waterbrook 2018 Malbec (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: There's no question about the variety, with its opulent aromas of plum and blue fruits. Generous flavors follow, framed by pillowy, soft tannins, with plum, orange peel and herb notes lingering on the finish.

name: Waterbrook 2018 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are reserved, with notes of vanilla, nougat and cherry. Plump raspberry and fruit leather flavors that dip in the middle, creating a doughnut hole, flesh it out. The warmth of the vintage carries it away at times, but there is still plenty of enjoyment and value to be had.

name: Washington Hills 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Washington)
review: Reserved aromas of plum, berry and gummy lead to plum and black-currant flavors. A pleasing amount of tannin holds it all together. It's a straightforward, Cabernet quaffer at the right price.

name: Walter Scott 2018 X-Novo Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: Mixed aromatics show dried leaves, pie cherries, breakfast tea and mushrooms, anticipating a marvelously complex palate comprised of 15 different clones. The nuanced subtlety is on full display, propelled by 30% whole clusters and a very sure touch by the winemaker. It's exciting to see the richness of detail that can result from finding the perfect time to pick these grapes. Drink this gem now into the early 2030s.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Seven Springs Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: Crafted from a blend of four Dijon clones planted in the Selection Massale (genetically diverse) method, this lovely wine is a perfect expression of what is arguably the most widely-celebrated vineyard in the Eola-Amity AVA. The aromas run from lavender to strawberry to raspberry, with a streak of vanilla coming up from aging 15 months in 35% new oak.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Temperance Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This iconic vineyard is notable for its remarkably diverse elevations and slopes. This one-acre block of 28-year-old vines gets hit hard by the Van Duzer Corridor winds, and the finished wine is redolent with scents of violets, spicy pine needles, sage and clove. Wild berries and plums, juicy acids and exceptional balance are among the core strengths. Enjoy this over the next decade or longer.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Sequitur Vineyard Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Sourced from Mike Etzel's vineyard above Beaux Frères, this is Walter Scott's only Ribbon Ridge offering. One-quarter whole clusters were included, yielding a rich palate, with bright raspberry fruit and highlights of light compost and cedar plank. It's a smooth full-bodied wine, and the young vines, farmed biodynamically, should continue to deliver more and more complexity in the years ahead.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Justice Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This vineyard is owned and farmed by the Casteel family, whose Bethel Heights winery is just up the road. Wild, sauvage berry flavors are highlighted with orange rind, and there's a touch of rhubarb also. It's an earthy, edgy wine, with drying tannins that carry a bottom note of mineral.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: A fine note of silty earth underscores the herbal accents particular to fruit from this outstanding vineyard. Brambly berries and tart pie cherries are the principal fruits, well balanced against the earthiness. Aging in 35% new oak adds a hint of allspice.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Sojeau Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: Renamed (formerly Sojourner), this mixes generous floral, herbal and earthy aromas, leading into a complex palate loaded with blackberry fruit. The fermentation included 30% whole clusters and the wine was aged in 228 liter barrels for 15 months, 35% new. The drying tannins leave an impression of astringent rock, and suggest that a couple more years of bottle age would be optimal.

name: Walter Scott 2018 Cuvée Ruth Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: A quartet of top tier vineyards comprise this lovely blend. The ferment included 30% whole clusters in 40% new barrels, without sacrificing any detail or overdoing the phenolics. Raspberry, marionberry and blueberry fruit comes with a vein of dark chocolate. The tannins are firm and polished, leaving a bit of black-tea astringency as they trail off.

name: Walt 2018 La Brisa Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Up-front earthiness invites one into the core of this full-bodied, richly styled wine, tangy in acidity with a lingering freshness and complexity of stems. Cool-climate elements of forest, tea and tannin combine around strawberry and mint.

name: Walt 2018 Bob's Ranch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This wine is ultimately elegant despite its considerable power—a force of richness and tannin that hits the palate with impact. Barnyard-like earth, forest and black tea give it an undercurrent of savory intrigue, as high-toned flecks of freshness keep it brightly in check.

name: Walt 2018 The Corners Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Almost-sweet flavors of butter and butterscotch complement plenty of ripe strawberry and black-plum notes in this full-bodied and very smooth-textured wine. It's generous, ripe and delicious.

name: Walt 2018 Sierra Mar Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Hearty aromas of blackberry, mace, cardamom and damp soil are rich yet nuanced on the nose of this bottling. It's soft and lush on the palate, combining red- and black-fruit flavors with star anise and cardamom cream. It's a very satisfying style of Pinot Noir, full of spice and richness.

name: Walt 2018 Rosella's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Deeply aromatic aromas of blackberry and black currant meet with unending layers of violet, lavender, rose petals, loamy soil and piles of herbs on the dynamic nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate expertly balances ripe pomegranate and cranberry flavors with zesty acid and precise tannins, bolstered by thyme, oregano and light pepper spices.

name: Walt 2018 Blue Jay Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Good depth of flavor and great structure make this wine appealing and complete. Smoky aromas of clove, cedar and black pepper lead to concentrated and rich black-cherry and red-cherry flavors on a moderately tannic texture. Best through 2025.

name: Walla Walla Vintners 2018 Sangiovese (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Bright, fresh aromas of raspberry, cranberry and plum are followed by brawny fruit flavors, backed by firm, lightly grainy tannins. A fine sense of acidity backs it up. It has all of the stuffing but needs time in the cellar for the tannins to stretch their legs. Best after 2023.

name: Voluptuary 2018 V. 05 Forestbed Grenache (El Dorado)
review: Funky, earthy aromas and iron, mineral and black-tea flavors certainly make this wine stand out from the crowd. Appealing sour-cherry and rhubarb notes give it a tangy fruit core, and the texture is a bit grippy, with tannin and nervy with acid.

name: Voluptuary 2018 V.04 The Source Red (California)
review: Cidery, sharp flavors and a murky, earthy aroma put this Pinot Noir and Grenache blend outside the traditional parameters of quality, but it has a good palate-cleansing effect and some vibrant sour cherry flavors at its core.

name: Voluptuary 2018 V.08 The Sower Tannat (Calaveras County)
review: This is a tight, lean, light-bodied wine that shows the smoky, rubbery aromas common in no-sulfites-added wines.

name: Virtue 2018 Vitality Malbec (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: All of the fruit for this 100% varietal wine comes from Solaksen Vineyard on the Royal Slope. Aged in second-fill and neutral oak, the aromas are brooding initially, with notes of cherry, plum, orange oil and dried herb, showing a sense of purity. The flavors are expressive and focused, showing density and richness.

name: Virtue 2018 Unity G-S-M (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Syrah makes up 48% of this wine, with the rest 32% Mourvèdre and 20% Grenache. With fruit coming from Wallula, Des Etes Long, and Thunderstone vineyards and seeing only second-fill and neutral oak, the aromas are light but intriguing, with notes of smoked meat, medicine, fresh parsley, plum and berry. It's beautifully textured but still sleek and velvety in feel, showing exquisite polish, structure and a long finish. It's gorgeous stuff at a can't-be-beat price.

name: Virtue 2018 Temperance Cabernet Franc (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Fruit for this 100% varietal wine comes from Destiny Ridge Vineyard. Brooding cigar-box, dried herb and cherry-licorice aromas lead to fruit flavors that show a lovely sense of purity, depth and freshness.

name: Virtue 2018 Sincerity Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: All of the fruit for this wine comes from Wallula Vineyard in the Horse Heaven Hills. The aromas pop, with notes of freshly chopped parsley, whole tangerine, fresh boysenberry and smoked meat. Rich, layered flavors follow. It brings intensity and depth, but there is still plenty of freshness. But it's the purity and lengthy finish that captivate.

name: Virtue 2018 Fortitude Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is 48% Merlot, 27% Cabernet Sauvignon and 25% Malbec, with fruit coming from Olsen and Solaksen vineyards. The aromas are compelling, with notes of raspberry, dried herb, plum and cherry, showing mesmerizing purity. Full, achingly pure flavors follow, with knee-buckling texture. It's an end-to-end delight.

name: Virtue 2018 Enthusiasm Grenache (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: All of the fruit for this wine comes from Thunderstone Vineyard on the Royal Slope, and is fermented as whole berries, with extended maceration. The aromas aren't fully ready to reveal their charms on first pour, but they eventually open to reveal notes of chopped parsley, black pepper and pure berry. The palate is full of grace and polish, ramping up in intensity over time, with a lingering finish. It has the stuffing to go the distance.

name: Virtue 2018 Authenticity Merlot (Yakima Valley)
review: Though not labeled as such, this is all single-vineyard Olsen Merlot. Roasted coffee-bean aromas are out front initially, followed by notes of herb and raspberry. Smooth fruit and barrel flavors follow, showing depth and structure. The barrel influence shows more prominently than other inaugural offerings from the winery, smoothing out over time, but it still impresses. Drink from 2022 to 2030.

name: Virtue 2018 Charisma Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit for this wine comes from Olsen and Solaksen vineyards. Seeing no new oak, the aromas are pulled back, with notes of herb, anise, fresh black currant and plum. Exquisitely pure dark-fruit flavors follow. It's a window into a pure expression of the variety, absolutely delicious.

name: Vinum Cellars 2018 The Insider Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is a value-driven wine that overdelivers on its price point, offering black cherry, vanilla and dried herb around an oaky center. Made in a full-bodied, fleshy and lush style, it finishes in earthy varietal character and drops of cinnamon.

name: Vina Robles 2018 Estate Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of roasted black cherry, fig, caramel and milk chocolate make for a rich and gooey entry to the nose of this appellation blend. Lavish, creamy and smooth tannins frame the sip, where roasted plum, fig and raisin flavors are framed by a more rigid structure toward the finish.

name: Vina Robles 2018 Estate Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)
review: Quite dark and inky in the glass, this bottling begins with aromas of cassis, roasted meat and a hint of baking spice. Thick, hearty tannins frame the savory sip, where teriyaki and roast lamb flavors lead to dark berries, moving to vanilla bean and espresso on the finish.

name: Vina Robles 2018 The Arborist Estate Red (Paso Robles)
review: This is a screamingly good deal, offering both hedonistic pleasure and complexity through a blend of 41% Syrah, 35% Petite Sirah, 12% Grenache and 12% Tannat. Aromas of roasted cherry, smoked meat and cola spice lead into a hearty palate of black currant, roasted game and licorice. It's balanced by bright acidity, making for a sip that will satisfy many.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Phoenix Red (Stanislaus County)
review: This robust wine leads with black-pepper and charred oak aromas, then pours ripe blackberry flavors on a moderately tannic texture. Blended from mostly Primitivo and Tannat it's boisterous and tasty.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Vinas Red (Sierra Foothills)
review: There is a nice meaty note in this full-bodied, rich wine blended from Mourvèdre, Syrah and Grenache grapes. It offers delicious berries, plums and a bit of woodsmoke backed by light tannins.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Merlot (Sierra Foothills)
review: Dark and luscious, this full-bodied but smooth-textured wine is filled to the brim with ripe black-plum and blackberry flavors and accented with cocoa.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Sierra Foothills)
review: A topping of woodsmoke and black pepper give a spicy edge to this full-bodied, moderately tannic wine that shows decent blackberry flavors underneath.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Tempranillo (Sierra Foothills)
review: Bold, flavorful and firmly tannic, this full-bodied wine wraps dramatic toasted oak and grilled plum flavors in plenty of grippy tannins and has the concentration needed to keep it balanced. Best through 2025.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Grenache (Sierra Foothills)
review: Black-pepper and wood-char aromas lead to concentrated blackberry and woodsmoke flavors in this hearty, spicy and full-bodied wine. It's not very tannic, so even with all that flavor it sips easily.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Syrah (Sierra Foothills)
review: Ripe, jammy but not quite sweet, this full-bodied wine overflows with blackberry and huckleberry-pie flavors that are rich and layered. It's easy to swallow and not very tannic.

name: Vina Moda 2018 Barbera (Sierra Foothills)
review: Beefy, meaty, toasted-oak and black-pepper flavors dominate this heavily spiced and full-bodied wine.

name: Villa Creek 2018 3 Syrah (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Roasted black-currant, charred beef crust and iodine aromas are redolent on the nose of this dark, inky wine. There's a hint of caramel on the palate, and then zesty flavors of blackberry, cola, brown sugar and cracked pepper, framed by deeply textured structure and tons of refreshing acidity.

name: Villa Creek 2018 Cherry House Red (Paso Robles)
review: Soft aromas of polished red cherry, licorice, red flowers and wet gravel show on the nose of this new offering from proprietor Cris Cherry. The core of dark-red fruits are lifted by carnation and rose flavors, with firm tannins and a light licorice pop on the finish.

name: Villa Creek 2018 Garnacha (Adelaida District)
review: Ripe but also sharp aromas of red berry, tart hibiscus and vanilla spice show on the nose of this bottling. Montmorency cherry skins pop on the palate, where a strong acidity upholds hints of sage, cola and licorice.

name: Verraco Ridge 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Dried cherry and cranberry aromas meet with red spice and light smoke on the nose of this bottling. There is a freshness to the sip, with flavors of hibiscus and tart raspberry, making for a lively sip.

name: Vennstone 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County-Monterey County-Santa Barbara County)
review: This wine would be good as a red blend, but it's out of place as a Pinot Noir, showing an excessively dark color, blatant oak flavors and a stiff, oaky mouthfeel.

name: Venge 2018 Scout's Honor Red (Napa Valley)
review: This is a lively proprietary red wine made from 63% Zinfandel, 16% Charbono, 12% Petite Sirah and 9% Syrah. From many old-vine sites, it explodes in juicy plum, jammy blackberry and cherry, all wrapped in soft tannins. The oak adds a generous and rich vanilla tone.

name: Valravn 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: This is a high-toned, earthy and textured wine that overdelivers on its price point, presenting complex warm spice, black-tea and candied cherry and raspberry flavor. The tannins are soft and well integrated, as is the oak, allowing plenty of room for nuance and elegant composure.

name: Valle de Inez 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Rich aromas of dark strawberry jam and cedar show on the nose of this bottling. There is a strong wood-spice element to the sip, where stiff and woody tannins carry snappy and fresh berry flavors.

name: Valle de Inez 2018 Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Aromas of plum, pomegranate, carnation and wet slate show on the nose of this value bottling by Doug Margerum. There's a gravelly texture on the sip, where light and bright cranberry flavors are fresh and fun.

name: V. Sattui 2018 Collina d'Oro Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: With a bright, fresh opening of wild strawberry and orange peel, this medium-bodied wine is balanced and well structured, the oak and tannin well integrated into the whole. A lasting note of cardamom and black tea sparks on the finish.

name: Underwood 2018 Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: This wine delivers a lovely core of black cherry-candy fruit accompanied by light, refined tannins and balanced acidity. It is a perfect choice for near-term drinking.

name: Ultraviolet 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendocino Ridge)
review: This is a good, full-bodied and sturdy wine, offering lots of tight black-cherry and black-olive flavors and a firm tannic texture that will cut through the richest meat and pasta dishes.

name: Tussock Jumper 2018 Zinfandel (California)
review: This full-bodied wine offers earthy, funky, black peppery aromas followed by good blackberry and strawberry flavors on a rich texture.

name: Tumwater 2018 Black Label Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Toasty aromas accompany well-ripened black cherry and fruit that tiptoe along the border of cassis. The dark color and overall depth suggest that this is a substantial wine that will cellar nicely for six to eight years. Give that lush fruit a bit more bottle age and it will match up with the barrel flavors from aging 18 months in 50% new oak.

name: Tumwater 2018 Estate Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Still quite young, the aromatics speak mostly of new barrels, but the flavors bind together that barrel toast with spicy cherry fruit. Tight and balanced, this wine needs to unwind a bit, but all the components are in place, including a lovely streak of milk chocolate through the finish.

name: Tuli 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: Skunky, thick and flat, this wine has a robust herbaceouness that detracts from any fruit component being able to shine.

name: Troon 2018 Côtes du Kubli Cowhorn Vineyard Syrah-Grenache (Applegate Valley)
review: While its estate vineyards are being replanted, Troon is sourcing some grapes from Cowhorn, as here. The blend is 72% Syrah and 28% Grenache, and it's a firm, even muscular wine, with a tight mix of blueberry, plum and black cherry fruit. There's a hint of leather among the complex and engaging aromatics. The concentration and length suggest that further aging will be rewarding.

name: Troon 2018 Cowhorn Vineyard Grenache (Applegate Valley)
review: While its estate vineyards are being replanted, Troon is sourcing some grapes from Cowhorn, as shown here. Bright and penetrating, this pure Grenache brings complex details of truffle and licorice around spiced apple and berry fruit. The mouthfeel is especially drying and astringent, as in some concrete-fermented wines, though this was not done that way. Match it to a fat, juicy burger for best results.

name: Trisaetum 2018 Family Reserve MMXVIII Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is a reserve level beyond the Estates Reserve, with pretty blackberry and black cherry fruit. It's wrapped in sweet spices that may indicate more new barrels were used, or different toast levels. In any event, it's set against citrusy acidity and finishes with a smooth lick of chocolate.

name: Trisaetum 2018 Estates Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Fruit from the three estate vineyards, located in three different Willamette Valley AVAs, comprise this blend. It's a full-throttle effort that opens smooth across the entire palate. Berry and cherry fruit flavors and a streak of Dr Pepper combine with tangy citrus to craft a lively wine that is lifted through the finish.

name: Trisaetum 2018 Wichmann Dundee Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Fruit flavors of hard cherry candy anchor this potent, focused wine. It's spicy and tart, with lemony acidity and a base of clean earth. It will profit from another year or two of bottle age prior to hitting a long drinking window. Drink 2023–2035.

name: Trisaetum 2018 Coast Range Estate Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: One of Trisaetum's three estate vineyards, each in a different AVA, this brings bright and tangy citrus and pie cherry fruit flavors front and center, along with a light dusting of cocoa. Firm acidity and overall elegant styling are the hallmarks here.

name: Trisaetum 2018 Ribbon Ridge Estate Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Lower in alcohol than its siblings from 2018, this offers briary berries and savory herbs in equal proportions. It shows some stem and skin flavors, and the tannins have a green tea astringency. The overall balance is fine, and this may well benefit from a couple of more years in bottle.

name: Trisaetum 2018 Willamette Valley Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Trisaetum's most widely available Pinot, this is a barrel selection from the estate vineyards, most likely younger vines. Tart raspberry, plum and cherry fruit comes up fresh and forward, and the wine is sculpted into clean varietal flavors. It reflects the same vinification and cellar care as the more exclusive bottlings and may be enjoyed over the next four or five years.

name: Trinity Oaks 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This firm-textured and tangy wine is nicely structured for pairing with rich protein dishes. Vivid cranberry and red-cherry flavors are accented by light toast and baking spices.

name: Trentadue La Storia 2018 Estate Block 303 Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)
review: Char and oak lead to a grippy concentration of blackberry, plum and cinnamon cola in this voluptuous, full-bodied wine.

name: Trentadue 2018 OPR Red (California)
review: Baking spices and marshmallow flavors vie for attention in this oaky full-bodied wine.

name: Trefethen 2018 Estate Grown Merlot (Oak Knoll District)
review: Herbaceous and tart in red currant, this is a graceful and balanced red wine that is soft and supple in tannin. Ready for the table, it delivers consistency and structure without fuss, delivering elegant cedar and clove tones as it finishes.

name: Trail Traveler 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A density of oak and tannin underlies simple layers of black cherry cola and nutmeg in this medium-bodied, smoothly textured wine.

name: Tortoise Creek 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: A strong beefy, peppery character runs through this full-bodied wine from first whiff to the finish. Mostly smoky, dark spices fill the palate while moderate tannins shape a firm mouthfeel.

name: Tortoise Creek 2018 The Chelonian Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: Earthy and black-peppery aromas lead to mostly oaky flavors of cedar, maple, brown sugar and plums in this full-bodied, and moderately tannic wine.

name: Tongue Dancer 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This bold wine is made from a blend of Pratt Vineyard Sexton Road grapes and fruit from the Putnam Vineyard on the far coast of Annapolis. Juicy, broad and lush on the palate, it is steeped in etched flavors of strawberry, white pepper, clove and orange peel, with a jammy richness on the finish.

name: Tondré 2018 Tondre Grapefield Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Sandalwood aromas wrap around the strawberry, light orange-rind and anise touches on the nose of this complex bottling. There's a baking-spice kick to the front of the palate, while green peppercorn, hibiscus, rooibos tea and fruit-punch flavors lead into a tangy red-plum finish.

name: Tolosa 2018 El Coro Vineyard Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: From a cool-climate site in the new appellation, this red is tangy in acidity and elusive in fruit, made in a delicate build that develops in the glass. Earthy spice underlies a core of rose petal, cherry and cranberry with firm, supple tannin providing structure.

name: Tolosa 2018 Drum Canyon Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Black cherry, vanilla, struck stone and hickory smoke aromas expertly combine fruit, rocks and earth on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Dried lilac and wisteria flavors meet with a black-plum core on the palate, where long-lingering acidity and grippy tannins ensure a long finish.

name: Tolosa 2018 Primera Edna Ranch Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Very lush and inviting on the nose, this top-shelf bottling by Frederick Delivert begins with aromas of softly poached Bing cherries, dark strawberry cream, hints of slate and well integrated oak. The palate is wrapped in perfectly chiseled tannins and crisp fresh acidity, offering lush red-fruit and baking-spice flavors.

name: Tolosa 2018 Solomon Hills Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: There's a strong kick of turned earth, dust and chaparral on the nose of this bottling from a very cool-climate site, as well as touches of mulberry and smoke. Clean lines of cranberry, red plum and wild strawberry pop on the complex palate, which is bolstered by earthy herbs.

name: Tolosa 2018 Hollister Edna Ranch Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Roasted game and dried meat aromas meet with smashed pomegranate, dried cranberry and mud cakes on the nose of this bottling. The sip is framed by upright tannins that start quite rigid but open up toward cranberry sauce and orange-rind flavors.

name: Tolosa 2018 1772 Edna Ranch Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Dark cherry and earthy, damp campfire aromas meet with wet gravel and savory teriyaki touches on the nose of this bottling. There is plenty of sizzle to the sip, where bright acid frames the smoked cherry and strawberry flavors.

name: Tolosa 2018 Apex Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Plump cherry, red-currant and cranberry aromas are very clean and ripe on the nose of this single-vineyard expression, yet it is the slight hints of earth, herb and oak that make it compelling. The palate pops with mulberry and raspberry flavors that are framed by smoky oak and racy hibiscus-laced acidity that make for a both bright and savory experience.

name: Tolosa 2018 1772 Pacific Wind Edna Ranch Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: A beautiful nose of Bing cherry, exotic black raspberry, hibiscus and rose petal makes for a fresh and bright entry to this bottling. Cherry, strawberry and raspberry flavors pop on the sip, enhanced by delicate touches of bay lead, wild thyme and sagebrush—delicate but expressive.

name: Tobias 2018 Formation Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma-Mendocino)
review: This is an impressive red at the price, a 50-50 split between vineyards in Sonoma and Mendocino counties that includes 20% Malbec. Approachable well-integrated tannins support black-cherry fruit and oak, with a powerful finish of dried herb and tobacco.

name: Tobias 2018 Pauli Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Howell Mountain)
review: A 100% varietal wine that beautifully captures the stellar vintage and appellation, this wine opens in voluptuous berry aromatics, with seasonings of clove, nutmeg, sage, cedar and cigar box. Elongated tannins substantiate the length and breadth, firming its structure and complexity. Enjoy best from 2028–2033.

name: Time & Direction 2018 Diamond Cutter Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Though labeled as a red wine, Syrah makes up fully 95%, with the rest Mourvèdre. The aromas are effusive, with notes of blueberry, raspberry, white pepper, herb and flower, showing an appealing sense of purity. The silky smooth, fresh-feeling palate more than delivers on all of that promise, while bringing a real sense of freshness. It's as complete delight and a superb value.

name: Tilth 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: The majority of the grapes in this wine are whole-cluster fermented and then aged 10 months in French oak, a minority of it new. This is a fresh, soft and supple red, with robust earthiness. Forest, black tea and tar ride along a wavelength of tangy strawberry and rhubarb, finishing focused and peppery.

name: Three Thieves 2018 Red (California)
review: Soft and smooth, this lightly sweet wine has generous berry and cherry flavors and a medium body.

name: Three Thieves 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This is a good-quality, dry wine at a good price. It offers moderate cherry and berry flavors that find balance with some fresh acidity and mild tannins.

name: Three Thieves 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: On the light side, this friendly and fruity wine carries tasty red-cherry and sweet plum flavors on a rather soft, smooth texture. It's medium bodied, smooth and easy to sip.

name: Three Sticks 2018 Price Family Estates Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Blended from a selection of estate sites, from Walala and Gap's Crown to Durell, this red is hearty in blue fruit, black cherry and earthy forest floor. Burly tannins wrap around the dense core of length and expanse—a bold statement of a wine.

name: Three Rivers 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are fruit forward, with notes of blackberry, black currant and boysenberry, followed by char, herb and tar. The flavors explode on the palate, bringing plushness and richness but also balance. Firm, slightly astringent tannins back it up.

name: Three Furies 2018 The Angry One Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Slightly sour aromas of blackberry juice, orange peel and loamy soil show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is a bit tart and reductive, with tart and blackberry flavors.

name: The Show 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This bold and full-bodied wine is very deep in color, offers exuberant crushed berry aromas and very fruity, concentrated flavors that are nicely wrapped in moderate tannins.

name: The Royal Nonesuch Farm 2018 Red (York Mountain)
review: Black plum, lilac and black pepper make for a deep and rich yet dry and savory combination on the nose of this blend of 67% Grenache, 18% Syrah, 14% Graciano and 1% Clairette Blanche. It's well wound on the sip, offering black-pepper, cassis, black-cherry and star anise flavors, all wrapped in chiseled tannins.
