name: The Royal Bull 2018 Crown Jewels Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Syrah, Grenache and Mourvèdre each make up about a third of this wine. The aromas intrigue, with notes of iron, funk, dried herb and brown spices. It's ripe in style, with plush feeling fruit and savory flavors that are quite jammy.

name: The Messenger 2018 Chara Red (Shenandoah Valley (CA))
review: Beef and black-pepper aromas give this wine a big personality without totally overdoing it. It's dry, full bodied, moderately tannic and backs up the oaky, spicy notes with good, subdued berry flavors.

name: The Joy Fantastic 2018 Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: There's impressive density evident on the nose of this bottling, starting with charcoal, roasted meat and beach tar, as well as savory tones of green olive and black pepper. Charred berry flavors come through on the sip, as do iodine, smoked meat, dried herbs, pepper and a hint of caramel. Drink 2022–2038.

name: The Joy Fantastic 2018 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Rugged aromas of juniper, bay leaf, toasted sage and Kalamata olive ride atop a core of black raspberry and plum on the nose of this bottling from a vineyard planted in 2014. The palate is utterly savory, offering sage, crumpled flowers, green olive and juniper against the dark-plum backdrop.

name: The Infinite Monkey Theorem 2018 Cabernet Franc (Colorado)
review: Raspberry and cherry pop on the nose, with some vanilla, pepper and leather tones adding depth. The tannins offer gentle grip, but the acidity is tart and sunny, keeping this fresh and structured, and giving a solid base to flavors of ripe plum and tart cranberry wrapped in vanilla. It finishes on tart berry, with lingering tannins making this a good match for fatty protein.

name: The Infinite Monkey Theorem 2018 Malbec (Colorado)
review: Aromas of blueberry and plum are highlighted by pink peppercorn on the nose. Juicy and fruit-forward upfront, it's loaded with cherry, pomegranate and cranberry. Sweet vanilla tones emerge with sarsaparilla and oak tones. The acidity is juicy and bouncy, while tannins add just enough structure to keep things interesting. Spice elements rule the finish, lacing around the fruit core.

name: The Infinite Monkey Theorem 2018 Syrah (Colorado)
review: This wine offers aromas of pomegranate, cherry and orange zest on the charming nose. The palate is juicy and tart, loaded with fruit flavors that mirror the nose, with an amped-up orange flavor, zesty acidity and notes of cracked pepper. Sandy tannins structure the wine and frame the fruit.

name: The Huntsman 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of sweet black currant and sugared plum are followed by lighter-styled, well-balanced currant flavors. The oak is pulled way back, giving an unadorned look at the variety that will win fans.

name: The Hideaway 2018 Time Will Tell, We Won't Cabernet Sauvignon (Santa Barbara County)
review: Fresh and snappy black- and red-fruit aromas meet with wet stone and light herbs on the nose of this bottling. The palate is fresh and fun with black peppercorns, crisp blackberry and richer black-cherry tones that ride acidity into the finish.

name: The Hideaway 2018 Motel Zinfandel (Santa Barbara County)
review: Pristine aromas of blackberry paste meet with white pepper and wet stone on the fresh nose of this bottling, which is one of the few Zinfandels from this county. Clean lines of boysenberry, fennel stalk and more pepper and stone consume the palate.

name: The Guide 2018 Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: Aromas of cinnamon, peppermint, spice, smoke, cherry and celery salt intrigue. The palate is light, elegant and well-balanced, fleshing out over time, with a light lick of tannins. Blood orange notes linger on the finish. It brings a lot of appeal.

name: The Four Graces 2018 Dundee Hills Reserve Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: A pretty violet aroma leads the way into a solid wine packed with blueberry, blackberry and plum fruit flavors. A hint of cola and a streak of dark chocolate contribute to a potent, powerful Pinot that can take on almost any sort of meat entrée, including a big burger.

name: The Four Graces 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Fruity pomegranate and blackberry flavors are front and center, with a gentle streak of licorice putting a dark note through the finish. It's forward and tasty, but doesn't show much depth as the finish fades away. Drink while it's young and fresh.

name: The Feminist Party 2018 G-S-M (Santa Barbara County)
review: Brisk hits of cherry and hibiscus are surrounded by more leathery tones of smoked meat on the nose of this blend of 50% Grenache, 30% Mourvèdre and 20% Syrah. The core flavors of red cherry and raspberry are lifted by toasted sage and seared pork, providing the lighter bodied wine with lots of intrigue.

name: The Calling 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Candied Bing cherry and fresh hibiscus aromas make this an immediately likable bottling by Jim Nantz and his team, while the star anise, gingerbread and sweet herb aromas add complexity. The palate is lively and fresh with more red fruit and flowers as well as cinnamon and wild chaparral spices, leading into a eucalyptus-oil-kissed finish.

name: Thacher 2018 Glenrose Vineyard Cinsault (Adelaida District)
review: This is an extremely fresh and zesty bottling, quite good chilled as well. It's a shade darker than rosé in the glass, and offers crunchy aromas of crisp red currant, hibiscus, fruit punch, sumac and cinnamon. The palate is zippy with acidity and lively with white-pepper spice that lifts up the cranberry, rose-petal and blood-orange-rind flavors.

name: Thacher 2018 Shell Creek Vineyard Valdiguié (Paso Robles Highlands District)
review: From a rarely seen grape once known as “Napa Gamay,” this bottling begins with hearty boysenberry, crumpled purple flowers and a strong streak of potting soil and turned loam on the nose. The palate is uniquely earthy and fresh at once, with grippy tannins and strong acidity delivering a gritty combination of wet soil and black raspberry.

name: Textbook 2018 Merlot (Napa Valley)
review: Cinnamon and clove accent an elegant core of plum in this supple, smooth and balanced wine. Offering great value for the impressive quality, it endures in length and texture, with a finishing touch of black pepper.

name: Terra d'Oro 2018 SHR Field Blend Zinfandel (Amador County)
review: This interesting and complex wine blends savory and mineral flavors with ripe berries on a full body and smooth, non-tannic texture. The 13% Petite Sirah and 7% Barbera add depth and lift to the Zin.

name: Terra d'Oro 2018 Home Vineyard Zinfandel (Amador County)
review: This is a very good, richly flavored wine, quite full bodied and generously spiced, with vanilla and brown sugar notes.

name: Terra d'Oro 2018 Teroldego (Amador County)
review: This full-bodied, bold wine is rich in color, flavor and texture, showing a black hue with deep ruby rim, aromas of cooked blueberries and dark chocolate, and generous black-fruit flavors wrapped in dense fine-grained tannins. Best through 2026.

name: Terra d'Oro 2018 Aglianico (Amador County)
review: This full-bodied muscular wine is bold in flavor and moderately tannic in texture, offering mineral, pepper and smoke nuances along with rich blackberry and black cherry. It nicely balances elements of strength and complexity.

name: Terra d'Oro 2018 Barbera (Amador County)
review: This is a bold, bright wine that packs plenty of fresh blackberry and black-plum flavors that are held by moderate tannins for a good, appetizing grip. Concentrated fruit flavors and subtle vanilla, nutmeg and clove accents add to its appeal.

name: Ternion 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County-Sonoma County-Monterey County)
review: Very shy aromas barely allow light cranberry and wet earth scents to arise on the nose of this three-county blend. Simple pomegranate flavors meet with light earth on the palate.

name: Tensley 2018 Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Crumpled purple flowers give a floral kick to the nose of this bottling, which is also full of dark blackberry at its core and pinches of pepper on the edges. Herbal tones of crushed thyme, peppercorn and bay leaf arise on the sip, giving nuance to the dark-berry core, with popping acidity driving into the finish.

name: Tensley 2018 Fundamental Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Coast)
review: Blackberry and dried mint meet with a light oak toast on the nose of this bottling. It's very dry on the palate, where black-plum, elderberry and violet flavors join with hearty tobacco-leaf elements.

name: Tensley 2018 BMT Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: One of the annual magnum-only releases from Joey Tensley, this one, named after his niece, blends syrah from Rodney's and Colson Canyon vineyards with 25% Grenache from the latter as well. Rich black-plum, cola, smoked beef and pepper aromas hit all the right notes on the nose, while the palate offers hearty cassis and sarsaparilla spice. Drink 2022–2038.

name: Tensley 2018 Noir Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Deep and unctuous on the nose, this bottling entices with rich aromas of violet, cassis and berry pie. It's thick and lush on the palate as well, where black-fruit and purple-flowers flavors dance in a powerful yet elegant harmony.

name: Tensley 2018 Colson Canyon Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Very dark in the glass, this single-vineyard bottling from a unique vineyard high in the rocky mountains above the Santa Maria Valley shows incredible intensity on the nose, with dark berry, plum, cocoa, cola and light floral freshness. The palate is purely purple and black fruits, enhanced by crushed espresso bean and cracked pepper—a head-turner wine.

name: Tensley 2018 Fundamental Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Baked boysenberrry, mahogany, clove and juniper aromas show on the nose of this bottling. Dark-plum flavors are framed by solid acidity and tannins on the palate, while hints of bay leaf and juniper also show.

name: Tensley 2018 OGT Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Named for winemaker Joey Tensley's son, Oliver Gunnar Tensley, this magnum bottling combines from the Tensley, Colson Canyon and Thompson vineyards. The nose is intense with pepper, violets and açai aromas, leading into a palate that is deep and rich with cassis and cracked pepper, yet leveled by tons of acidity. Drink 2022–2040.

name: Tensley 2018 Tensley Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Crumpled lavender, tart black plum and a hint of teriyaki show on the nose of this bottling from the Tensley family's home vineyard. Savory flavors of roasted beef, crusted in rosemary and lavender, meet with cassis and are wrapped in firm tannins on the palate.

name: Tensley 2018 All Blocks Red (Santa Barbara County)
review: Crumpled purple flowers, cola and blackberry aromas show on the nose of this blend of 85% Grenache, 14% Syrah and 1% Mourvèdre from the Tensley and Colson Canyon vineyards. Purple-fruit flavors hit the palate first, following by toasty mocha and charred meats.

name: Tensley 2018 Turner Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Joey Tensley's expertise in rich Syrahs makes a perfect partner to this cool-climate site in the Sta. Rita Hills. Intense aromas of peppercorn, roast lamb, tar and elderberry coulis lead into a dense, viscous and yet enlivening palate, loaded with flavors of spicy cracked pepper and beef yakitori in a blackberry dipping sauce. It only grows more intense with time. Drink now through 2038.

name: Tenshen 2018 Red (Santa Barbara County)
review: Dense blackberry, black plum, oak and coconut husk show on the nose of this blend. There is dense blackberry fruit and dried herbs on the palate, whose medium-bodied mouthfeel leads into a coffee finish.

name: Tehachapi 2018 Malbec (Paso Robles)
review: From the Shoemaker Vineyard, this bottling begins with aromas of baked black cherry, cardamom and cola, proving very lush and enticing on the nose. The richness carries to the sip, where black-cherry, coffee-cream and vanilla flavors align.

name: Tehachapi 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: A heavy woody character coats this wine, from the aroma of sawdust and toast to baked earth and meat flavors. It shows very little varietal character.

name: Tehachapi 2018 Reserve Syrah (California)
review: This full-bodied well-concentrated wine offers a tamarind aroma, beefy and rich berrylike flavors and a dash of black pepper. It shows attractive cedar and toast nuances and has appropriately firm tannins.

name: Tehachapi 2018 Reserve Merlot (California)
review: This dark and opulent wine grown at 3,900 feet elevation in Southern California is a revelation. It seamlessly blends rich baking spices with concentrated blackberry, dark chocolate and black fig flavors, while fine-grained tannins add to its luxurious texture.

name: Tehachapi 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: A dusty, earthy aroma leads to quite oaky flavors and a firmly tannic texture in this full-bodied wine. Some ripe blackberry and blueberry flavors do come through to help balance it out.

name: Technique 2018 Proprietary Red (Stags Leap District)
review: This blend offers juicy dark-fruit and cocoa-powder flavors around powdered tannin and generous oak. An undercurrent of dried herb lingers on the finish.

name: Teachworth 2018 Pinot Noir (Los Carneros)
review: Thick and juicy in dark black fruit, this wine offers a wealth of toasted oak, forest-floor and black-tea components that work well together. The tannin weight and oak give it a hearty heaviness for fans of a bigger style.

name: Tassajara 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Dark-berry and plum aromas meet with roasted meat and red flowers on the nose of this bottling. A creamy texture carries cranberry and river-stone flavors across the pleasant (if simple) palate.

name: Taraval 2018 Brick Kiln Red (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of muddled mulberry, strawberry, nutmeg and light cardamom are fresh and spicy on the nose of this bottling. It's clean and straightforward on the palate, with flavors of boysenberry pie, rose petals and baking spice, wrapped in fine tannins and a fresh acidity.

name: Table Eleven 2018 La Encantada Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Pristine aromas of black raspberry, crushed river stone and a delicate flake of mace converge for a complex and complete nose on this bottling of the Pommard clone. The palate is lively and zesty at first, then offers waves of fresh berry and light fennel pollen—altogether a very focused expression.

name: Tablas Creek 2018 Cotes de Tablas Red (Adelaida District)
review: There's an utter smoothness to this wine that must come from the decades of experience that the winery has in blending Rhône varieties. This mix of 45% Grenache, 33% Syrah, 12% Counoise and 10% Mourvèdre starts with cherry, hibiscus, crushed gravel and cola on the nose. The palate combines berry, baking-spice and cappuccino flavors, wrapped in a leathery structure.

name: Tablas Creek 2018 Patelin de Tablas Red (Paso Robles)
review: There's a strongly stony streak on the nose of this blend of 40% Syrah, 33% Grenache, 21% Mourvèdre, 3% Counoise and 3% Terret Noir, which also offers dried berry and purple-flower aromas. The palate is earthy and full of rocky flavors, yet also offers black-raspberry, red-plum and wild herb elements.

name: Syncline 2018 Estate Grown Steep Creek Ranch Red (Columbia Gorge (WA))
review: This wine is a blend of 48% Mondeuse, 28% Syrah, 14% Grenache and 10% Gamay Noir. The aromas aren't ready to reveal their charms at first, with light notes of dried herb, raspberry, crushed flower, mineral and cranberry that open over time. The flavors are medium bodied, tart, puckering and focused. Firm but polished tannins back it up. It brings a vibrant sense of acidity seldom seen in the state on red wines, along with a pleasing rusticity and plenty of crunchy red fruit. It's a statement wine for this appellation.

name: Syncline 2018 McKinley Springs Vineyard Cinsault (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Initially locked up, the aromas open to reveal notes of wild strawberry and other red berries followed by achingly pure soft, layered red fruit flavors. It's a knee buckler with an exquisitely long finish, drinking like a bowl of fresh fruit.

name: Syncline 2018 Subduction Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Syrah makes up half of this wine, with the rest Grenache (18%) and near equal parts Mourvèdre, Carignan and Cinsault. The aromas are arresting, with notes of blue fruit, smoked meat, strawberry and dried herb, showing pleasing complexity. The palate starts out quite elegant in style, fleshing out with time, juicy, with bright, focused, pure fruit flavors. A long, fruit-filled finish caps it off. There's a lot going on. Pair it with roasted pork loin.

name: Syncline 2018 Estate Grown Gamay (Columbia Gorge (WA))
review: This is the first release of this variety off the winery's estate vineyard and a rare offering from the state. The aromas provide vibrant notes of raspberry, Red Vines candy, peppery spice, red gummy and strawberry. The palate is light, tart and elegant, with the achingly pure fruit front and center. It's flat-out delicious—required reading for anyone interested in one of the cutting edges of Washington wine.

name: Sweet Cheeks 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: The winery style is to override fruit and varietal specificity with dominating flavors of tobacco and vanilla. Tannins are astringent and quickly follow. This is unquestionably a flavorful effort that will appeal to some palates, but don't look for classic Willamette Valley Pinot Noir styling.

name: Sutcliffe 2018 Pinot Noir (Colorado)
review: Ruby red and transparent. Light vanilla and raspberry aromas round out the tart edges of strawberry, apple, red plum and tart cherry. The palate is dry and flat, offering flavors of red apple peel and cocoa powder that lack concentration. Tannins linger on the tip of the tongue and dry out the palate, creating a scratchy wool texture dying on a short, dull finish.

name: Sunstone 2018 Villa Soleil Red (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Strong aromas of berry and cassis kick off the nose of this blend of 54% Merlot, 24% Cabernet Franc and 22% Syrah. It lands with a rich heft on the palate, where aged strawberry and gooey black-currant flavors are wrapped in vanilla tones.

name: Sunstone 2018 Founder's Reserve Red (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Aromas of dark-red berries, warm fruitcake and a touch of toasted grape seed kick off the nose of this blend of 80% Merlot and 20% Cabernet Franc. There is a pleasant freshness to the palate, where boysenberry and blueberry flavors meet with roasted wood, cured meat and a rocky minerality.

name: Sunstone 2018 Rapsodie Red (Santa Barbara County)
review: Roasted blackberry, coffee cream, liquid lilac and grape-seed aromas show on the nose of this blend of 70% Grenache, 24% Syrah and 6% Mourvèdre. Crushed berries and grape-seed tones also show on the sip.

name: Sunstone 2018 Reserve Petit Verdot (Paso Robles)
review: Very dark in the glass, this reserve bottling shows smoked meat, black-currant, cola, fig, coffee and crushed black-rock aromas on the dynamic nose. There is a firm tannic structure to the sip, where roasted plum and berry flavors meet with nutmeg spice and a caramel finish.

name: Sunstone 2018 Grenache (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Light and delicate on the nose, this bottling from El Camino Real Vineyard offers red-rose, cherry and tangy red-plum-skin aromas. There is a tartness to the palate, where red-currant and raspberry-juice flavors are hit with pepper and vanilla spices.

name: Sun Break 2018 Cortell-Rose Vineyard Ariane Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: A hundred percent whole-cluster fermented, this elegant, plummy wine is tart to the point of sour, but in a good, pie cherry sort of style. It was aged 16 months in mostly neutral barrels, and like the other Sun Break Pinots, this had no added sulfites. There are hints of mocha and a smooth, tangy finish.

name: Sun Break 2018 Marie-Paule Cortell-Rose Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This cuvée was half fermented with whole clusters. It's a precise, focused wine—the blackberry and black cherry fruit has a gentle squirt of lemony acidity. It's lively and clean, with no sulfites added, and a lovely showcase for this outstanding vineyard.

name: Sun Break 2018 Cortell-Rose Vineyard Colette Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: One of a trio that provide an interesting glimpse into the use of whole clusters (or no whole clusters as here) in the fermentation. Vibrant and lushly aromatic, this lip-smacking wine is jam-packed with raspberry fruit, blackberry fruit and juicy acids. It's tart and streaked lightly with espresso and baking spices.

name: Succession 2018 Malbec (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: With fruit coming from Antoine Creek Vineyard, light aromas of varnish and cherry lead to tart, sleek, center-focused flavors. The variety seems hard to find.

name: Succession 2018 Reserve Syrah (Red Mountain)
review: Fruit for this wine comes from Quintessence Vineyard. The aromas pop, with notes of raspberry and Craisins. It's lighter than the aromas might suggest, front loaded and pulling up on the finish. It seems quite overripe, with the fruit dried out.

name: Succession 2018 The 5th Year Red (Yakima Valley)
review: Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon each make up a third of this wine. The aromas are quiet out of the gate, with notes of plum and currant. The palate is full of plump raspberry flavors. There's an appealing sense of purity.

name: Succession 2018 Reserve Candy Mountain Merlot (Yakima Valley)
review: Light aromas of cherry pit and herb lead to sleek, tart flavors. The palate seems light and disjointed.

name: Succession 2018 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)
review: All the fruit for this wine comes from Quintessence Vineyard, which is quickly establishing itself as one of the best in the state. Generous aromas of raspberry, chocolate and dried herb lead to a palate full of fruit of tightly wound tannins. The acidity brings a sense of vibrancy. Give it a bit of additional time in the bottle or decant.

name: Succession 2018 Syrah (Yakima Valley)
review: Fruit for this wine comes from Candy Mountain Vineyard, which recently became its own subappellation of the valley. Aromas of earth, raspberry and spice aromas lead to flavors that show a pleasing sense of elegance. It's a pretty offering of this variety.

name: Succession 2018 Bridge Builder's Blend Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This wine is 51% Cabernet Sauvignon, 32% Cabernet Franc, 11% Merlot and 6% Malbec. Aromas of flowers, freshly chopped herbs and orange rind lead to flavors that bring a pleasing sense of freshness.

name: Succession 2018 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Famiglia and Couette vineyards comprise the fruit for this 100% varietal wine. Musk, shoe polish and flower aromas are followed by lightly styled flavors. It's an odd mix.

name: Succession 2018 Cabernet Franc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit for this wine comes from Weinbau, Famiglia and Quintessence vineyards. Aromas of raspberry jam, tar and spice are followed by ripe red fruit flavors that display a sense of elegance. It's alternately a bit overripe and seemingly a little under extracted—a strange mix.

name: Succession 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is 100% varietal, with fruit coming from Lawrence, Weinbau, Famiglia and Quintessence vineyards. The aromas offer notes of char, dried herb, roasted coffee, graphite and dark chocolate. Chocolate and coffee flavors follow, showing a pleasing tartness. The oak plays a prominent part in the show but plays it well.

name: Structure 2018 Foundation Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This blend of Stillwater Creek, Wallula and Red Willow fruit is everything there is to love about this variety: compelling aromatics and great depth of flavor. Alluring aromas of flower fresh, chopped parsley, boysenberry, smoked meat and medicine lead to full-bodied layered flavors. Though delicious now, it appears it will be near eternal.

name: Structure 2018 Bauhaus Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: A blend of Wallula (60%) and Stillwater Creek fruit, the aromas are captivating, with notes of dark raspberry, bramble and herb that show a piercing sense of purity. The flavors are dazzling, showing intensity and depth but also elegance and balance. Coffee notes linger on the finish. It's a frankly delicious wine, but it's the structure—and the quality at this price—that impresses most. Best after 2023 with an extremely long life ahead of that.

name: Structure 2018 Spire Merlot (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas bring notes of whole orange, sweet flower, raspberry and plum. Plush plum and pomegranate flavors follow. Coffee notes linger on the finish. It brings a pleasing sense of texture, freshness and terrific staying power.

name: Structure 2018 The Newel Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is 60% Cabernet Sauvignon and 40% Syrah. Its fresh garden-herb, flower stem and cherry aromas are out front. The fruit flavors are palate coating, showing pleasing density and intensity.

name: Stringer 2018 Shokrian Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Ripe and easily accessible aromas of black cherry, star anise and cardamom draw the nose into this bottling from a vineyard near Los Alamos. The big fruit flavors blow up on the palate too, with cherry, hibiscus, vanilla and oak toast, yet a firm tannic texture and plenty of acidity maintain balance.

name: Stressed Vines 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Juicy black cherry and strawberry highlight a thick, concentrated center of balanced tannin and integrated oak in this well-made wine. Forested spice accents and enlivens the fruit.

name: Storm 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Raspberry, hibiscus and carnation make for a zesty, tart and floral nose on this countywide blend by South African winemaker Ernst Storm. There's a vibrant explosion on the palate of black-raspberry sorbet, decorated by pinches of thyme and sagebrush.

name: Stolpman 2018 Mourvèdre (Ballard Canyon)
review: There's a pure focus to this bottling that will benefit cellaring. Aromas of roasted black plum, black pepper and lavender lead into a palate loaded with iron shavings, graphite, charred beef and taut elderberry fruit. It's tight, and ready for years of enjoyment. Drink now through 2035.

name: Stolpman 2018 Para Maria de los Tecolotes Red (Santa Barbara County)
review: It's hard to find a better wine for the price than this blend of 80% Syrah and 20% Petite Verdot. Blistered black-plum, charcoal and fresh boysenberry aromas kick off the nose, while the palate blends black pepper, boysenberry and smoke. It is a versatile table wine, as apt for tacos and barbecue as it is for prime rib and roast lamb.

name: Stolpman 2018 Estate Grown Syrah (Ballard Canyon)
review: Clean aromas of boysenberry, wet stone and sifted ash show on the lively nose of this bottling. The palate is intensely grippy with earthy tension, combining freshly smashed berry flavors with aromatic rosewater flavors and just a hint of game.

name: Stolpman 2018 Grenache (Ballard Canyon)
review: Crunchy red currant, carnations, rose petals and wet slate hint at some carbonic fermentation on the nose of this bottling. The palate falls in line as well, with refreshing red-fruit and red-rose-petal flavors that are crisp and zippy.

name: Stolpman 2018 La Cuadrilla Red (Ballard Canyon)
review: This bottling was a pioneer in uplifting the education and funding of farmworkers when it began years ago, and the results keep getting better. Aromas of cooked plums, cracked pepper, wood spice and beef jerky show on the nose of the blend of 70% Syrah, 15% Grenache and 15% Sangiovese, while smashed berries, toasted sage and coffee beans arise on the sip.

name: Stolo 2018 Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Quite light in the glass, this bottling offers wild aromas of rust, tarry earth, mossy forest floor and crisp raspberry. There's a strong wood-spice streak across the palate, where toasted cedar and sandalwood flavors surround the black-plum and white-pepper flavors.

name: Stolo 2018 Syrah (San Luis Obispo County)
review: This extremely cool-climate style of Syrah can be divisive, but this is a brilliant version for those who crave the savory experience. Aromas of underripe blackberry, seaweed, green peppercorn, rosemary, lavender and charred lamb lead into a palate that bursts with acidity. Flavors of violet, bay leaf, wet rocks and more pepper decorate the purple fruits.

name: Stoller 2018 Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Production has climbed substantially on this cuvée. It's a tightly wound mix of sharp red berry and peppery herb. The leaf and stem flavors wrap into a lightly earthy finish. The wine was aged in mostly neutral and 15% new French oak.

name: Stoller 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Lighter than the previous vintage, at least in flavor, this mixes rhubarb and strawberry fruit, with a dash of sweet cherry. The tannins are proportionate, and finish with a green and bitter edge. The wine spent just under 11 months in 38% new oak.

name: Stockman 2018 Love Ranch Syrah (Madera County)
review: This is an earthy, savory-tasting wine that offers sunbaked aromas, medium body and dusty, leathery accents to the modest fruit flavors.

name: Stockman 2018 Cotes et Vallee Red (Madera County)
review: An earthy, leathery aroma and mature, evolved, gamy flavors make this wine a kind of curiosity for a new release.

name: Stockman 2018 Laurie's Vineyard Grenache (Madera)
review: Juicy, concentrated and well balanced, this full-bodied wine is packed with ripe red and black fruits, and well structured with moderate tannins and good acidity. It has spicy graphite and tobacco aromas, a generous, mouthfilling texture and an attractive ripeness in flavor.

name: Sterling 2018 Meritage (California)
review: This full-bodied concentrated wine made mostly from Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon shows some wet-rock minerality in the aroma as well as rich red- and black-fruit flavors and supports them with a lightly tannic texture.

name: Sterling 2018 Vintner's Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Well concentrated and full of plump plum and blackberry-jam flavors, this full-bodied wine also sports moderate tannins that give a good grip to the texture. Additions of 17% Petite Sirah, and dashes of Petit Verdot and Syrah help to boost its color and structure.

name: Sterling 2018 Vintner's Collection Merlot (California)
review: Another example of the excellent recent wines in Sterling's Vintner's Collection. Supple and flavorful, this full-bodied pour feels smooth and velvety as it offers quite ripe and rich black fruit flavors that are subtly accented by vanilla and milk chocolate nuances.

name: Sterling 2018 Vintner's Collection Pinot Noir (California)
review: This is a relatively light-bodied wine, showing great balance, a lithe texture and lip-smacking red-cherry and cranberry flavors. It would be a versatile pour at the dinner table.

name: Stephen Vincent 2018 Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: This easy-going wine is full bodied and offers good, pure cherry and berry flavors and good balance. Not complicated, and very attractive.

name: Stephen Vincent 2018 Crimson Red (North Coast)
review: Deep, ripe blackberries and sweet-seeming baking spices make an attractive combo in this full-bodied superfruity wine.

name: Stasis 2018 Murmur Vineyard Gamay Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Very light in the glass, this bottling offers all the hallmarks of this exciting grape: crushed raspberry, white pepper and a pastry warmth. The is a great snap to the sip where fresh cranberry flavors meet with that peppery spice, held in place by a taut, energetic texture.

name: Starside 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: More than half of this wine comes from Discovery Vineyard, which is quickly establishing itself as one of the state's top Cabernet Sauvignon sites. High-register aromas of purple fruits, flowers, dates and herbs are followed by voluptuous, palate-staining flavors with sweet black currant notes on the finish.

name: Starmont 2018 Merlot (Napa Valley)
review: Blended with 15% Malbec, this wine is reductive and high toned, with fresh acidity lifting its otherwise intensely built layers of clove, toasted oak, cedar and iron. Red-currant flavor provides a worthwhile contrast and crisp finish.

name: Stag's Leap Wine Cellars 2018 Artemis Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: From a blend of beautiful sites across the valley, including Arcadia Vineyard in Coombsville and Rancho Chimiles in Wooden Valley, as well as small amounts of grapes from S.L.V. and Fay, this wine is inviting in dense aromas of red fruit, caramel and brioche. With dashes of Petit Verdot and Malbec, it finishes on accents of tobacco and cedar.

name: Spicerack 2018 G-S-M (Santa Barbara County-Mendocino County)
review: Layered and rich with dark-fruit flavors, this full-bodied wine made from Syrah, Mourvédre and Grenache is concentrated and seductive. Moderate tannins and a supple texture build a broad mouthfeel that helps all the wonderful fruity, spicy characters spread out on the palate and linger on the finish.

name: Sparkman 2018 Kindred Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of brown spices, herbs, vanilla and cherry lead to silky-feeling flavors with plentiful barrel accents. Raspberry notes pop on the finish. There's enough tannic heft to tie it together.

name: Sparkman 2018 Reunión Graciano (Snipes Mountain)
review: The seldom seen Graciano makes up 92% of this wine. The rest is Touriga Nacional, with all of the fruit coming from Upland Vineyard. Char, coffee, whole orange, vanilla and raspberry, with the palate delivering more of the same. There's a lovely lick of acidity that ups the interest.

name: Sparkman 2018 Baba Sangiovese (Yakima Valley)
review: This wine is Sangiovese (86%) and Malbec, with the fruit coming from Boushey and Gamache, respectively. The aromas offer notes of coffee, sautéed herb and chocolate. It's loaded with coffee and cherry flavors. Lightly grainy tannins back it up. It's delicious, but the oak (34% new French) seems to get in the way of the fruit at times.

name: Sparkman 2018 Gaga Touriga Nacional (Snipes Mountain)
review: This is a rare bottle of 95% Touriga Nacional, with the rest Graciano, coming from a growing region that rarely sees appellated bottles. The aromas bring fascinating notes of coffee, freshly peeled tangerine, red fruit, musk and butterscotch. The palate is full-bodied, textured and flavorful. It has a big yum factor.

name: Sparkman 2018 Holler Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is a pan-Washington Cabernet, with fruit from White Bluffs, Horse Heaven, Red Mountain and Yakima Valley. The aromas offer notes of vanilla, sautéed dried herb, ground aniseed, coffee and cherry. Plump chocolate and cherry flavors follow, with a smooth feel upping the appeal.

name: Sparkman 2018 Wonderland Grenache (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas fascinate, with notes of gravel, green herb and cigar box. The palate mixes fruit and savory flavors. It hasn't fully settled in yet but has all of the stuffing. Best after 2021.

name: Soter 2018 Mineral Springs Ranch Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: The biodynamically farmed grapes bring layered and detailed flavors. Raspberry purée adds intensity to the fruit, with accents of toasted grain and lemon. Some 30% of the ferment included whole clusters, and 40% of the wine was barreled in new French oak. The long, drying finish adds highlights of chamomile tea.

name: Soter 2018 Estates Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Soter produces four tiers of Pinot Noir, the Estates being the newest, a blend of vineyards on Ribbon Ridge and in the Eola-Amity Hills AVA. This young wine needs another year or two to completely integrate, but the components are all in place—bright raspberry fruit, a strong citrus flavor adding zest and acidity, and crisp definition through the finish. It was aged in 40% new French oak. Drink from 2023 on.

name: Soter 2018 North Valley Classic Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This earns the ‘Classic' designation with a spot-on mix of blueberry and purple fruit that fill the palate. Those fresh flavors are bounced up with well-matched acids and lightly earthy tannins, along with hints of cedar from aging in 10% new oak.

name: Soter 2018 Planet Oregon Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Planet Oregon is the value brand from Soter Vineyards, offering exceptional quality at an affordable price. Scents of raspberries laced with pine needles greet you with light and pretty flavors of rhubarb and raspberry, along with more of that pine-forest accent. It's a delightful wine for near-term enjoyment, with enough tannins to show some grip through the finish.

name: Soquel Vineyards 2018 Doctor's Vineyard Partners' Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Earthy aromas of crushed rocks and loamy soil open up toward dark hibiscus, rose petals and strawberry torte on the nose of this bottling. Fresh strawberry and baked raspberry flavors are cut by an acidic tang on the palate and enhanced by sagebrush spice.

name: Soquel Vineyards 2018 Library Selection Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Black-cherry pie with flaky vanilla crust, light clove and a hint of potpourri are all aromas that emerge from this bottling. The palate snaps with fresh and buoyant raspberry, picking up the slightest hints of vanilla and clove toward the finish.

name: Soquel Vineyards 2018 Regan Vineyards Partners' Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Rounded and fresh aromas of strawberry, cherry and raspberry sorbet meet with delicate herbs on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate is so floral that it's like supping on a cup of red and purple flowers, from lavender to potpourri, with herbal hints of tarragon and anise and a rich strawberry core.

name: Soquel Vineyards 2018 Estate Grown Partners' Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Fresh and candied cherry aromas meet with hints of pine and dried sagebrush on the very engaging nose of this bottling. It's also lively on the palate, offering thyme, sagebrush and marjoram as well as red-currant and raspberry flavors.

name: Soquel Vineyards 2018 Saveria Vineyard Partners' Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Ripe aromas of black raspberry and cranberry meet with crisp red plum, thyme, lavender and sumac on the nose of this bottling. There's a fresh herbal array on the sip, from marjoram and thyme to peppercorn and dried rosemary, set against the brisk red-fruit backdrop.

name: Soquel Vineyards 2018 Partners' Reserve Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Light red-cherry, strawberry and hibiscus aromas swirl with star anise and vanilla on the nose of this bottling. Soft and rounded flavors of strawberry and watermelon meet with rose petals and lavender on the elegant palate.

name: Somersville 2018 Meritage (California)
review: This full-bodied wine based on Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc tastes like chocolate-covered cherries, and has a mouthcoating richness. It wraps extremely ripe black-cherry and cooked plum flavors in soft tannins.

name: Solomon Hills Estate 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: A brisk raspberry aroma meets with bushels of bay leaf and green peppercorn on the excitingly herbaceous nose of this bottling from the appellation's most coastal vineyard. The same pepper and herb tones show on the savory sip, where juniper also arises alongside the cranberry and raspberry core.

name: Solminer 2018 Ricci Vineyard St. Laurent (Carneros)
review: Lively aromas of tangy plum, crunchy cranberry, dark red currant and cracked pepper make for a fascinating nose on this bottling from perhaps the only producing St. Laurent vines in California. Dark cranberry, peppercorn and dried anise flavors show on the sip.

name: Solminer 2018 Delanda Vineyard Blaufränkisch (Los Olivos District)
review: An ode to proprietor Anna deLaski's Austrian upbringing, this bottling of an exceedingly rare grape in California is a head-turner, starting with unique aromas of raspberry, hibiscus, tarragon and dried flowers. The texture is taut on the palate, where flavors of sour and dried plum met with violet—an extremely distinctive set of smells and tastes.

name: Solminer 2018 Ampelos Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: David and Anna deLaski turn to the biodynamic vineyard of Peter and Rebecca Work for this bottling of the Pommard clone, which begins with tangy red plum, pomegranate, wild thyme and tart sumac on the nose. Bay leaf and more sumac spice show on the palate, where tart red-fruit flavors dominate.

name: Solitary Cellars 2018 Barbera (Shenandoah Valley (CA))
review: Bright red-cherry flavors mingle with black plums in this full-bodied and moderately tannic wine. It has a firm texture and holds is flavors tightly.

name: Solitary Cellars 2018 Syrah (California)
review: A bright candied-cherry aroma and raspberry flavor give this medium-bodied wine a tangy, lean effect at first that broadens with time in the glass.

name: Solitary Cellars 2018 The Pardon Red (California)
review: Lots of vanilla and toasted bread aromas meet similar smoky, oaky flavors in this full-bodied blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah.

name: Soléna 2018 Hyland Vineyard Pinot Noir (McMinnville)
review: Many vintners seek out Hyland fruit, but Soléna owns the vineyard, so one must assume they get the pick of the crop from the old-vine mix of Coury, Pommard and Wädenswil clones. Licorice, coffee and chocolate highlights set the tone here, with blackberry and black-cherry fruit providing an ample foundation.

name: Soléna 2018 Prince Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: A lively streak of citrus elevates the blackberry and black-cherry fruit, and the flavors continue focused and piercingly tight through the palate. There's a streak of chocolate also, and the overall structure suggests giving this wine another two or three years of bottle age before popping the cork.

name: Soléna 2018 Zena Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This tasty wine is silky smooth, with a parade of black-cherry fruit and accents of mocha and cocoa powder. The tannins are polished and hint at a pleasantly dusty character, with further highlights of cut tobacco.

name: Soléna 2018 Domaine Danielle Laurent Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: This dark and layered wine is front-loaded with black-cherry fruit and highlights of licorice, espresso and sweet tobacco. Those flavors seem to fade away in the finish, leaving a residual trace of bitter skin tannins.

name: Soléna 2018 Grande Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Quite young and showing clean primary fruit flavors of tart red berries, this is backed with ample acidity, lending a pleasing citrusy snap to the mouthfeel. It finishes with a wash of minerality. Good now, but another year in the bottle wouldn't hurt a bit.

name: Sokol Blosser 2018 Hyland Vineyard Pinot Noir (McMinnville)
review: Hyland is a well-represented single-vineyard selection among a great many producers. The light and pretty flavors of red cherries are at the core of the appeal here, and there's an underlying minerality that is common in the AVA. A whiff of rose petals adds a delicate note to the opening, and the finishing tannins leave a trail of herbal tea.

name: Sokol Blosser 2018 Redland Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Earthy and herbal, this entry-level blend seems to have been sanded smooth. Despite the lack of full fruit ripeness, there are no rough edges to the dominating flavors of tart berries and dried herbs, and the tannins have no bite to them. It's a serviceable wine with a quick, tight finish.

name: Sojourn 2018 Ridgetop Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Made from a high-elevation site farmed by Ulises Valdez, Jr., at the extreme coastal reaches of Annapolis, this wine is juicy in black cherry and strawberry. Firm, substantial tannins add to the power and concentration, finishing in baking spice and toasted oak.

name: Sojourn 2018 Riddle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Reductive, with a sizable tannin profile, this hearty red is from Green Valley, on a ridge top and planted at a high density. Dark berry, earth, pine and forest meet an intensity of oak and plenty of lasting depth, with lush, plush texture.

name: Sojourn 2018 Wohler Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a Forestville site, this wine is bright, tannic and hearty in dark cherry and a light sprinkling of baking spice. Cardamon, cola and black tea give it additional character and intrigue, with balanced acidity throughout.

name: SoDo 2018 Wingman G-S-M (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas offer notes of raspberry Jolly Rancher, berry and generous amounts of orange peel and green herb. This wine is medium bodied, with orange peel and berry flavors following. A medicinal note lingers on the finish.

name: Snowy Peaks 2018 Cabernet Franc (Grand Valley)
review: Aromas of lingonberry, black berry and flower stem are scrambled together on the nose. The palate offers red apple skins, black raspberry and currant, with plenty of peppery spice and oak tones. Good tannic grip with sandpaper texture offers plenty of structure and balanced acidity. Oaky tones take over the finish with vanilla, toasted wood and charred Shishito pepper paired with red berry acidity.

name: Smashberry 2018 Red (Paso Robles)
review: Roasted blackberry, mahogany, walnut and slightly sour plums aromas show on the nose of this kitchen sink blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Merlot, Lagrein and Grenache. There's a slight sweetness to the palate, where candied blackberry and cassis flavors are sprinkled with herbs.

name: Sling & Stone 2018 Tondre Grapefield Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dried raspberry, hibiscus, rose-water and oak aromas show on the nose of this bottling. There's a delightful mix of thyme and light sage on the palate, giving depth to the dried cherry core.

name: Sling & Stone 2018 Knott Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Bright aromas of bursting strawberry and red currant are layered in cinnamon, nutmeg and clove on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The 20% new French oak shows right away on the sip, and then come waves of smoked cherries and meat, finishing with a savory dashi-broth kick.

name: Sling & Stone 2018 Paraiso Vineyard Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Black raspberry sorbet, wet slate and some mint aromas kick off this bottling, with more herbal and savory qualities emerging as the wine sits open. The palate is refreshing and taut in texture, offering snappy raspberry flavors and chaparral herbs set against bountiful acid, with a light vanilla finish.

name: Skyside 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (North Coast)
review: Well structured and balanced, this full-bodied wine fills the palate with ripe but not jammy plum and black-cherry flavors backed by moderate tannins. It's a very good, substantial wine to pair with red meat.

name: Skyside 2018 Red (North Coast)
review: A tempting black-fig aroma leads to rich black-fruit flavors and good balance in this dry, lightly tannic and well-concentrated wine.

name: Sketchbook 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendocino County)
review: This full-bodied wine is well structured, well balanced and packed with good, ripe black-cherry and blackberry flavors. Firm fine-grained tannins enrich the texture while a bit of tangy acidity keeps it lively.

name: Sketchbook 2018 Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: This full-bodied wine leads with plenty of smoky oak character, offering strong wood-char and grilled meat aromas followed by beef and black-pepper flavors.

name: SIX26 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Coast)
review: Lighter aromas of red currant, dried herb, pepper and wet rocks show on the nose of this bottling. There's a compelling herbal touch to the palate, where oregano and marjoram meet with brisk red-plum flavors, leading into a riper finish.

name: SIX26 2018 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Black raspberry and dark cherry meet with thyme and turned earth on the nose of this bottling out of the Summerland Wine Brands facility in Buellton. The palate is both hearty with dark red fruits yet snappy with acidity, leading into a sandalwood and mace finish.

name: Sineann 2018 TFL Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is a four-barrel selection from Yates Conwill fruit, bottled exclusively in 375 ml bottles. Stunningly good fruit coalesces around flavors of wild berry jam and cherry pie. The acidity and tannins are proportionate and balanced. It's simply delicious already, but could be enjoyed over the next half decade.

name: Sineann 2018 Yates Conwill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: A favorite site of winemaker Peter Rosback, this vineyard, adjacent to the famed Résonance vineyard, produces a muscular, detailed wine with a core of cherry compote. Firming elements of stem and leaf carry into the tannins, along with lovely baking spices that reflect a limited time in new and once-used barrels. Drink now on through the decade.

name: Sineann 2018 Daffodil Hill Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This is the first time Sineann has offered a designated wine from this site, which it expresses beautifully. A supple mix of wild berry fruit and lightly peppery herbs, it also brings highlights of green tea and green olive. Complex, layered and balanced, it should drink nicely over the next decade.

name: Sineann 2018 Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: Five stellar vineyards, all sources for Sineann's vineyard select wines, contribute to this blend. A rich purple plum color, it's loaded with marionberry and blueberry fruit, and there's just a light whiff of wintergreen mint on the nose. Well-balanced herbal notes contribute to the flavors also, especially lemon thyme. The balance, pull-through and length are impressive, as are the polished tannins.

name: Sineann 2018 Hirschy Vineyard Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: A new vineyard for Sineann, this seems at first a bit astringent and feels not quite pulled together at this young age (barely a year past fermentation). Stay with it. Tart pie cherry fruit, tannins that suggest dried tea leaves, and highlights of lemon rind contribute complexity that should pull all the flavors together with additional bottle age.

name: Silver Totem 2018 Wallula Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: The aromas are light, with notes of asphalt, herb and whole orange. Whole orange flavors follow. The fruit doesn't seem to have fully ripened.

name: Sightglass 2018 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Winemaker Sean Boyd made his name at Woodinville Wine Cellars in part by his Little Bear Creek wine that was one of the best values in the state. This is his new winery's iteration. Made of 50% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Franc, 20% Malbec and 5% Syrah, the wine has plentiful aromas of coffee grounds, dried herb, dark chocolate and cherry, which are followed by well-balanced flavors that show density, length and plenty of structure that's pleasingly chewy. It's thoroughly enchanting.

name: Sightglass 2018 Cabernet Franc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is all Cabernet Franc from Stillwater Creek. The aromas are generous, with notes of roasted coffee bean, cherry, plum, flowers and fresh herbs. The flavors pop, with density and a sense of structural tension. Fruit and barrel play equal parts.

name: Sightglass 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Malbec makes up 25% of this wine, with the fruit coming from Stillwater Creek Vineyard. The aromas are immediately appealing, with plentiful notes of roasted coffee, savory herb, chocolate, licorice, plum and cherry cordial. The palate is flavorful, layered and rich but also has a sense of liveliness, with the Malbec giving it a lift. The finish carries. It's pure, unadulterated hedonism, a stellar example of the variety.

name: Sightglass 2018 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This wine is a mixture of StoneTree Vineyard and Dineen fruit. The aromas pop, with notes of blueberry pie, plum, coffee and spice. Intensely rich, layered blue fruit and coffee flavors follow. It's a hedonistic interpretation of this variety, with fruit and barrel both playing big roles.

name: Siduri 2018 Willamette Valley Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is bright and spicy, with cranberry fruit at the core. It's more like a Northwest Gamay than most Willamette Valley Pinots, dark and lively with an astringent minerally finish.

name: Siduri 2018 Parsons Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Weighty and textured, this richly layered wine shows a density of baked strawberry, pomegranate and rhubarb, the fruit seasoned in clove. Toasted oak supports and complements, finishing in a subtle twist of black tea and cola.

name: Siduri 2018 Perry Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is fruity in blueberry and black cherry, with an inviting nose of black tea and clove. Earthy and refined in tannin, it is softly layered and approachable in its lasting richness, the oak well integrated.

name: Siduri 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This appellation wine impresses in its delivery of supple herbal, earthy and high-toned flavor. Dipped in rhubarb, pomegranate and strawberry, it has the richness of cola and a spicy hit of cinnamon beneath its dense layers of cherry pie.

name: Siduri 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: There's sour plum and lanolin-like funkiness to the nose of this bottling, which also shows dried red-flower aromas. The palate is loaded with dried flower and fruit flavors, offering sumac, violet, game and dried cranberry flavors.

name: Shooting Star 2018 Zinfandel (Lake County)
review: This rich, rounded wine is packed with delicious black-fruit flavors on a mouthfilling and lightly tannic texture. It's fruity, ripe and lip-smacking good.

name: Shokrian Vineyard 2018 Opulent Goat Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: A crisp black-raspberry-sorbet aroma makes for a fresh, inviting interpretation of Syrah by Winemaker Alice Anderson. The palate is grippy in structure, offering tart blackberry and lavender flavors, clinging to that snappy and fresh vibe.

name: Shokrian Vineyard 2018 Harvest Moon Unfiltered Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Extremely light in the glass, this wine by Drake Whitcraft is sprightly and refreshing, starting with aromas of cranberry juice, raspberry, strawberry and hibiscus. It's crisp and tight on the palate, where those red fruit and flower flavors meet with bright acidity. This one goes down all too easily.

name: Shannon Ridge 2018 High Elevation Collection Zinfandel (Lake County)
review: This full-bodied and nicely structured wine offers a mix of ripe blackberry and plum flavors, with light nuances of dried sage and aged beef. It's balanced, complex and distinctive.

name: Shannon Ridge 2018 Buck Shack Small Batch Red (Lake County)
review: This bold and full-bodied wine boasts a superdeep color, dense and concentrated blackberry flavors and a firm structure. It has the power to stand up to a juicy steak or roast, bolstered by fine-grained tannins and filled in nicely by opulent black-cherry and cassis flavors.

name: Shannon Ridge 2018 Buck Shack Cabernet Sauvignon (Lake County)
review: Lush, dark and saturated with flavor, this velvety textured wine is practically syrupy in its degree of concentration and ripeness. Full body and moderate tannins give it a good grip on the palate.

name: Shannon Ridge 2018 High Elevation Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Lake County)
review: Effusive fruitiness gives this well-structured wine a very tempting profile. It is relatively soft and creamy on the palate, stuffed with black-cherry and chocolate-covered-cherry flavors.

name: Shannon Ridge 2018 Urgency Red (California)
review: Concentrated and full bodied, this deeply colored wine packs in dried plum and raisin flavors that are tinged with black pepper, milk chocolate and mint.

name: Shannon Ridge 2018 High Elevation Collection Petite Sirah (Lake County)
review: This full-bodied wine offers big, ripe flavors and smooth tannins. It's approachable for Petite Sirah, and packed with dark chocolate and black pepper notes that will appeal to many.

name: Sextant 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: This impressive Cabernet Sauvignon offers smooth aromas of vanilla, root beer, cedar, fig and prune on the nose. The palate is firmly framed by chalky tannins, delivering rich black-fruit flavors alongside hints of thyme and cracked pepper.

name: Seven Apart 2018 Expedition Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: The inaugural release made by Andy Erickson, this comes from the owner's Stags Ridge Vineyard atop Atlas Peak, at 1,400-feet in elevation and planted in 1999. Thick, tannic and earthy, it buzzes in dried herb, sage, flint and a rich intensity of blackberry, plum and vanilla.

name: Seghesio 2018 Todd Brothers Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)
review: From head-trained vines, this wine is thick, concentrated and richly layered in red and black berry and cherry. High-toned acidity lifts the density of the fruit as shocks of black pepper, nutmeg and leather weigh in.

name: Seghesio 2018 Zinfandel (Sonoma County)
review: This offering is blended with smaller amounts of Petite Sirah, Carignan and mixed reds. Juicy and generous in blackberry and vanilla, it is a broadly appealing style, utilizing both American and French oak.

name: Second Growth 2018 Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: Well balanced and showing a mix of red fruits, this runs from hints of rhubarb right through raspberries and lightly peppery cherry flavors. It's an easy-drinking wine with a straightforward, no frills approach.

name: Second Growth 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This opens with a bit of a rough and rugged mouthfeel. The strawberry and cherry fruit is supported with blocky tannins, and it finishes with just a slight burn. Nonetheless, this is a sound and solid example of Willamette Valley Pinot, and with a grilled salmon steak would work quite well.

name: Sebastopol Oaks 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Wood-smoke aromas lead to wood-char and black-pepper flavors in this very oaky, full-bodied wine.

name: Sebastopol Oaks 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This smooth, well-balanced wine deftly matches ripe black cherry and mild baking-spice flavors on a supple texture that has a melt-in-the-mouth aspect. It is so easy to like and enjoy.

name: Seawolf 2018 Marcucci Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a tiny production field blend led by Zin with smaller amounts of Alicante Bouschet, Carignan, Petite Sirah and Golden Chasselas. Cofermented, it was given 14 months in French oak, a minority new. Baked red fruit, spicy cinnamon and sweet oak contribute soft, rounded layers of flavor boosted by underling acidity.

name: SeatPocket 2018 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Cherry, raspberry and wet stone aromas make for a familiar and fine entry to the nose of this bottling. A chalky texture frames the sip, which is full of cranberry and raspberry, with a light vanilla and toast finish.

name: Sean Minor 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick velvety texture is met by a wealth of jammy black cherry and strawberry in this highly likable, affordable wine. Toasted oak adds cinnamon spice and structure without getting in the way.

name: Sean Minor 2018 Nicole Marie Red (North Coast)
review: Vanilla and marshmallow aromas lead to effusively ripe, jammy, blackberry and boysenberry flavors in this sweet-seeming full-bodied wine.

name: Sean Minor 2018 Point North Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Despite its youth, this thins out quickly, leaving a liquorous impression and a hard, tannic edge. The fruit that's there touches on cherry but seems encased in a hard tannic shell. Further bottle age and/or aggressive aeration are recommended.

name: SeaGlass 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Deep aromas of blackberry, turned soil, crushed rock and a touch of wood show on the nose of this bottling. The palate features flavors of red fruit, spice and purple flowers, finishing on a lively vanilla spice note.

name: Scott Harvey 2018 J & S Reserve Syrah (Amador County)
review: Wonderful oak complexity gives this full-bodied wine a bounty of clove, cedar and chocolate aromas, leading to a focused core of blackberry and black licorice. It offers major fine-grained tannins and a mouthfilling texture. Best through 2028.

name: Scott Harvey 2018 Mountain Selection Syrah (Amador County)
review: Menthol and dill aromas lead to rich black-fruit flavors in this spicy, oak-accented and full-bodied wine. It's broad in mouthfeel and leaves a lingering spicy finish.

name: Schug 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Herbal and teased in menthol aromas, this light-bodied wine is peppery and spicy in clove and cinnamon, with shy fruit. The tannins are quietly integrated and firm.

name: Scar of the Sea 2018 Seven Leagues Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Light in the glass, this appellation blend starts with inviting aromas of crisp cherry, hibiscus and raspberry, with hints of anise and fennel pollen on the nose. The palate snaps with cranberry skin and chaparral spices, leading into more rounded cherry flavors in the midpalate, as clean acidity pushes through the finish.

name: Scar of the Sea 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: This county-wide blend is light in the glass, offering crisp aromas of Alpine strawberry, red currant, mint and wet stone. The palate is zippy and taut in structure, delivering flavors of underripe raspberry, cranberry and damp wool.

name: Say When 2018 When Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Earthy and meaty aromas combine with baked black plum and violet on the dynamic nose of this bottling. The palate runs into slightly sour cranberry and raspberry flavors before the game and sagebrush flavors return, leading into a vanilla-laced finish.

name: Say When 2018 Rasi Rancho La Vina Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Roasted dark cherries, cardamom and star anise make for spicy, fruity entry to the nose of this bottling. The mouthfeel is plush and inviting at first, and then the tannins make a quick stand, ushering in flavors of blistered strawberry and more compelling baking spice.

name: Say When 2018 Weird Parties Martian Ranch & Vineyard Tempranillo (Santa Barbara County)
review: This is a fun and fresh take on Tempranillo, with an equally playful label. Aromas of lavender, boysenberry, potting soil and a touch of telltale leather lead into a palate framed by rounded tannins, a bit more of that earthy leather and a full array of fresh fruit flavors.

name: Saviah 2018 The Jack Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Merlot (73%) takes the lead on this wine, with the rest Malbec (11%), Cabernet Franc (8%) and Petit Verdot (8%). The aromas offer notes of plum, red and black currant and dried herbs. Full-feeling, somewhat jammy flavors follow, with a soft sense of tannic structure. A warm finish caps it off.

name: Savage Grace 2018 Dineen Vineyard Côt (Rattlesnake Hills)
review: The achingly pure aromas of wild strawberry, ground rock, earth, spice and herb are arresting. The palate brings all that and more, with notes of herb, and spice that show delicacy, purity and restraint along with a whole lot of acidity. This is as far from a cocktail wine as it gets and needs food alongside it to be fully appreciated. Paired it with grilled lamb chops.

name: Savage Grace 2018 Copeland Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Rattlesnake Hills)
review: The aromas are arresting, with notes of fresh raspberry, strawberry, cranberry, whiffs of manure and dried herb that show vitality. The palate starts out light and delicate, drinking like an elegant Pinot Noir, with vivid, pure flavors, filling out over time. It's a very pretty expression of the variety, with a strong emphasis on purity. Best after 2023.

name: Savage Grace 2018 Two Blondes Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of flower, raspberry, dried and fresh green herb, mushroom, dried orange rind, cherry, spice and moist earth, showing a mesmerizing sense of purity and detail. The flower-filled palate is sleek and lithe, with a mouthwatering sensibility and a long finish. It's a dazzler. Put it on the dinner table to see it at its best.

name: Savage Grace 2018 Underwood Mountain Vineyards Pinot Noir (Columbia Gorge (WA))
review: Piercingly pure Rainier cherry, red plum and dried herb aromas capture the attention. There's a lovely sense of balance and tension to the fruit flavors that carries through the cinnamon-filled finish. Lightly sandy feeling tannins and citrusy acidity provide support. Give it a few years in bottle to see it at its best, but it's best is impressive.

name: Savage Grace 2018 Pollard Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Yakima Valley)
review: This is a new offering from the winery, coming within a stone's throw of Two Blondes Vineyard, where it also makes a varietal offering. Aromas of cinnamon, strawberry, mushroom, cherry and red plum rise up from the glass. Light, pure, exquisitely balanced flavors follow. Nuance, intensity and an alcohol level unheard of in modern days in the state for a red or even most whites (11%). It's an accomplishment of a wine. Might be too light and airy for some tastes, but others will swoon. As always with this producer, put it on the dinner table to see it at its best.

name: Sarah's Vineyard 2018 Tondre Grapefield Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Candied cherry aromas meet with light earth, wet moss and mint on the nose of this bottling. The palate is bright, zesty and flavorful, offering more cherry, dried mint and a hint of wild sage, with lots of acidity on a still-young frame.

name: Sante Arcangeli 2018 Split Rail Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Baked black-plum, cherry, clove, nutmeg and roasted meat aromas arise on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate snaps with buoyant fruit flavors of raspberry and pomegranate, enhanced by dried red flower, toasted sagebrush and a bright acidity.

name: Sante Arcangeli 2018 Ananda Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Named after Winemaker John Benedetti's late wife, Melania Ananda Benedetti, this bottling combines the grapes fromSplit Rail and Lester Family Vineyards. It opens with candied cherry, red currant and wild mint on the nose, leading into a grippy, fresh palate of tart red fruit, peppery spices and a smooth finish.

name: Sante Arcangeli 2018 Lester Family Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Many winemakers pull from this vineyard, but John Benedetti might be closest to mastering the site. Tightly wound aromas of strawberry, red plum and hibiscus are wrapped in a slightly earthy sage-root element. The palate snaps with crisp cranberry and rose petal flavors, lifted by white pepper and sumac.

name: Sante Arcangeli 2018 Hand in Hand Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Extremely aromatic in the glass, this bottling from a vineyard in the Corralitos area shows smashed cranberry, crushed thyme, bay leaf, green peppercorn and green tea in the glass. The wild mix of herbs and cracked pepper pops on the palate, which also offers a juicy cranberry and cherry tone while remaining light on its feet.

name: Sans Wine Co 2018 Three Jacks Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: This bright, approachable red wine made for the can is spicy in clove and tobacco, with a back note of eucalyptus that appeals. Bright and effusive, it has tame tannins and sultry black fruit.

name: Sans Wine Co 2018 Poor Ranch Vineyard Coyote Rock Carbonic Carignan (Mendocino County)
designation: Poor Ranch Vineyard Coyote Rock Carbonic
review: This rustic-style wine in a can reveals smoky, meaty aromas and flavors and a spare, tangy texture. It's lean and rather tart in mouthfeel.

name: Sangiacomo 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Winemaker James MacPhail employs Sangiacomo-grown grapes from Roberts Road, Amaral and Fedrick Vineyards in this powerhouse red. Juicy, broad and fleshy, it broods in blood orange, pomegranate and strawberry tea, with hints of woody tannin and clove.

name: Sandler 2018 Cuvée Marcy Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Light in the glass with a slight have, this bottling offers aromas of thyme, sagebrush, rustic cherry and a touch of tangy red-plum skin. The palate pops with cranberry and strawberry while flirting with bay leaf and dried ginger spice.

name: Sandler 2018 Peterson Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Very light on the nose, this bottling offers hints of hibiscus and tangy plum. The palate shows dried cherry, light oregano, a touch of licorice and a light leather flavor, with balanced acidity and tannins.

name: Sandler 2018 Boer Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chalone)
review: Stewed plum, hibiscus and crushed grape-seed aromas show on the nose of this bottling from the rugged Chalone appellation. Muddle mulberry and wild cherry flavors meet with loamy soil, dried sage and red flowers on the palate, where the rustic tannins lead into a bourbon-like finish.

name: Sandler 2018 Boer Vineyard Grenache (Chalone)
review: Spicy aromas of cinnamon meet with tangy red plum and rust on the nose of this bottling. Earthy flavors of sagebrush, dried soil, crushed rock and red-plum sauce show on the palate.

name: Sandler 2018 Madzia's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Bennett Valley)
review: Dark, baked cherry aromas meet with spice cake and the slight tang of black plum on the nose of this bottle. There's a tart edge to the palate, where muddled boysenberry and black-plum flavors meet with light gamy tones as well as crumpled oregano.

name: Sandler 2018 Cortada Alta Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Heavier aromas of liquefied violets and black-plum syrup show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is also rich and quite extracted, with flavors of roasted black plum, crushed pepper and thyme.

name: Sandler 2018 Nolan Vineyard Syrah (Bennett Valley)
review: Dark tones of smashed black raspberry are dusted with thyme, green peppercorns and wood ash on the dynamic nose of this bottling from just south of Santa Rosa. The palate is tightly wound in texture yet rich in flavors of blackberry, boysenberry and lavender, all cut by savory hits of cracked pepper and tobacco leaf.

name: Sandler 2018 Lazare Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Oak Knoll District)
review: Dark aromas of black plum, violet and a hint of iodine show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is relatively light and bright, offering fresh black plum, white pepper, a pinch of oregano and an espresso bean darkness toward the finish.

name: Sandar & Hem 2018 Red (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: This blend of 60% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Franc and 10% Cabernet Sauvignon explores the elegant edge of Bordelaise style, starting with aromas of blackberry jam, dark plum and dark strawberry. The palate is framed by sharp acidity that cuts into the darker purple fruits and maintains a medium body throughout.

name: Sandar & Hem 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Very light in the glass, this brisk and lively bottling starts with aromas of pomegranate, rose petal and the lightest brush of strawberry. The palate is very light in flavors of more rose and pomegranate, proving refreshing albeit very subtle in expression.

name: San Simeon 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Baked cherry and red currant aromas meet with light caramel and crumpled carnation on the nose of this bottling. It's medium bodied in feel, where a tense, chalky structure delivers bright flavors of strawberry and red plum.

name: San Simeon 2018 Estate Reserve Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Plump aromas of baked strawberry, earthy slate and cinnamon brioche show on the nose of this bottling. There is a grippy tension to the palate, where dark plum and berry flavors meet with a slate minerality and vanilla-herb finish.

name: San Rucci 2018 Cabernet Franc (California)
review: This well-balanced and engaging wine offers aromas of blueberries and blackberries, snappy black-fruit flavors and moderate tannins for a full, grippy mouthfeel. Paired with a lamb chop, this will be perfect.

name: San Lorenzo Winery 2018 Rock Garden Hillside Red (Alexander Valley)
review: Seamless tannin, earth, cedar and pencil shavings accent this bold blend of 90% Zinfandel with 5% Petite Sirah and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon. Clove and vanilla flavors build on the midpalate. The body weight is substantial and brightened in blackberry, currant and cassis.

name: San Lorenzo Winery 2018 The Pearl Red (Alexander Valley)
review: This is a field blend from a vineyard bought by proprietor Pete Seghesio's grandfather in 1896, the site itself dating back to 1892. Leading with 55% Zinfandel, there's Petite Sirah, Alicante Bouschet, Carignane and other mixed reds. Together, they make for a bright, thick and earthy expression of dense red fruit, cedar and cigar box, coming together into a full-bodied, impressive whole.

name: Samuel Robert 2018 Family Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Not the same as the winery's Vintner's Reserve, this offers comparable value and quality in a different style. A floral component, candied black cherry fruit, a lick of cola and sweet brown spices elevate the flavors above and beyond most sub-$20 Pinots. In this instance, the use of the unregulated term “Reserve” seems totally justified.

name: Samuel Louis Smith 2018 Sandstone Terrace Syrah (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Fresh and snappy aromas of boysenberry sorbet and rose petals are also Pinot Noir-like on the nose of this Syrah. The palate is extremely floral, like sipping from a punch of rose petal and violet, with a backdrop of black raspberry and white pepper. Drink now–2038.

name: Samuel Louis Smith 2018 Montañita de Oro Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Aromas of black raspberry and boysenberry meet with plum, hibiscus and rose petal on the stunning nose of this bottling from a vineyard that's 1,700 feet high and rich in shale and granite soils. There's a tremendously compelling texture on the palate, where raspberry, violet and mint flavors lead into a long finish dusted lightly in baking spice.

name: Saint K 2018 Gravesend Carignan (Santa Barbara County)
review: Toasted oak aromas meet with crushed cranberry and fresh raspberry on the somewhat reserved nose of this bottling. The mouthfeel is pleasantly creamy, with flavors of roasted plum and baking spice that lead into a lingering, vanilla-kissed finish.

name: Saint K 2018 Old Potrero Vineyard Mourvèdre (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: There is an extremely earthy kick to this entire bottling, beginning with turned soil, rocky gravel, black pepper, dried kelp, crumbled violet and elderberry aromas on the nose. The palate is surrounded by peppery spices, from white to green peppercorns, while also offering boysenberry pie and charred meat flavors that lead into a buzzing Sichuan peppercorn finish.

name: Saini 2018 Angelo's Paint Brush Red (Dry Creek Valley)
review: This blends near-equal parts Grenache, Carignan and Sangiovese into a pleasing, rustic whole, reductive and oaky at first, but balanced and bold as it goes. Thick black fruit is complemented in peppery tobacco and leather, finishing dry.

name: Sagelands 2018 Dark Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of freshly cut red apple and black currant are followed by off-dry-drinking black-currant flavors. It has a place.

name: Sagelands 2018 Riverbed Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of sugared plum and sweet black currant are followed by ripe straight-down-the-fairway fruit flavors. Soft tannins back it up. It's a crowd-pleaser.

name: Sagelands 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are reserved, with notes of plum, berry and apple. Jammy fruit flavors follow. It's a ripe, slightly sweet style but the price is right. It will find plenty of admirers.

name: Sagelands 2018 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit-forward aromas of plum and black currant lead to green-pepper and plum flavors. The oak is dialed back, putting a pleasant focus on the freshness of the fruit. It doesn't directly identify as Merlot, but it brings enjoyment.

name: Sabelli-Frisch 2018 Gordon W Alicante Bouschet (Lodi)
review: Heady spices of new oak like cinnamon, clove and sawn wood dominate the aromas and flavors of this deep-colored, medium-bodied, firmly tannic and slightly astringent wine.

name: Sabelli-Frisch 2018 La Malinche Mission (Mokelumne River)
review: This charming wine from a grape variety introduced to California 250 years ago is unusually light colored but has more to show in the aromas and flavors than it might appear. Scents of baking spices and cedar lead to raspberry and cranberry on the palate, while tangy acidity and light tannins lift the mouthfeel.

name: Ryder Estate 2018 Merlot (Central Coast)
review: Turned soil, dried herbs, clay and dried red fruits show on the nose of this bottling. The soil and clay flavors are prominent on the palate, where light red-berry tones show in the background and tannins add grip toward the finish.

name: Ryder Estate 2018 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Tarry aromas of cranberry, toast and asphalt show on the nose of this bottling. There is a roasted plum core to the palate, where strong soil and rock flavors are lifted by dried tarragon on the finish.

name: Rural 2018 Pinot Noir (Eagle Peak)
review: Very fruity and nicely concentrated, this full-bodied wine backs ripe black-cherry flavors with subtle baking spices on a good, hearty structure of moderate tannins and fresh acidity.

name: Rubus 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This smooth and medium-bodied wine is generous and fruity, adding baking spices for a bit of complexity.

name: RR Wines 2018 Ridgecrest Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Sedimentary soil flavors are dominant in this wine, with aromatic red fruits and peppery spice to back it up. The flavors all linger gracefully, showing good texture and balance. Native yeast was used, and one-quarter of the barrels were new.

name: Rowdy Bush 2018 Petite Sirah (Lodi)
review: A deep, dark color and bold aromas of road tar and baked blueberries precede dark-chocolate and blackberry flavors in this full-bodied and moderately tannic wine. It's superflavorful but doesn't overwhelm the palate with the typical stiff texture.

name: Row Eleven 2018 Viñas 3 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This wine is ripe and spicy, blending cherry-candy and raspberry flavors with cinnamon and cedar accents from oak. A full body and soft tannins give it a big but soft mouthfeel.

name: RouteStock 2018 Route 116 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A wealth of savory earthiness is met by layers of balanced black cherry, wood and sage in this well-made, attractively priced wine. The oak and tannin are well mannered and integrated, allowing room for the acidity and subtle baking spice to linger.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2018 Little G Grenache (Washington)
