review: The aromas intrigue, with vivid notes of freshly peeled orange along with gravel and red fruit. Orange-peel and smoked meat flavors follow. It's a very pulled-back offering of the variety, interesting though a bit one-note at times.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2018 Dre Mourvèdre (Washington)
review: All of the fruit for this 100% varietal wine comes from Upland Vineyard on Snipes Mountain. It's chock-full of aromas of freshly ground black pepper, along with fresh and dried herb. The palate has an acidic snap, with plentiful black-pepper accents. Tasty stuff.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2018 Northern Blend Rocks Estate Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (OR))
review: Boom! The aromas explode from the glass, with notes of fresh flowers, whole and squeezed Satsuma orange, blueberry pie, black pepper, plum, smoked meat and earth, showing a captivating sense of purity and clarity. It's pillowy soft, full of fruit, olive and smoked meat flavors that undulate across the palate. The smoked meat and orange twist finish is a full 30-plus seconds. Dee-licious.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2018 Southern Blend Red (Washington)
review: Grenache makes up 70% of this wine, with the rest Syrah (20%) and Mourvèdre. The aromas pop, with notes of cracked pepper, mountain strawberry, fresh satsuma peel, flower, blueberry and stem. The palate brings a supreme amount of delicacy but also a sneaky richness. It sails on the satsuma filled finish. The purity is mesmerizing.

name: Rotation Vineyards 2018 Red (Lodi)
review: A smoky, rubber-tire aroma and black-pepper flavors give this full-bodied wine a dark, charred profile. It's moderately tannic and pretty extreme in flavor.

name: Rotation Vineyards 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)
review: A strong smoky, roasted character from oak takes over this full-bodied and firmly tannic wine, dominating some light cherry and berry flavors underneath.

name: Rootdown 2018 Dry Bone Ranch Mourvèdre (Amador County)
review: This attractive wine takes a fruity, focused approach to the notoriously earthy grape variety, and comes out with tangy red- and black-cherry flavors and light accents of meat and spice. It is firm and somewhat tannic in texture for good grip.

name: Rootdown 2018 Jane's Vineyard Sangiovese (Mendocino County)
review: Savory, complex and nervy, this fascinating medium-bodied wine binds together earthy, herbal aromas, tart raspberry and cranberry flavors, vivid acidity and firm tannins. It centers all those nuances around a deeper fruit core that comes out on the palate and continues to develop on the finish.

name: Rootdown 2018 St. Amant Vineyard Trousseau (Amador County)
review: This light-colored and light-bodied red wine is floral and spicy in aroma, moderately fruity on the palate and has a smooth, gentle mouthfeel. Try this charming Trousseau in place of a rosé.

name: Ron Noble 2018 Estate Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: Stemmy and earthy, this intriguing estate wine is lifted in red fruit, with persistent accents of mixed dried herbs. From a relatively cool site, it offers structure, lushness and length, with bright acidity helping to keep it balanced and poised.

name: Ron Noble 2018 Estate Reserve Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: This reserve-level wine from a cool-climate site is weighty and robust in style, with a creamy texture and plenty of upfront fruit intensity. Brooding black cherry and wild strawberry layer over sumptuous notes of cardamom, nutmeg and clove.

name: Rodney Strong 2018 Knotty Vines Pinot Noir (California)
review: Dramatic notes of brown sugar and charred oak in the aroma linger and dominate the good cherry and berry flavors in this medium-bodied, highly stylized wine.

name: Roco 2018 Gravel Road Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is a fine value and widely available (for Oregon Pinot). Rhubarb and strawberry fruit flavors roll into sweet red cherry, lightly spiced. The medium-long finish is textured and balanced, the tannins smooth and lightly toasty.

name: Rockpile Winery 2018 Westphall Ridge Vineyard Zinfandel (Rockpile)
review: This estate site is the highest elevation in the winery's portfolio, the vines steeply planted in red volcanic soil. Its minerality is front and center, as are inviting aromas of violet and tea. Balanced, bright and complex, it provides supple texture in contrast to its crisp red and black fruit, finishing in a subtle swirl of vanilla latte.

name: Rockpile Winery 2018 Jack's Cabin Vineyard Zinfandel (Rockpile)
review: Blended with 5% Petite Sirah, this estate wine from high-elevation vines tastes of crushed rock and tilled soil. With elegance and understated power, it delivers fresh, focused flavors of black cherry, cinnamon and chocolate, the tannins plushly layered and well integrated.

name: Rockpile Winery 2018 Rockpile Ridge Vineyard Zinfandel (Rockpile)
review: This estate wine is among the last picked by the producer due to the high-elevation vineyard. It tastes of blackberry, cherry, nutmeg and cola, with underlying notes of dried herb and crushed rock. The texture is velvety, with a lasting chewiness.

name: Rockets Red 2018 Grenache (Edna Valley)
review: A beautiful shade of ruby in the glass, this bottling's aromas are just as enticing as its color, from baked cherry and strawberry to rosewater and light caramel. Flavors of frozen strawberries and hibiscus make for a fruit-bomb feeling, yet the acid keeps it balanced.

name: Rock Wall 2018 Kent's Legacy Heritage Red (Sonoma County)
review: This is winemaker Shauna Rosenblum's nod to her late father and mentor, Kent. It is a best-barrel selection of field-blended Zin from several iconic sites: St. Peters Church, Maggie's and Monte Rosso. Soft, supple and structured, with rounded shoulders of fruit and tannin, it offers full-bodied, unctuous flavors of cherry, berry and leather, just the way Mr. Rosenblum would have liked.

name: Rock Wall 2018 Maggie's Reserve Zinfandel (Sonoma Valley)
review: Mocha and nutmeg season a soft, restrained and elegant core of blueberry and candied plum in this well-made wine. From vines planted in the 1800s, this Zin was cofermented with Sémillon, Muscadelle and Palomino.

name: Roblar 2018 Cabernet Franc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Fresh carnations, hibiscus and pomegranate meet with rustic black pepper and a hint of game on the nose of this bottling. Riper cranberry flavors, more pepper and a pinch of oregano converge on the refreshing palate.

name: Robert Hall 2018 Cavern Select Carignan (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Aromas of black plum, blackberry and a hint of lavender make for a compelling focus in this bottling, which includes 9% Grenache. There's a crisp snap to the palate, where raspberry jam and violet flavors ride a solid structure and bright acidity, making for a dark and dense yet lively sip.

name: Robert Hall 2018 Cavern Select G-S-M (Paso Robles)
review: This blend of 49% Grenache, 43% Syrah and 8% Mourvèdre is quite rich on the nose, but not overripe, offering aromas of açai, blueberry, flower petals and thick vanilla and caramel tones. The palate is rich in flavors of blackberry syrup and elderflower, while bright acidity drives into a sprightly finish.

name: Robert Hall 2018 Cavern Select Tempranillo (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Pleasant aromas of cola and baked boysenberrry meet with lavender and crushed lava rocks on the complex nose of this bottling, which includes 12% Syrah. The palate starts smooth on chiseled tannins that slowly turn leathery in texture, carrying flavors of blueberry, cracked pepper, oregano and wild thyme. Drink through 2033.

name: Robert Biale 2018 Black Chicken Zinfandel (Napa Valley)
review: The producer's marquee wine and a nod to its long-standing ties to farming, this is fresh and earthy in notes of crushed rock, slate, dried herb and black pepper. The fruit is layered and sumptuous in flavors of raspberry and black cherry, while supple tannins and a hint of toasted oak provide framing.

name: Robert Biale 2018 R.W. Moore Vineyard Zinfandel (Coombsville)
review: First planted in 1905, this is a relatively cool-climate site that imparts great brightness and tart acidity to the wine. A dark cherry flavor is powerfully represented, alongside baked plum, blackberry and black pepper. A taut structure suggests this will do well with time in bottle. Best from 2028–2033.

name: Robert Biale 2018 Varozza Vineyard Zinfandel (St. Helena)
review: Brawny and thick in notes of blackberry, dark cherry and cola, this wine comes from a 40-acre site of head-trained vines at the northern edge of St. Helena. A plush, opulent texture supports the powerful fruit, finishing on teases of nutmeg and black pepper.

name: Robert Biale 2018 Falleri Vineyard Zinfandel (Calistoga)
review: Thick, supple tannins wrap around lifted tones of strawberry and raspberry in this wine. From a site planted in 1924, it shows the successful partnership between producer, site and ideal vintage, finding balance and nuance that impress.

name: Robert Biale 2018 Gaudi Carli Vineyard Barbera (Calistoga)
review: This tiny production wine is made from some of the oldest Barbera in Napa Valley, planted more than 80 years ago. Tangy red fruit leads to flavors of just-picked blackberry, with bright acidity carrying the experience to an earthy mushroom finish.

name: Robert Biale 2018 Valsecchi Vineyard Zinfandel (Carneros)
review: One of the few and oldest vineyards planted to the variety in Carneros, this wine opens in sharp, tangy cherry and strawberry flavors dusted with baking spice. Bright acidity turns up at every level, though the wine still maintains plenty of full-bodied power, concentration and a creamy texture. A finishing touch of citrus underscores its lively nature.

name: Robert Biale 2018 Old Kraft Vineyard Zinfandel (St. Helena)
review: Juicy and pleasing, with undeniable complexity, this great wine tastes of brambly blackberry, cinnamon and plum accented by leather, black pepper and nutmeg. While full in feel, it shows balance and elegance. The tannins are well structured and supportive of the fruit.

name: Roar 2018 Sierra Mar Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Candied boysenberry and black-raspberry aromas meet with ample baking spice on the nose of this bottling, which manages to be both sharp and spicy. Fresh and lovely fruit qualities dominate the palate, from alpine strawberry to lush baked cherry, although the mace and star anise qualities are what make it shine.

name: Roar 2018 Rosella's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Snappy aromas of candied raspberry, lively hibiscus, subtle rose petal and star anise make for a very fresh and inviting nose on this bottling. The palate's baked raspberry and red-cherry flavors are cut by orange peel and licorice, making for a hedonistically complex sip.

name: Roar 2018 Garys' Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Red- and ripe baked cherry and strawberry-cream aromas pair with crushed slate and wild mint on the nose of this bottling. The palate is aggressively ripe on the sip, where more red-fruit flavors meet with candied plum and wild mints.

name: Roar 2018 Pisoni Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Brown sugar and Luxardo cherry aromas meet with nutmeg and cola spices on the layered, inviting, rich and delicious nose of this bottling. The single-vineyard expression is voluptuous on the palate, delivering joyous and vivid black-cherry flavors that are cut by an underlying acidity and structure, which allows the baking-spice backdrop to shine: a giant wine, with balance.

name: Roar 2018 Soberanes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Ripe cherry-pie aromas meet with lots of clove, fennel pollen and a hint of chipped slate on the nose of this bottling. The wine lands with the winery's characteristically juicy red fruit on the palate, blending raspberry-sorbet flavors with sharp mace and cinnamon spice, capturing such elements at their peak.

name: Roar 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: This appellation blend offers aromas of candied red cherry, strawberry and mint on the nose. The palate packs fresh, zesty and ripe red fruit with a complex array of herbs and spices, from tarragon and chamomile to marjoram, sage and aniseed.

name: Roaming Dog 2018 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas offer notes of boysenberry and huckleberry. Plush, palate-coating flavors follow, showing impressive balance. It's a lovely, fruit-filled wine.

name: Roaming Dog 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Spice and cherry aromas lead to lightly sweet dark-fruit flavors. It's a pleasant easy drinker.

name: Riley's Rows 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Dark-cherry and orange-peel flavors wrap around inviting layers of clove and cardamom in this easygoing, approachable wine. Lasting acidity keeps it fresh and focused.

name: Ridgecrest 2018 Gamay Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Tart and sappy, this is sourced from vines that are 30-plus years old. The blackberry fruit just tips over into ripeness, but keeps an herbal emphasis, along with a light streak of cola.

name: Ridge 2018 Benito Dusi Ranch Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Aroma of crushed blackberry, black plum and fig meet with a toasted grape-seed touch on the nose of this bottling from a historic ranch. Firm tannins and flashes of acid frame the sip, where roasted fig jam, smoked pork and light prune flavors arise.

name: Rickshaw 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: The aroma of this medium-bodied wine is quite earthy at first, then good flavors of black cherry and sour cherry take over, supported by light tannins and touch of tangy acidity.

name: Rickshaw 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Quite fruity and relatively light, this wine offers bright cherry and plum flavors, a moderate layer of baking spices and soft tannins.

name: Rickshaw 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This medium-bodied wine is dry, lean in flavor and has an earthy, metallic edge that heightens its taut impression on the palate.

name: Retzlaff 2018 Estate Bottled Merlot (Livermore Valley)
review: This expressive wine aged in French oak barrels offers a spicy bouquet of mint, cedar and cigar box followed by powerful and focused black-cherry flavors. It's all supported nicely by fine-grained tannins.

name: Replica 2018 Just Right Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: A dusty aroma leads to rather jammy flavors and baking-spice accents in this basic-quality, medium-bodied wine.

name: Replica 2018 Label Envy Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: This full-bodied and moderately tannic wine offers subtle wood-smoke aromas and black-cherry flavors spiced with black pepper and tea. It's dense with fruit and oak nuances and has plenty of grip in the texture.

name: Renwood 2018 Premier Old Vine Zinfandel (Amador County)
review: Ripe fruit flavors dominate this bold and full-bodied wine. Powerful berry and cherry aromas spill from the glass on the nose, while the palate follows suit with rich high-octane fruit.

name: Red Stitch 2018 Soberanes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of muddled black plum, turned earth and mahogany show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is framed by sandy tannins that will hold their structure for quite a while, framing roasted boysenberry, vanilla, clove and nutmeg flavors. Drink through 2033.

name: Red Stitch 2018 Sun Chase Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Red-cherry candies, wet slate, carnation and a hint of tarragon make for an inviting nose on this bottling. The slightly tart red-cherry and baked red-plum flavors meet with dried hibiscus and rose-petal flavors on the sip.

name: Red Stitch 2018 Rosella's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dark in the glass while quite earthy on the nose, this single-vineyard expression offers aromas of blackberry and crushed rocks. There are rich blackberry flavors to the jammy, explosively flavorful palate, with cardamom and clove providing spicy depth.

name: Red Newt Cellars 2018 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)
review: This red shows a pleasing mix of fruit and earth on the nose, displaying raspberry alongside tilled soil, red flowers and black pepper. There's solid intensity to the red fruit on the palate, with a floral note lingering throughout. A gentle cherry-pit astringency marks the close. Enjoy with a slight chill.

name: Reata 2018 Three County Pinot Noir (San Benito County-Sonoma County-Monterey County)
review: Smooth and ripe aromas of Bing cherry meet with caramel and nutmeg on the nose of this blend of three California counties where Pinot Noir rules. Clean flavors of cherry and dark berry are bolstered by oak tones and a firm tannic structure.

name: Read Holland 2018 Deep End Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: The black-fruit flavors go deep in this full-bodied, well-balanced, firm-textured wine made using 30% whole clusters. Light clove and cinnamon in the aromas lead to plenty of black-cherry and blackberry notes that fill the palate.

name: Raywood 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Central Coast)
review: Dried cherry, cured meat and lava-rock aromas show on the nose of this twist-top bottling. Fairly stiff tannins uphold the palate, where dark red berries meet with tea-leaf and coffee-bean flavors.

name: Raywood 2018 Merlot (Central Coast)
review: Light aromas of incense, dried blackberry and leather show on the nose of this bottling. It's slightly sour on the palate, where dank herbs and incense flavors arise.

name: Raymond 2018 Family Classic Cabernet Sauvignon (North Coast)
review: Vibrant, concentrated flavors of black currant and black cherry meet mint and clove accents in this full-bodied, effusive wine. Moderate tannins and a not-too-big body keep it in the easy-drinking mode.

name: Rancho Boa Vista 2018 Grenache (Ballard Canyon)
review: The winemaking hand of Doug Margerum shows on this delightful bottling that blends the bright and ripe sides of Grenache together. Hearty aromas of black cherry meet with earthy dust and concentrated rose water on the inviting nose. The palate is plump, flavorful and yet very tense and vibrant, offering pomegranate, raspberry and spice-rack elements.

name: Ranch 32 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: An earthy tone on the nose of this bottling gives way to sour cherry, oregano and rust. The palate is full and lush, with baked cherry and cranberry flavors yet not much tension.

name: Ram's Gate 2018 Silver Eagle Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Meaty and thick in a full-bodied style, this vineyard-designate is also racy in acidity and freshness. Forest floor, black tea and dried herb accent the ample ripeness and girth of black cherry and strawberry.

name: Ram's Gate 2018 Estate Pinot Noir (Carneros)
review: Given 16 months in 34% new French oak, with a small percentage of whole-cluster fermentation, this wine is earthy and fresh in tangy, tart strawberry and rhubarb. Thick tannins wrap around and provide structure and breadth without overt weight, allowing the wine to finish in sage and dried herb.

name: Ram's Gate 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Fruity and earthy in good measure, this memorable wine is effusive in baked cherry and racy acidity, the spice components equally intriguing in cardamom, clove and pepper. Cohesive and supple on the palate, it is a great showcase for the vintage and appellation.

name: Ram's Gate 2018 Roberts Road Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Spicy and earthy on the nose, this wine continues those themes on the palate, showing high-toned acidity and a wealth of black tea and forest floor. Cool climate in nature, it has balance and supple tannins, with flavors of cherry, strawberry and rhubarb.

name: Ram's Gate 2018 Bush-Crispo Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Spicy red cherry and strawberry highlight this seamlessly bright, complex and delicious wine from a long-standing vineyard designate. With secondary accents of vanilla, clove and black tea, it provides varietal specificity within a lush, opulent and ultimately balanced framework.

name: Raeburn 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Sharp, tangy acidity lies beneath layers of thick, grainy tannin and oak in this intense, full-bodied wine. Forest floor, clove and rhubarb highlight the palate.

name: Rabble 2018 Red (Paso Robles)
review: Blackberry juice aromas meet with an herbal array of oregano and chaparral spice as well as tarry earth on the nose of this blend. The palate shows plenty of dried herbs as well, from oregano to marjoram to tarragon, which give depth to the black-plum backdrop.

name: R. Bryce 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Light red-cherry aromas meet with sagebrush and white pepper on the tart nose of this bottling. The palate is bright, with fresh raspberry flavors that grow more concentrated toward the finish, lifted throughout by crushed sage.

name: Qupé 2018 Syrah (Central Coast)
review: Though founder Bob Lindquist is no longer part of this winery, the brand still delivers value-driven Syrah. Aromas of roasted meat, black pepper, lavender and light berries kick off the nose, while the savory palate blends pepper, crisp fruit and dried herb flavors into an easy-to-quaff, light-bodied expression.

name: Qupé 2018 Grenache (Santa Barbara County)
review: Elegant and lush aromas of baking spice and pie crust surround a more tart display of Montmorency cherry on the nose of this bottling. The palate is heavily spiced with star anise, cardamom-cream and rose-extract flavors, as creamy tannins frame the lush cherry flavors. It's a complete wine.

name: Quady North 2018 Bomba Grenache (Rogue Valley)
review: This is a pleasant, though rather light, and well-balanced wine. The rhubarb and wild red-berry fruits are embroidered with hints of spice and cocoa. It was aged in neutral French puncheons for nine months, and should be consumed over the next three years.

name: Purple Hands 2018 Latchkey Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: All Pommard clone, this is loaded with herbs and spices, along with smooth red berry fruit. Highlights of rosemary, cinnamon, sea salt and sandalwood add detail, texture and length, and the citrusy tangerine completes a well-balanced finish, along with a touch of chocolate. Enjoy this now and over the next half decade.

name: Purple Hands 2018 Kropf Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: A look at the rim of the wine shows hints of sunset reds, suggesting that it is farther along the aging process than many of the winery's other 2018s. No worries—this is the one to drink first. Smooth and rich with plum and berry fruit, it's dusted with sweet barrel spices and underscored with lightly herbal, tea-touched tannins.

name: Purple Hands 2018 Wichmann Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: The aromatics stand out here, with clover, wet hay and suggestions of watermelon, pomegranate and strawberries. Though it feels lightly ripened, the flavors are persistent and detailed, the finish textured. Aging 11 months in one-third new French oak adds a dash of cinnamon.

name: Purple Hands 2018 Shea Vineyard Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: There's a pretty floral top note here, leading into a lightly ripened wine with flavors of sliced orange and strawberry shortcake. As it rolls across the palate, a hint of compost emerges, along with tea-flavored tannins. It's well integrated and already in a fine drinking window.

name: Purple Hands 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This starts out sharp and slightly volatile, with fruit flavors of rhubarb and wild red berries. Fermented with a mix of wild and inoculated yeasts, it spent 11 months in one third new French oak and the rest neutral. It finishes with a hint of chocolate and tannins with the taste of chamomile tea.

name: Purple Hands 2018 Lone Oak Ranch Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is compounded from eight vineyards scattered over five Willamette Valley AVAs. A lovely wine, it tastes of ripe plums accented with savory herbs and red licorice. It's seamless and compelling, clearly given the same care as the unblended single-vineyard wines of which it is composed.

name: Publix 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Light in color and delicately structured, this red picks up weight and body as it opens, revealing layers of bright orange and strawberry. It has grippy tannin and a length of baking spice on the finish.

name: Prospice 2018 Resurgent Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: The aromas bring notes of flower, dried herb and cherry. The palate is all about tannin and acid structure at first, with the fruit filling in over time.

name: Prospice 2018 Alder Ridge Vineyard Mourvèdre (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: The aromas are unique for this variety, with notes of black raspberry, cranberry and maraschino cherry, also showing a bit of reduction. There's a sensuous feel to the palate that dances lightly. It sticks around on the finish. A pleasing sense of acidity ups the interest. It should only get better with some additional time in bottle.

name: Prospice 2018 Seven Hills Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: The aromas intrigue, with notes of graphite, fresh raspberry and spice. Red and black fruit and herb flavors coat the palate from end to end, going full-throttle without going over the top. It's a lovely, riper-style Washington Cabernet Sauvignon.

name: Prospice 2018 Resurgent Vineyard Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: This vineyard, which has cobblestone soils reminiscent of the Rocks District, is quickly distinguishing itself as a premier Syrah site. The aromas pop, with notes of smoked meat, potpourri, wet stone, fresh chopped parsley and purple fruit. The palate brings swoon-worthy, sleek savory flavors.

name: Prospice 2018 Les Collines Vineyard Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: This comes from one of the state's premier Syrah sites. The aromas are brooding, with notes of berry, whole parsley, violet and purple fruit. Sleek, sumptuous purple fruit flavors follow, bringing a lively sense of acidity along with granular tannins that smooth out with time open.

name: Prophecy 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This simple and fruity wine tastes rich and jammy and seems sweet, while the texture is soft and broad.

name: Proper 2018 Estate Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: This wine could only come from one place, with its aromas of wet rock, savory herb, flower, plum, orange rind and olive brine. The palate shows a sense of deftness to the fruit and savory flavors, along with the Rocks District's plush feel.

name: Private Property 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Aromas of plum, light red currant, red apple, hibiscus and smoked meat are both pretty and rustic on the nose. The palate offers suave and rich cherry flavors alongside a spicy star anise kick.

name: Primarius 2018 Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: The aromas display a sense of purity, with notes of strawberry, cinnamon stick, peppermint and spice. Fresh fruit-forward flavors follow, backed by bunched up tannins. A lingering finish caps it off. It needs time to open up, but it delivers.

name: Prima Materia 2018 Vinum in Agris Negroamaro
review: Meaty, gamy aromas and rich plummy flavors make this full-bodied wine likeable and different. It feels extra smooth in texture, low in tannins but high in fruit and subtle peppery spices.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Prototype Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: Nicely focused fruit flavors and good balance make this medium-bodied wine both delicious and appetizing. Moderate acidity and a touch of tannin firm it up, carrying the fresh plum and cherry flavors well.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Prototype Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This wine is uncomplicated in flavor, medium-bodied and mild in texture. Light herb aromas meet low-key plum flavors that verge on sweetness.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: Herbal and oaky, this is a sizable red wine with grippy tannin weight and a lasting taste of cedar and toasted oak.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Bound Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)
review: A thick, herbal nose of clove and cedar lead the way to a core of thick blackberry and oak in this value-minded, 100% varietal wine. Full bodied and rich, it has a densely viscous finish.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)
review: This wine has a mix of sweet and herbal components that never find compatibility, with planky wood framing dark, brambly berry and tobacco.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Gabrielle Ashley Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)
review: With an up-front perfume of woody oak, this wine develops hints of cedar and herb atop its core of jammy red fruit. Soft, simple and a tad flabby, it is approachable and meant to enjoy now.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Blended with 10% Petit Verdot, this wine opens with the sweet perfume of American oak before delving into more char and barbecue flavor. Dark berry and leather add to the overtly ripe mix.

name: Precision Wine 2018 Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)
review: This is a thick, viscous and grippingly ripe wine, sweet in vanilla and berry flavor, with touches of charred oak and smoked meat.

name: Postmark 2018 Merlot (Napa Valley)
review: Perfumed in baking spice, this is a soft, juicy and rounded wine that will appeal widely and is value-minded in price. Dark-plum, cherry and vanilla flavors wrap around the toasted oak and supple, integrated tannin.

name: Postmark 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: As the debut vintage of this wine, this impresses in its easygoing nature of soft, smooth tannins and accents of tobacco, dried herb and earth. Sourced from multiple appellations, it finishes in bursts of blackberry and dark chocolate flavors.

name: Poseidon 2018 Estate Pinot Noir (Carneros-Napa Valley)
review: This is a dark, dense, full-bodied wine influenced by smoky toasty oak in the aromas, flavors and texture. Fruit flavors seem submerged in charred wood and coffee beans, giving it a brooding expression and a big presence.

name: Portlandia 2018 Momtazi Vineyard Pinot Noir (McMinnville)
review: These biodynamic grapes add texture and subtle aromatics, driving up the complexity and incorporating a pleasing mix of floral, fruit and herbal components. It's a full bodied wine, with marionberry and blackberry fruit and highlights of nutmeg spice and cut tobacco.

name: Portlandia 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This Pinot Noir includes 30% Hyland Vineyard fruit. Brisk black-cherry scents and flavors come with a roasted, grilled quality. Those charred notes carry over into the finish, which is surprisingly substantial for a wine at this price.

name: Portalupi 2018 L Venturi Vineyards Petite Sirah (Mendocino County)
review: This full-bodied wine is serious, powerful and age-worthy. The warm layer of fine-grained tannins that cloaks the enticing woodsmoke, game and blackberry flavors now will melt with time and bring out even more complexity. Best from 2025.

name: Portalupi 2018 Pauli Ranch Barbera (Mendocino County)
review: A savory, earthy expression of bay leaves and grape stems gives this medium- to full-bodied wine an interesting twist while plummy flavors, good acidity and light tannins balance it out well.

name: Portalupi 2018 Shake Ridge Ranch Barbera (Amador County)
review: A deft touch of new oak and sumptuous, concentrated blueberry and black-cherry flavors are delicious and impressive in this full-bodied wine. It is big and superexpressive, layering cinnamon and nutmeg over the dramatic, ripe fruit notes framed by moderate fine-grained tannins. Best through 2028.

name: Portalupi 2018 Shake Ridge Ranch Primitivo (Amador County)
review: Absolutely gorgeous black-fruit flavors burst out of this deep, dark, full-bodied Primitivo. It is a supergenerous, ripe and lip-smacking expression. Blackberry and black-cherry flavors practically overwhelm the palate and linger on the finish.

name: Portalupi 2018 Shake Ridge Ranch Zinfandel (Amador County)
review: Opulent fruit flavors and a firm but velvety texture make this full-bodied wine big and bold. The baking-spice, grilled plum and blackberry-syrup flavors will make it a great after-dinner pour.

name: Portalupi 2018 Shake Ridge Ranch Barbera (Amador County)
review: Reduced at first, this full-bodied wine brings on powerful black-cherry and sour-cherry flavors backed by firm tannins and lively acidity. It's a palate-cleansing wine that will pair well with the richest roasts and risottos.

name: Porlier 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Templeton Gap District)
review: Candied red-cherry, tangy berry and dried fennel-stalk aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate combines smashed plum and cherry with savory peppercorn, leather and dried meat flavors.

name: Point of the Bluff 2018 Blaufränkisch (Finger Lakes)
review: Aromas of rubber, warm spices and a bit of mentholated black pepper arise on the nose, while the smooth, supple palate is drive by tart red cherry. Polished tannins and juicy acidity frame it all, with a bit of pink peppercorn on the midpalate that extends to the finish.

name: Point of the Bluff 2018 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)
review: Pepper, menthol and dried leaves are lifted on the nose, with cherry and raspberry coulis at the core. It shows a juicy raspberry sauce note on the palate, with more earthy, forest floor-driven tones. There's good depth and structure to this red, with a well-knit frame of tannins and integrated acidity lending ample lift.

name: POE 2018 Van Der Kamp Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)
review: Compost, tea and crunchy red fruit mark the profile of this light-bodied, delicately layered wine from the mighty mountain site. Textured and savory, it offers complexity and structure, with lasting acidity.

name: POE 2018 Van Der Kamp Pinot Meunier (Sonoma Mountain)
review: This is a light and earthy red wine from the rare variety, sourced from a high-elevation site. Red cherry, orange peel and pomegranate give it a tanginess that matches the crisp acidity, followed by a complementary slate of black tea and clove.

name: POE 2018 Pinot Noir (Mendocino Ridge)
review: An aroma of violets leads to very fruity and floral flavors in this medium-bodied wine from relatively new vintner Samantha Sheehan. Light tannins add a bit of grip to the texture, while good, tart cherry and cranberry flavors open up on the palate. No rush to drink it; best through 2024.

name: Pisoni 2018 XX Anniversary Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: This anniversary bottling is both titillating and suave in all aspects. The nose starts with loads of black cherry, vanilla and sarsaparilla aromas. The palate hits with a flash of acidity that fades into smooth flavors of black plum and Luxardo cherry alongside layers of cardamom and licorice.

name: Pike Road 2018 Shea Vineyard Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: This stellar vineyard shines in this wine, offering firm, well-defined tones of purple and black fruit. It's polished to an almost-velvety smoothness, and the lush fruit is coated with darker flavors of coffee and chocolate.

name: Pike Road 2018 Meyer Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Fruit purchased from this Lemelson vineyard is featured here, in a forward style that wraps in broad streaks of tobacco and cola around core fruit flavors of blackberry and cherry. It's quite young and the best drinking window is some years away, but the components are there for a quality bottle.

name: Pike Road 2018 Xander Taryn Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: Along with entry-level varietal wines, Pike Road offers limited-production single-vineyard Pinots. This young wine is still knitting itself together. In one corner is fresh blackberry fruit, while across the way it brings up wet rock minerality. There's a bit of a gulf in between these dominant components, which may be resolved with a bit more bottle age.

name: Pike Road 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Production of this wine has skyrocketed, making it one of the most widely available and affordable all-Willamette Valley Pinots on the market. A clean, basic take on the variety, this is stiffly bound up at the moment, and requires decanting. Earthy highlights underscore black cherries, and the tannins finish with a burst of black-tea astringency.

name: Picayune 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Forest pine and strawberry flavors are boosted by lively acidity in this well-made, balanced wine. With integrated oak and tannin, it allows the savory fruit to speak, finishing with an elegance of nutmeg and clove.

name: Pendulum 2018 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Light aromas of spice, plum and cherry lead to a palate chock-full of blue fruit flavors, backed by lightly astringent tannins that soften over time. Give it brief time in the decanter to see it at its best. There's a lot of quality and value to be had.

name: Pence 2018 Gamay (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Snappy aromas of carnation, rosebuds, raspberry sorbet and wet gravel quickly indicate that this is a Gamay Noir, such a promising grape for the entire Central Coast. There is grippy texture on the sip, where underripe strawberry, red-plum and raspberry flavors meet with wet stone and fresh acidity.

name: Pence 2018 PTG Pinot Noir-Gamay (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This winery is on the frontline of California's Gamay renaissance, and this bottling of 50% Pinot Noir and 50% Gamay is great evidence of Gamay's potential. Aromas of carnation, rose petal, smashed raspberry, red currant and wet stone lead into a crisp, lively sip, where white pepper and river stone decorate the fresh fruit-punch and candied cranberry flavors. The structure remains taut.

name: Pellegrini 2018 AV Old Vines Zinfandel (Alexander Valley)
review: Aged in French oak barrels, this 100% varietal wine opens in a lightness of baking spice, cranberry and dark cherry. Thick tannin and a touch of toasted oak make it robust on the palate, the underlying acidity subtle and solid, with length and breadth.

name: Pellegrini 2018 RRV Cuvée Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earth, clove, nutmeg and forest floor dominate the medium-bodied palate of this smooth, spicy wine, a blend from several neighborhoods within the appellation. Rhubarb and strawberry develop through a creamy center.

name: Peirano 2018 Illusion Red (Lodi)
review: Floral and licorice aromas are backed up by good, rich grapey, jammy flavors in this smooth and broad-textured wine.

name: Peirano 2018 The Heritage Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)
review: This wine is in the classic style, offering ample, concentrated black-fruit flavors held rather tightly by moderate tannins and balanced acidity. Subtle baking spices and chocolate come out on the palate to add extra interest.

name: Peirano 2018 The Heritage Collection Petite Sirah (Lodi)
review: Relatively light and superfruity, this medium-bodied wine shows an approachable, quaffable side to the varietal. Sweet plums, raspberries and a touch of cherry syrup stand out.

name: Peirano 2018 The Other Red (Lodi)
review: A light herbal aroma leads to nicely ripe and rich red- and black-cherry flavors while a full body and moderate tannins give the wine good structure.

name: Peirano 2018 The Heritage Collection Tempranillo (Lodi)
review: Savory aromas of tobacco, bay leaf and beef stew meet abundant black-cherry and blackberry flavors in this full-bodied, moderately tannic wine. It has more than enough tasty fruit flavors to balance out the firm structure.

name: Peirano 2018 The Heritage Collection Malbec (Lodi)
review: This wine shows an unusual vegetal, flinty aroma and moderate flavors of peppercorns and cooked plums. It's a bit edgy but decent to drink.

name: Pedroncelli 2018 Signature Selection Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Light and delicate in style, with spicy red fruit and a length of balanced acidity, this is a value-minded red that delivers good flavor and texture. Orange peel and toasted oak finish things out.

name: Peake Ranch 2018 John Sebastiano Vineyard Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Darkly roasted aromas of seared beef, charcoal, blueberry, lavender and purple flowers make for a very intense nose on this bottling. It's lavish on the palate, where dark berries, nutmeg, star anise and cardamom converge atop a creamy yet tense mouthfeel. It offers a great cohesion of spice, fruit, power and finesse.

name: Peake Ranch 2018 Bellis Noir Red (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Wet stone, lavender, black raspberry and char show on this blend of Syrah and Grenache from a property where the Rhône grapes are thriving. The palate is hearty with cassis, tart with black raspberry, floral with purple buds, and finished on a savory kick of charred meat.

name: Peake Ranch 2018 Peake Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Expertly focused and intense aromas of black cherry, clove and star anise make for a very exuberant and delicious nose on this bottling from this brand's estate, which surrounds the winery. Layers of wood spice and black cherry are wrapped in soft voluptuous tannins on the palate, making for a very full and satisfying sip.

name: Peake Ranch 2018 John Sebastiano Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Baked black cherry, nutmeg cream and a prominent amount of licorice spice show on the nose of this bottling from a steeply sloped vineyard on the eastern side of the appellation. The palate tightens up a bit, showing tart and brisk red plum as well as brushes of eucalyptus oil and mint.

name: Peake Ranch 2018 Peake Ranch Vineyard Grenache (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Lightly candied raspberry and cherry aromas meet with rosewater and vanilla on the soft and lush nose of this estate bottling. Soft, plush tannins and a bright acidity frames the palate, where rich cherry and baked strawberry flavors meet with rose-petal and red-spice elements, leading into a toasty vanilla finish.

name: Peake Ranch 2018 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Concentrated aromas of toasty oak, caramel and dark-plum sauce show on the nose of this intense appellation blend. The palate shows a crisper side, with black-raspberry and red-plum tones, layered in dried herbs and cardamom before finishing with a kiss of oak.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Ms Behave Malbec (Paso Robles)
review: Dense aromas of blackberry, ham, coffee and a hint of leather show on the brooding nose of this bottling. The palate is loaded with earthy and savory flavors of bloody meats and tobacco, as sandy tannins frame the dark fruit flavors.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Devine Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Savory aromas of dried meat, crushed herbs, peppercorn and ripe, dark fruits show on the nose of this bottling. There's a strong cherry-jam streak to the palate, which is lifted by dried herbs and spearmint tones.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Bailey Zinfandel (Adelaida District)
review: Juicy aromas of black plum remain focused and intense on the nose of this bottling. The palate is more complex, carrying flavors of roasted blackberry, black pepper, elderberry and purple flowers.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Mustard Creek Zinfandel (Adelaida District)
review: Aromas of smoked blackberry, fennel and oregano are intense but not overripe on the nose of this bottling, which includes 11% Petite Sirah. Generous flavors of baked red plum, rose petals and tea leaves lead into a graham cracker finish, all framed in a delicate tension.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Vortex Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of dark berry, smoke, leather and crushed black rocks show on the nose of this bottling, which includes 9% Petite Sirah and 8% Counoise. A baked boysenberry flavor meets with dried fig and rock tones on the palate.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 G-S-M (Adelaida District)
review: Roasted cherry, black tea, iodine and charred blood-sausage aromas make for a heady mix of earth, fruit and game elements in this blend of 69% Grenache, 22% Syrah and 9% Mourvèdre. It's thick, full and rich on the palate, with flavors of mulberry jam, roasted plum, cola and tarragon, all framed by polished tannins.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Especial Zinfandel (Adelaida District)
review: Precise aromas of strawberry sorbet meet with a bit of smoke and a pinch of baking spice on the nose of this bottling. There's a strong amount of smoke on the palate, with lots of hickory spice, but also clove, star anise, rich red-fruit and barbecued pork flavors, making this an excellent pairing for the summer grill party.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Mustang Springs Zinfandel (Adelaida District)
review: There's an unexpected floral elegance to the nose of this bottling, with hints of potpourri playing alongside the more standard roasted red-fruit aromas. The palate aims for freshness, combining strawberry and hibiscus with a hint of rose petal.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Willow Zinfandel (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Dark strawberry, turned soil and a sweet cherry-wood-smoke tone show on the nose of this bottling from the iconic and pioneering winery. Blackberry and cracked pepper flavors ride a mellow texture on the palate.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)
review: Heavy aromas of roasted coffee, soy-crusted-beef roast and a light brush of purple flowers make for a savory nose in this bottling. The wine lands smoothly on the palate, with just enough tannins to hold up the blackberry, purple flower and pepper flavors, finishing with a vanilla-caramel swirl.

name: Peachy Canyon 2018 The Odd One Red (Adelaida District)
review: Bright, fresh aromas of raspberry and hibiscus make for a zesty entry on the nose of this atypical blend of 62% Zinfandel, 15% Carignan, 15% Counoise and 8% Petite Sirah. The palate is refreshing in flavors of black raspberry compote, fennel pollen and peppery spices that would sing even with a slight chill.

name: Paul O'Brien 2018 Pinot Noir (Umpqua Valley)
review: Sharply focused raspberry fruit with a citrus tang carries this wine. The fermentation in open-top concrete included 24% whole clusters, and the finished wine was aged for 14 months in 30% new French oak. There's a light whiff of silage, not unpleasant, as it fades out with drying tannins.

name: Paul Dolan 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendocino County)
review: Minty and spicy in aroma, showing deep fruit, tobacco and leather flavors, this full-bodied wine is generous, complex and well structured. It compares to wines two or three times more expensive. Firm tannins and full body do a great job of supporting all the ripe black cherries and spicy oak notes. Best from 2023.

name: Paul Dolan 2018 Made with Organic Grapes Zinfandel (Mendocino County)
review: Complex aromas and nice flavor concentration give this full-bodied wine an extra boost. Well-defined mint and floral aromas give way to black cherry and blackberry on the palate, firmed up by moderate tannins and good acidity.

name: Patton Valley 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This affordably priced wine seems to have been given extra careful hands-on treatment, with native yeast, basket pressing and all estate-grown fruit. A bright burst of raspberry fruit comes with a dash of pepper, and adds on more fruit flavors of plum and blackberry. A pretty floral component enhances both the aroma and the finish.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Durant Vineyard Madrone Block Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Yet another standout among the two dozen Pinots from Patricia Green Cellars, this melds scents of pretty baking spices to spicy cherry fruit. Complex and expressive, among the details are hints of orange peel and a whiff of composted earth. It's a stylish and unique expression of this quality site in the Dundee Hills, and at this price represents a sensational bargain.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Estate Vineyard First Vines Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: The previous owners planted these vines in 1984, and they've been overtaken by phylloxera, so this is likely the last edition of the ‘First Vines' cuvée. With old-vine elegance and subtle detail, it mixes brambly berries along with a streak of cherry cola. Balance and texture are its hallmarks, and if this indeed is the end of it, it's a graceful, generous goodbye.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Notorious Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This multi-vineyard, barrel-select reserve wine sports a green label and is given the royal treatment of 100% new French oak. It's rich and toasty, loaded with ripe berry and cherry fruit, and streaked with cola. The full-bodied, deeply-driven flavors build into a lush, sexy finish. It's instantly impressive, long and smooth, and beautifully proportioned.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Dijon 115 Clone Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: A striking, dense and muscular wine, this is a standout among a slew of Freedom Hill designates from Patricia Green Cellars. Sappy blackberry and black-cherry fruit are streaked with highlights of red licorice, coffee and a whiff of charred wood. The tannins are polished and firm, and at 14 years of age, these vines are just entering their prime.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Estate Vineyard Etzel Block Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: This block borders the vineyards of Michael Etzel (Beaux Frères). A mix of Dijon 114 and Pommard clones, it's a sculpted wine, stylish and tart. It seems younger than the rest of the 2018s, and more in need of further bottle age to flesh out. Blackberry fruit and tart tangy acids define a compact wine with pinpoint focus. Drink this from 2022 on and over the next decade.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Balcombe Block 1B Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: This brightly fruity, downright pretty wine brings silky blueberry and cherry fruit front and center. The slightly loamy tannins have a generous texture, and the finish is supported with balanced acidity and a sprinkling of baking spices.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Olenik Vineyard Wadensvil Block Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: Licorice and espresso highlights cloak ripe black-cherry fruit sourced from vines planted in 1991. There's a lightly roasted, slightly charred character, adding texture and aromatics, and the finishing tannins are ripe and polished.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Pommard Clone Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This bursts open with lovely blackberry and black-cherry fruit, along with generous streaks of mocha and cola. It's balanced and well integrated already, with a long, clean, textured finish: a very fine value.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Coury Clone Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: The cuttings were obtained from Hyland vineyard vines planted in 1972. It's an aromatic wine, with clean and fresh scents and flavors of rhubarb and raspberry. Some pretty toasty accents come up through the elegant, complex finish.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Medici Vineyard Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: The color already shows hints of bricking, and this wine seems to be maturing quickly, and may be enjoyed immediately. There's a whiff of compost in the nose, muted cherry fruit, and an impression of sawdust. That said, it's forward, full bodied and quite delicious once it's breathed open a bit. Drink now and over the next two or three years.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Weber Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: The vines date back to 1983, among the oldest in the entire Patricia Green portfolio. Brambly purple plum and berry fruits come with a streak of graphite or pencil lead, which underscores the slightly grainy tannins. There's a whiff of fresh ground coffee also.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Mysterious Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: From an unnamed, apparently high-profile vineyard, this is 100% Pommard from vines over 20 years old. Bright red fruits favor strawberry and raspberry, with tart acidity under some black-cherry muscle. The finish brings in astringent minerality, with little apparent new barrel influence (so far).

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Hyland Vineyard Coury Clone Pinot Noir (McMinnville)
review: A strong citrus streak runs through this wine, from top to bottom. Flavors highlight oranges, limes and tart berries. As is typical in this small AVA, there's a sliver of minerality anchoring the finish. Unspecified (new, neutral?) barrel aging shows in a lightly toasty frame.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Olenik Vineyard Anklebreaker Block Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: In this vintage this cuvée, named for the rocky soils, seems severely astringent, with assertive minerality. The flavors are tart and thin, wild raspberries and mixed red fruits. Perhaps a bit more bottle age will help to open it up.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Estate Vineyard Bonshaw Block Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: A small block from the estate that regularly overperforms, thanks to its combination of nearly 30-year-old vines and 100% Pommard (as in yummy) clones. Tart cherry, cola and sandalwood highlight this nicely textured wine, with excellent balance throughout. There are lovely touches of mineral, toasted nut and a whiff of popcorn. Drink now through the mid-2020s.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Balcombe Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Sourced from Pommard clones planted in 1990, this perches ripe blueberry and black raspberry fruit atop drying, mineral-tinged tannins. The concentration and depth, even at such a young age, argue for cellaring this gem at least another five years, and enjoy prime-time drinking through the back half of the decade.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Ridgecrest Vineyard Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: This new cuvée is sourced from the oldest vineyard on Ribbon Ridge, which dates to the early 1980s. Aromas of herb and earth with a hint of compost accompany a deeply extracted wine loaded with black cherry fruit. The firm, substantial tannins bring flavors of dark chocolate and espresso into play, all buttressed with ample acidity. A finishing note of cinnamon spice adds the final farewell.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Estate Vineyard Old Vine Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Happily this old vine selection is among the winery's biggest production Pinots. The texture and detail that often accompany old vine wines are here in abundance. Flavors deftly mix black cherry, coffee and sandalwood, with toasted walnut highlights. The fruit-acid-tannin balance is textbook perfect.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: One of the winery's biggest production Pinots, this deftly expresses its core of blueberry, blackberry and black cherry fruit, in a textured, mineral-soaked framework. Light scents and streaks of cola and tobacco contribute more detail and depth. An early release, this should continue to develop and drink well over the next five to eight years.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Marine Sedimentary Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: Rich and steely, this offers soil-driven suggestions of iron and graphite, along with a range of black fruits. Released quite young, it rewards extra breathing time with a broader palate, good balance and a lightly toasty finish.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Volcanic Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: This is a blend designed to showcase the volcanic Jory soils of the Dundee Hills, an interesting companion to the winery's Marine Sedimentary bottling. In this vintage the Volcanic is the more fruit-driven and broadly open of the two, with tart cherry fruit highlighted with citrusy acidity. The herbal side of the grape is also on full display, though the overall balance keeps everything in proportion.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Durant Vineyard Bishop Block Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: Smooth and fruity, the flavors run to strawberry and pomegranate rather than darker fruits. It's an elegant, medium-bodied wine, all Pommard clone, balanced and good for near-term drinking.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Freedom Hill Vineyard Wadensvil Clone Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Sourced from 20-year-old vines, this single-clone cuvée charms with pretty floral overtones around tart red fruits. At this early stage it turns a bit chalky in the mouth, though more bottle age may help to smooth it out. The finish brings up a mix of fresh herbs. Drink after 2021.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Estate Vineyard Wadensvil Block Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: From vines planted to a three-acre block in 1997, this is a dense, textural wine with ample savory notes. There's a salty hint to the black cherry fruit, along with streaks of cola, earth, bark and coffee grounds. As with all the Patty Green wines, aeration is recommended in order to release the aromatics and open up the palate.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Pungent with spicy herbal intensity, this offers balanced yet tart berry and cherry fruit, with an earthy base. It smooths out nicely with a bit of breathing time, and at this entry-level price it is a fine introduction to this exemplary Ribbon Ridge estate.

name: Patricia Green Cellars 2018 Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: The winery's reserve is actually the low-priced, entry-level wine among close to two dozen Pinots from Patricia Green each vintage. The most widely produced as well as the most affordable, it brings a nice mix of blackberry and cherry fruit, ripe tannins and a streak of graphite. Though the grapes were sourced from throughout the Willamette Valley, the wine retains its focus and finishes with a hint of smoky coffee.

name: Paso Highlands 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles Highlands District)
review: Dense aromas of blackberry and black cherry meet with caramel and oak on the nose of this bottling, which also offers some dried herbs and dusty rocks. It's ripe and juicy on the palate, but there is plenty of acidity and stiff tannins to hold up the roasted blackberry flavors.

name: Parducci 2018 True Grit Reserve Petite Sirah (Mendocino)
review: Powerful and poised, this full-bodied wine takes the grape variety to a new level, combining just-ripe black fruits with pepper, earth and mineral flavors on a layered texture of fine grained tannins. Great depth, concentration and a brilliant smoky berry character make it a classic. Best from 2027.

name: Parducci 2018 Small Lot Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendocino County)
review: Nicely balanced and sleek in texture, this medium-bodied wine is easy to enjoy and uncomplicated, offering a smooth texture, good acidity and well-blended berry and plum flavors.

name: Parducci 2018 True Grit Reserve Red (Mendocino County)
review: So polished and creamy that it's seductive, this full-bodied blend of Syrah, Zinfandel and other varieties lavishes tempting baking spices on top of deep ripe black-cherry and dark-chocolate flavors. With 48% Petite Sirah in the blend, it's very concentrated and should develop well through at least 2025.

name: Parducci 2018 Small Lot Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Great acidity and lively red-cherry and raspberry flavors light up this well-balanced and full-bodied wine. Light but firm tannins boost the mouthfeel and keep the impression fresh through the finish.

name: Paradise Springs 2018 K III Red (California)
review: This twist-top blend of 60% Grenache, 20% Syrah and 20% Mourvèdre is all about freshness and fun, starting with refreshing aromas of black raspberry, uncracked peppercorns and wet stone. Those same elements show on the palate, which includes some carbonic touches as well as fennel-pollen spice.

name: Panther Creek 2018 Winemaker's Cuvée Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Bright and fruity, this jumps up with a broad wash of blueberry fruit across the palate. The tannins seem to dry it out a bit, with flavors of black tea and graphite, but it all works seamlessly through a lingering finish. Roughly one-quarter of the wine was aged in new French oak.

name: Pali 2018 Riviera Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This fantastic value is primarily sourced from the winery's estate plus another small family vineyard in Sonoma Coast. Intense, fresh and pristine aromas of boysenberry meet with cocoa on the nose. The palate delivers the same berry intensity atop a strong splash of acidity.

name: Pali 2018 Huntington Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Aromas of roasted black-cherry, blistered plum and cola make for a hearty nose. The palate combines roasted strawberry with toasted sage and thyme, finishing on a fresh hibiscus kick.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2018 Comes A Time Alta Colina Vineyard Mourvèdre (Adelaida District)
designation: Comes A Time Alta Colina Vineyard
review: Extremely elegant aromas of black cherry wrapped around black lava rocks are lifted further by dried violets, light herb and toasted wood on the nose of this bottling. There's a great grip and stickiness to the tannins that will ensure long aging, yet the dark-cherry and cardamom flavors are immediately accessible. Drink now through 2030.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2018 Either Side of the Hill Glenrose Vineyard Syrah (Adelaida District)
designation: Either Side of the Hill Glenrose Vineyard
review: A beautiful and dense shade of dark purple, this intense bottling begins with aromas of elderberry jam and black-currant pie that demand repeated returns on the nose. It's soft and lushly texture on the palate, where cassis, charred wood and peppercorn flavors prove very complex and compelling.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2018 The Golden Road Yankowski Vineyard Red (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Lavish and intense aromas of baked blackberry, light licorice and crushed slate on the nose of this blend of 60% Graciano and 40% Mourvedre. There's a fascinating character to the sip, where candied cassis, coffee dust, mocha and blueberry syrup flavors align.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2018 Been Away Too Long Denner Vineyard Mourvèdre (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
designation: Been Away Too Long Denner Vineyard
review: This bottling is extremely complex in both flavors and texture. Very dark in the glass, it shows aromas of dense black plum, violet, dried meat and black olive. The palate's cola and sarsaparilla spice lead into stewed plum, teriyaki and grilled meats flavors. The tannins are firm for cellaring but also open to immediate enjoyment.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2018 The Other One Glenrose Vineyard Mourvèdre (Adelaida District)
review: Smashed boysenberry, baking-spice and cracked peppercorn aromas converge for a vibrant nose on this single-vineyard expression. There is plenty of pepper spice on the palate, along with black raspberry, lavender and a slightly nutty tone of cashew brittle.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2018 Songs of Its Own Glenrose Vineyard Red (Adelaida District)
designation: Songs of Its Own Glenrose Vineyard
review: Dark raspberry and boysenberry-jam aromas meet with a strong hint of crushed gravel and dried herb on the nose of this blend of 60% Grenache and 40% Mourvèdre. Roasted berries and smoked meats make for a cohesive flavor profile on the palate.

name: Pacific Redwood 2018 Organic Merlot (North Coast)
review: Lots of fresh fruit flavors—raspberry and strawberry—are nicely accented by baking spices in this full-bodied, uncomplicated wine that contains no added sulfites.

name: Pacific Redwood 2018 Organic Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: An appetizing grip and lively mouthfeel make this organic wine a winner, supported by good, uncomplicated berry and red-cherry flavors and great balance from mild tannins and good fruit acidity.

name: Pacific Redwood 2018 Organic Cabernet Sauvignon (North Coast)
review: This is a firm, tannic and flavorful wine, showing again that dense, red varietals tend to hold up better without added sulfites than others. Earthy, spicy, meaty aromas lead to ample black-cherry and cranberry flavors, while the tight tannins leave an astringent finish.

name: OZV 2018 Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: This smooth, full-bodied and very ripe-tasting wine seems infused with clove and cedar flavors that rob attention from the blackberry notes.

name: Owen Roe 2018 Sinister Hand Red (Yakima Valley)
review: Grenache makes up 55% of this wine, with the rest Syrah (19%), Mourvèdre (17%) and Cinsault. The aromas rise out of the glass, with notes of raspberry, flower, white pepper and orange slice. Light, elegant red fruit flavors follow, almost tasting like a Pinot Noir. It shows an appealing sense of balance.

name: Osmote 2018 Cabernet Franc (Seneca Lake)
review: Lifted aromas of black pepper and violet ride over a raspberry core on the nose. The palate is silken and smooth in feel, showing a pleasing cherry juice tone that's lifted by integrated acidity and a delicate frame of tannins. It's on the lighter side of things, but wholly delicious.

name: Original House Wine 2018 Steak House Cabernet Sauvignon (America)
review: The aromas bring notes of spice, green pepper, red apple and sugared plum. Sweet, sugary plum flavors follow. It has broad appeal.

name: Original House Wine 2018 Mountain Merlot (America)
review: Black currant, sugared plum and whole green pepper aromas lead to off-dry drinking dark plum flavors. The sugar is heavy-handed, but the fruit flavors still manage to bring appeal.

name: Original House Wine 2018 Dark Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Effusive aromas of fresh black currant, whole green pepper, dark plum and gun smoke are followed by medium-bodied, off-dry drinking blue and black fruit flavors. It's sure to win a lot of fans, though it's quite sweet.

name: Original House Wine 2018 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit-forward, appealing aromas of dark plum, herbs and spice lead to sweet, generous plum and blue fruit flavors backed by lightly grainy tannins. It's a sure-fire crowd-pleaser.

name: Oregon Solidarity 2018 Pinot Noir (Rogue Valley)
review: This is the third and final release of the Oregon Solidarity wines, created to help southern Oregon growers whose contracts were cancelled by the California buyer during the 2018 harvest, ostensibly due to smoke damage. Like the Chardonnay and Rosé of Pinot Noir that preceded it, the wine proves that there was nothing wrong with the grapes. Black cherry and cola flavors open this young and approachable wine, with soft tannins and a touch of lemon tea. It's a food-friendly, solid effort, and all proceeds return to the growers.

name: Opolo 2018 Reserve Collection Zinfandel (Adelaida District)
review: Earthy aromas of crushed slate and wet wood meet with slightly charred beef and black-cherry cream on the nose of this bottling. There is a subtlety to the palate, where rounded flavors of baked cherry, strawberry and black plum converge with cardamom and nutmeg.

name: Opolo 2018 Reserve Collection Zinfandel (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Baked black cherry, clove and light cocoa aromas show on the nose of this reserve bottling. Rich black-cherry and caramel flavors are opulent, if a tad boozy, on the palate, where plenty of clove spice carries into the finish.

name: Opolo 2018 Forte Reserve Collection Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Deep, dark and gooey aromas of cassis and kirsch meet with fudge and caramel on the thick nose of this bottling. There's a lot of smoke on the palate, where sandy tannins wrap around ripe black-plum flavors in a slightly boozy show of power.

name: Opolo 2018 Mountain Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Muddled blueberry, clove and nutmeg aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is thick and heavy with flavors of blackberry paste, backed up by loads of baking spice and oak.

name: Opolo 2018 Summit Creek Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: With intense blackberry, chipped cedar, light vanilla, crushed rocks and ample oak on the nose, this bottling is a full package for a fair price. The full-throttle palate packs blackberry jam and toast flavors alongside lively enough acidity.

name: Opolo 2018 Summit Creek Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of cedar and leather recall a rustic cabin on the nose of this bottling, which also offers a dried cherry core. The cedar continues to the sip, where roasted red-fruit flavors meet with a touch of pine needle and a slightly resinous texture.

name: Opaque 2018 Tempranillo (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of blackberry sorbet, light cola, a touch of oak and a pinch of herbs all show on the nose of this bottling. Scorched blackberry and blueberry flavors cling to cement-like tannins on the sip, that need a touch of time to smooth out. Drink 2023–2033.

name: OneHope 2018 Vintner Collection Red (California)
review: Generous red- and black-cherry flavors give this full-bodied wine plenty to like, while the snappy, lightly tannic texture is appetizing.

name: Omen 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Ample fruit flavors and a good, firm texture give this medium-bodied wine the right varietal framework. It is appetizing and lip-smacking to sip, and offers ripe blueberry and black-cherry flavors.

name: Omen 2018 Rorick Heritage Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Calaveras County)
review: One of the best Calaveras wines in memory, this full-bodied Cab has great concentration and a firm but creamy texture. It offers plenty of immediate appeal and also bodes well for continued aging. Blueberry, vanilla and dark chocolate dominate the flavors while fine-grained tannins shape the mouthfeel. Best through 2026.

name: Omen 2018 Red (Sierra Foothills)
review: Salty, meaty flavors intertwine with ripe blackberry and clove in this full-bodied, richly textured wine. Good fruit concentration floods the taste buds while appropriately firm tannins add grip to the mouthfeel.

name: Olema 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: This is a well-priced wine made from multiple sites in the region. Immensely likable, it offers approachable layers of rhubarb, strawberry and orange, the texture soft and mild in dried herb and earthiness.

name: Olelo 2018 Merlot (Paso Robles)
review: Dried plum-paste, oregano, light pepper and gamy leather aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate offers dried oregano and marjoram atop the red-plum and berry flavors.

name: Oleandri 2018 Lindsay's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This is an ethereally light style of Pinot Noir, yet is extremely complex and engaging on all fronts. Fresh aromas of strawberry, wild mint and tarragon lead into a snappy palate of pomegranate and hibiscus, with a savory white-pepper spice.

name: Old Soul 2018 Pinot Noir (Lodi)
review: Spicy in aroma and flavor and medium bodied, this wine exudes toasted wood, cinnamon and clove notes on a smooth, lightly tannic texture.

name: Odonata 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Baked cranberry, salted plum, savory prosciutto and a hint of rose petals carry through the nose of this county-wide bottling. Earthy and meaty flavors show on the palate, combining black raspberry and dark cranberry with savory herbs and spices.

name: Odonata 2018 Falcon Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Ripe berries and cherries meet with hints of stone, turned earth and a bit of hibiscus on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. There is a tightness to the palate, where earthy, dried herb flavors are sprinkled across the ripe cranberry and potpourri core.

name: Odonata 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Carmel Valley)
review: Light berry, peppercorn and pinches of oregano and thyme show on the nose of this bottling. The palate hits with a ripe rush of baked berry and then emerges into a more herb-inflected expression, recalling complex Cabs of old Carmel.

name: Odonata 2018 Circle B Vineyard Carignan (Paso Robles)
review: Freshly plucked huckleberry, wet stone, crushed slate and dried rose-petal aromas make for an earthy nose on this bottling. There is a strong snap to the sip, where boysenberry and strawberry flavors meet with mint tarragon elements, wrapped in a taut structure.

name: Ocnautic 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey County)
review: Dark-purple flower-petal aromas meet with blackberry and slate on the nose of his bottling. The palate is dark, with violet, lavender and boysenberry-syrup flavors, all cut by wet gravel minerality and a bright acidity.

name: Ocnautic 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Dark berry, wet earth and light wood aromas show on the shy nose of this bottling. Ripe boysenberry and black-raspberry flavors show on the plate, where there is a light touch of sandalwood.

name: Oceano 2018 Spanish Springs Vineyard Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: From a very cool vineyard, this bottling offers a strong herbal kick to the nose, where singed oregano, thyme and marjoram aromas meet with a cran-raspberry fruit base. That singed herb tone carries to the sip as well, where bay leaf decorates the pomegranate core.

name: Obsession 2018 Red (California)
review: Ripe jammy tempting fruit flavors highlight this full-bodied wine that's broad in mouthfeel and relaxed in texture due to low tannins and soft acidity.

name: Oblivion 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Smoky, oak-driven aromas wrap around the extracted roasted blackberry core of this bottling. Decent tannins frame the sip, where blackberry flavors add a little tang and the wood spice adds warmth.

name: Oberon 2018 Merlot (Napa Valley)
review: With the addition of nearly 11% Syrah and just under 2% Zinfandel, this is a hearty red with both fruit and spice on the nose. Supple, velvety texture is highlighted in bold flavors of cassis, raspberry and plum, finishing with ease.

name: Oakstone 2018 Reserve Estate Piedras Grandes Vineyard Petite Sirah (Fair Play)
review: This ripe-tasting full-bodied wine is big but friendly, lifting jammy blackberry flavors with firm, fine-grained tannins and layering attractive baking-spice notes throughout.

name: Oakstone 2018 Estate De Cascabel Reserve Mourvèdre (Fair Play)
review: This full-bodied wine shows very good clarity and focus in its flavors and excellent balance that livens up the texture. Red- and black-cherry notes blend with subtle baking-spice accents that linger on the finish.

name: Oakstone 2018 Naylor Vineyard Barbera (Fair Play)
review: This full-bodied and well-balanced wine from a little-known and very consistent producer is smooth as velvet and well-saturated with ripe plum, red beet and a hint of chocolate.

name: Oakstone 2018 Obscurity Cellars Estate Paso Vista Petit Verdot (Fair Play)
review: This full-bodied wine offers concentrated black-fruit flavors, a smooth and moderately tannic texture and excellent concentration, which helps create a lingering finish.

name: Oak Grove 2018 Family Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This is a medium-bodied, popular-style wine that offers ample fresh cherry and plum flavors, a coating of sweet baking spices and a smooth, lightly tannic texture.

name: Oak Grove 2018 Family Reserve Pinot Noir (California)
review: This well-made, straightforward wine offers black cherry flavor and a smooth texture. It's medium in body, light in tannin and easy to sip.

name: Oak Grove 2018 Family Reserve Merlot (California)
review: Smooth and fruity, this medium-bodied wine is uncomplicated in flavor, offering good raspberry and black-cherry notes, and rather soft in texture.

name: Oak Grove 2018 Petite Sirah (California)
review: Heavy aromas and flavors of sawn oak and wood char dominate this medium-bodied and tannic wine. It's fun and rowdy to drink now, enjoying the spiciness and gripping texture.

name: Oak Grove 2018 Red (California)
review: Lightly smoky, beefy aromas and flavors fill this medium- to full-bodied wine with a savory mixture, as mild tannins and black-cherry notes complete the picture.

name: Oak Farm 2018 Estate Grown Sangiovese (Lodi)
review: Deeply colored, very ripe and concentrated, this full-bodied wine is big but elegant. Extra-rich red-cherry and raspberry flavors feel layered, while silky tannins firm up the texture just enough.

name: Oak Farm 2018 Estate Grown Malbec (Lodi)
review: Superdark and flavorful, this densely concentrated wine manages to be both generous and firmly structured at the same time. It exudes graphite and cedar aromas, and boasts plenty of velvety, mouthcoating tannins to bolster a wealth of dark-chocolate, warm blueberry and blackberry flavors.

name: Oak Farm 2018 Tievoli Red (Lodi)
review: Attractive oak spices dress up this ripe and robust wine with vanilla and cedar, while full body and light tannins add structure and texture. Attractive blackberry flavors develop on the palate.

name: NSO By Dusty Nabor 2018 NSO Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Clean lines of strawberry, raspberry and cherry meet with light oregano, marjoram, pepper and light smoke on the nose of this bottling. The palate is dusted with fennel pollen that gives depth to the ripe cherry-paste lines that approach sweetness through the sip.

name: Novelty Hill 2018 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of blueberry pie, tangerine peel and sweet barrel spices are followed by a palate that mixes equal parts fruit and barrel, with plentiful nougat notes on the finish. The oak plays a leading role in the show.

name: Noble Vines 2018 337 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Polished and well balanced, this flavorful and moderately tannic wine fits the bill nicely for a well-priced Cabernet. It has a full body, a concentrated black cherry flavor and subtle hints of chocolate and vanilla.

name: Noble Vines 2018 181 Merlot (Lodi)
review: This is a full-bodied and concentrated wine. Deep, potent black-fruit flavors blend seamlessly with notes of dark chocolate, cinnamon and vanilla.

name: Noble Vines 2018 667 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: A solid option for fairly priced Pinot Noir, this clonal selection starts with aromas of chaparral spice, wet earth and a hint of cranberry. There's a strong sense of bay leaf and pine on the palate, along with boysenberry and bold acidity that washes into the vanilla-kissed finish.

name: Noble Vines 2018 Marquis Red (California)
review: Strong cedar, wood-smoke and charred oak flavors take over this full-bodied wine, masking the fruit flavors that lie beneath. Best for oak lovers.

name: Noble Tree 2018 Sons and Daughters Ranch Grenache (Chalk Hill-Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy and broad on the palate in strawberry jam, this wine has velvety texture and soft tannins. The moderate structure gives in to a fleshy, opulent style with a dusting of baking spice on the finish.

name: Niner 2018 Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Extremely fresh aromas of Bing cherry sorbet, red currant syrup, red flowers and underlying licorice make for an intensely fruity and delicious nose. The palate tightens up around dried cherry and hibiscus flavors, with luxurious amounts of mace, sumac and chai spice leading into a lingering finish of lavender and mint.

name: Neyers 2018 Vista Notre Zinfandel (California)
review: Fresh, fruity and almost-sweet, this easy-to-drink wine blends light oak spices with rich plum and strawberry flavors, kicking in hints of wood smoke and pepper for complexity.

name: Neyers 2018 Sage Canyon Red (California)
