review: This full-bodied Carignan-based wine wraps potent fruit and dark-spice flavors in a firm texture, framed by bright acidity and moderate tannins. Its good structure and concentration will pair well with many rich proteins. Best through 2025.

name: Navarro 2018 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Beautiful cherry aromas and deep, pure black-cherry flavors are lifted by fresh acidity in this finely balanced wine. It has a snappy, appetizing texture strengthened by moderate tannins and should hold nicely through 2024.

name: Navarro 2018 Methode a l'Ancienne Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Deep in flavor and rich in texture, this medium- to full-bodied wine overflows with black cherry, black tea and cedar flavors that mingle nicely, creating complexity and extending into a long finish. Best through 2026.

name: Napa Valley Quilt 2018 The Fabric of the Land Red (Napa Valley)
review: Softly structured, this crowd-pleaser combines Petite Sirah, Merlot, Zinfandel and Petite Verdot. Herbal and dotted in black pepper, the jammy fruit is defined in raspberry and blackberry.

name: Municipal Winemakers 2018 Nault Rieslin Red (Santa Barbara County)
review: Winemaker David Potter tips his toe into the avant-garde, tradition-bucking natty wine scene with this blend of Cinsault and Riesling. It's bizarre and first but quickly grows pleasurable. Rusty and cloudy in the glass, the wine's aromas of tart red-currant punch lead into an energetic and spicy palate of cranberry, dewy sage and rainbow peppercorns.

name: Municipal Winemakers 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: This earthy and herby style of Pinot Noir typically comes at much higher price points. Aromas of dill, coriander, cracker pepper and bay leaf meet with brisk red currant on the nose. The herbaceousness explodes on the palate, with pine needle and thyme decorating the taut cranberry core.

name: Muirwood 2018 Merlot (Arroyo Seco)
review: This brand is consistently producing affordable wines that punch above their weight class. This wine begins with aromas of ash, chalk, red berry and light pomegranate. These tones continue to the palate, with added streaks of stone, red currant and chalk that hold firm into the finish.

name: Muirwood 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Arroyo Seco)
review: Well-packaged aromas of blackberry, dark cocoa and rich violet make for a polished nose in this bottling. There is an energetic zing of acidity on the sip, where flavors of black plum and dried purple flower ride atop a chalky texture.

name: Muirwood 2018 Vaquero Block Cabernet Sauvignon (Arroyo Seco)
review: Aromas of hickory smoke and black plum juice show on the nose of this block-designated wine. The smoke is persistent on the palate as well, which is light in body and also offers more plum and berry juice flavors.

name: Muirwood 2018 Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)
review: Strong aromas of iron and potting soil meet with reserved, slightly sour fruit and a hint of oak on the savory nose of this bottling. The wine lands very dry on the palate, where cranberry and crushed rock flavors lead into a lingering cherry finish.

name: Mountain Tides 2018 Petite Sirah (Contra Costa County)
review: Beefy, salt-and-pepper aromas precede good cherry and black-plum flavors in this medium-bodied, moderately tannic wine. It's a good bit lighter and leaner than many Petite Sirahs and thus easier to enjoy young.

name: Mountain Tides 2018 Petite Sirah (California)
review: This wine is layered and drinkable in an appealing way, offering lots of ripe black fruits on a structure of firm tannins but not being so massive as to need long aging. Enjoy now through 2025.

name: Mountain Tides 2018 Palmero Family Vineyard Petite Sirah (Borden Ranch)
review: Made with 50% whole clusters in the fermentor, this wine delivers a big shot of bold blackberry, blueberry and wood-char flavors backed by very firm tannins. It is deep, concentrated and built for the long haul. Best after 2024.

name: Mount Veeder 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)
review: Tasting of blueberry coffee cake, this is a soft, supple and approachable red wine, firm in tannin and robust in dusty rock and mocha. It delivers bold, robust flavor without fuss.

name: Mount Morgan 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Aromas of cinnamon, mint and clove meet extra deep blackberry and dark-chocolate flavors in this stylized, oaky and firmly tannic wine. The texture is rich and grippy, while baking spices and wood smoke linger on the finish.

name: Morgan 2018 Tempranillo (Arroyo Seco)
review: This is an interesting and refreshing take on a grape that's typically thick and leathery. Aromas of barbecued strawberries, crushed slate, gravel and dried rose petals lead into a lightly bodied palate, where light strawberry and pomegranate flavors cling to a taut texture.

name: Morgan 2018 Cotes du Crow's Red (Monterey)
review: This blend of 66% Grenache, 31% Syrah and 3% Tempranillo is slightly effervescent in the glass at this young age, delivering aromas of red currant, hibiscus and crushed gravel. Cranberry and cherry meet with stony acidity on the zesty and fresh palate.

name: Morgan 2018 G 17 Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Lively aromas of black raspberry jam on French toast, dark hibiscus, tar and lavender show on the cool-climate nose of this bottling, which includes 3% each of Grenache and Tempranillo. The palate is vibrant with flavors of pepper, lavender and lilac, set against the richer black-raspberry and black-plum backdrop.

name: Montoya 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Candied raspberry, delicate flower petals and a hint of forest moss show on the nose of this bottling. Clean lines of cola, light cranberry, cherry and a wet clay minerality arise on the simple and light palate.

name: Montinore 2018 Red Cap Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: The light and pretty red fruits—principally cranberry and raspberry—are faintly earthy. They run hard up against drying tannins, but the finish resolves pleasantly with highlights of seashells and minerals.

name: Montagne Russe 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick and intense with a savory underbelly of dried herb, black tea and forest floor, this big wine offers an array of fruity orange peel, rhubarb and strawberry. The tannins are structured and strong.

name: Montagne Russe 2018 Terra de Promissio Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From the well-farmed site outside of Petaluma, this wine is robust in toasted oak and baked, jammy black fruit. Succulent, fleshy and ripe, it has structure and complexity, with lasting tannin weight on the palate.

name: Montagne Russe 2018 Roberts Road Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From a Sangiacomo-farmed site in the coolness of the Petaluma Gap, this wine opens in earthy rose and forest floor. Stemmy, it unfurls layers of dark cherry and berry, lengthy with a robust, rounded texture and body.

name: Mon Frere 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This wine is medium bodied, moderately flavorful and has no rough edges. While straightforward in its berry-cherry flavors, it is well balanced and just lightly tannic.

name: Mon Frere 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This wine is soft and gentle, melting in the mouth and offering mild cherry-candy and plum flavors on a low-acid texture.

name: Mirassou 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This smooth, gentle medium-bodied wine offers a rich, soft texture and ripe cherry flavors accented by a touch of cinnamon.

name: Millbrook 2018 Cabernet Franc (New York)
review: A bright note of crushed raspberry and cranberry carries accents of pink peppercorn and herbes de Provence. The palate is light and racy, showing streamlined flavors of tart strawberry and hibiscus wrapped in a green, herbal undertone. The tannins are delicate, but a bit raspy, giving grip to an otherwise fleeing palate.

name: Millbrook 2018 Pinot Noir (New York)
review: A heady nose of pink grapefruit, hibiscus and cherry kicks off this delightfully lifted Pinot. The palate is sultry and smooth, displaying ripples of coiled red fruit, citrus and moss. The tannins are polished yet firm, with bright acidity lifting it all up. A cherry bitters note marks the close, giving it a real sense of grip and length.

name: Milbrandt 2018 Family Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are effusive, with notes of gun smoke, green pepper and plum, not seeming to have entirely settled in yet. Lighter-styled flavors follow. The oak is pleasantly pulled back.

name: Midnight 2018 Full Moon Red (Paso Robles)
review: Deep and dark aromas of cherry, boysenberry, cola and root beer show on the nose of this affordably priced blend. The palate is fruity, with cherry, pomegranate, hibiscus and carnation flavors, proving fresh and easy to enjoy.

name: Michael Sullberg 2018 Reserve Lot 91 Merlot (California)
review: This rustic, medium-bodied wine starts off with a light matchstick aroma and then lays on metallic, dried-meat and berrylike flavors that are not bad but pretty unusual.

name: Michael Sullberg 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This soft and easygoing wine offers cinnamon and butter aromas, jammy red-fruit flavors and very low tannins on a medium body.

name: Michael Pozzan 2018 Giapoza Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This is a big yet nicely restrained wine, backing its flavors of black cherry and dark chocolate with moderate tannins. Its appetizing texture slowly releases layers of depth and flavor with time in the glass. Best through 2026.

name: Michael David 2018 Sixth Sense Syrah (Lodi)
review: Delicious and mouthfilling, this full-bodied wine is drenched in jammy blackberry and blueberry flavors on a rather soft and easy texture. The saturated fruitiness stays on the palate through a lingering finish.

name: Michael David 2018 Freakshow Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: Full in feel and firmly structured, this wine is a kind of gentle giant. It offers ripe but not overt flavors of blackberry and blueberry, with a well-polished texture despite the moderate tannins.

name: Michael David 2018 Ancient Vines Bechthold Vineyard Cinsault (Lodi)
review: A wonderful floral aroma and abundant fruit and spice flavors make this smooth and polished wine almost irresistible. It has a charming freshness and fruitiness backed by good acidity and layers of flavor.

name: Michael David 2018 Lodi Red (Lodi)
review: Quite concentrated but also fresh and lively, this deeply colored and moderately tannic wine oozes blackberry-jam and blueberry flavors. It's exuberant and hard to resist.

name: Meyer Family Cellars 2018 Monument Tree Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: An almost sweet maraschino-cherry aroma leads to subtle earthy, spicy, mulled fruit flavors in this individualistic and light-bodied wine. It's very smooth and deep on the palate, lightly tannic, and shows a slightly wild mushroomy aspect in the flavors that keeps it interesting.

name: Metz Road 2018 Riverview Vineyard Pinot Noir (Monterey)
review: Subtle at first on the nose, this bottling offers aromas of strawberry, cranberry, cherry paste and a hint of rust. The palate is tightly woven and clean, with flavors of cranberry and cracked pepper.

name: Mettler Family Vineyards 2018 Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)
review: This full-bodied, rich and saturated wine offers abundant black cherry, black olive and toasted oak flavors on a firm texture of moderate, fine-grained tannins. It brings a lot of sensory pleasure from its deep, dark color to the velvety mouthfeel and baking spice accents that linger on the finish.

name: Mettler Family Vineyards 2018 Epicenter Old Vine Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: This spicy, generous wine does a nice job of blending the baking spice and maple notes of new barrels with jazzy raspberry and strawberry flavors. Full bodied and just moderately tannic, it has a big personality.

name: Mettler Family Vineyards 2018 Estate Grown Petite Sirah (Lodi)
review: Deep, layered and saturated, this inky-colored wine is massively concentrated and tannic, but still comes off velvety and generous. Woodsmoke, blueberry and dark chocolate are accented by touches of vanilla and cassis. Best from 2023–2038.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Warren's Hill Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Sharp and tangy in crisp citrus, rhubarb and pomegranate, this lively, complex and structured wine shows secondary characteristics of forest floor, tea and dried herb. Moderately structured and ripe, it offers richness within a context of balance and grace.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Supple in tannin and earthy in black tea, this appellation wine is delicious, with depth and freshness. Lively acidity boosts a core of dark cherry, pomegranate and cherry—the fruit well contrasted in nutmeg and clove.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Olivet Lane Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy with brawny black fruit, this wine offers soft, supple tannins that support fleshy ongoing richness on the midpalate. Varietally satisfying and expressive in velvety texture, it finishes in baking spice and a depth of earth.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Georganne Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Textured, tangy and tart in wild-berry flavor, this wine is powerfully concentrated, full bodied and expansive, the tannins supple and supportive of the rich, dense fruit. Secondary characteristics of violet, rose and earthy black tea give it additional intrigue and beauty.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Coopersmith Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Creamy with high-toned, underlying acidity, this wine is well structured and weighty. Rich layers of strawberry and cranberry highlight a core of concentrated tannin and oak, with underlying complements of baking spice and cola.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Meredith Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is exuberantly layered in bright tones of pomegranate and strawberry, while textured with integrated tannins and oak. Complementary elements of forest floor, black tea and clove play well with the fruitiness and enduring lushness on the palate.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Flax Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This vineyard-designate is tart and rich in crunchy red fruit, the body, which is full and fleshy on the palate, is rendered in an opulent style. Toasted oak and supple tannin add to the weight, breadth and complexity of the wine.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Klopp Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Forest, clove, oak and thick tannin make for a hearty entry into this perennially impressive vineyard-designate—one of the producer's earliest partnerships. Weighty and powerful, it offers forest floor, tea and wild strawberry, the acidity lasting and lively.

name: Merry Edwards 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Jammy plum, strawberry and dark cherry ooze from the core of this beautifully coastal wine, seasoned in dried herb, clove and black tea. Expressive and varietal, with a thick tannin structure, it has room to open further and unwind, its oak well integrated and its tannins structured.

name: Member's Mark 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)
review: A smooth, approachable wine that is soft and simple, it offers light cinnamon spice atop dense flavors of cherry, strawberry and plum.

name: Melville 2018 Sandy's Block Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tangy aromas of raspberry and rose petal meet with dried sage and tarragon on the nose of this block-designated bottling. Brisk raspberry flavors are lifted by bay leaf and eucalyptus sap on the zesty palate.

name: Melville 2018 Indigene Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Brisk black raspberry, crumpled red flowers and hint of grape seed show on the nose of this bottling. There are plenty of herbs on the palate, from thyme to coriander seed and green peppercorn, set against a brisk raspberry background.

name: Melville 2018 Donna's Block Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Cracked black pepper, black currant and dark violet make for a cool-climate expression with tons of floral smoothness. There is heft to the sip, where black pepper, dried herbs and black fruits are sliced by a zesty acidity.

name: Melville 2018 Block M Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Light in the glass, this herb-forward bottling begins with aromas of bay leaf, green peppercorn, cracked pepper and wet forest, set against the brisk raspberry core. The palate snaps with underripe raspberry and bramble, veering into the herbal tea tones throughout the sip for a refreshing and complex experience.

name: Melville 2018 Anna's Block Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Candied Bing cherry aromas meet with savory stemmy aromas of damp underbrush, forest floor and cannabis-like funk. The palate is complex with blistered cherry tomato, slivered strawberry, minty tarragon and bay leaf flavors.

name: Melville 2018 Estate Syrah (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Extreme amounts of cracked black pepper spice up the crumbled lilac, dark-black-plum and snappy red-currant aromas on the dynamic nose of this cool-climate bottling. There's a wild zestiness to the palate, where the peppery spice plays well with black-raspberry, lavender and rosemary flavors.

name: Melodramatic 2018 Red (California)
review: A bit of sweetness rounds the edges of this full-bodied, rich and jammy wine. It is saturated with blackberry and plum notes and has a smooth texture.

name: MCV 2018 Red (Central Coast)
review: Dark in color and expression, yet still mineral-driven and fresh, this bottling starts with aromas of crisp blackberry, black plum and violet against a crushed granite streak. The palate is almost ashy in its stony qualities, which frame the black-plum and white-pepper spice quite well.

name: MCV 2018 Petite Sirah (Paso Robles)
review: Hearty aromas of violets and acai are powerful but not overblown on the nose of this bottling. It's a big, brawny wine on the palate, with cassis and more acai at the core, yet sprinkled with lavender and white pepper, proving complex while rich.

name: MCV 2018 Grenache (Paso Robles)
review: Deep and dark aromas of cherry, sweet smoke and roasted meats show on the robust nose of this bottling. Powerful flavors of boysenberry, roasted cherry and smoked meat show on the sip, where a rocky texture holds the ripeness together.

name: McPrice Myers 2018 High on the Hog Red (Paso Robles)
review: Dark baked cherry, cassis, violet and woody earth aromas show on the nose of this affordably priced red blend. There's a hearty soil component on the front end of the palate, where oregano and marjoram meet with dark-fruit, soy and char flavors.

name: McPrice Myers 2018 Right Hand Man Syrah (Central Coast)
review: It's hard to find better value than what McPrice Myers is doing with his “Hard Working Wines.” This bottling offers roasted coffee, toasted wood and blistered black plum on the savory, engaging and comforting nose. The palate is also toasty, and focused on grilled blackberry flavors.

name: McPrice Myers 2018 Beautiful Earth Red (Paso Robles)
review: This blend of 64% Syrah, 12% Cabernet Sauvignon, 12% Grenache, 6% Mourvèdre, 4% Petite Sirah and 2% Clairette Blanche is still young and tight on the nose, but it allows aromas of black currant, cola and seared wood to emerge. The palate combines black raspberry and lavender onto a taut frame of tannins that release their grip with time.

name: McPrice Myers 2018 Bull by the Horns Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Blackberry, lilac, cinnamon and clove aromas converge on the complex nose of this bottling. The palate ranges from hibiscus and cherry to strawberry and rose petals, with plenty of layers to keep the taste buds entertained.

name: McPrice Myers 2018 Pound for Pound Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Deep and dark aromas of black cherry and turned loam make for a focused nose on this bottling. Full flavors of dark red fruit meet with oak smoke on the palate, making for a clean and big expression.

name: McManis 2018 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Zinfandel (Lodi)
review: This wine is ripe and juicy, offering concentrated blackberry and blueberry aromas, rich fruit flavors and a hint of spicy oak. It's plush in texture and pretty soft in tannin. It's interesting that 15% of Teroldego and 8% of Petite Sirah were blended in.

name: McManis 2018 Jamie Lynn Vineyard Estate Grown Barbera (California)
review: This oaky wine pits strong smoky aromas and flavors against rather tart raspberry flavors for a tense balance and lean mouthfeel.

name: McManis 2018 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Petite Sirah (Lodi)
review: This potent, black-colored wine sends up cocoa and cola aromas, floods the palate with intense blackberry and smoke flavors and wraps them in a warm blanket of soft tannins. It's a lip-smacking, tooth-staining wine that's been tamed just enough to enjoy.

name: McManis 2018 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Merlot (Lodi)
review: Smoke and sage aromas meld with cherry and wood-smoke flavors to make a savory combination in this medium- to full-bodied wine. It shows good balance and light tannins, making it a great choice for drinking with protein-rich foods.

name: McManis 2018 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)
review: This full-bodied wine is tasty and stylistic, accenting its deep, jammy blueberry and black-cherry flavors with a smoky, menthol note. Firm tannins give it plenty of grip on the palate.

name: McKahn 2018 Mourvèdre (Livermore Valley)
review: This substantial, fruit-packed and full-bodied wine overflows with delicious dark-berry and dark-chocolate flavors while the rich, clinging texture is backed by light tannins. With time in the glass, intricate, complex notes of beef, woodsmoke and mushroom appear on the palate and linger on the finish.

name: McClean 2018 One Last Syrah (Paso Robles)
review: There's a lot going on in this bottling, starting with aromas of tart boysenberry syrup, crushed purple flowers and a hint of bubblegum. The palate is dense, with rich blackberry flavors, almost peppery tannins and a pinpoint acidity, proving fruity and candied yet also refreshing.

name: McClean 2018 Simone Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: This is a powerful and rich wine, especially for the price point. Opulent aromas of black currant and blackberry are wrapped in hickory smoke, iodine and rust on the nose. The palate packs blueberry jam, caramel and vanilla into a hearty experience.

name: McClean 2018 Clemence Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)
review: Ripe strawberry sorbet topped with spearmint and licorice spice shows on the nose of this bottling, which is quite light in the glass for the variety. Loads of wild mint and ripe raspberry flavors show on the palate.

name: Mauritson 2018 Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)
review: This exuberant, complex red is made from a mix of estate vineyards as well as grower sites, like Treborce and Montecillo. While it is concentrated in black cherry, its soul leans more savory and herbal to display an understated elegance.

name: Matthew Fritz 2018 Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: This is a snappy, relatively light-bodied wine at a good price, showing tasty raspberry and red-cherry flavors and fresh acidity to keep it lively.

name: Matchbook 2018 Estate Bottled Petite Sirah (Dunnigan Hills)
review: This wine is richly flavored and boldly tannic, literally coating the mouth. It is deep and dark in color, packed with blackberry jam and strawberry flavors, with a mouthfeel that is velvety in texture. Best through 2028.

name: Matchbook 2018 Black's Station Malbec (Yolo County)
review: This deeply colored, quietly potent wine broods in tones of dark chocolate, blueberry jam and baking spices wrapped in layers of thick, fine-grained tannins. Everything is held together tightly, yet softly.

name: Matchbook 2018 Estate Bottled Red Gravel Cabernet Sauvignon (Dunnigan Hills)
review: This is a brawny, full-bodied and firmly tannic wine that's nevertheless seductive, complex and layered. A dark color and impressions of tobacco, black currant and sage make it delicious to sip. Best through 2027.

name: Masút 2018 Big Barrel Pinot Noir (Eagle Peak)
review: This seductive and full-bodied wine is scented with red cherry and warm raspberry, then pours rich black cherry and generous baking spices onto the palate. The texture is supersmooth and mouthfilling and the finish lingers nicely. Winemakers Jacob and Benjamin Fetzer aged it in 100% new French oak puncheons which hold the equivalent of two regular barrels.

name: Masút 2018 Estate Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eagle Peak)
review: Friendly and mouthfilling, this full-bodied wine brims with red and black cherry, accented by light vanilla, coconut and cedar from oak aging. It's an engaging, broad and delicious wine.

name: Martin Woods 2018 Hyland Vineyard Pinot Noir (McMinnville)
review: This aromatic young wine offers sweet scents of cherries, with tart cherry fruit to follow. One-quarter was whole clusters, from 30-year-old vines. It needs aggressive aeration to open up, and as with the other 2018 Pinots from this winery, the tannins are quite astringent with an herbal edge.

name: Martin Woods 2018 Jessie James Vineyard Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: This is an austere, somewhat challenging, palate-drying wine, fermented with 20% whole clusters and aged in 8% new oak. The Pommard and Wädenswil vines were planted in 1996. Light, tart raspberry fruit is cloaked in fresh herbs and plenty of acid before resolving in tea-flavored tannins.

name: Martin Woods 2018 Bednarik Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Lightly ripened fruit flavors of rhubarb and dried apples, along with miscellaneous fruit-leather notes, lead you through this herbal, astringent wine. From a 1988 Pommard planting, one-third of the ferment included whole clusters, and 8% was aged in new oak.

name: Martin Woods 2018 Gamay Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Fermented partially by carbonic (whole berry) maceration, this spicy wine is loaded with red fruits and marked with sharp acidity. It's full bodied and fresh, and should be consumed over the next couple of years. It finishes with a spot of lemon tea as it fades.

name: Martin Ray 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Thick grainy red fruit fits around a tight core of tannin and medium-bodied weight in this approachable red, varietally earthy in forest and finished off with a bite of oak.

name: Marshall Davis 2018 Marshall Davis Vineyard Cask Series Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: This interesting wine was vinified in a single large oak tank. It's 100% Pommard clone, with spicy and tart cranberry and raspberry fruit prominent. The acidity brings a touch of orange peel. It should be in a prime drinking window by the mid-2020s.

name: Marshall Davis 2018 Township No. 3 Pinot Noir (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: This new cuvée blends fruit from three vineyards, including the estate. It is still pulling itself together, and there's some bite to the back, with hints of raw wood and green tannins. The cherry fruit is a good anchor, and with additional bottle age the score could go up.

name: Marshall Davis 2018 Ryan Davis Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Aromas of strawberries and pie cherries lead into comparable fruit flavors. It's a pretty wine, modest in scale, balanced and immediately appealing. It seems to thin out rather quickly through the finish, but extra aeration helps to add some stuffing.

name: Marnet 2018 Campbell Ranch Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Richly interwoven in red cherry and strawberry fruit, with well-integrated tannin and oak, the wine offers a cool-climate imprint of forest floor and black tea. Shocks of cardamom figure into the mix with ease, adding to a spicy, savory character that is incredibly appealing.

name: Marnet 2018 Alder Springs Pinot Noir (Mendocino County)
review: Excellent depth and bold, focused flavors seem to energize this well-balanced and medium- to full-bodied wine. Black-tea, black-cherry and mushroom notes give it an attractive complexity.

name: Maritana 2018 Jenkins Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine is 50% whole-cluster and aged in 90% new oak. From the great site farmed by Charlie Chenoweth, it is buzzy in crisp acidity, with savory layers of black tea, forest and clove. A richness of cherry-cordial flavor complements and contrasts.

name: Maritana 2018 Le Russe Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Donald Patz's blend of several sites and clones within the appellation, this hearty red is beautifully layered and complex. Wild strawberry, rhubarb, dried herb and black tea give it varietal distinction amidst an underlying tension of acidity. Thick tannin and oak weight contribute power.

name: Maritana 2018 Moonshine Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This wine has a beautiful opening nose of rose petals and cardamom spice. The fresh, lean palate has balanced weight and elegance, showing flavors of raspberry, strawberry and pomegranate that are richly woven within a foundation of structure and grace.

name: Margerum 2018 Uber Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Though this wine starts a little shy on the nose, it slowly reveals elegant blackberry and violet aromas with time. The palate takes those purple-fruit and flower flavors and amplifies the experience with espresso-bean touches.

name: Margerum 2018 Black Oak Vineyard Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: Aromas of tar, charred blueberries, rose petals and black-raspberry sorbet make for a very fresh entry to the nose of this bottling. Fruity flavors of black currant and blueberry are fresh on the palate, followed by spicier hints of white pepper.

name: Margerum 2018 Syrah (Santa Barbara County)
review: This county-wide blend is made in a powerful style, offering rich aromas of black currant, blackberry, bacon and maple sugar. The palate is quite satisfying in that a lush way, showing blueberry and violet flavors in a strong yet accessible and balance manner.

name: Margerum 2018 Margerum Estate Vineyard Syrah (Los Olivos District)
review: There's a spicy core of black pepper on the nose of this bottling along with taut fruit tones of wid cranberry and elderberry. The palate is loaded with acid and tightly woven in texture, delivering flavors of crumpled lavender, crisp black raspberry, Dr Pepper spice and an incredibly enlivening cracked pepper streak.

name: Margerum 2018 Grenache (Santa Barbara County)
review: Pomegranate, hibiscus and rose-petal aromas combine to create a fresh, bright and expression nose on this bottling. Those bright red-fruit flavors make for a punch-like expression on the sip, where an edgy texture meets with white-pepper spice and rides a powerful acidity that lingers long into the finish.

name: Mansfield-Dunne 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Rich aromas of black plum, blackberry, melted licorice and hearty oak spice show on the powerful nose of this appellation blend. The palate is packed with deliciously extracted fruit, offering deep, hearty and rugged flavors of boysenberry and garam masala spice.

name: Mansfield-Dunne 2018 Cortada Alta Vineyard Dijon Clones Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: This is a robust and hearty style of Pinot Noir, built for the cellar. Aromas of blackberry, violet and charred meat lead into flavors of boysenberry, black plum, tarragon, rose petal and licorice on the palate. The mouthfeel is heavy, yet balanced by strong tannins. Drink through 2028.

name: Mansfield-Dunne 2018 Cortada Alta Vineyard Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: The Santa Lucia Highlands is one of the best places to grow Syrah in California, despite being overshadowed by Pinot Noir. This bottling is thorough evidence, with suavely integrated aromas of black pepper, black currant, lavender and crushed black stones. Firm, sticky tannins frame the palate, where pepper, green olive and bay leaf flavors decorate the inky black-fruit core. Drink 2023–2043.

name: Mansfield-Dunne 2018 Cortada Alta Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Enticing aromas of baked boysenberry pie meet with sarsaparilla and cola on the rich nose of this bottling. The palate leads with sharp spice tones of aniseed and mace, leading into the full flavors of black plum and loamy earth.

name: Mansfield-Dunne 2018 Peterson Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Black raspberry and lavender aromas show prominently on the nose of this single-vineyard bottling. The floral tones hold firmly on the palate, where violet and lilac also arise, playing against a tightly wound flavor of açai and more purple fruits.

name: Majuscule 2018 Cabernet Franc (Mount Veeder)
review: Reduced and somewhat muted in its youth, this wine has yet to unwind, showing a disjointed element of herbaceousness against a sweetness of vanilla and cola.

name: Maison L'Envoyé 2018 Two Messengers Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Part of a global portfolio focused on Pinot Noir, this pulls fruit from a number of top vineyards scattered throughout the Willamette Valley. It's a mix of red and black fruits, framed with citrusy acidity and light barrel toast from aging in one-quarter new French oak. It's well crafted, and finishes with a whiff of truffle.

name: Maison L'Envoyé 2018 Straight Shooter Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is the best vintage to date for this affordable Pinot Noir, packed with juicy blackberry and black cherry fruit. There are highlights of black olive and polished tannins, and a firm, balanced, medium-bodied finish.

name: Maggy Hawk 2018 Jory Hills Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)
review: This Pinot-centric Jackson Family brand expands into Oregon with this smooth, dark and toasty wine. It's layered with whiffs of smoke, espresso and black licorice, matched with dark fruit flavors of black cherry and cassis. It's a big, balanced, sexy wine with polished tannins that leave a trail of tobacco highlights.

name: Maggy Hawk 2018 Unforgettable Estate Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: A dark heart of black fruit centers this complex and well-balanced wine. Enticing, spicy clove, cedar and earth aromas lead to a supersmooth but well-structured palate that reveals black-cherry, rhubarb and black-tea nuances. It's distinctive and should age well in the mid-term. Best from 2023.

name: Maggy Hawk 2018 Stormin' Estate Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Silky and polished, this elegant wine offers restrained oak aromas and flavors like cinnamon and nutmeg backed by vibrant red- and black-cherry flavors. Great balance from light tannins and good acidity give it an appetizing mouthfeel and will help it age well. Best from 2023.

name: Maggy Hawk 2018 Jolie Estate Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: A captivating blend of savory characteristics along with great balance and a good fruit foundation make this wine especially interesting. Elements of sage, tobacco and earth mingle with red and black cherries, and are backed by moderate tannins and good, nervy acidity.

name: Maggy Hawk 2018 Afleet Estate Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Light baking spices blend with red cherry and a touch of sour cherry in this medium-bodied, deftly balanced wine. An elegant texture and subtleties in flavor seem to improve it with each sip. Best from 2023.

name: Maggio 2018 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Lodi)
review: This is a gutsy but well-balanced wine, offering exuberant black-cherry and blackberry flavors, medium to full body and a snappy texture. It's very generous and has just a nip of tannin.

name: Maggio 2018 Merlot (Lodi)
review: This medium-bodied wine is centered on good black-fruit flavors and generously accented by notes of menthol, black pepper and olive.

name: Maddalena 2018 Zinfandel (Paso Robles)
review: Deep, dark and oaky aromas of black cherry, toast, vanilla and caramel are quite ripe and woody on the nose of this bottling. There is more lushness to the palate, where those same exact elements are all at play.

name: MacRostie 2018 Nunes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Thick and savory forest and tea elements combine effortlessly with crunchy fruit flavors of cranberry and orange in this light-bodied, elegant vineyard-designate. The concentration gives way to a silky smoothness that rides along the lengthy finish.

name: MacRostie 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Hearty and full bodied in style, this wine is nonetheless high toned in edgy acidity and cool-climate vibes, giving a balanced perspective of brightness within its power. Rhubarb, raspberry and forest notes dominate along a silky smooth texture of layered tannin and oak.

name: MacRostie 2018 Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Comforting aromas of black cherry and baking spices are warm and inviting on the nose of this bottling, which is quite light in the glass. The palate is fresh and fruity with cherry juice, vanilla and light nutmeg flavors, bringing plenty of spice to the nimble structure.

name: MacRostie 2018 Manzana Vineyard Pinot Noir (Green Valley)
review: Grippy, layered and textured, this impressive wine is elegantly lean and balanced, with contrasting notions of blood orange, cranberry and forest floor. It impresses in its enduring acidity and bold structure.

name: MacRostie 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Rich in cola and vanilla flavors that accent the core of strawberry, rhubarb and orange peel, this wine is cohesive and integrated. Silky and creamy on the palate, it builds tannin weight over time, finishing in a balance of black tea.

name: MacRostie 2018 Putnam Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Robust tannins wrap around a core of forest floor, black tea and savory, edgy intrigue in this coastal wine. High-toned acidity buzzes along a focused palate of wild strawberry, cranberry and rhubarb.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Lynn's Blend Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a full and fleshy red, with length and breadth, that is complex and layered in juicy black cherry, wild strawberry and a taste of licorice. The oak adds accents of caramel and vanilla that balance and complement effortlessly, adding to a lasting richness.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Quail Hill Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: With a floral, inviting nose of rose and cherry blossom, this beautiful wine is fleshy and broadly textured. With a touch of grip, it presents a balanced approach and integrated tannin and oak, with a tangy citrus and black-cherry flavor that delves into forest floor and cardamom.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Susanna's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Earth, black tea and cardamom meet an elegantly rich concentration of strawberry and blueberry in this coastal-influenced wine, the tannins supple and supportive. Bright, lifted acidity keeps it fresh and crisp on the lengthy palate.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Summit Block Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a block planted to the Calera clone, this is an outstanding wine, complex, robust and broadly textured. The fine structure supports a balanced weight of strawberry compote, vanilla bean and dried herb, with just a touch of black pepper and cinnamon.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: A tremendously impressive expression of the appellation, this wine is broadly lush and velvety on the palate. Juicy black cherry, strawberry and earth highlight a supple tannic backbone that's dusted in black tea and herb.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Juicy and brooding in spicy cinnamon, clove and depths of red cherry and raspberry, this is a well-made, well-rounded appellation wine that displays the diversity and complexity of the place. Expert winemaking turns it into a cohesive whole of richness and length.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Old Vines Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From an aptly named section of the estate's Quail Hill Vineyard, this wine is intensely focused and textured, exuding brooding notes of plum, wild strawberry, black tea and clove. The oak and tannin are well behaved, accentuating a duality of dust and grip.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Monastery Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earth, flowers and a grip of black fruit pop along a textured, structured core in this remarkable, memorable wine. The oak and tannin integration is high-level, providing a sense of cohesion and elegance throughout.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Kanzler Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This vineyard-designate is salty and silky in tannin, with a refined elegance. Mineral-driven elements of crushed gravel balance out layers of rose, black tea, cardamom and black cherry, finding cohesion in well-integrated power and length.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Hawk Hill Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a site in cool-climate Freestone, this wine astounds in its firm grasp of crisp tangerine, strawberry and cherry. Elegant, rounded and lengthy, it offers everything one wants in the variety—a mix of cardamom, nutmeg and white pepper, with lasting notes of black tea and forest floor.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Five Sisters Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Aged 15 months in new French oak, this wine is built to gracefully age. A powerful expression of the grape, it opens in a floral bouquet of rose, with a citrus-driven palate that progresses to intense red fruit. The texture is grippy yet laced in underlying acidity. Enjoy from 2028–2032.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Bliss Block Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a two-acre parcel within the producer's estate Quail Hill Vineyard—its coolest point—this wine has a savory entry of black tea, dried herb and compost, an impressive earthiness that persists through a lengthy palate. Along the way, crisp red fruit and a welcome salinity complement each other effortlessly, finishing bright.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Block 10 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From a specific block of the estate Quail Hill Vineyard, this wine is robust in oak and tannin, with subtle power and ripe black-cherry fruit. The texture becomes supple and round on the palate, with an enduring richness and salty, spicy character.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Anisya's Blend Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: From Susanna's Vineyard close to the coast, this is a perfumed, elegant and complex wine that's named after the proprietor. Flavors of strawberry-rhubarb compote, rose petal, black tea and forest floor contribute depth and intensity while buoyant acidity adds impressive length.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 AMDG Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Earthy cardamom, black tea and rose petal infuse the nose, with an expansive palate of lifted raspberry and pomegranate building the palate of this bold, beautifully layered wine. Powerfully built, it retains plenty of underlying acidity to find freshness and balance.

name: Lynmar Estate 2018 Adam's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Sizzling acidity gives this vineyard-designate energy and life—a fresh complement to its deeper, darker instincts. Generous tannin weight and structure and ample oak provide a dense backbone as a buildup of tangy, juicy black cherry, strawberry and pomegranate meet notes of forest floor and cardamom.

name: Lumen 2018 Portico Hills Vineyard Grenache (Santa Barbara County)
review: This is a bright, fresh, playful and delicious spin on Grenache by pioneering female winemaker Lane Tanner from a vineyard next to the town of Los Alamos, where co-proprietor Will Henry runs Pico restaurant. Fruity aromas of strawberry, raspberry, ginger and snickerdoodle spice lead into a grippy palate of red plum, underripe strawberry and mint. Glou-glou style.

name: Lumen 2018 Julia's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Maria Valley)
review: Aromas of dried hibiscus, raspberry, red plum skin and game pop on the nose of this bottling by Lane Tanner and Will Henry. There is a strong core of meat on the palate yet also touches of bright raspberry and cherry. It's a very interesting and complex sip.

name: Lula 2018 Costa Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: Arresting aromas of red cherry and raspberry lead to vivid, tangy, mouth-watering red-fruit flavors in this well-balanced and vibrant wine. Its firm structure of fine-grained tannins and fruit acidity bode well for aging. Best from 2023.

name: Lula 2018 Peterson Vineyard Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: Delicious oak spices light up this full-bodied and dark-fruited wine while a good structure of fine tannins and elegant acidity balance it nicely. Cinnamon, clove and wood smoke put the finishing touches on black-cherry and black-tea flavors that have excellent depth.

name: Lula 2018 Lula Vineyard Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Abundant, ripe and rich black-fruit flavors are well-matched by a firm appetizing structure of moderate tannins and lively acidity in this concentrated and complete wine. Black-cherry, blackberry and cinnamon notes build on the palate. Best from 2023.

name: Lula 2018 Founders Cuvée Pinot Noir (Mendocino)
review: This is a plush, smooth, seductive wine underpinned by light tannins and tangy acidity. It offers deep, ripe plum and black cherry accented by black tea and a whiff of wood smoke.

name: Lula 2018 Rescue Block Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Gorgeous just-ripe fruit flavors, subtle baking spices and a firm layered texture make this wine a stunner. Made with 25% whole clusters, it offers good depth, great focus on red- and black-cherry flavors, a rich wrapping of fine-grained tannins and a lingering finish. Best from 2024.

name: Lula 2018 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: A savory, leafy expression lends extra interest and complexity to this well-balanced, trim but flavorful wine. A full body and light, integrated tannins support generous black-cherry and strawberry flavors.

name: Luke 2018 Reserve Syrah (Wahluke Slope)
review: Blackberry and other dark-fruit aromas meld with notes of smoked meat, coffee and mineral. The palate packs a big wallop of rich, layered black-fruit flavors, with plenty of tannic structure to provide support. It's a power-packed, hedonistic delight.

name: Luke 2018 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Wahluke Slope)
review: Highly regarded StoneTree Vineyard is the dominant fruit source for this wine. Coffee, dried herb and black-cherry aromas lead to a palate that brings a whole lot of seriousness to it. It's rich and dense, full of tannic heft. Give it time to stretch its legs—and drink it at a cool temperature—to see it at its best. Best after 2025.

name: Luke 2018 Syrah (Wahluke Slope)
review: Aromas of black raspberry, scorched earth and pomegranate lead to rich fruit flavors. It's a no holds barred, rich, intense Syrah. Decant if drinking in the near term.

name: Luke 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Wahluke Slope)
review: Aromas of fresh raspberry, bittersweet chocolate and dried herb pop on the nose. The palate is ripe, delicious, fruit filled and lip-smacking, displaying a lot of density, intensity and texture. Give this beauty a short decant if drinking in the near term.

name: Luke 2018 Merlot (Wahluke Slope)
review: The aromas pop, with notes of dark raspberry, chocolate and cherry, showing a bright sense of varietal purity. Ripe, full-bodied, layered fruit flavors follow, with the acidity bringing a lot of freshness and exquisite balance. It lingers on the finish. Delicious and fruit filled, with plenty of structure, it's hedonism at its best—a surefire crowd pleaser. Give a short decant if drinking in the near term.

name: Luke 2018 The Companion Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is a blend of 60% Cabernet Sauvignon and equal parts Merlot and Syrah. Plum, raspberry, chocolate and cherry aromas lead to an explosion of blue- and red-fruit flavors on the palate. There is dazzling freshness, a spot-on sense of balance and plenty of acid and tannin structure.

name: Lucky Rock Wine Co. 2018 Pinot Noir (Monterey-Sonoma-San Benito)
review: Fresh and fruity but also showing a touch of woodsy, tea-like complexity, this medium-bodied wine is well balanced and complete. It offers plenty of cherry and plum aromas, ripe but nicely tangy cherry and raspberry flavors and light tannins for good grip on the palate.

name: Lucienne 2018 Doctor's Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Brisk raspberry and baked pomegranate aromas meet with tea leaf, toasty oak and turned earth on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Smoked meat and wild cherry flavors converge on the sip, where bright floral elements lead into an acid-driven, vanilla-spiced finish.

name: Lucienne 2018 Smith Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Rich, opulent aromas of black-cherry cordial and caramel are thick but delicious on the nose of this bottling. Sandy tannins frame the creamy cherry and blackberry flavors, which are lifted by star anise and licorice accents and lead into an oaky finish.

name: Lucienne 2018 Lone Oak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dense aromas of cranberry, crushed rock and sage blend the fruit, earth and herb elements quite well on the nose of this bottling. The palate balances rich cherry and dark-strawberry flavors with touches of dried mint and turned loam.

name: Lucienne 2018 Hook Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Tightly packaged aromas of black raspberry, sarsaparilla, turned loam and dried herb show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. There is a light herbal kick of bay leaf and juniper at first on the sip before ripe tones of mulberry and peppery spice take hold, with a sense of oak on the finish.

name: Lucid 2018 L.03 Manifesto Barbera (Sierra Foothills)
review: Generous aromas of black pepper, flint and wood smoke lead to light blackberry and cranberry flavors in this medium-bodied experimental wine. The makers used native yeast and Belgian beer yeast in the fermentation, and cherry, maple and hickory wood in the aging process. No sulfites were added.

name: Lucia 2018 Garys' Vineyard Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: This wine is only beginning to gather the elements that will make it a classic for years to come, starting with aromas of savory dried meat, kelp and charred asphalt. The palate's dried purple fruits are lifted by peppercorns, tar and more seashore influence—elements that will all converge in true harmony with time. Drink 2022–2038.

name: Lucia 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Dark boysenberry, hibiscus syrup and light licorice aromas lead into an acid-driven palate, where rich boysenberry and cherry flavors meet with toasted marjoram and cracked pepper. This is an affordable appellation blend that's a great entry point to explore the winery.

name: Lucia 2018 Soberanes Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: A deep mahogany aroma meets with baked boysenberry on the nose of this bottling by the Pisoni family. It's rich on the palate, where boysenberry jam is lifted by fennel, licorice and wild-sagebrush flavors, all wrapped in silky tannins.

name: Lucia 2018 Garys' Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Intense aromas of black-cherry compote, star anise, rosewater candy and caramel show on the lavish nose of this bottling. There is great tension to the sip, where snappy red-currant and dark berry flavors meet with freshly ground nutmeg and vanilla spices.

name: Lucas & Lewellen 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: This countywide blend of the winery's Goodchild and Los Alamos estate vineyards is a great investment. Aromas of fresh raspberry, plum, thyme, wet gravel and toasted sage lead into riper cranberry and pomegranate on the palate, where dusty herbs add complexity.

name: Love Noir 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This straightforward, medium-bodied wine shows attractive black-cherry and sour-cherry flavors, a low level of tannin and a smooth texture.

name: Lost Poet 2018 Red (California)
review: This medium-bodied wine offers light herb, smoked meat and ripe plum flavors accented by smoke and baking-spice notes. It's well balanced and only lightly tannic.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Spanish Springs Vineyard Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Sour red-plum and dewy sagebrush aromas show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate is more ripe with wild berry and cranberry flavors, cut by orange-rind and bay leaf touches.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Cargasacchi Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Candied raspberry, concentrated hibiscus and a hint of caramel candy show on the nose of this bottling. There is a grippy structure to the palate, where orange-rind and plum-peel flavors meet with bay leaf and eucalyptus.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Peterson Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Strong aromas of plum and raspberry meet with herbal spice on the nose of this bottling. The sip also offers that herbal array, including hints of black tea, and there is also a sightly tangy black-raspberry-fruit component.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Peake Ranch Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Crisp and tart black-raspberry aromas pair with star anise, ginger snaps and the light touch of oak on the nose of this bottling. The sip is both snappy and opulent, with raspberry-cream and alpine-strawberry flavors riding a sharp acidity, and fresh hibiscus that lingers into the finish.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Cooper Jaxon Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Plum juice, rose petals, pastry and tarragon aromas converge on the dynamic nose of this bottling. A strong core of plum fruit is enhanced by lavender and sagebrush on the vibrant palate.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Tangy red-plum aromas meet with a sharp sense of strawberry-balsamic sauce on the nose of this appellation blend. There is a good snap to the sip, which warms up with star anise spice yet clings to the sharp and tangy red-fruit-skin core.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Barbara County)
review: Ripe and yet light at the same time, this bottling offers aromas of sweet strawberry, caramel and sea salt. Soft and light flavors of tangy cherry meet with sagebrush on the palate.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Rancho La Vina Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Plush and fun aromas of candied cherry cola, sandalwood, potpourri, strawberry cream and rose water make for a deliciously inviting nose on this bottling by Brian Loring. It's juicy on the palate with plush red fruits and flowers, leveled by the slightly earthy tang of sumac.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Cortada Alta Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Stewed cherries, dried plums and fresh rose petals are lifted by white pepper on the lush nose of this bottling. The palate is loaded with full flavors of alpine strawberry, hibiscus and dried mints, proving rich and ripe while complex and vibrant.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Tangy red-plum sauce and raspberry aromas are lifted by a pinch of tarragon on the nose of this bottling. There's a strawberry balsamic touch to the sip, leading into a tangy, vanilla-laced finish.

name: Loring Wine Company 2018 Boekenoogen Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Candied cherries and red currants meet with fresh hibiscus on the playful nose of this bottling. It's big boisterous on the palate, with loads of bright and ripe red fruits and a strong amount of gingerbread spice, proving big but balanced.

name: Longvale 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Strong oak and clove aromas and flavors give this full-bodied wine a very spicy character that dominates the varietal fruit flavors.

name: Longvale 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: Not a bad blend of charred oak aromas and hearty cherry-berry flavors, this sturdy and fairly concentrated wine has a full body and a touch of tannin that bolsters the mouthfeel.

name: Longevity 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Bold flavors and a firm texture give this full-bodied wine a big presence. It is drenched in oaky aromas and flavors of cinnamon, nutmeg and vanilla, but has good fruit and structure too.

name: Longboard 2018 Mer Sauvage Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Employing proprietary French oak, this wine for aged 15 months. This is a feral, wild wine ideally named by the surfing aficionado who makes it. Meaty game, wild strawberry and supple tannin combine to provide a lengthy and textured experience of varietal complexity.

name: Lone Madrone 2018 La Mezcla Roja Red (Paso Robles)
review: This blend of 60% Cinsault, 25% Counoise and 15% Grenache explores little used grape varieties to deliver an excellent table wine perfect for a wide variety of foods. Aromas of dark plum, hibiscus and scarlet-red rose petal lead into a fresh and floral palate of tart red and purple fruits, proving easy to quaff.

name: Lone Birch 2018 Estate Grown Cabernet Sauvignon (Yakima Valley)
review: This wine shows much of what there is to love about the 2018 vintage: a mouthful of plush, expansive fruit flavors, with plenty of acidity to back them up. One would like more aromatically from this quiet wine, but the palate delivers plenty.

name: Lone Birch 2018 Estate Grown Syrah (Yakima Valley)
review: Showing some reduction initially, the aromas suggest notes of char, black raspberry and gravel. The palate is deft and up front, trailing a bit toward the finish but overall showing good balance. There's plenty of value to be had.

name: Lombardi 2018 Giusti Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Red and black cherry, juicy citrus peel and black tea highlight a depth of tannin and earthiness in this full-bodied, richly concentrated wine. Supple tannins add to the weight and breadth, finished in toasted oak.

name: Lombardi 2018 Hill Justice Vineyard Pinot Noir (Petaluma Gap)
review: From a nine-acre rocky site at 1,100-feet elevation, this is a layered, juicy and complex wine, grippy and lengthy, with balanced depth and concentration. Blackberry, black cherry, cardamom and oak wrap around supple tannins and a lasting taste of dark chocolate.

name: Lombardi 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This comes from two vineyard sites: Giusti and Gunsalus, vineyard-designates in their own right. Earthy and herbal, it is layered and powerful with grip, with focused acidity and tangy flavors of cranberry, currant and black cherry.

name: Lola 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Calistoga)
review: Thick in a sweetness of tannin and cassis, this wine is balanced and well structured, with secondary elements of toasted oak, caramel and toffee. The length impresses, stretching across the palate in textured grip.

name: Lola 2018 Zinfandel (St. Helena)
review: Made in a light, bright style, this elegant wine is tangy and spicy, aged in neutral oak. Sweet tobacco, black cherry and plum hit the palate with restraint.

name: Lola 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This wine is supercharged with baking spices and charred oak notes. These aspects make it entertaining and attention-getting but also smother the varietal fruit characteristics.

name: LOLA 2018 10th Anniversary Reserve Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Native yeast-fermented, neutral-oak aged and fermented whole-cluster, this light, bright wine is delicate in frame and body, with elegant, earthy layers of strawberry and tea. It has a carbonic-light buoyancy and energy.

name: Living Roots 2018 Woodland Cherry Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)
review: Bright, defined aromas of raspberry preserves, dried herbs and pepper carry the concentrated nose. The palate is rounded and silky, yet properly lifted by integrated acidity and a framing of well-knit tannins. It's a tasty, juicy red, showing raspberry and bramble berry flavors, with shades of herbs on the side. Drink with a slight chill.

name: Lingua Franca 2018 The Plow Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: For this cuvée the grapes were fermented with wild yeast in both stainless and concrete. Lovely aromatics bring a pleasing plummy note, lightly kissed with toast and cocoa. The firm dark fruit flavors come with a streak of espresso and lead into slightly grainy, textural tannins. It trails away with a hint of lemony herb.

name: Line 39 2018 Petite Sirah (California)
review: Strong aromas of wood smoke and charcoal coat ripe blackberry flavors in this full-bodied wine. Very firm tannins tighten up the mouthfeel.

name: Liminal 2018 High Canyon Series WeatherEye Vineyard Grenache (Red Mountain)
review: The first thing one notices about this wine is the depth of color for the variety. Coming from the Amphitheater block, the aromas pop in notes of black raspberry, rose petal, pomegranate and crushed rock. Red and black fruit flavors undulate across the palate, showing deftness, richness and detail. The finish goes on as long—longer even than one cares to count. Throughout it all, it retains a sense of balance and freshness. It's a yardstick against which future Washington Grenache will be measured.

name: Liminal 2018 High Canyon Series WeatherEye Vineyard Syrah (Red Mountain)
review: Coming from a canyon high up on Red Mountain and aged in 18% new French oak, the aromas pull you into the glass, with notes of mulberry, blackberry, kirsch, earth, crushed rock, coffee and bacon fat. There's depth, intensity, richness, layering, polish, and texture to the palate. The finish is endless. This wine has it all, with the impressive balance, intensity, acidity and length separating it far out front. It sings from the top of Red Mountain. Best after 2026.

name: Liminal 2018 Block Highlight Series Block 16 WeatherEye Vineyard Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
designation: Block Highlight Series Block 16 WeatherEye Vineyard
review: Fruit for this wine comes from the highest block currently planted on Red Mountain, north facing at the top of the ridge, outside the AVA boundaries. Aged in 25% new French oak, it's the most brooding of the winery's inaugural releases, with notes of dried herb, blackberry, coffee and clove. It's dazzling in its intensity and layering, saturating the palate from end to end while still showing fleetness of foot. Packed full of tannin and acid, it absolutely sails on the finish. The balance is exquisite. Best after 2025.

name: Liminal 2018 Vineyard Series WeatherEye Vineyard G-S-M (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit for this inaugural wine comes from a new site up and over the top of Red Mountain and down onto the north side. Grenache makes up 42%, followed by Mourvèdre (38%) and Syrah. The aromas bring notes of pomegranate, mulberry, crushed rock, savory herb and black pepper. It's a big mouthful of a wine, rich, layered and intense, with earthshaking intensity and plenty of tension. There's impressive tannin and an abundance of acidity to enliven it all. The fruit-filled finish seems near endless. It's a new benchmark for Rhône-style blends in Washington. Best after 2025.

name: Limerick Lane 2018 Estate Cuvée Red (Russian River Valley)
review: Combining 63% Zinfandel, 27% Syrah and 10% Petite Sirah from the producer's estate, this blend invites one in with aromas and flavors of fresh blackberry, nutmeg and vanilla bean. Soft and supple on the palate, it has full-bodied intensity with complex accents of black pepper, leather and coffee.

name: Limerick Lane 2018 Marquis Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a spectacular showcase and field blend of several incredible sites, including Banfield (36%), Limerick Lane (23%), Soldini (18%), Bedrock (14%) and Carlisle (9%), familiar names to followers of exceptional Zin. Possessed of tension, power and surprisingly high-toned freshness, it is layered in crushed rock, iron and just-picked blackberry. The structure suggests aging; enjoy best from 2028–2033.

name: Lightpost 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Candied Bing cherry, hibiscus and dried rose-petal aromas make for a very delicious nose on this bottling. The palate combines savory herbs, light roasted meat and garden herbs with a strong black-raspberry core, proving dark and complex.

name: Lightpost 2018 Ferrari Vineyards Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Red cherry, potpourri, caramel-laced pastry and a kiss of oak show on the nose of this bottling, which needs a bit of air to fully develop. Once it does, the palate is redolent with raspberry and cranberry flavors, bolstered by high acidity and firmer tannins that are common in the high-elevation appellation. Drink now–2038.

name: Lightpost 2018 Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Veteran vintner Christian Roguenaut knows San Luis Obispo County like few others. Here, he puts together a great appellation blend in this bottling. Snappy aromas of fresh raspberry and red currant meet with elegant rose peels on the nose. The palate begins with joyous black raspberry fruit and then tightens around earthy hints of thyme, sage and mossy pine needles.

name: Lightpost 2018 Spanish Springs Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Elegantly floral aromas of violet and rose petal meet with candied raspberry on the nose of this bottling from a vineyard very close to Pismo Beach. The palate is brisk, with sharp raspberry and more flower-petal elegance, woven together by a mineral tone of crushed gravel.

name: Light Horse 2018 Pinot Noir (California)
review: This full-bodied wine offers mellow oak aromas of cedar and clove while mulled cherry notes join in on the palate. It's broad in texture and soft in mouthfeel.

name: Light Horse 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This wine tastes mellow and smoky, giving a savory edge to the subdued, ripe plum flavors underneath. Medium body and soft tannins make it easy to sip.

name: LifeVine 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Tasty blackberry aromas and black-cherry flavors carry this medium-bodied wine across a broad mouthfilling texture firmed by light tannins.

name: LifeVine 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Rosewater and black-cherry scents and flavors are accented with a dash of savory herbs. The flavors are up front, rather light but balanced, with a quick fade and finish.

name: Lieb 2018 Estate Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)
review: Bright, fruity raspberry and cherry form the nose. Shades of violet and spice give a bit of depth, but the fruit remains front and center. This is medium in weight, with a juicy, lifted palate of zesty bramble berries and cranberries. There's a tangy, salivating element to the midpalate, while light accents of pepper and flowers skirt around the edges. Mildly raspy tannins arise on the spicy finish.

name: Lieb 2018 Estate Cabernet Franc (North Fork of Long Island)
review: A fruit-forward nose of mixed berry jam shows a shade of tilled earth. While light in feel, the palate is smooth and silky. A bouncy cherry flavor fills it out, with just enough acidity and gossamer-thin tannins providing the frame.

name: Lichen Estate 2018 Moonglow Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Superfresh and powerfully fruity, this medium- to full-bodied wine braces bright, focused blackberry and black-cherry flavors with tangy acidity and moderate, grippy tannins. It has a vibrancy and drama that are wonderful to experience. Best from 2023.

name: Les Belles Collines 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a light, delicate and nuanced wine, 30% whole-cluster pressed and retentive of a feral quality that appeals and intrigues. Strawberry, cardamom and black tea top a buzzy framework of lively acidity and a powerful tannic backbone, with a peppery finish that lasts for days.

name: Lepe 2018 Gladys Reserve Red (San Antonio Valley)
review: Elegant aromas of boysenberry, violet and blueberry show on the nose of this bottling from a warmer stretch of southern Monterey County. It lands smooth on the palate with more blueberry and Concord grape flavors, proving fruity in a very pleasant manner.

name: Leonetti Cellar 2018 Merlot (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: This wine is 100% varietal—what a glorious example of Merlot. Aromas of dark-raspberry compote, plum, dark chocolate and cedar are followed by textured, layered, rich flavors that a firm spine of tannins stands up. Lovely acidity brightens it.

name: Leah Jørgensen Cellars 2018 Cabernet Franc (Southern Oregon)
review: Aromatic, as are all of Leah Jørgensen's wines, this weaves a bit of smoke through a mix of sweet and bitter herbs and red fruits—cranberries and pomegranate and stewed cherries. Still young and pulling those flavors together, it builds upon balanced and complex components that trail into a lingering finish, reflecting far more care and polish than most wines at this price. Give this another year or two before opening that first bottle.

name: Leah Jørgensen Cellars 2018 Oregon Tour Rain Vin Rouge Red (Oregon)
review: The blend remains as previously, 60% Cabernet Franc and 40% Gamay. The wine is clean and fresh, with bright red fruits, spice and savory components. The tannins add earthy bark to the mix. It's a fun, medium-bodied red blend that may be enjoyed with everything from pizza to spicier fare.

name: Leah Jørgensen Cellars 2018 Le Coeur de Tour Rain Havlin Vineyard Gamay (Willamette Valley)
designation: Le Coeur de Tour Rain Havlin Vineyard
review: The fruit runs from cranberry to black cherry, with a touch of pepper adding spicy highlights. The tannins are moderate, set against ample acidity. A whiff of leather will please some palates more than others, but seems proportionate, at least for the time being.

name: Le Cadeau 2018 Diversité Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: There is indeed great diversity here in the flavor mix of cilantro, raspberry, cherry and citrus. Those citrus components up the acidity, with lemon, grapefruit and even a touch of green pineapple coming through. Though young, the wine shows good focus, with lightly grainy but ripe tannins. The prime drinking window should open fully in a couple more years.

name: Le Cadeau 2018 Rocheux Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Blending Pommard and Dijon 777 clones, this is aromatic to the brink of pungent, with a striking scent of Italian herbs, plums and cherries. As with all the Le Cadeau Pinots, there is a compelling smoothness and a well-integrated mix of herb, fruit, acid and tannin. The barrel aging puts a streak of mocha through the finish.

name: Le Cadeau 2018 Côte Est Pinot Noir (Chehalem Mountains)
review: From an east-facing block, this seems just a bit short of fully ripened, with strong herbal flavors dominating. It's still tight and a bit short, and the berry-flavored fruits also carry a hint of green.

name: Le Cadeau 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Easily identified by its red label, this is a terrific introduction to the new vintage, and it bodes well for the reserves to follow. Ripe, robust and packed with fruit flavors of strawberry and cherry, this barrel selection of estate-grown fruit is highlighted with barrel flavors of toasted coffee beans. The tannins are balanced and finish with a lightly bitter note.

name: Layer Cake 2018 Pinot Noir (Central Coast)
review: Wild aromas of sagebrush and damp earth meet with mulberry, baked cherry and smoke on the nose of this bottling. The palate shows a similarly earthy streak, with dried desert herbs and a hint of game, with wild-cherry fruit at the core.

name: Layer Cake 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: Handsomely dressed in oak spices, this medium to full-bodied wine carries cedar, cinnamon, clove and dill flavors on a broad, lightly tannic texture.

name: Layer Cake 2018 Bourbon Barrel Aged Cabernet Sauvignon (California)
review: This is a full-bodied, inviting wine that smoothly blends alluring spices with tasty, generous fruit flavors. Oak spices of cinnamon, vanilla and nutmeg merge with ripe red cherry, black plum and strawberry, while moderate fine-grained tannins frame it all.

name: Lava Cap 2018 Sangiovese (El Dorado)
review: Hints of mint and cedar help give this full-bodied wine a mature, oak-aged, reserve-style personality despite its young age. Medium tannins and rather strong alcohol lend plenty of appetizing grip to the texture.

name: Lava Cap 2018 Barbera (El Dorado)
review: Mouthfilling and plush in feel, this mellow yet complex wine offers mulled fruit flavors, a hint of ragù and subtle accents of earth and tobacco. It's extremely smooth, offering light tannins and subdued acidity.

name: Latimer Ranch 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: From the folks at Chalk Hill Winery, this is named for a pioneering homestead once at the site. With grapes from the producer's own Trenton Road Vineyard, this tastes of cherry cola and vanilla, with soft, smoothly robust tannin and a lasting note of coffee.

name: Last Light 2018 Pinot Noir (San Luis Obispo County)
review: There's a wild amount of explosive aromas going on in this bottling from Derbyshire Vineyard, from boysenberry compote to dank forest herbs to toasty baking spices. There is similar layering on the tart and tangy palate, where black raspberry mixes with wild thyme and sage to yield an extremely fresh expression.

name: LaRue 2018 Emmaline Ann Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Youthful and still untangling, this wine is peppery and spicy—a mix of white pepper and clove that season complementary components of forest floor and black tea. Crisp orange fruit, raspberry and strawberry provide a tanginess that appeals and coats the delicate palate with just a hint of oak.

name: LaRue 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: This is a bringing together of three vineyard sites, 46% Thorn Ridge, 38% Rice-Spivak and 16% Emmaline Ann. Gorgeous and delicious, it sings in black tea, rhubarb, violet and orange peel, with bright, delicate structure and underlying acidity. A tease of dark chocolate lingers on the finish.

name: Lapis Luna 2018 Pinot Noir (North Coast)
review: Smoky aromas and cured ham flavors dominate this full-bodied wine as it delivers modest fruit notes and a lean texture.

name: Lapis Luna 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Lodi)
review: Rich, round and darkly fruity, this wine offers extra ripeness and soft tannins for a very mouthfilling, almost sweet effect.

name: Lapis Luna 2018 Zinfandel (North Coast)
review: This full-bodied wine offers very fresh crushed berry aromas and plenty of straightforward grapey plummy flavors. It tastes unusually generous and vivid.

name: Lange 2018 Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Here you'll find a tart mix of wild blackberries, with a strong herbal element that resolves in tannins with a green-tea astringency. Aged in one-third new French oak, there's a toasty twist of caramel threading through the finish.

name: Lange 2018 Classiqué Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: A sharp pine-needle note cuts through tart and herbal cranberry-raspberry flavors. This value bottling includes fruit from separately fermented blocks from a cross-section of Lange vineyards, all high quality fruit. It was aged in 15% new French oak.

name: Landform 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: From Josh Phelps, this new Oregon extension of his Grounded Wine Company portfolio brings ripe blackberry and black cherry fruit, grounded (dare I say) in a pleasing earthy quality. Some 20% was whole-cluster fermented, which adds texture and some green-tea flavors to the tannins, without becoming too herbal. Off to a good start!

name: Lamoreaux Landing 2018 T23 Unoaked Estate Bottled Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)
review: This unoaked Cabernet Franc opens to a nose of charred green bell pepper. The palate is soft and light in feel, with a cranberry-raspberry flavor that's sprinkled with more green notes. The acidity is well honed, with slightly raspy tannins marking the close.

name: Laissez Faire 2018 Red
review: This young release red offers plum, orange peel, currant and spice aromas. A plump, flavorful, elegant palate follows. It's a wine that makes you want to come back for more.

name: Laetitia 2018 Estate Pinot Noir (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Clean aromas of red cherry, dark strawberry and star anise are very familiar and classic for Central Coast Pinot Noir on the nose of this bottling. The acidity runs from the front to the back of the sip, driving through cherry pie, rosewater and light herb flavors.

name: Lady of the Sunshine 2018 Chene Vineyard Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Wondrous aromas of flowers and crunchy fruit introduce this bottling, reminiscent of walking through a blooming rose garden in the dewy dawn surrounded by wild tarragon with a bowl of raspberry sorbet in hand. There is intense structure to the sip, as the wine is quite young and tightly wound, yet rose-petal, carnation and snappy red-currant flavors all emerge with precision.

name: Lady Hill 2018 Champoeg Vineyard Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This vineyard selection is of medium concentration, with red berry fruit flavors, light citrus and a dash of sweet baking spices. The tannins are smooth and subtle. It's good to go and should be consumed by mid-decade.

name: Lady Hill 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: This is a light, pleasant wine, with mixed red fruits, principally just-picked raspberries. Lighter veins of cola and brown sugar run through the finish. Best enjoyed over the next two or three years.

name: Lady Hill 2018 Radicle Vine Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: A second label, this very light wine is front loaded with flavors of cherry cola, sassafras and sandalwood. There's little weight or depth, so that first burst of flavor tells the whole story. A good wine to drink slightly chilled.

name: La P'tit Paysan 2018 Old Vines Cabernet Sauvignon (San Benito County)
review: From a secret selection of old vines, Ian Brand delivers aromas of dried blackberry, leather, cocoa and a touch of lavender on the very dry nose. The palate is tightly wound with tannins and a rocky minerality, offering flavors of black currant, pastry and coffee bean.

name: La Honda Winery 2018 Syrah (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Black tea and reduced cassis aromas meet with wood spice and a hint of tarry reduction on the nose of this bottling. Charred cherry-wood and oak tones show prominently on the palate, where pepper-spice, black-plum and soy flavors lead into a vanilla finish.

name: La Honda Winery 2018 Sequence Pinot Noir (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Muddled mulberry, dried sage and wild purple-flower aromas converge on the nose of this bottling—a blend of many small vineyards throughout the region. The flavors are aggressive but pleasant on the sip, from tangy red currant and cranberry to orange peel and underlying brown-spice elements.

name: La Crema 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Part of the Jackson Family portfolio, this widely-available wine marries spicy berry and black-cherry fruit to barrel flavors of cocoa powder. Tannins are buffed to a fine gloss, and come to a final focus with a hint of green tea. Some 23% was aged in new French oak.

name: L'Ecole No. 41 2018 Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Blended with 18% Grenache and 2% Mourvèdre, this red offers aromas of dark raspberry, huckleberry and dried herbs on the nose. There's a savory sense to the pillowy, soft flavors on the palate. It's high octane but keeps it all in check.

name: L'Ecole No. 41 2018 Frenchtown Red (Columbia Valley (WA))
