review: Blue fruit, milk chocolate and mint aromas are followed by sumptuous, rich, layered blue and red fruit flavors. Coffee notes linger on the finish. It's thoroughly delicious—a big-time value at this price.

name: Kudos 2018 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)
review: Tight and tannic, yet an improvement over the bitter 2017, this has a thin core of cherry fruit with a woodsy flavor of underbrush. The compact, short finish leaves an impression of a pat of butter.

name: Kosta Browne 2018 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)
review: Sites along the lines of Zio Tony, Silver Eagle, Jenkins, Keefer, Treehouse and more contribute the rich reward of the appellation, opening with juicy earthy delicate fruit. The black cherry and raspberry are spice-laden and peppery in cola, with length and breadth on the palate.

name: Kosta Browne 2018 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)
review: Sourced from a myriad of spectacular sites, this red is thick in approach and nuanced in spice, bursting in dark cherry, currant, rhubarb and clove. Classic elements of forest floor and black tea accent the brawny structure and long finish.

name: Kosta Browne 2018 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Delightful aromas of pomegranate, rose petal, cherry, dark herb and crushed rocks show on the nose of this bottling. The palate offers black raspberry at the core with touches of light thyme, clinging to a tense texture and persistent acidity. Drink now through 2030.

name: Kosta Browne 2018 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: Black-raspberry jam, rose petals and a hint of lavender show on the nose of this appellation blend. The palate is consumed with flavors of rich cranberry sauce, orange rind, cinnamon stick and light clove.

name: Kosta Browne 2018 Pinot Noir (Anderson Valley)
review: Fascinating sour-cherry and red-cherry flavors take the lead in this impeccably balanced and nicely concentrated wine. Medium body and moderate tannins give it excellent structure for mid-term aging, Best from 2023.

name: Kokomo 2018 G-S-M (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Blending 39% Grenache, 36% Syrah and 25% Mourvèdre, this wine is tart and tannic, with powerful concentration. The ripe strawberry and plum flavors are seasoned in nutmeg, earth and white pepper.

name: Kokomo 2018 Malbec (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Earthy leather, tobacco and dried herb confront a midplate of thick black fruit in this well-balanced, exuberant wine. Sinewy, supple tannin structure gives it a framework that adds length and breath on the palate.

name: Kokomo 2018 Grenache (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Juicy in strawberry and blueberry flavors, this is a broadly expansive, fleshy and opulent wine that is velvety smooth on the palate. A hint of ash and garrigue give it a complex complement of savory contrast.

name: Kokomo 2018 Pauline's Vineyard Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)
review: This is a lushly concentrated and ripe wine, viscously textured and rich in cherry and vanilla. The oak takes on a sweetness that lingers.

name: Kirkland Signature 2018 Cabernet Sauvignon (Washington)
review: Aromas of cherry, coffee, tire rubber and herb lead to linear, sleek, coffee-accented pit-fruit flavors that show pleasing balance. There's plenty of enjoyment to be had.

name: Kiona 2018 Estate Grown Lemberger (Red Mountain)
review: The fruit for this wine comes from 40-year-old vines. It kicks off with fruit-forward aromas of boysenberry, blueberry and plum. The palate is soft, bringing a compelling sense of purity to the blue fruit flavors. Reminiscent of a blue-fruited Syrah, it's thoroughly delightful and a big-time value to boot.

name: Zinke 2019 Morro View Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Edna Valley)
review: Lime, lemon and dried apple aromas meet with an earthy funk on the nose of this bottling. There is a sizzle of acidity to the sip, where apple-flesh and lime-pith flavors meet with a touch of wet stone.

name: Zinke 2019 Coquelicot Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Bright aromas of honey and freshly cut Pink Lady apples lean ripe but remain refreshing on the nose, tightening up around citrus peel. It's sharp on the sip with lime-peel and wet stone flavors, and then opens toward warmer honeysuckle elements.

name: Ziata 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This impressive wine opens with a touch of reduction. As it sits, tropical fruits mark the palate, surrounded by grassy, racy verve and lasting freshness.

name: Wrath 2019 Ex Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey)
review: There's something rugged and rough about this wine that makes it so compelling. Aromas of beach sand and drying kelp recall the seashore, yet there's Anaheim chili, too. The palate combines that seaweed with jalapeño, making for a visceral, slightly funky experience.

name: Wrath 2019 Ex Unoaked Chardonnay (Monterey)
review: Honeysuckle, lime-blossom, light pear and peach aromas show on the nose of this stainless-raised bottling. It's tight on the sip, showing linear and fresh tones of lime juice and sharp pear.

name: Wölffer 2019 Cellar Series Gewürztraminer (Long Island)
review: Zesty aromas of pink grapefruit, tangerine and ginger mix with a richer core of ripe peach on the nose. This is rounded and silken on the palate, displaying a wealth of peppery spice and flowers alongside grapefruit pith. It shows admirable power and intensity, perfect for those who enjoy the spicy side of the variety.

name: Wolff 2019 White Wolff White (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Boisterous aromas of poached pear, gardenia and guava are ripe but cleanly presented on the nose of this blend of 50% Viognier, 27% Albarino and 23% Grenache Blanc. Strong peach-blossom-oil and tropical tones show on the palate, but it's zesty and lively to sip.

name: Winemakers Selection 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: This fruit bowl of a wine is packed with apples, peaches and melons on a smooth, soft texture that brings a sensation of sweetness on the finish.

name: Winemakers Selection 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: Buttery flavors and an extra-thick texture define this full-bodied, mouthcoating wine. The vanilla and melted butter flavors don't let much fruit come through in this pastry-like pour.

name: Winemaker's Folly 2019 Chapter 1 Vino Blanco White (Sonoma County)
review: Vintner Jake Bilbro goes back to his roots here, as the son of Marietta Cellars' founder and winemaker Chris Bilbro, in this deliciously approachable, cheerful white. Combining 80% Chardonnay with the remainder a mix of Riesling, Gewürztraminer, Trousseau Gris, Grenache Blanc and Berger, it's a tribute to simpler times that remains flavorful, textured and bright.

name: Wine by Joe 2019 Chardonnay (Oregon)
review: This solid quaffer brings fresh fruit flavors of apple and pear, with a hint of green banana. Fermented entirely in stainless steel, it's crisp and easy-drinking. Put a light chill on it and it's a fine everyday Chardonnay.

name: Wine by Joe 2019 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: No frills here—the wine was put through all stainless-steel fermentation and no malolactic. The result is a clean and refreshing expression of crisp apple fruit, with a touch of white radish. The underlying wet-stone minerality keeps it lively through the finish.

name: Wilson Creek 2019 Chloe Vineyard Viognier (Temecula Valley)
review: The nose on this bottling is very inviting, offering lemon-rind, peach-flesh, white-flower, lemongrass and chiseled stone aromas. The palate proves rather sweet, with ripe flavors of peach and pear.

name: Willowbrook 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Fresh flavors of lemon, lime and grapefruit provide varietal quality and distinction in this lithe, crisp and appealing wine. A taste of fennel rises on the finish.

name: Whitehall Lane 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Rutherford)
review: With 11% Sémillon, this white is balanced in acidity and weight, with a viscosity that presents as opulent and lush. Stone fruit, Meyer lemon and green apple accent a lengthy, complex palate of varietal expression and exuberance.

name: Weibel Family 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: This medium-bodied wine is fruity and friendly, topping pineapple and mint flavors with hints of butter and vanilla, and wrapping them in a soft texture.

name: Waterbrook 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of dried rose petal and herb lead to flavors that fall a bit flat, looking for a hook to hang the hat on. Tart acidity lingers on the finish.

name: Waterbrook 2019 Pinot Gris (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: As much about feel as flavor, this wine is a bit reductive out of the gate. Beyond that are medium-bodied apple favors that bring some heft on this otherwise neutral-seeming wine.

name: Washington Hills 2019 Riesling (Washington)
review: Aromas of diesel, medicine and dried rose petal lead to flavors that show more of the same. Lively acidity runs throughout. It's all an uncomfortable mix.

name: W.T. Vintners 2019 Underwood Mountain Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Columbia Gorge (WA))
review: Left on skins for 30 hours, the wine is then aged 60% in stainless steel with the rest in neutral barrel. Whole lemon, wet stone, pear, nut and talc aromas lead to a flavorful, textured palate backed by a vibrant sense of acidity. It's flat-out delicious.

name: Volker Eisele Family Estate 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Chiles Valley)
review: Blended with 19% Sémillon cofermented in stainless steel, this is from the producer's estate in the eastern outskirts of the valley. Herbal legume, metal and stone are light in weight and style, with lovely, lasting flavors of green apple, apricot and tangerine.

name: Vintage Cowboy 2019 Grenache Blanc (Edna Valley)
review: Crisp yellow-flower blossoms and apple-flesh aromas are fresh and tight on the nose of this bottling. The palate is also tightly wound, offering pastry, apple and almond-cream flavors.

name: Vincent Vineyards 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Los Olivos District)
review: Very light in the glass, this bottling begins with aromas of light passion fruit, wet stone and lemongrass on the tightly wound nose. There's a compellingly grippy texture on the sip, where apple-flesh flavors meet with a wet concrete minerality.

name: Vina Robles 2019 Huerhuero Vineyard Vermentino (Paso Robles)
review: Very light in the glass, this bottling is incredibly aromatic in all the right ways, offering just enough hints of jasmine, plumeria and coconut oil as well as a brisk line of citrus peel. The palate is vibrant with acidity and scintillating texture, which balance the tropical tones of tuberose and plumeria extract.

name: Vina Robles 2019 Huerhuero Vineyard Viognier (Paso Robles)
review: This is a very exuberant style of Viognier, from the aromatics and flavors to the acidity and texture. Aromas of peach, guava, tuberose and Stargazer lily kick off the nose, leading into a palate that is very fresh while loaded with bubblegum and guava flavors.

name: Vina Robles 2019 Mistral Vineyard Chardonnay (Monterey)
review: This wine is very peachy and somewhat viscous on the nose, with hints of tuberose and coconut oil. There is a strong amount of acidity on the palate, where extremely tropical flavors of guava, plumeria and more coconut arise.

name: Valle de Inez 2019 Chardonnay (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Tightly woven aromas of chalk and lemon syrup drizzled on nectarine show on the nose of this bottling by Doug Margerum. There is a chalky tension to the palate, where lemon zest and riper orange flavors lead into a stony finish.

name: Valcan 2019 Bare White Malbec Malbec (Rogue Valley)
review: Winemaker JP Valot makes this along with a Malbec rosé. The ‘Bare White' version is a very pale copper hue, with aromas of peppery herbs, thistle and pine. The fruit touches rhubarb and wild strawberries. It's a delicate but interesting light white produced from this red grape.

name: V. Sattui 2019 Carsi Vineyard Semillon (Yountville)
review: Jalapeño and grass flavors and aromas are lifted by a tang of biting acidity in this earthy wine that is light in ripeness and quick on the finish.

name: Uvaggio 2019 Il Nostro Vermentino (Carneros)
review: Made from a variety more commonly found in Sardinia, this wine makes the case for Carneros, showing a wealth of fresh, inviting peach, lychee and green apple. High-toned acidity highlights a dry crisp salinity and lively style.

name: Unturned Stone Productions 2019 Vecino Vineyard Musqué Clone Sauvignon Blanc (Potter Valley)
review: This broad and buttery wine fermented in plastic egg-shaped casks blends vanilla and coconut aromas with rich pear and honey flavors, making it seem more full bodied than the low alcohol number would suggest, filling the palate and lingering on the finish.

name: Two Vintners 2019 From Mars Boushey Vineyard White (Yakima Valley)
review: This is the inaugural release of this wine, a blend of Picpoul (53%), Marsanne (44%) and Grenache Blanc. The aromas intrigue, with notes of lemon pith, wet stone, freshly peeled tangerine and honeycomb. The palate is light, bringing a mixture of tart citrus and sweeter orange rind flavors. It's equal parts hedonistic and intellectual enjoyment.

name: Turnbull 2019 Josephine Sauvignon Blanc (Oakville)
review: Lightly tropical in mango and lychee, this impressive white is sophisticated, creamy and lush in style, with a bite of fresh acidity. With a floral edge and stony mineral undertone to the texture, it has structure and lasting grace.

name: TruthTeller 2019 Frolic Viognier (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas pop, with notes of cantaloupe, canned peach, fresh flower and freshly peeled orange rind. The palate is sleek in style, with plenty of floral flavors and citrus flavors along with tart acidity. It's an odd mixture of overripe and underripe fruit.

name: Troon 2019 Kubli Bench Amber White (Applegate Valley)
review: An orange wine, this was produced from a blend of three-quarters Riesling and one-quarter split between Vermentino and Viognier, all fermented on the skins. Scents of orange blossoms abound, and the flavors are exceptionally fresh and clean. The sharp acids emphasize flavors of citrus flesh and rind, and there are noticeable tannins, firming up the finish. Drink now and into 2022.

name: Trisaetum 2019 Estates Reserve Riesling (Willamette Valley)
review: Drawing upon fruit from all three estate vineyards, the reserve is full-bodied and roundly fruity, with a mix of stone and tropical fruits. Listed at 47 g/L residual sugar and just 9.5% alcohol, it's buoyed by natural acids and a touch of minerality in the finish.

name: Trisaetum 2019 Wichmann Dundee Estate Riesling (Dundee Hills)
review: The fruit/sugar balance is spot on here, with honeyed flavors of apple, peach and papaya. Finished at Auslese-style levels, it's just over 9% alcohol and 42 g/L residual sugar. Though lacking the depth and detail of the 2018, it's a splendid bottle for near-term enjoyment.

name: Trisaetum 2019 Ribbon Ridge Estate Riesling (Ribbon Ridge)
review: Firm and off-dry, this is richly fruity with a mix of apple, peach, guava and papaya. It's full-bodied and round, lightly honeyed and balanced throughout. It's finished at 45 g/L residual sugar.

name: Trisaetum 2019 Coast Range Estate Riesling (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: Off-dry and finished at 46 g/L residual sugar, this is significantly lower in alcohol than the 2018 version. It's a nifty balance between citrus and apple fruit, rock candy sweetness, and a hint of honey. Another couple of years of bottle age are recommended.

name: Trione 2019 River Road Ranch Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Picked before the Kincade fires hit the Alexander Valley, this white is grassy and herbal, with a hint of matchstick on the nose. It is stony and chalky on the palate, tasting of candied lemon peel and honeydew melon.

name: Tribute 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey County)
review: Rounded aromas of apple and ripe pear meet with peach, apple blossoms and a mix of kiwi and jicama on the dynamic nose of this bottling. The palate's ripe pear flavor is cut by wet cement and white pepper.

name: Tres Sabores 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma Mountain)
review: Grass, pea shoot and lemongrass combine around sizzling acidity and a lasting sense of freshness in this impressive white that has a stainless-steel influence for maximum fruitiness and verve. A creamy lushness forms on the finish, adding length and breadth.

name: Trentadue 2019 OPR Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Fragrant, fresh herb aromas and a good, crisp texture give an assertive appeal to this medium-bodied well-balanced unoaked wine. It shows tons of varietal character in the scents of rosemary, parsley and thyme, and the bright citrus flavors.

name: Tree Fort 2019 Pinot Grigio (Paso Robles)
review: Light herbs and dried grass show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is somewhat flat in texture but delivers acceptable flavors of Granny Smith apples and more dried grass.

name: Tree Fort 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey)
review: Clean aromas of green grass, wet stone, kelp and light nectarine show on the nose of this bottling. It's simple yet crisp on the palate, where green- and yellow-apple flavors strike quickly then fade away.

name: Tree Fort 2019 Chardonnay (Monterey)
review: This is a very successful wine at this price. Delicate aromas of daffodil, lychee and wet stone lead into a focused palate that is crisp with apple and Asian pear.

name: TP Reserve 2019 Chardonnay (Napa Valley)
review: In an attempt to make light of the hoarding tendencies of the pandemic, with the motto “we're all in this sh*t together,” this white is nonetheless yummy and fairly priced. Creamy in anise and pear, it has an opulent texture and moderate oak, made in a balanced, fruity style.

name: Toad Hollow 2019 Francine's Selection Unoaked Chardonnay (Mendocino County)
review: This uncomplicated, medium-bodied wine offers straightforward crisp apple flavors and a hint of butter. Good acidity keeps it refreshing on the palate.

name: Time & Direction 2019 Fretboard Corfu Crossing Vineyard Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Pear and peach aromas leap up out the glass, along with notes of flower, herb and melon. There's a sleekness and brightness to the white-peach flavors that ups the interest for this often over-weighted and oily variety.

name: Tilth 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Floral and fleshy, this white wine is appealing in exotic layers of lychee, green apple and peach, seemingly conflicting flavors that instead complement and overlap. Softly textured, it is nicely balanced in acidity.

name: Three Thieves 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: This ripe and mouthfilling wine shows a soft fruitiness, a nicely full body and laid-back pear flavors.

name: The Vice 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Yountville)
review: This white is floral and fleshy, with steely, metallic streaks of pineapple, melon and peach. It finishes in an herbaceous tone.

name: The Great Oregon Wine Company 2019 Yamhill Valley Vineyard Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: This deftly mixes fermentation styles, half in stainless and half in neutral oak, which helps to smooth out the youthful edges. It's a racy wine with crisp apple and a dash of white pepper, underscored with the vineyard's natural minerality.

name: The Four Graces 2019 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: Tart yet still rather soft on the palate, this brings a mix of light green herbs, apples, lemons and limes. The overall weight is light and tangy, the balance spot on. It's a most refreshing style to enjoy through 2022.

name: Textbook 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Smoothly integrated and soft on the palate, this well-made white is light in style and brimming in fruity peach and apricot. It has a touch of grass and dried herb on the finish that contrast against the fruit.

name: Terra d'Oro 2019 Pinot Grigio (Clarksburg)
review: Lively green-apple aromas meet heavier flavors and a thick texture in this full-bodied wine.

name: Terra d'Oro 2019 Chenin Blanc-Viognier (Clarksburg)
review: This light-bodied wine tastes crisp and is absolutely refreshing. It holds back from complete ripeness, and the slightly restrained green apple and white peach flavors are mouthwatering.

name: Talley 2019 Riesling (Edna Valley)
review: Tight aromas of lime, grapefruit and rainy cement show on the nose of this brisk bottling. There's a flash of sweetness on the palate but it tightens quickly around nectarine, peach and seared orange flavors, extending into a very long and textural finish.

name: Table Eleven 2019 Quail Valley Vineyard Viognier (Los Olivos District)
review: Crisp and fresh on the nose, this bottling begins with light honeysuckle and plumeria aromas that are held in place by a citrusy edge of Meyer lemon peel. That tension carries through the sip as well, where Anjou and Asian pear-flesh flavors are framed by a chalky backbone and a zesty Key lime acidity.

name: Tablas Creek 2019 Vermentino (Adelaida District)
review: Proprietor Jason Haas continually reveals the magic potential of this grape for a warmer weather region like Paso Robles. In this vintage, crisp and light aromas of pear, lemon and lily lead into a fascinating palate of nectarine, honeysuckle and ripe melon, all wrapped in an immensely chalky structure.

name: Syncline 2019 Boushey Vineyard Grenache Blanc (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas intrigue, with notes of orange twist, fig and citrus. The palate is broad down the middle, lighter along the edges, fleshing out over time. Lively acidity keeps it all in focus.

name: Syncline 2019 Boushey Vineyard Picpoul (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas offer alluring notes of lemon zest, white flower and crushed rock. The medium bodied palate brings a zing of tart, puckering, lemony acidity that is the hallmark of this lip-stinging grape. It's an acid lovers light. Pair it with shrimp ceviche with lime and jalapeño.

name: Syncline 2019 Bloxom Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Intriguing aromas of herb, slate, pear and lentil are followed by broad, textured feeling flavors that linger on the finish. It shows pleasing complexities along with the vibrant acidity of the vintage.

name: Swanson 2019 Pinot Grigio (San Benito)
review: Tangy aromas of orange and lime rind meet with warm hints of freshly cut pear on the likable nose of this bottling. The palate is rounded with honeydew and white-melon flavors, made more complex by wet rock elements. It is incredibly easy to drink.

name: Sutro 2019 Warnecke Ranch Sauvignon Blanc (Alexander Valley)
review: Tank fermented and aged in half stainless steel and half neutral oak, this robust, intensely fruity white is robust in green apple, slate and stone. Complex, it offers lasting acidity that lingers and invites one in for more.

name: Sunstone 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Green-apple-peel, wet stone and light peach aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The palate hits with grassy flavors and then fades a bit quickly, without much texture.

name: Succession 2019 Our Evolution White (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Sémillon makes up 61% of this wine, with the rest Sauvignon Blanc (21%) and Viognier. Aromas of ripe peach, fig and flower are followed by medium-bodied flavors. It displays the tartness of the vintage. Stone fruit flavors linger on the finish

name: Succession 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: With fruit coming from Double D Vineyards just outside of the Lake Chelan appellation, the aromas are light and hard to tease out, with whiffs of herb. The palate is more giving, with medium-bodied, almost thick-feeling lemony flavors. It's hard to see exactly where it's all going, but there is promise.

name: Succession 2019 Antoine Creek Vineyard Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit for this wine comes from Antoine Creek Vineyards in the Lake Chelan appellation. Aromas of canned peach lead to fruit flavors that paint around the edges. It has a pleasing tartness.

name: Succession 2019 Riverview Vineyard Chenin Blanc (Wahluke Slope)
review: Chenin Blanc is increasingly rare in the state, with this example coming from a warmer appellation. You'd never know it on the wine. The aromas are light and appealing, with notes of corn husk, mineral and whole lemon. The palate is medium-bodied, with tart acidity. It's a lovely example of the variety.

name: Structure 2019 Oculus Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Expressive aromas of apricot, flower and jasmine are followed by full-bodied, plentiful stone-fruit flavors. It's weighty, but with ample acidity to keep it tied together.

name: Storm 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Very stony aromas of wet cement lead into riper tones of Asian pear and guava on the nose of this bottling. The palate is very dry and taut, delivering grassy flavors of green pineapple, lemongrass and kaffir lime leaf.

name: Stolo 2019 Gewurztraminer (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Crisp and yet floral aromas of honeysuckle, elderflower and plumeria meet with scratched citrus peel on the dynamic nose of this bottling. It's extremely lively on the palate, offering grapefruit-peel, Asian pear and wet cement flavors, staying brisk and mineral-driven deep into the finish.

name: Stolo 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Light and bright aromas of green flower buds, green pear, wet cement and a hint of kelpy seawater show on the nose of this bottling. The palate verges from crisp Alpine creek water tones to more oceanic flavors, proving relatively light in both flavor and texture.

name: Stewart 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Whole-cluster pressed grapes are fermented and aged in stainless steel and neutral oak, leading to tangy citrus- and peach-flavored goodness on the palate, with an undercurrent of vanilla bean. Fleshy and lush, the steely acidity balances the richness without dampening it completely, leading to a satisfying, lengthy finish.

name: Standing Stone 2019 Riesling (Seneca Lake)
review: Under the leadership of the Hermann J. Wiemer team, this entry-level, off-dry Riesling is an outstanding success. A boisterous, perfumed nose yields aromas of citrus blossoms, pomelo, lemon oil, tangerine and fresh yellow apple. The juicy palate gives way to ripples of juicy pear, apple and pineapple flavors, lifted by a persistent vein of orange-laden acidity. It's impeccably balanced and incredibly concentrated and fresh, with a gentle stoniness riding underneath it all. While enjoyable now, it has the structure and fruit to hold well through 2030.

name: St. Supéry 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Made in a rich, rounded style, this has lovely layers of peach, apricot and lemon chiffon, leading to a soft, supple and seamless finish.

name: St. Huberts The Stag 2019 The Stag Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)
review: Tight aromas of lime pith, sweetgrass, baked apple and rainy cement show on the nose of this bottling. It's more viscous than expected on the palate, with flavors of orange juice and roasted pear that border on sweet.

name: St. Francis 2019 Dry Stack Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Bennett Valley)
review: Rounded and creamy, with ample acidity to balance out the palate, this vineyard-designate is made in small amounts from a great site. Melon, apricot and a hint of lemongrass ride well together.

name: St. Francis 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: With a sweetness of oak, this approachable white is rounded and rich, with a creamy texture. Pear, pineapple and vanilla flavors add to the fleshy style and approach.

name: Spottswoode 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa-Sonoma)
review: The producer sources from a handful of exceptional sites here, including Hyde in Carneros and Farina on Sonoma Mountain, as well as its own St. Helena estate. Juicy and lengthy with power and substance, it is fresh and well integrated, blossoming in bright lemon peel, peach and guava.

name: Spellbound 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: This pleasant, easygoing wine blends vanilla, butterscotch and ripe apple flavors on a medium body. It is smooth and soft in texture.

name: Sparkman 2019 As You Wish Old Vines Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is 100% varietal, with all of the fruit coming from Block 23 at Gamache Vineyard in the White Bluffs. With the wine having been aged on lees in stainless steel, second fill and neutral oak, the aromas are quiet, with notes of almond, tropical fruit and lemon. Full-bodied, rich pineapple and kiwi flavors follow. It's rich but manages to maintain its balance. A warm finish caps it off.

name: Sparkman 2019 Pearl Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Almost two-thirds of the fruit for this wine comes from Oasis Vineyard. The aromas bring notes of grass, Meyer lemon, kiwi, pineapple and herb. The palate is full-bodied and rich, chock-full of tropical fruit flavors that lead to a warm finish. It's a compelling, warm-site offering of this variety, with plenty of acidity backing it up.

name: Sonoma Collection 2019 Wines for Lunch Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma Valley)
review: Steely and high-toned in citrus-driven flavors of lime and grapefruit, this wine is light bodied and simple in approach.

name: Soléna 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Soléna kills it with this grape, and this is the best vintage yet. Classy wine styling is here on full display, with ripe and fleshy fruit flavors of pear and melon, alongside splendid details of cinnamon and toast. It's taken decades, but Oregon's best examples of Pinot Gris belong with the nation's top-tier white wines from any grape.

name: Sling & Stone 2019 Silacci Vineyard Chardonnay (Monterey)
review: This stainless-steel expression starts with loads of honeysuckle, jasmine and ripe apple on the nose. The palate is framed by tantalizing acidity and a seashell-like minerality, which cuts through the richer apple and pear tones.

name: Sleight of Hand 2019 The Magician Evergreen Vineyard Riesling (Ancient Lakes)
review: Aromas of mineral, dried rose petal, citrus and dried herb lead to just off dry Nestea flavors. There are lots of good things happening but the components aren't all entirely in harmony.

name: Sketchbook 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino County)
review: Tangy and appetizing, this light- to medium-bodied wine is alive with grapefruit and fresh herb flavors and feels nicely crisp and firm in texture.

name: Sin Banderas 2019 Riesling (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas are quite subdued for the variety, with notes of lime and herb. The dry palate brings plenty of citrus and Nestea iced tea flavors. It needs some time to be fully appreciated.

name: Silver Spur 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: Classic varietal aromas of snap peas and citrus lead to vibrant, fruity and herbaceous flavors that are absolutely fresh and appealing. There is just enough acidity to deftly balance the fruitiness.

name: Sightglass 2019 Artz Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Red Mountain)
review: Bright aromas of citrus, herb and melon bring direct appeal. The palate is creamy in feel, packed with tart citrus, kiwi and herb flavors backed by mouthwatering acidity. A sense of purity only heightens the interest. It's a standout with a big-time yum factor.

name: Sightglass 2019 Conner Lee Vineyard Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This has seen no oak. The aromas bring a sense of purity, with notes of melon, lees and apple that continues on the palate. A warm finish follows. It's an engaging, stainless steel example of this variety, not seeming to have fully settled in.

name: Shooting Star 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: This attractive and well-balanced wine blends snappy fruit flavors with savory, figgy and minerally accents that give it depth and interest. Full body and a crisp texture add to its appeal.

name: Shannon Ridge 2019 Urgency Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: This superflavorful wine is definitely an extrovert. It practically shouts out fresh herb and veggie aromas followed by tangy citrus flavors. It feels zingy and nicely acidic on the palate and has enough concentration for a lingering finish.

name: Shannon Ridge 2019 Vigilance Sauvignon Blanc (Red Hills)
review: Full bodied and packed with fruit and mineral flavors, this is a pretty big, very smooth wine that soothes the palate and lingers on the finish. Rich pear, fig and stony notes make it interesting to sip.

name: Shannon Ridge 2019 High Valley Sauvignon Blanc (High Valley)
review: This nicely polished and full-bodied wine tastes ripe but has lively acidity to keep it balanced. Tangy citrus notes blend with subtle fig and minerals.

name: Seven Hills Winery 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: There's no mistaking the variety, with its aromas of kiwi, grass and white grapefruit. Textured, medium-bodied flavors follow. A fine sense of acidity stitches it all up tightly.

name: Seghesio 2019 Keyhole Ranch Vineyard Vermentino (Russian River Valley)
review: Heady, floral and lemony fresh, this light-bodied wine is fleshy, soft and appealing. More commonly found in Italy and France, here the grapes offer lively character and an inviting taste of peach.

name: Section Wines 2019 Tierra Alta Vineyards Arneis (Ballard Canyon)
review: Winemaker Marco Lucchesi is originally from Pisa, Italy, so it makes sense that he's exploring this Italian grape, relatively new to California. Aromas of pink lemonade, surf wax and coconut show on the nose, while the palate is broad with peach rind, apricot and an underlying waxy texture and flavor.

name: Section Wines 2019 Tierra Alta Vineyards Viognier (Ballard Canyon)
review: Broad and bright aromas of plumeria and ripe apple kick off the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate is more tightly wound and focused on a lime-zest flavor, with a more rounded sense of bubblegum lingering in the background.

name: Section Wines 2019 Vermentino (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Tightly wound on the nose, this bottling of a very promising grape for the entire Central Coast shows aromas of wet stone, lemon peel, light honeysuckle and a gunmetal-like minerality. The texture is very stony on the palate, where subtle flavors of white flower and passion fruit emerge.

name: Second Growth 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This delicious young wine is a close match to the company's less expensive ‘Greetings' version. Ripe pear fruit, along with some crisp apple, highlights a clean and fresh-tasting style, while the supporting acids lend a squeeze of lemon. Drink over the next couple of years.

name: SeatPocket 2019 Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: Light aromas of lime blossom, jasmine and orange peel are pleasant if simple on the nose of this bottling. It's very easy on the palate with lemon-brioche and toasty oak flavors.

name: Sean Minor 2019 Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: Poached pear, lime pith and a waft of oak smoke show on the nose of this bottling. There are tropical elements on the sip of tuberose, guava and ripe pineapple, proving rich but in balance.

name: Sean Minor 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: This earthy, light-bodied wine boasts wet rock and matchstick aromas, tangy veggie flavors and a fairly smooth mouthfeel.

name: SeaGlass 2019 Pinot Grigio (Central Coast)
review: Aromas of guava and honeydew carry the fresh nose of this widely available bottling. There is a pleasing grip to the sip, where guava peel, Asian pear and cherimoya flavors meet with a hint of wet stone.

name: SeaGlass 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Zesty aromas of Key lime, lemongrass, apple blossom and a slight hint of jalapeño show on the nose of this bottling. It's more ripe on the palate, with pear, apple and melon flavors, yet sansho pepper spice keeps the ripeness in check.

name: Scotto Family Cellars 2019 Silvaspoons Vineyard Verdelho (Alta Mesa)
review: This full-bodied wine shows savory, dried grass aromas and nutty flavors on a rich texture. It will make an interesting detour from the usual Chardonnay.

name: Scotto Family Cellars 2019 Mohr-Fry Ranch Sauvignon Blanc (Lodi)
review: Charming and relatively light bodied, this mineral- and dried herb-driven wine blends citrus and thyme flavors in an appetizing way. It's easy and light for sipping by itself and with lunch or appetizers.

name: Schug 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma Coast)
review: Crisp, lively and textured, this white delivers balanced flavors of white peach and tangerine, offering nice length, breadth and underlying acidity.

name: Scheid 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey)
review: Aromas of green pear, lime peel and lawn clippings show on the zesty nose of this bottling. There's a fresh and easy energy to the palate, where green apple peel, red apple and guava flavors slide into the finish.

name: Savage Grace 2019 Red Willow Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas fascinate, with notes of sweet herb, wet slate and kelp. The palate is sleek but flavorful, with a strong sense of acidity. Pair it with briny oysters for a transcendent experience.

name: Savage Grace 2019 Blanc Franc Red Willow Vineyard Cabernet Franc (Yakima Valley)
review: A one-of-a-kind wine in the state, aromas of whole green pepper, herb and citrus are followed by tart, acid driven green pepper and herb flavors. It's downright lipsmacking.

name: Sarver 2019 Elhanan Vineyard Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Partly barrel-fermented in neutral oak, this straight-ahead, estate-grown wine has clean and tart flavors, mostly of citrus skin and green apples. It's best served lightly chilled, and should be consumed over the next year or two.

name: Sand Point 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: Lively and well-concentrated, too, this medium-bodied wine has good acidity to make it appetizing and plenty of tasty green-apple, white-peach and apricot flavors to savor.

name: San Simeon 2019 Stefano Vineyard Viognier (Paso Robles)
review: Light aromas of ripe peach, orange and honeysuckle kick off this well-honed Viognier. There is tactile energy and grippy texture to the sip, where broad flavors of orange cream, jasmine and more peach ride bright acidity into the finish.

name: San Simeon 2019 Estate Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Ripe peach, guava, wet stone and a hint of cut grass show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is clean and familiar, showing more of the same flavors as the nose, with an easygoing texture.

name: San Rucci 2019 Benvenuta White (California)
review: This is a big, bold and even somewhat tannic wine that brings out strong citrus, almond and mineral flavors. A grippy texture and assertive acidity make it dry in mouthfeel.

name: Salvestrin 2019 Crystal Springs Sauvignon Blanc (St. Helena)
review: Tangy and dry, this white spent five months in neutral oak and stainless steel, preserving a stony texture that runs lean. Lime, melon and lemon-peel flavors permeate through a lengthy palate.

name: Saint K 2019 Cellular White (California)
review: Baked apple and tart pome-fruit aromas start the nose on this atypical blend of 34% Chardonnay, 33% Chenin Blanc and 33% Malvasia Bianca. Dried citrus, seared apples and light cashew flavors play on the intriguing if slightly confusing palate.

name: Saint K 2019 Flower Bomb Malvasia Bianca (Paso Robles Highlands District)
review: Strong aromas of orange blossom and apple blossom are extremely floral on the nose, which is typical for this variety. There's a pleasant sizzle to the sip, where lime-blossom, green-plum, mango and papaya flavors make for a dynamic sip.

name: Ryder Estate 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Central Coast)
review: This reliable white wine combines sharp tones of grass, rainy cement and lime peel with rounder yellow apple on the nose. The palate pulls a similar trick, offering juicy pear with wet-gravel minerality and apple-rind tartness.

name: Ryan Patrick 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are reserved, with notes of melon, herb, grapefruit and tropical fruit. The palate is light and lively, with a zing of acidity that ups the interest of the lemony flavors.

name: Rutherford Ranch 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Grassy, herbal and opulent on the palate, this is a smooth, fruity expression of melon and peach that works on every level. Light, crisp acidity maintains freshness and balance.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2019 Grenache Blanc (Washington)
review: This wine hasn't fully settled in on first pour, but once it does it shows aromas of wet stone, talc and lemon are followed by a sleek, puckering palate with lemony acidity. The acid is outrageous.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2019 Northern White (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: This wine is about two-thirds Marsanne and one third Roussanne. The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of pear, nectarine, sea shell, fig and flower. The palate is sleek, tart and focused, with racy, mouthwatering acidity and a pleasing depth of flavor.

name: Rôtie Cellars 2019 Southern White (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Viognier (65%) is on the lead on this wine, followed by Roussanne (20%) and Marsanne. The aromas pull you into the glass, with notes of lemon balm, wet rock and white flower. The flavors bring plenty of intensity and texture, backed by live wire acidity. In keeping with the vintage and producer, it's an impressively restrained offering of these varieties.

name: Rotation Vineyards 2019 Chardonnay (Lodi)
review: Earthy aromas and milky, lemony flavors don't quite come together in this medium-bodied wine.

name: Rombauer 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa County-Sonoma County)
review: Fresh and focused in a steely frame, this wine shows plenty of fruit intensity in pineapple, melon, lemon and grapefruit tones. The neutral oak influence is subdued yet adds length.

name: Rodney Strong 2019 Charlotte's Home Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: This crowd-pleasing white boasts fruit-forward flavors of green apple, baked pineapple and passion fruit. With just a hint of oak, it showcases the variety well, offering nuanced ripeness and well-integrated acidity.

name: Robert Hall 2019 Cavern Select Terrace Vineyard Fumé Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Ripe pear, wet stone and strong gardenia aromas show on the nose of this bottling. There's a crisp lemon-peel flavor on the midpalate as well as riper tones of red pear and a light brush of mango.

name: Robert Hall 2019 Cavern Select Vermentino (El Pomar District)
review: Broad, ripe aromas of pineapple, yellow pear and apple peel show on the nose of this bottling. There is a stony streak to the palate, where tightly wound flavors of ash and pome fruit are quite engaging.

name: Robert Biale 2019 Clementina White (Oak Knoll District)
review: The Zinfandel specialist offers a brilliant white wine from its estate here, 100% Greco—an Italian grape variety not officially recognized in the United States, thus not listed on the label. A tribute to the matriarch of the Biale family, it is crisp and sleek in slate, grippy tangerine and pomelo.

name: Riley's Rows 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (North Coast)
review: This full-bodied wine feels weighty and tastes quite ripe and warm. Its subdued apple and lemon flavors are tinged with vanilla.

name: Ridge 2019 Adelaida Vineyard Grenache Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Very fresh and pure aromas of honeysuckle, coconut cream and a light kiss of wood show on the nose of this unique bottling, which includes 18% Picpoul and 5% Roussanne. The wine is expertly balanced on the palate, which is smooth with honeydew yet flecked with flashes of lime peel.

name: Revelshine 2019 White (Russian River Valley)
review: Presented in a 500 ml aluminum bottle that's refillable and recyclable, vintner Jake Bilbro combines 95% Chardonnay and 5% Grenache Blanc into a delicious, gulpable package of bright stone fruit and pineapple, with plenty of underlying acidity to keep it fresh and crisp.

name: Replica 2019 Knockoff Chardonnay (California)
review: Ripe and complex, this full-bodied wine is well packed with juicy peach and sunny pineapple flavors complemented by a mouthfilling texture and a lingering, honey-like finish.

name: Refugio Ranch 2019 Malvasia Bianca (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Tight aromas of pomelo pith, honeydew and orange blossom make a tonic-like display on the nose of this bottling. There's a ton of sizzle to the lively sip, where energetic flavors of orange blossom and quinine make for an intriguing quaff.

name: Read Holland 2019 Wiley Vineyards Riesling (Anderson Valley)
review: This well-rounded, juicy and fresh-tasting wine carries vivid peach and apricot flavors on a surprisingly light body. It tastes sunny, dry, and nicely concentrated, and will be easy to enjoy.

name: Raywood 2019 Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: This offshoot of Castoro Cellars in Paso Robles makes a solid lineup of affordable wines. This is a fresh style of Chardonnay, offering clean melon-rind, white-peach and lime aromas. The palate is bright and tight, with lime peel, honeydew and a light touch of guava.

name: Rascal 2019 Pinot Gris (Oregon)
review: Done in a tart, sharp-toned style, there's little varietal specificity here. It falls into a generic white wine category, peppery and finishing with some bitter herbs.

name: Ransom 2019 Albariño (Willamette Valley)
review: Despite its startlingly low finished alcohol, this is quite dry and extremely tart. The flavors indicate that the grapes were picked at very low brix, and the citrus fruit mix of lemon, lime and grapefruit is searingly sour. Try this with raw oysters and you may find a good match.

name: Ram's Gate 2019 Estate Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Carneros)
review: A majority stainless-steel fermented, with a hint of new French oak, this white is floral with flavors of apricot, green apple and lime. High-toned acidity boosts a balanced, light style that finishes in a touch of oyster shell.

name: Rabble 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Soft aromas of green-pear skin, apple flesh and a light touch of cut grass are simple and easy on the nose of this bottling. There is a tightness to the palate, where lime, green apple and grass elements converge.

name: Rabble 2019 Murmur Vineyard Pinot Gris (Santa Barbara County)
review: Light pear and grapefruit-peel aromas are clean and bright on the nose of this bottling. The palate is simple while pleasant, offering pear and tart apple flavors.

name: Provenance Vineyards 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Rutherford)
review: Aged predominantly in stainless steel, this includes tiny percentages of Sémillon and Muscat. Melon, metal and vanilla flavors accent the creamy opulence of a big and robustly ripe style, finishing in a snap of anise.

name: Prospice 2019 Gamache Vineyard Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Coming from perhaps the state's premier Viognier site, aromas of melon rind, citrus, flower, peach and pear lead to sleek, lighter styled stone fruit flavors that still bring a sense of intensity. The acid balance is impressive for the variety. It carries on the finish.

name: Primarius 2019 Pinot Gris (Oregon)
review: The aromas of whole pear, wet stone and apple are followed by light bright pear flavors. It's a straight-down-the-fairway offering of this variety, with acid driving it.

name: Priest Ranch 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Light, bright and complex in structure and texture, this wine offers ample weight and length, the fruit nuanced and fresh in lemon, apple and fig.

name: Portlandia 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This wine is bursting with scents and flavors of pear and grapefruit. Incredibly refreshing, it's textured and leesy, with appealing minerality and a long, clean, pour-me-more finish.

name: Pope Valley Winery 2019 1897 Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Fresh and fruity, this white tastes of racy acidity, tangy melon and lemon candy. Full bodied and lush once it settles in, it is an opulent expression of the grape.

name: Plein Air 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Amador County)
review: Light and fresh yet nicely concentrated too, this medium-bodied wine combines citrus and fresh herb aromas with focused and deep lemon and melon flavors. It comes off as complete and well balanced, refreshing and satisfying.

name: Piocho 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)
review: A bit shy on the nose at first, this bottling, which includes 15% Sémillon, opens up to show aromas of pomelo pith, wet stone and a light pear flesh. The texture is light but engaging on the palate, where apple, rounded kiwi and tighter lemon-pith flavors converge.

name: Pine Ridge 2019 Chenin Blanc-Viognier (California)
review: This medium-bodied wine tastes quite ripe and fruity and feels rich on the palate. Banana, melon and kiwi flavors vie for attention, and they're nicely wrapped in a smooth texture.

name: Pike Road 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This is a light, leesy and fresh style, keeping it simple and straightforward, all to good effect. A mix of citrus fruit flavors, notably grapefruit and lime, take center stage. It's crystal clear, clean and zesty, just a perfectly quaffable white wine.

name: Picayune 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma-Napa)
review: The small producer's 10th vintage of this wine, this lively white spends time in both stainless steel and neutral French oak. Floral and herbal in legume, it evolves to reveal fleshy and fruity flavors of apricot and peach.

name: Peltier 2019 Estate Grown Vermentino (Lodi)
review: This lean and tangy wine offers plenty of crisp apple flavors, good acidity and light body.

name: Peju 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This is a fresh and unfussy white wine, lively in acidity and lengthy in grapefruit, fig and dried herb. It stays light and bright throughout, offering balanced weight and ripeness.

name: Pedroncelli 2019 East Side Vineyards Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)
review: With a Musqué-like perfume, this light white shows aromas of jasmine and lifted flavors of Meyer lemon, fresh peach and mango. The acidity is focused and fresh, underscoring the wine's overall crispness.

name: Pearl and Stone 2019 Old Rickety Grenache Blanc (Yakima Valley)
review: This shows amazing concentration and depth for a wine with such a low level of alcohol. It's all Boushey vineyard fruit, superfresh, with juicy apple, lemon, grapefruit and pineapple flavors. It's a big enough wine to come off the bench and pinch hit for your favorite unoaked Chardonnay.

name: Paul Dolan 2019 Made with Organic Grapes Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino County)
review: Fresh herb flavors light up this well-balanced, concentrated and distinctive wine. Basil and parsley aromas are followed by lemongrass flavors that carry a lot of energy through the tangy finish.

name: Patton Valley 2019 Chenin Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: Bone-dry despite the low alcohol, this tangy, delicious wine brings bright grapefruit, waxy lemon, apple and white peach goodness into focus. Almost unknown in Oregon, the potential here is limitless for this under-the-radar variety. Estate-grown and barrel-fermented with native yeast, this is a sappy, sexy and beautifully balanced wine.

name: Parducci 2019 Small Lot Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino County)
review: Very crisp and lively, this light-bodied wine packs in plenty of vivid lemon, lime and mineral flavors. Good acidity keeps it dancing on the palate and feeling tingly on the finish.

name: Paradise Ridge 2019 Grandview Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Grassy and herbal, this resplendent white is creamy on the palate in a refined richness of ripe peach with a trail of vanilla. Made in a lush style, it retains plenty of underlying acidity to remain fresh and focused in the glass.

name: Paradise Ridge 2019 Vineyard Select Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Light and bright with a creamy underlying palate of smooth richness, this impressive white shows a wealth of passion fruit and guava flavor. It's ready to be enjoyed at the table or as an apéritif.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2019 Maison Mason Vineyards Ugni Blanc (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Always high in the running for best Paso Robles white wine each vintage, this bottling does anything but disappoint. Bright and fresh aromas of melon, mango and jasmine are quite floral, while the palate zips across the tongue with glee, sprinkling honeysuckle, Key lime pith and even delicate bubblegum flavors into the finish.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2019 Glenrose Vineyard Picpoul Blanc (Adelaida District)
review: Tightly wound aromas of tangerine pith, talc and struck limestone show on the nose of this bottling. The wine is very stony on the palate, with uplifting flavors of honeysuckle, jasmine and brisk lemon.

name: Paix Sur Terre 2019 Paper Street Vineyard Clairette Blanche (Paso Robles Willow Creek District)
review: Fresh nectarine, frozen pineapple and faint coconut aromas are reminiscent of a piña colada on the nose of this bottling. Those tropical elements carry to the sip, where a fresh acidity washes through the pineapple and ripe apple-flesh flavors.

name: Pacific Rim 2019 Dry Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Generous aromas of honeysuckle, pear, dried rose petal and orange are followed by mandarin orange flavors and a floral finish. A dried rose note is a bit of distracting on the finish.

name: Pacific Rim 2019 J Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Exuberant aromas of orange rind, ginger and freshly peeled satsuma are followed by medium-sweet flavors and vibrant acidity. Rose petal notes linger on the finish.

name: Pacific Rim 2019 Sweet Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Bright aromas of tangerine peel, flower, dried rose petal and citrus are followed by sweet, full feeling citrus flavors and plenty of acidity. There's a lovely sense of balance to it all. Dried rose petal notes linger on the finish.

name: Ovum 2019 Big Salt White (Oregon)
review: As production ramps up, quality remains consistently high in this proprietary blend of cofermented Muscat, Riesling and Gewürztraminer. Spicy, penetrating aromas of pear blossoms, ginger and kumquat lead into a lively palate with citrus skin flavor, a dash of sea salt and drying minerality. It's a well-balanced, unique white wine with splendid food pairing options.

name: One Hope 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: This smooth and spicy medium-bodied wine is frankly buttery and oaky, but incorporates the baking-spice and sawn wood flavors nicely, with ripe pear and peach notes.

name: Old Soul 2019 Estate Grown Chardonnay (Lodi)
review: A steely, dusty aroma starts this full-bodied wine off, then rich, ripe pear and butterscotch flavors fill the palate, giving it plenty to appreciate.

name: Okapi 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Floral, earthy and citrus-driven, this tiny production is made by Ted Osborne. White-flower aromas are followed by a midpalate of grapefruit and tangerine, the flavors taking on a candied quality on the finish.

name: Odonata 2019 Marins Vineyard Viognier (San Antonio Valley)
review: From the warm southern reaches of Monterey County, this bottling begins with aromas of peach, cashew cream and a pinch of sea salt. There are lean lines of peach and melon on the sip, where a penetrating acidity and rocky tension keep the wine moving across the palate.

name: Odonata 2019 Kapoor Vineyard Chardonnay (Santa Cruz Mountains)
review: Subtle aromas of citrus and muddled stone fruit lay into brisk honeysuckle and chalk on the nose of this wine. There is a great zip to the sip, where a river-stone minerality pairs with an underlying guava flavor.

name: Oberon 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: With nearly 25% Sauvignon Musque, this white is earthy and concentrated in flavors of legume and Meyer lemon. Grapefruit and stone fruit follow on the steely palate, finishing in a swirl of lemongrass and vanilla.

name: Oakstone 2019 Lemley Ranch Pinot Grigio (Fair Play)
review: Quite rich and concentrated, this ripe and peachy-tasting wine offers a mouthfilling full body and ample fruit flavors.

name: Oak Grove 2019 Family Reserve Viognier (California)
review: This medium-bodied wine is nicely concentrated and fruity, but also brings subtle butter and mineral flavors to the table. A creamy texture adds to its depth and complexity.

name: Oak Grove 2019 Family Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: This is a lively, nervy wine that nicely matches bright citrus flavors with tasty mineral and white-pepper notes. Subtlety and complexity in a light-bodied package make it something special.

name: Oak Farm 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Lodi)
review: Fresh and fruity, accented by dried herbs, this medium-bodied wine is straightforward and easy to sip.

name: NSO By Dusty Nabor 2019 Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: It's quite stunning that this brand can make wines from all-star appellations at this price point. In this bottling, aromas of plumeria, ripe pear and wet stone lead into a palate of more ripe pome fruit and tropical-flower flavors that are cut by lime zest.

name: Novelty Hill 2019 Stillwater Creek Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Reticent aromas of apple, herb, and spice are followed by lightly creamy flavors backed with edgy acidity. It still seems in its shell but has good stuffing.

name: Novelty Hill 2019 Stillwater Creek Vineyard Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are bright, with notes of flower, pear, peach and honeysuckle. Ripe stone fruit flavors follow, with the acidity of the vintage keeping it all in check.

name: Novelty Hill 2019 Stillwater Creek Vineyard Roussanne (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Lemon balm, spice, herb and nori aromas lead to a palate that brings a pleasing amount of acidity. It's equal parts intrigue and delivery. Best after 2021.

name: Nola Grace 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Rich and round, this satisfying, medium-bodied wine offers hints of vanilla and butter alongside a honeydew melon flavor.

name: Noble Vines 2019 242 Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey)
review: Crisp aromas of green apple skin, cut grass and white flowers show on the nose of this bottling, the latest in this numbered series of affordable wines. It's fresh on the palate in flavors of lime peel, fresh grass and peach, framed by a tense minerality that holds the ripe part in check.

name: Noble Vines 2019 152 Pinot Grigio (Monterey County)
review: Candied green apple, honeydew and hints of jasmine show on the nose of this bottling. The palate offers green touches of cut lawn and Granny Smith apple, but there is also a riper hint of red apple.

name: Niner 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Edna Valley)
review: Grassy aromas of cut lawn, lime peel and light cement show on the nose of this bottling. It's very lean and grassy on the palate as well, with touches of lime peel and a smooth texture.

name: Niner 2019 Albariño (Edna Valley)
review: Broad peach, nectarine and melon aromas gain compelling complexity with brushes of wet slate and wild spearmint on the nose of this bottling. The palate is very zesty and fresh, offering pear flesh, struck stone and white-pepper flavors.

name: Nichelini Family Winery 2019 Roman Press White (Napa Valley)
review: Combining 70% Muscadelle and 30% Sauvignon Blanc, without the addition of any oak, this white is light, floral and fruity, with a simple approachability.

name: Nichelini Family Winery 2019 Caterina Reserve Semillon (Napa Valley)
review: Opening with a buttery nose and touch of matchstick, this bright white possesses an underlying richness and a lush mouthfeel. Lively and floral, it tastes of lemon cake and apple.

name: Nichelini Family Winery 2019 Old Vine Muscadelle (Napa Valley)
review: There's not much of this Bordelaise white grape in the Napa Valley and so this is a rarity. High toned in citrus, lime and grapefruit, it is crisp and straightforward on the palate, unfettered by oak.

name: Neal Family 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This is a creamy, rounded and approachable white made from organically grown grapes that traffics in a lightness of honeydew melon and herb.

name: Navarro 2019 Pinot Blanc (Mendocino)
review: Smooth, rich and rounded in texture, this comfortably fruity wine is most notable for the luxurious mouthfeel but it also has a snap of green apple to back the full pear notes.

name: Navarro 2019 Cuvée 128 Sauvignon Blanc (Anderson Valley)
review: This light-bodied wine serves up simple and refreshing lemon and crisp apple flavors on a rather soft texture.

name: My Story 2019 Chardonnay (Paso Robles)
review: Aromas of lime blossom and Key lime pie meet with a chalky restraint on the nose of this bottling, whose label invites customers to write their names on the front. Flavors of wet stone and lime zest make for a delicate palate, where sharp acidity cuts into the finish.

name: Municipal Winemakers 2019 Bright White Riesling (Santa Barbara County)
review: Brisk and crisp aromas of lime peel and white flowers show on the nose of this bottle from Kick On Ranch, a longtime standard in the winery's lineup. It zips on the sip with grapefruit-driven flavors and a sharp, racy acidity.

name: Muirwood 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Arroyo Seco)
review: Golden-apple, lime, grapefruit and struck stone aromas show on the nose of this bottling. The flavors are quite bold and ripe on the palate, where pear-flesh and tropical mango flavors are cut by a lemony acidity.

name: Muirwood 2019 Chardonnay (Arroyo Seco)
review: Toasty aromas of sandalwood, vanilla bean, baked orange and pear-topped brioche show on the nose of this bottling. Oak is prominent at first on the sip, but then a racy lemon and citrus-blossom edge cuts through, leading into baked apple flavors on the finish.

name: Montoya 2019 Chardonnay (Monterey)
review: Broad aromas of buttered peach, roasted apple, sea salt and daffodil make for a pleasant entry to the nose of this bottling. The palate is ripe in flavors of poached apple and pear, with ample baking spice to keep it intriguing.

name: Montinore 2019 Almost Dry Riesling (Willamette Valley)
review: Sourced from old vines with an average age of 37 years, this outstanding wine will show best if it's not over-chilled. Fermented entirely in stainless steel with a German yeast strain, it's smooth and supple, with rounded flavors of apples, citrus, pears and kumquats. The lengthy finish adds a streak of breakfast tea.

name: MonksGate 2019 C12 Dry Riesling (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: Fermented in stainless and finished dry, the fruit is ripe enough to bring a sweet tang to the tongue. Flavors mix apple, melon and guava, with a dusting of cinnamon spice. In terms of texture and detail, it transcends most of its peers.

name: Moniz Family 2019 Grenache Blanc (California)
review: This is a spare and tangy wine that holds its apple-skin and flint flavors pretty tightly. Vivid acidity makes it a great palate cleanser.

name: Moniz Family 2019 St.Cin Albariño (California)
review: Flowers, apricot and flint are the key aromas and flavors of this crisp, appetizing, medium-bodied wine. It is bright and refreshing and should be versatile in food pairings.

name: Mirassou Sun 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: This light-bodied wine could pass for a Riesling, with its not unpleasant petrol aroma and lean peach and apricot flavors.

name: Milbrandt 2019 Pinot Grigio (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Light aromas of green and yellow apple and Oregon pear lead to a dry, flavorful palate etched with lively acidity that draws out the finish. There's a whole lot to like.

name: Milbrandt 2019 Family Grown Evergreen Vineyard Riesling (Ancient Lakes)
review: Coming from the state's premier Riesling vineyard, the wine offers aromas of green apple, grass and pear followed by off-dry-drinking Nestea lemon iced tea flavors that stretch out on the finish. The acidity and balance keep the interest high.

name: Midnight 2019 Chardonnay (Paso Robles)
review: Lime and grapefruit-pith aromas meet with taut nectarine, apricot and a hint of chalk on the nose of this bottling. The palate is more tropical in tone, with guava and pineapple flavors most apparent.

name: Midnight 2019 Andromeda Grenache Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Orange peel and melon aromas show on the nose of this bottling. It's tightly wound on the sip, where underripe pineapple and cantaloupe rinds move toward plumeria flavors by the finish.

name: Midnight 2019 Aurora White (Paso Robles)
review: Candied peach, daffodil and candied melon aromas show on the nose of this blend of 38% Viognier, 38% Grenache Blanc, 12% Picpoul Blanc and 12% Roussanne. The palate is peachy but in balance, with cantaloupe and coconut-oil flavors also arising.

name: Michael David 2019 Whole Cluster Pressed Stainless Sauvignon Blanc (Lodi)
review: Ripe green-apple and lemon-lime flavors converge in this richly textured but not heavy wine. It's smooth on the entry and has enough good acidity to keep it lively on the palate.

name: Mettler Family Vineyards 2019 Estate Grown Albariño (Lodi)
review: Dazzling green-apple flavors and a zippy intensity in the texture make this unoaked wine very appetizing and energized. It's crisp, tangy and delicious.

name: Merryvale 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This 100% varietal white spent seven months in 30% new French oak barrels and the oak leaves an imprint of concentration and texture. Legume, anise and earthy floral components highlight a medium-bodied richness of pear and melon.

name: Melville 2019 Clone 76 Inox Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: This steel-fermented and steel-aged bottling starts with floral aromas of honeysuckle, orange blossom, poached pear and light pastry. There's a burst of acidity on the sip, where tuberose and guava flavors are kept in check by the crisp texture.

name: Meeker 2019 Evina Vineyard Verdelho (Contra Costa County)
review: Aromas of flint and fresh herbs are followed by crisp acidity and a nervy, green-apple vibrancy in this medium-bodied wine. Not your average California white wine, this one emphasizes savory and mineral attributes.

name: McManis 2019 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Viognier (River Junction)
review: Light floral aromas lead to fairly rich peach and Anjou pear flavors in this medium-bodied, smooth and fresh wine. The aromatics and just-ripe flavors make a good combination.

name: McManis 2019 Estate Grown Certified Sustainable Chardonnay (River Junction)
review: This wine is rich, almost thick, in texture and packed full of sweet vanilla, marzipan and butterscotch flavors.

name: McKahn 2019 Catie's Corner Viognier (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a bright and effusive white from the great site, steely in texture with vibrant characteristics of lychee, peach skin and pineapple. The nose is deeply defined in honeysuckle, winding its way to a complete, cohesive finish of neutral oak.

name: Mauritson 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)
review: From the family's Hardie Ranch, this complex, weighty wine was fermented and aged in both stainless steel and oak foudres. Sleek, sublime flavors of wet stone, mango and melon are boosted by ample acidity and a fleshy texture.

name: Mauritson 2019 Muscat Blanc
review: The first vintage of this wine for the Sonoma-based producer, it comes from organically grown grapes farmed on an estate site. Fermented and aged in stainless steel, the clean, focused and freshly conceived white is dry, light and silky on the palate, bursting in lime, orange peel and a hint of lychee.

name: Materra Cunat Family Vineyards 2019 Viognier (Oak Knoll District)
review: Sizzling acidity lies beneath oily apricot and green apple in this earthy, viscous and brightly high-toned white wine.

name: Materra Cunat Family Vineyards 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Oak Knoll District)
review: This wine is quite opulent in style and moderate in acidity, offering lush roundness and approachability. Pear, vanilla and peach flavors emphasize a fruity palate of inviting character.

name: Massican 2019 Gemina White (California)
review: A rare, beautifully conceived blend of Napa Valley Pinot Bianco and Sonoma County Greco, Gemina is refreshing and hugely memorable in crunchy fruit and freshness. Slatey, with a taste of the ocean, it evolves in the glass to impart a fleshy lushness of fig.

name: Massican 2019 Annia White (Napa Valley)
review: This is a consistently beautiful and beguiling white wine, a blend of Tocai Friulano, Ribolla Gialla and Chardonnay, the latter from Hyde Vineyards. Structured, mineral-driven and explosive in flavors of lemon zest, green apple and wet stone, it is a wine well worth stocking.

name: Massican 2019 Hyde Vineyard Chardonnay (Napa Valley)
review: This is a barrel-fermented and -aged white from the famous grower site in Carneros, which does justice to its soulful sense of place. Brioche and nutmeg aromas lead to a midpalate of succulent green apple, pear and oak, with lifted, bright and layered acidity. Complex and completely delicious, it remains focused and balanced throughout.

name: Massican 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This is winemaker Dan Petroski's nod to the Sauvignons of northern Italy. Lemony and sleek, it conjures notes of wet stone, brine and complex, layered acidity, with a lasting tease of green apple and lemongrass.

name: Massalto 2019 Sauvalanche White (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is 58% Chenin Blanc and 42% Sauvignon Blanc, cofermented and with extended maceration. With fruit coming from La Reyna Blanca Vineyard on the Royal Slope, the aromas are bright, with notes of cantaloupe, melon, kiwi skin, banana and apple. Rounded, fresh flavors follow, bringing some weight but with enough acidity to pull it off. It needs time to fully settle in, but there are lots of interesting things happening.

name: Mason Cellars 2019 Yount Mill Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This is an austere, high-toned white, with strong flavors of dried herb and jalapeño. Medium bodied, it has an underlying lushness of melon and tropical fruit.

name: Maryhill 2019 Proprietor's Reserve Roza Hills Vineyard Chenin Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Chenin Blanc is increasingly rare in the state, with this example coming from old vines planted in the late 1970s. Candied banana, apple, wet stone, honeycomb and white peach aromas are followed by medium-plus bodied flavors and a slightly warn finish. An intriguing sense of acidity stands it up.

name: Maryhill 2019 Proprietor's Reserve Bloxom Vineyard Grüner Veltliner (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of pear, citrus and pea pod are followed by dry, medium-bodied flavors, framed by a lovely sense of acidity. There's a lot to like.

name: Markus Wine Co 2019 Nativo White (Lodi)
review: This light-bodied and ultrasmooth wine wraps fresh melon and fig flavors in a slightly mineral structure, and subtly spices them with vanilla and almond. It's an interesting and enjoyably complex wine blended from Kerner, Bacchus and Riesling grapes.

name: Markus Wine Co 2019 Joey Insieme White (Lodi)
review: Vivid lemon and fresh herb flavors light up this medium-bodied wine as good acidity balances a sense of concentration and richness. Made with 95% Torrontes grapes from Lodi it also incorporates 5% of Traminette grapes from North Carolina.

name: Markus Wine Co 2019 Vermentino (Lodi)
review: Veggie-like aromas of chives and snap peas meet up with tangy, fresh herb and green-apple flavors in this medium-bodied wine. It's refreshing, savory and unoaked.

name: Margerum 2019 M5 Margerum Estate Vineyard White (Los Olivos District)
review: Complex and yet utterly refreshing, this estate blend of 45% Grenache Blanc, 21% Marsanne, 14% Viognier, 13% Roussanne and 7% Picpoul Blanc begins with wet stone, chalk, nectarine, Rainier cherry and creamed pear on the crisp nose. The palate sizzles with acidity and ashy, chalky tension, delivering flavors of kiwi, lychee and more stone fruit.

name: Margerum 2019 Margerum Estate Vineyard Viognier (Los Olivos District)
review: Crisp aromas of pure apricot and freshly squeezed lime meet with a chalky tightness on the nose of this bottling. There's an ample sizzle of acidity on the sip, where those apricot tones meet with flavors of lemon-glazed pastry and chalky wet earth.

name: Margerum 2019 Margerum Estate Vineyard Picpoul Blanc (Los Olivos District)
review: The grape is showing much potential along the entire Central Coast, and this bottling from Doug Margerum's estate falls right in line. Aromas of melon, honeysuckle and Mandarin rind lead into a tightly wound palate of clean nectarine and honeydew flavors, with a touch of lemon pith. It is both racy and ripe.

name: Margerum 2019 D Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)
review: Clean aromas of lemon peel, grapefruit pith and freshly cut apple flesh show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is fresh and zippy, with lime and grapefruit zest flavors, yet there is also a subtle underlying richness that slowly emerges with patience.

name: Margerum 2019 Sybarite Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)
review: Very light in the glass, this always reliable white wine starts with aromas of pear soda, red apple and slightly tropical white and green flowers. The texture is taut and dry, with brisk flavors of red apple, seashell, lemongrass and a fast-fading flash of guava.

name: Maidenstoen 2019 Lafond Vineyard Riesling (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Warm tones of seared lemon peel, crisp and dried apple and apple blossom show on the dynamic nose of this bottling, which comes from vines that are more than 45 years old. There's a flashy zing of acidity on the sip, where flavors of nectarine, Rainier cherry and chiseled sandstone cling to a relentlessly taut texture.

name: Maidenstoen 2019 Zabala Vineyard Riesling (Arroyo Seco)
review: Though appropriately tense and stony on the nose, there's an approachable aspect of kumquat and honeysuckle on the nose of this bottling. The palate is full of citrusy freshness, delivering lemon and grapefruit-juice flavors against lush tones of gardenia and more honeysuckle.

name: Maidenstoen 2019 Wirz Vineyard Riesling (Cienega Valley)
review: From vines planted in 1964, making them the second oldest Riesling in California, this is a revelatory wine. Tense aromas of lime skin, underripe melon, wet gravel, blossoms and fresh grass lead into an extremely textural and tightly wound sip. Flavors of lime peel, mandarin, lemon bar and melon prove both complex yet approachable.

name: Maidenstoen 2019 Grüner Veltliner (San Luis Obispo County)
review: There's a dynamic array of tightly spun aromas on the nose of this bottling, including lime peel, grapefruit pith and a brush of white rose petal. The palate is all about zip and grip, proving extremely tense and offering plenty of citrus pith flavors.

name: Maggio 2019 Chardonnay (Lodi)
review: Quite ripe-tasting and mouthfilling, this full-bodied wine pours on the poached pear flavors and accents them with vanilla and nutmeg on a soft texture.

name: Maddalena 2019 Pinot Grigio (Monterey)
review: A light shade of pink, this bottling begins with tightly wound aromas of brisk lemon spray and wet soil. It's refreshing on the palate with citrus peel and a slight pear flavor.

name: Luke 2019 Chardonnay (Ancient Lakes)
review: All of the fruit for this wine comes from Evergreen Vineyard. Aged in third-fill barrels and neutral oak with 50% malolactic fermentation, the aromas of apple, pumpkin and talc are plentiful. The palate is bright and fresh, with lemony flavors. It brings texture and tension.

name: Lucky Rock Wine Co. 2019 County Cuvée Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: Thick in melon and apricot flavors, this white has a metallic edge and enduring earthiness.

name: Lucas & Lewellen 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Very light in the glass, this bottling starts shy with a delicate nose of wet stone, light grass, wet cement and grapefruit skins. The palate is grippy in texture, offering fresh flavors of guava peel and ripe apple.

name: Longevity 2019 Vintner Select Muscat Canelli (Clear Lake)
review: Very aromatic and packed with floral and fruity flavors, this medium-bodied wine charts an interesting and different course in this category. It's dry, well balanced and somewhat earthy overall.

name: Longevity 2019 Vintner Select Pinot Grigio (Livermore Valley)
review: This unusual wine appears as a light-copper-colored rosé, showing the true color of the Pinot Gris grape skins. It's full of prosciutto and melon flavors, has a rather full body and a hint of tangy acidity for good balance.

name: Longevity 2019 Vintner Select Pinot Blanc (Livermore Valley)
review: Smooth, suave and slightly nutty, this layered and concentrated wine is luxurious in texture and ripe in flavor, offering peach, pear and vanilla notes that are tasty and lingering.

name: Longevity 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: Rich vanilla, coconut and baking-spice flavors fill the palate of this attractive, medium-bodied and smooth-textured wine. The oaky richness is well balanced by good underlying acidity.

name: Longboard 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Inviting floral aromas dominate the nose of this delightfully light, balanced wine. It is buzzy in acidity and lifted in juicy flavors of peach and apricot. A sleek, sublime texture gives it a rounded softness.

name: Long Meadow Ranch 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Rutherford)
review: Textured and grippy, this light-bodied, high-acid white is refreshing and complex, with layers of Meyer lemon, grapefruit rind and tangerine. It rides the palate with a seamless energy of lasting freshness and subtle herbaceousness that appeals.

name: Lone Birch 2019 Estate Grown Pinot Gris (Yakima Valley)
review: Bright aromas of pear and cantaloupe lead to fleshy kiwi, papaya and other tropical-fruit flavors. It's a cornucopia of fruit, with a zing on the finish tying it together.

name: Lola 2019 Malvasia Bianca (St. Helena)
review: From 40-plus-year-old vines grown in the northern part of St. Helena, this wine has hugely inviting aromas of peach. Dry, it sparkles in acidity and remains sizzling fresh on the palate, with a stony minerality that contributes texture to the light racy core.

name: Lobo Hills 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Yakima Valley)
review: The aromas are expressive, with notes of gooseberry, kiwi, underripe pineapple and lemon zest, and are thoroughly compelling. The palate shows a lovely sense of restraint and freshness to the richly textured kiwi flavors. Mouthwatering acidity enlivens it all.

name: LifeVine 2019 Chardonnay (Clements Hills)
review: Flinty aromas and slightly earthy flavors blend with herb and green-apple notes for an almost lean, Old World expression in this full-bodied wine that was made from organic grapes.

name: Lepe 2019 Zabala Vineyards Sauvignon Blanc (Arroyo Seco)
review: Dried grass, kelp, sea spray and a hint of peach aromas show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. There's a light grassy tone to the sip, which is clean and pure with faint nectarine and more oceanic flavors.

name: Left Coast 2019 White Pinot Noir (Van Duzer Corridor)
review: If you've not yet tried a white Pinot from Oregon, here's a great opportunity. A distinctive style is emerging, positioned halfway between Pinot Blanc and Chardonnay. Some are styled more like a rosé; others, like this one, lean more toward white wine flavors. Here the soft entry is lightly buttery, with pretty fruit flavors of white peach and melon. There's suggestion of some barrel time in the scents of toasted cashews. The blend includes 6% Pinot Meunier and 3% Pinot Blanc.
