name: Le Vigne 2019 Il Fiore Viognier (Paso Robles)
review: Bright aromas of honeydew soda, cantaloupe balls, watermelon and peach show on the fruity nose of this bottling. The palate is rounded in peach and pear flavors that are cut with a splash of lime juice, finishing on a banana and bubblegum kick.

name: Le Vigne 2019 Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: Ripe but clean aromas of poached pear, daffodil, Stargazer lily, coconut and pineapple make for a layered nose on this bottling. There's buoyant acidity on the sip, where zippy freshness cuts through the pineapple, mango and jasmine flavors.

name: Le Vigne 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Clean aromas of guava, melon, apple flesh and lime peel show on the well-rounded nose of this bottling. The palate combines green tones of cut grass, with more tropical, riper elements of guava peel, and is only lacking a touch of texture.

name: Le Casque 2019 Roussanne (Sierra Foothills)
review: This attractive, full-bodied wine offers subtlety in flavor, richness in texture and a good sense of concentration that gives it length on the finish. It's a good bet for cellaring, since time should open up more mineral and lanolin notes. Best from 2022.

name: Le Casque 2019 Marsanne (Sierra Foothills)
review: This subtle, tangy, medium-bodied wine shows a great acid-to-ripeness balance and offers a combo of subdued apricot and white-peach flavors to savor.

name: Le Casque 2019 Grenache Blanc (Sierra Foothills)
review: This medium-bodied wine is fashioned in a richer style than many, offering tempting lemon-zest aromas and generous pear and fresh apple flavors. A luscious mouthfeel and an addition of 18% Picpoul Blanc add to its charm.

name: Le Casque 2019 Adrian White (Sierra Foothills)
review: Great depth, layering and complexity make this full-bodied wine something to linger over. Notes of pear, cream and vanilla harmonize on a nicely viscous texture that extends the finish. It's a blend of Grenache Blanc, Roussanne and Picpoul Blanc.

name: Le Casque 2019 Damiano Vineyard Viognier (Placer County)
review: Aromas of citrus flowers and flavors of ripe pear and marzipan make this full-bodied, barrel-fermented wine tempting and delicious. A creamy texture, good depth of flavor and lingering finish all contribute to its impressive quality.

name: Le Casque 2019 Calotte White (Sierra Foothills)
review: This well-balanced blend combines good fruit concentration with a touch of tanginess in the texture, offering crisp apple and ripe peach notes that build on the palate. It is superclean and fresh to sip.

name: Lava Cap 2019 Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (El Dorado)
review: Figgy aromas lead to subdued but tangy flavors of green apple, mineral and mild herb in this subtle, medium-bodied, well-balanced wine. It's not overtly fruity, but well worth sipping for its complexity.

name: Las Positas 2019 Estate Albariño (Livermore Valley)
review: Vibrant, crisp and fruity, this well-balanced wine offers crystal-clear flavors of green apple and white peach on a lively texture. It is extremely appetizing and food friendly.

name: Las Positas 2019 Estate Verdelho (Livermore Valley)
review: Honey-like aromas and generous honeydew-melon flavors make this medium-bodied wine superfruity and focused. As ripe and bursting as the flavors are, the balance is good from a touch of tangy acidity.

name: Lapis Luna 2019 Pauli Ranch Sauvignon Blanc (North Coast)
review: An abundance of tropical-fruit flavors gives this full-bodied wine a festive feel. Scents of kiwi, pineapple and banana lead to fresh and vivid fruit flavors, lightly accented by vanilla.

name: LangeTwins 2019 Diablo Vineyard Moscato (Clarksburg)
review: This semisweet wine makes a good case for Moscato as a dinner, or better, lunch wine. Picture the petrol and peach aromas and lush honey and apricot flavors with fried chicken or fish and chips.

name: LangeTwins 2019 Estate Grown Moscato (Clarksburg)
review: Soaring honeysuckle aromas and vivid peach and pistachio flavors make this slightly sweet and light-bodied wine a pleasure to sip.

name: LangeTwins 2019 Estate Grown Musque Clone Sauvignon Blanc (Lodi)
review: Effusive floral and fruit-bowl aromas lead to fresh, tangy lemon-zest and grapefruit flavors in this light-bodied wine. It's something bright and palate-cleansing for the cocktail hour.

name: Lange 2019 Classiqué Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)
review: A fine value, this is loaded with vivid flavors of grapefruit, apple flesh, apple skin and mixed citrus. It's tart but not sour, with the sort of steely acidity that makes a great match with shellfish.

name: Lange 2019 Reserve Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: There's some extra concentration in the reserve, which retains the tart, bracing freshness of the regular bottling. Fermented in a mix of neutral oak puncheons and concrete, it's loaded with gooseberry, citrus, lemon rind and minerals.

name: Lange 2019 Classiqué Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: So young and fresh it's still got a bit of light fizz (CO2) going on, this is a bracing wine, with lemon verbena, green apple, lemon oil and a palate-cleansing minerality. Made for early drinking, it's best with a light chill.

name: Lail 2019 Blueprint Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This white is fermented in French oak barrels, 30% of them new, and then aged in stainless steel—offering the best of both worlds. Quenching flavors of stone fruit highlight a rounded, rich palate, dusted in dried herb and earth.

name: Laetitia 2019 Estate Chardonnay (Arroyo Grande Valley)
review: Soft and subtle aromas of nectarine and peach are cut by lime juice and baked grapefruit, with a warmer macadamia nut tone as well. The palate is fresh in feel, with light lemon flavors that are rounded by a baked apple element.

name: Lady Hill 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Fermented in both steel and oak vessels, this combines steel's zest and spice with oak's smooth and slightly buttery mouthfeel. Lemon rind, grapefruit and apple flavors are featured, and a hint of baking spices adds further flavor interest.

name: Lady Hill 2019 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: Clean and crisp, this opens with fresh scents of herbs and citrus, and follows with tart fruit flavors of citrus and apple. Lemon is the dominant citrus note, and the tangy acidity reflects the moderate level of alcohol.

name: Ladera 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: From a site in Oak Knoll planted to both Sauvignon Blanc and Sauvignon Musque grapes, the latter of which is represented here at 25%, this is a high-toned white, with grip and texture. Legume, quince and lime meet floral aromas of jasmine that accent the fruit and delicate oak well.

name: La Randonnée 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: A severe style with the emphasis on searingly tart, grassy gooseberry flavors. The high acids bring grapefruit and lemon flavors front and center. Despite the acids, there's just enough remaining grape sugar to soften it up a touch. Try this with shellfish, especially raw oysters.

name: La Marea 2019 Kristy Vineyard Albariño (Monterey County)
review: Rainy cement, Meyer lemon rind and very tight apple aromas show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. The palate shows both brisk, sizzling acidity and warmer tones of baked pear, with a taut texture holding firm into the bubblegum-kissed finish.

name: La Honda Winery 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Green-apple, blooming jasmine and peach aromas show on the nose of this bottling, which is a blend of Santa Cruz Mountains and Dry Creek Valley grapes. There's a brisk sizzle of acidity at first on the sip, and then come ripe and broad peach, melon and green apple flavors.

name: La Crema 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: Given time in both oak and stainless steel, this lively white is grassy and lively in stone fruit, with an enduring crispness to the texture. Bright and food-friendly, it offers wide appeal and elegance.

name: La Chouette 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This is solidly varietal, with strong fruit components of melon and pear. Full bodied and fruit-forward, it's styled for drinking while young, perhaps chilled a bit, and served outdoors with a summer picnic.

name: L'Ecole No. 41 2019 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of toast, corn silk, spice and peach. Full feeling stone fruit flavors follow. A pleasing sense of acidity ties it all together.

name: L'Ecole No. 41 2019 Luminesce Estate Grown Seven Hills Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc-Semillon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
designation: Luminesce Estate Grown Seven Hills Vineyard
review: This wine is equally split between the two varieties. Pleasing aromas of pear, citrus, fig, mineral and herb are followed by rounded, textured flavors that lead to a warm finish. The acidity is heightened compared to recent vintages, making the flavors snap. Give it a few years in the cellar to see it at its best.

name: L'Ecole No. 41 2019 Old Vines Chenin Blanc (Yakima Valley)
review: This wine is consistently one of the best values from the state or the country for that matter. The aromas leap up, with notes of passion fruit, guava and kiwi. The palate is broad and flavorful, with vibrant, mouthwatering acidity. It's a stylistic shift from recent vintages, with the coolness of the season and October frost making it considerably more nervy and edgy.

name: Kunde 2019 Magnolia Lane Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma Valley)
review: High-toned layers of lemon, grapefruit and wet stone highlight sizzling acidity in this light, crisp white from the estate. Floral aromas of honeysuckle add a pretty element that accents the fruit.

name: Kudos 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This is done in a straightforward, no-frills style. Crunchy pear fruit comes across clean and simple, with balancing acidity and a quick, crisp finish.

name: Kudos 2019 Riesling (Willamette Valley)
review: Succulent, dry and tangy, this bring zippy citrus fruit flavors of grapefruit and tangerine. It's refreshing and well balanced, with just enough residual sugar to offset the searing acidity.

name: Kokomo 2019 Timber Crest Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Dry, steely and stony, with a floral backbone and fleshy midpalate, this crisp vineyard-designate delivers focused flavors of green apple, grapefruit and tangerine. The acidity persists through a textured finish with just a tease of cut grass.

name: Knights Bridge 2019 Pont De Chevalier Sauvignon Blanc (Knights Valley)
review: Made in a sweetly effusive and rich style, this tastes of mango, pineapple and a hint of vanilla, the oak minimal and well integrated.

name: Knights Bridge 2019 Fairview Sauvignon Blanc (Knights Valley)
review: With a lush, opulent structure, this estate white is weighty and thick, with dense layers of vanilla bean and stone fruit. Dried herb, lemongrass and a touch of nutmeg ride an oak-influenced finish.

name: Kirkland Signature 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: This wine bursts with bright fruit aromas and flavors ranging from fresh mint to pineapple and poached pear. A medium body and soft acidity keep the mouthfeel soothing.

name: Kirkland Signature 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: Don't be put off by the box format. Floral aromas and ripe melon and citrus flavors dominate this enjoyable, medium- to full-bodied wine. It feels nicely round and mouthfilling in texture.

name: Kiona 2019 Chenin Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of whole honeydew melon and pear lead to tangy, lightly spritzy, off-dry flavors that show a lot of intensity. The acidity is lovely, upping the interest. It needs some time to full settle in, but it has all of the stuffing.

name: Kinero 2019 Alice Grenache Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Tightly wound aromas of Asian pear and pomelo pith make for a tense and vibrant entry to this bottling. It's soft and delicate on the palate yet with a firm line of lime-driven tension that cuts through the baked apple and poached pear flavors.

name: Kerloo 2019 Blue Mountain Vineyard Grenache Blanc (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of almond skin, wet stone, cheese, whole lemon and herbs lead to a full-feeling palate that shows a lot of texture and nuance. It glides on the finish. The lees impact is a little upfront at present. Perhaps it integrates with time?

name: Kenzo Estate 2019 Asatsuyu Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This remarkable white includes 9% Sémillon, the whole aged in half French oak and half stainless steel. Invitingly floral, with an effusive landscape of citrus, pear and vanilla cookie. Focused, bright and opulently layered, it has structure and grace that last for days.

name: Kendric 2019 Proprietor Grown Viognier (Petaluma Gap-Marin County)
review: Big flavors and a full rich texture make this wine very robust, mouthfilling and delicious. Luckily it has good acidity to nicely balance the ripe apricot and orange-blossom notes.

name: Kendall-Jackson 2019 Pinot Gris (California)
review: This well-balanced and nicely fruity wine offers honeydew-melon flavors, lively acidity and a very fresh, palate-cleansing texture—a versatile wine for all seasons.

name: Kendall-Jackson 2019 Vintner's Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Lots of bright, ripe fruit flavors and great acidity give this full-bodied wine plenty of charm. Tangy citrus and apricot aromas meet zesty but rich flavors in this superconsistent wine.

name: Kendall-Jackson 2019 avant unoaked Chardonnay (California)
review: Lots of fresh apple and a touch of lemon give this medium-bodied wine an appealing, fruity profile, while a touch of good acidity keeps it feeling fresh and well balanced.

name: Kason 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Despite the low finished alcohol, this is not a tart or acidic wine. Rather it feels almost syrupy, though it is completely dry. There are thick fruit flavors of apple and pear, and a wash of vanilla cream as it trails out. Drink now and over the next two years.

name: Joyce 2019 Mission Ranch Albariño (Arroyo Seco)
review: Gardenia, pear-flesh and lime-balm aromas converge of the nose of this refreshing bottling. There's a strong lime-peel tension to the sip, where touches of green pear are wrapped around a tightly spun texture.

name: Josh Cellars 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: Subtle oak spices and a lot of ripe, delicious pear and peach flavors drive this medium- to full-bodied wine. The texture is broad and mouthfilling while a crisp apple acidity keeps it lively.

name: Josh Cellars 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: This light-bodied wine offers vibrant lemon and lime flavors, good acidity and a mouth-cleansing texture.

name: Josh Cellars 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: A slightly smoky, petrol aroma and lip-smacking apricot flavors help this light-bodied wine stand out. It's a bit earthy and unorthodox but fun to drink.

name: JosephJane 2019 Pinot Gris (Southern Oregon)
review: This approaches off-dry with its honeyed semi-sweet fruit flavors. Apple, melon and pineapple are in the mix, forward and fresh, with a touch of herb framing the finish.

name: JosephJane 2019 Chardonnay (Southern Oregon)
review: Precise, punchy and fruit-focused, this offers lightly tropical flavors of banana, green pineapple and papaya. A bit of barrel aging has helped to smooth it out, and added a finishing note of vanilla.

name: Joseph Phelps 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (St. Helena)
review: Offering tremendous value for the price, this is a lively, seamless and textured white wine from estate grapes that shows elegance and length. Green apple, apricot and lemongrass highlight a sublime core of integrated finesse and complexity.

name: Joseph Jewell 2019 Grist Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)
review: Floral and steely more than fruity, this grippy white is light and structured, with strong herbal and earthy elements of wet stone and legume. The finishing touch is defined in lemon peel, both in flavor and texture.

name: Joseph Jewell 2019 Raymond Burr Vineyard Vermentino (Dry Creek Valley)
review: From the vineyard grafted to Vermentino in 2017, this wine shows how wise it was to try such a variety in this hot appellation while being beautifully floral in lemon peel. Grip and texture present along a steely, high-toned palate of green apple.

name: Joel Gott 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Full bodied and mouthfilling, this is a big, rich wine that lavishes ripe apple, melon and vanilla flavors on a rather viscous texture.

name: Jax 2019 Y3 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Stainless-steel fermented, this white is grassy and herbal, with sizzling acidity. With no oak influence it dazzles in fresh, focused lemon and peach.

name: Januik 2019 Sagemoor Vineyards Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Ask Mike Januik what makes Washington Sauvignon Blanc so good and he gives a simple answer: the Sémillon. That's the case here, with the variety making up 20% of the wine and showing itself. Aromas of fig and spice are followed by textured flavors and a lingering finish.

name: Jaine 2019 Sustainable Vineyards Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is a new, white wine dedicated winery and a sensational debut. The aromas are compelling, with notes of lemon pith, herb and the underside of a pineapple, unmistakably Sauvignon Blanc. The flavors are generous, intense, with a lot of texture. Mouthwatering acidity finishes it off. It's a big-time winner—everything you could want from this variety. Bravo!

name: Jaine 2019 Sustainable Vineyards Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This is stainless steel fermented and aged. The aromas are bright, with notes of cantaloupe, pear and banana. Fresh fruit flavors follow, with an appealing sense of texture and the acidity that this vintage brings.

name: J.C. Somers 2019 Croft Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: Jay Somers, who has worked with Croft fruit for the past 20 years, launches his new brand with this outstanding Sauvignon Blanc. Powerful flavors of grapefruit, pineapple and gooseberry pierce through with peppery thistle. The flavors are crisp, lingering and beautifully defined.

name: J. Wilkes 2019 Pinot Blanc (Santa Maria Valley)
review: A testament to why this grape deserves more attention in the region, this white begins with lightly aromatic hints of jasmine, pear and wet stone. The palate is brisk and taut, offering underripe orange, apple peel and nectarine flavors.

name: J. Lohr 2019 Flume Crossing Sauvignon Blanc (Arroyo Seco)
review: For those seeking a crisp and grassy Sauvignon Blanc, this is always a great option. Aromas of lawn clippings, lime peels and grapefruit skins lead into a palate of the same, along with Granny Smith apple peels and a hint of bell pepper.

name: Itä 2019 1 of 2 Les Collines Vineyard Sémillon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Fermented and aged in stainless steel, aromas of fig, talc and lemon are followed by focused, sleek flavors and tart, lemony acidity. It's a wonderfully acid driven offering of this variety.

name: Itä 2019 2 of 2 Les Collines Vineyard Sémillon (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Fermented and aged in neutral oak, the aromas are light initially, with notes of wet rock, fig and spice. The palate brings a sense of creamy texture yet remains sleek, with lemony acidity stitching it together. It's as much about feel as flavor, with acid in the driver's seat.

name: Imagery 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: This smooth and straightforward wine is medium bodied, fairly neutral in flavor and easy to sip.

name: Iconic 2019 Sidekick Chardonnay (California)
review: This wine tastes lively, medium bodied, and not oaky, relying on crisp citrus flavors and a tangy texture for its considerable charm.

name: Hyland 2019 Old Vine Single Vineyard Riesling (McMinnville)
review: There's no telling how much longer these old vines, planted in the early 1970s, will last. This is a landmark wine for Oregon, one of a vanishing breed. It's finished dry and tart, with lip-licking lemon, grapefruit and green apple crunch and bite. Let it breathe and flavors of pear and lemongrass develop, with a lasting scent of honeysuckle.

name: Husch 2019 Estate Bottled Dry Gewurztraminer (Anderson Valley)
review: Wonderful litchi aromas and rose-petal flavors give this smooth and richly textured wine a compelling expression of a famously floral grape variety. With just a touch of residual sugar, the wine's full body and mouthfilling effect help the flavors linger on the finish.

name: Husch 2019 D Chenin Blanc (Mendocino)
review: Quite tangy but also concentrated, this medium-bodied wine blends crisp apple-peel flavors with ripe peaches for a mouthfilling, almost-sweet yet vibrant profile.

name: Hungry Hawk 2019 Pinot Gris (San Diego County)
review: Clean aromas of wet stone, lime and nectarine show on the crisp nose of this bottling. There is a strong sizzle at the front of the sip, which drops into riper flavors of pear and apple.

name: Hungry Hawk 2019 Viognier (San Diego County)
review: Few San Diego County wineries are exploring the terroir as diligently as this family-run winery. This estate bottling is fresh yet rich, with light peach and creamy lemon aromas. The palate is clean and full, with more peach and apple-pie flavors.

name: Hungry Hawk 2019 Elliot Ranch Albariño (San Diego County)
review: Exuberant aromas of tuberose, pineapple, honeydew, guava and jasmine make for a very tropical nose on this bottling. The palate tightens up with a fresh sizzle, delivering melon and lime Popsicle flavors in a full yet balanced display.

name: Hungry Hawk 2019 Mélange Blanc White (San Diego County)
review: Almost pink in the glass, this unique estate blend of 87% Viognier and 13% Pinot Gris offers cantaloupe and wet chalk aromas. There is a compelling sizzle to the palate, where lemon-blossom and dried apple flavors meet with a dusty minerality.

name: Hook or Crook 2019 Vintner's Select Chardonnay (Lodi)
review: This brass-colored wine slathers sawn oak and vanilla aromas over a combo of crisp and ripe fruit flavors.

name: Hook or Crook 2019 Reserve Chardonnay (Lodi)
review: Smooth and well balanced, this medium-bodied wine offers a seamless combination of crisp apple and generous pear flavors with some complexing elements of butter and vanilla.

name: Holly's Hill 2019 Estate Roussanne (El Dorado)
review: Bright aromas and flavors of Anjou pear and Gravenstein apple make this medium-bodied wine lively and satisfying. It feels relatively light in mouthfeel and is absolutely refreshing and appetizing.

name: Highway 12 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: Robustly tropical, this likable white is creamy in peach, pineapple and guava, with a dense richness and enduring likability.

name: High Pass 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: As with the winery's Pinot Gris, this low-alcohol wine brings some residual sugar with it. Cotton candy and lemon drop flavors combine in a light, almost ephemeral palate. Unusual for Oregon Sauvignon Blanc, this will appeal most to those who prefer some sweetness in their white wines.

name: High Pass 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This wine is soft in the mouth, full bodied with ripe pear fruit, and a cottony impression. Though simple and fruity, it lingers nicely through the finish.

name: High Heaven 2019 Cloud Mountain Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas seem a bit muddled, with notes of apple and toast. Full-feeling fruit flavors follow, pulling up on the finish.

name: High Heaven 2019 Estate Grown & Bottled Starshower Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Honeysuckle, lime and peach aromas are followed by off-dry peach and Nestea Iced Tea flavors. The concentration seems a little light at times on an otherwise enjoyable wine.

name: Hearst Ranch 2019 Glacier Ridge Chardonnay (Monterey County)
review: Light aromas of lemon peel, melon rind, honeysuckle, smashed stone and flint show on the nose of this bottling. There's a pleasant citrusy sizzle to the sip, where rounded apple and baking-spice flavors show.

name: Head High 2019 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)
review: A youthful and approachable wine, this is fleshy, lush and opulent in sweet white peach, pineapple and cantaloupe. The full-bodied concentration has a hint of anise and vanilla around robust toasted oak.

name: Hayes Valley 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Central Coast)
review: Rounded pear, melon and bubblegum aromas kick off the nose on this bottling. The palate is a bit flat in texture, offering soft, nearly sweet flavors of honeysuckle and ripe pear.

name: Hawley 2019 Hopkins River Ranch Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Lime, lemon and salty wet stone combine invitingly in this crisp white wine, tart in lasting acidity. Light bodied and balanced in ripeness, it develops further characteristics of green apple, melon and peach as it opens in the glass.

name: Hawley 2019 Viognier (Sonoma County)
review: From grapes grown in both the Dry Creek and Russian River valleys, this medium-bodied white opens in an effusive expanse of floral jasmine. Citrus, stone fruit and lemon peel follow along a sleek, high-toned palate that shows refinement and length.

name: Happy Camper 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: Almost overwhelming baking spice and butter flavors make this medium-bodied wine very obvious and assertive. Recommended for oak lovers.

name: Hall Ranch 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Strong aromas of melon, lemongrass and grass clippings are fresh, clean and bright on the nose of this bottling. There's a stony start to the sip before riper flavors of peach and guava arise, with a streak of grassiness that holds from start to finish.

name: Hahn 2019 SLH Pinot Gris (Santa Lucia Highlands)
review: The slightest shade of pink in the glass, this bottling offers a simple yet pleasant nose of wet cement, white flower and nectarine aromas. Tangerine-peel and stone-fruit flavors show on the easy-to-enjoy palate.

name: Hahn 2019 Pinot Gris (Monterey County)
review: Crisp and clean lines of lime, chalk and peach start the nose on this bottling. The palate is fresh, with a pleasant texture, offering light stone-fruit flavors and a hint of salt.

name: H3 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Appealing aromas of grass, green pepper, guava and citrus lead to crisp, tart, lemony flavors. It's everything there is to love about this variety at a terrific price.

name: Guenoc 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Subtle in flavor and well balanced, this wine is a bit steely and mineral-like in aroma, then brings light cucumber and honeydew melon flavors to the palate. It will be a superversatile wine for lunch or dinner.

name: Grounded Wine Co. 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: This is a remarkably fresh, fruity and grassy white wine, overdelivering on its price point, with balanced flavors and texture. Dried herb, earth, lychee and wet stone give it lengthy appeal and memorability.

name: Groth 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: With a hearty helping of 18% Sémillon, this white has a creamy, viscous entry adorned in jasmine. Lemon pith, grapefruit and tangerine present as lush and lengthy on the palate, balanced by fresh, persistent acidity.

name: Grosgrain 2019 Philips Vineyard Albariño (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Newcomer Grosgrain is quickly making a name for itself with wines like this. Primarily fermented and aged in concrete egg, with the rest in neutral barrel and stainless steel, its bright compellingly fresh aromas of tangerine, citrus peel and melon are followed by full-feeling, lively, lemony flavors, with racy acidity.

name: Grey Stack 2019 Rosemary's Block Dry Stack Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Bennett Valley)
review: Nutty almond and a bite of cucumber lead to an opulent midpalate of mango and citrus in this wine. The palate is rounded and opulent, but still maintains freshness.

name: Greetings 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: The wine is much better than the looks of the label might suggest. It's packed with ripe flavors of sliced Bartlett pears, with that slightly grainy texture also. Though there isn't a whole lot more going on, the basic fruit is so good, clean and lingering, that it's more than sufficient as a fine all-purpose white wine.

name: Great Bear 2019 Chardonnay (Yolo County)
review: This well-balanced and well-concentrated wine shows a rich array of baking spice, butter and ripe pear flavors that play nicely off the full body and subtle fruit acidity.

name: Great Bear 2019 Roussanne (Yolo County)
review: This flavorful, distinctive and well-balanced wine overflows with orange-blossom aromas and vivid apricot and almond flavors. It's rich in texture, but nicely crisp, too, carrying complexity and depth into a lingering finish.

name: Grassini Family Vineyards 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)
review: There is a strong mineral quality of wet stone that balances the aromas of guava and lemongrass on the nose of this estate bottling. The palate is lifted in acidity, with a chalky grip that frames the crisp grapefruit and pear flavors.

name: Granite Lion 2019 Viognier (South Coast)
review: Very mellow and subtle on the nose, this wine eventually shows aromas of light peach blossom, wet gravel and a slightly candied citrus tang. Brushes of peach, nectarine and pear show on the sip, which is fairly tight in texture.

name: Granite Lion 2019 Reserve Chardonnay (South Coast)
review: Broad aromas of poached pear, apple juice and a hint of lemon blossom show on the nose of this bottling. It lands rather fat on the palate, with the slightest hint of a chalky frame, delivering pleasantly ripe flavors of white peach.

name: Granite Lion 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (South Coast)
review: Dried lemon peels, crushed rocks, dried apples and a slightly earthy funk show on the nose of this bottling from an estate just miles from the Mexican border. Crushed gravel flavors met with lime peel and dried green-apple skins on the palate.

name: Granite Lion 2019 Pinot Gris (South Coast)
review: Aromas of wet earth, lanolin and mushrooms kick off the nose on this slightly reductive bottling. There are apple and pear flavors on the palate, with a lingering hint of petrol.

name: Gramercy 2019 Viognier (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Gramercy has charted a new course for this often overripe variety. Citrus, white-peach and mineral aromas are follow by exquisitely balanced pear and peach flavors. It has a pleasing, mouthwatering feel that is rare in Viognier in this state or elsewhere and that ups the interest.

name: Gramercy 2019 Picpoul (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: Aromas of citrus zest, whole lemon and wet stone are followed by medium-bodied tart and puckering white-grapefruit flavors backed by electric acidity. This wine has always been a standout, but this vintage brings more of everything: more aromatic and flavor complexity and even more acidity. It requires food alongside it to be fully appreciated. Pair it with grilled halibut with a lemon oregano sauce.

name: Gordon Estate 2019 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas suggest notes of toast, caraway seed and spice. Roasted pumpkin and stone fruit flavors follow, with light ale notes on the finish.

name: Gnarly Head 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Monterey)
review: Aromas of Honeycrisp apple and cut grass make for a familiar and pleasant nose in this widely available bottling. The palate is standard and reliable, with tart green-apple and rounded peach flavors.

name: Girasole 2019 Muscat Canelli (Mendocino County)
review: Quite sweet and also quite packed with fresh ripe fruit flavors, this light-bodied, charming wine tastes fresh and floral.

name: Girasole 2019 Pinot Blanc (Mendocino County)
review: Lively acidity keeps the ripe, luscious honeydew melon flavors in balance while a smooth, buttery texture is energized by citrus accents. This full-bodied, vegan wine has distinct, enjoyable parts that come together as a whole.

name: Girasole 2019 Chardonnay (Mendocino County)
review: This light bodied wine tastes clean and fresh, showing just enough apple and pear flavor and a hint of vanilla to make it satisfying. It will be very versatile at mealtime and a good pour for wine o'clock as well.

name: Gilbert Cellars 2019 Unoaked Chardonnay (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Fermented and aged in stainless steel, this has vibrant aromas of cantaloupe, watermelon and yellow apple followed by full-bodied flavors. It's an enjoyable example of the variety and style.

name: Gård 2019 Freyja Lawrence Lawrence Vineyards White (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Roussanne makes up 52% of this wine, with the rest Viognier. The aromas are bright, with notes of melon, ripe peach and citrus, showing a sense of purity. Full-bodied, rounded feeling stone-fruit flavors follow. A tart finish caps it off. It's delightful.

name: Garcia 2019 Santa Ynez Vineyard Albariño (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)
review: There's a richness to the nose of this bottling, with aromas of baked apple, white flowers and a toasty coconut element. The palate is ripe with honeysuckle and baked peach flavors, proving viscous in texture and the slightest bit sweet, with a toasty finish.

name: Gamble Family Vineyards 2019 Gamble Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Yountville)
review: Earthy and dusty on the nose, this wine explodes into a palate of citrus peel, lime and grapefruit, with underlying exoticism in the form of honeysuckle, banana and papaya. Lengthy and fresh, it delivers light, balanced flavors that meld and complement, finishing in steely wet stone.

name: Gainey 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Mellow on the nose at first, this wine opens up toward aromas of green chile, delicate honeysuckle, sweet lemon and warm concrete. There's a mineral hit of wet gravel at first on the sip, before red-apple flesh and ripe melon flavors complete the picture.

name: G & C Lurton 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: A sleek, crisp white wine fermented in a majority of stainless steel, this is layered nicely in bright apple, lemon and lychee flavors, with a lasting sense of wet stone.

name: Fullerton 2019 Three Otters Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This sensational wine was sourced from old-vine plantings at the Lavelle vineyard. It's breathtakingly floral, with an elegant frame that dapples scents of clover around tart fruit flavors of apple and peach. It was fermented in a mix of stainless tanks and neutral barrels. Enjoy this over the next two years.

name: Frey 2019 Sun & Rain Organic Chardonnay (Mendocino)
review: This full-bodied wine feels powerful and tastes a bit hot, offering butterscotch, pineapple and toasted oak flavors that are dry and high toned. The mouthfeel is structured and firm, giving a lot of impact.

name: Four Vines 2019 Naked Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: This unoaked bottling carries all the tropical hallmarks of the style, starting with jasmine and guava flesh on the nose. The palate is generous in both acidity and flavor, showing pineapple, lychee, guava and stone fruit that linger into the grippy finish.

name: Foundry 2019 White on White (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Marsanne makes up 45% of this wine, with the rest near equal parts Roussanne, Viognier and Grenache Blanc. Slightly cloudy in appearance, the aromas offer notes of crayon, peach and lees. Lightly spritzy, tingly peach flavors follow. It doesn't seem to have fully settled in.

name: Foris 2019 Pinot Gris (Rogue Valley)
review: A chunky palate mixes fresh apples, a touch of Bartlett pear and the refreshing suggestion of just-sliced cucumber. It's textured and clean, underscored with a wash of wet stone.

name: Foris 2019 Estate Grown Pinot Blanc (Rogue Valley)
review: Crisp acid-driven fruit flavors of lemon, grapefruit and green apple dominate this wine, and turn it somewhat generic. It's a steely wine, with scents of iron filings and tight skin flavors wrapping up the finish. Try it with fresh caught trout.

name: Foris 2019 Estate Grown Dry Gewürztraminer (Rogue Valley)
review: Foris makes a distinctive version of this grape, more akin to those from Italy than Alsace. The varietal perfume is there lightly, a touch of rose petals and talcum powder on the nose. Lychee and lemon, citrus rind and grapefruit play across the palate. This would be optimal with Indian fare.

name: Foris 2019 Riesling (Rogue Valley)
review: Not quite fully dry, this skirts the border of off-dry with a touch of residual sugar, just enough to round out the tart grapefruit flavors that hold down the center palate. It opens further with more citrus and green apple fruit, underscored with refreshing minerality.

name: Forgeron 2019 Otherworldly Unoaked Chardonnay (Yakima Valley)
review: Aromas of freshly cut apple and pear are followed by full-feeling flavors. Tart acidity backs it up. It has a pleasing sense of varietal purity.

name: Forgeron 2019 Blacksmith Blanc Crawford Vineyard White (Yakima Valley)
review: Viognier makes up two-thirds of this wine, with the rest Roussanne. The aromas are light, with notes of melon, peach and pear. Full-feeling flavors follow. A slight bitter note lingers on the finish.

name: Forgeron 2019 Viognier (Walla Walla Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are bright, with notes of pear, flower and peach. Medium-plus bodied, quite pure-seeming melon and stone-fruit flavors follow. It brings a lot of appeal and spot-on balance.

name: Foppiano 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a light and floral white from the producer's own vineyards. Herbal and lemony, it has dashes of melon, peach and stone, showing no oak influence and a lasting aroma of jasmine.

name: Folktale 2019 Brosseau Vineyard Chenin Blanc (Chalone)
review: Taut aromas of lime peel, lime blossom and cement show on the nose of this bottling of a grape that's pretty rare in this small appellation. There's a great crisp cut to the sip, where zesty lime and lemon-balm flavors enliven the palate with acid-driven energy.

name: Folie à Deux 2019 Pinot Gris (Sonoma Coast)
review: Invitingly crisp and floral, this white wine is tropical with a fleshy opulence and lasting note of sweet vanilla.

name: Flora Springs 2019 Soliloquy White (Napa Valley)
review: This is an estate-grown white blending 45% Sauvignon Blanc, 40% Chardonnay and 15% Malvasia. Together they form a robust, succulent and fleshy core of tropical flavor, herb and earthy character, that is quite creamy and lush on the palate.

name: Flocking Fabulous 2019 Curtis Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Hints of honeysuckle mix with riper apple and peach tones on the nose of this bottling, which comes in a squat bottle with a fun flamingo label. The palate is easygoing and enjoyable, with fresh lime-peel and brisk apple flavors.

name: Firesteed 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Pinot Gris from Burgundy is a rather rare beast, so the back label claim that this is “Burgundian-style” doesn't shed any light on the matter. Light it is however, with clean flavors of apple and pear, medium weight and a touch of skin bitterness in the finish.

name: Firesteed 2019 Riesling (Willamette Valley)
review: A very fine effort, this way over-delivers for the price. Punchy lemon-lime fruit anchors a dry wine with juicy acidity and pretty accents of clover and stone fruits. The powerful fruit and impressive balance and length all advocate for long-term aging potential.

name: Fielding Hills 2019 Estate Chenin Blanc (Wahluke Slope)
review: The aromas offer notes of cut, slightly oxidized seeming red apple, along with plentiful notes of pear. A licorice note lurks in the background. The palate is textured in feel. It brings the acidity this variety is known for.

name: Ferrari-Carano 2019 Pinot Grigio (Russian River Valley)
review: Entirely stainless-steel fermented and aged, this lively white wine has wide appeal in its sweet floral nose and layers of vanilla and peach. Expressive, textured and bright, it is well made.

name: FEL 2019 Pinot Gris (Anderson Valley)
review: Brilliant Fuji apple and Anjou pear flavors light up this smooth and elegant, medium-bodied wine that was aged on its lees for five months. A silky mouthfeel and subtle acidity make it instantly appealing and hard to put down.

name: Fasi Estate 2019 Malvasia Bianca (California)
review: Very dry and tangy, this light-bodied wine offers fresh herb aromas and nervy, veggie flavors. It makes a refreshing change from heavy, rich whites.

name: Etude 2019 Grace Benoist Ranch Estate Grown Pinot Gris (Carneros)
review: Creamy and viscous in stone fruit, mango and fig, this white is opulent in texture with a subdued, balanced sense of ripeness. The acidity is equally poised and integrated, providing lasting freshness to the fruit.

name: Eternal 2019 Eternal Lust Albariño (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Fruit for this wine comes from Otis Vineyard. Fermented and aged in neutral oak, the aromas pop, with notes of fresh citrus zest, melon rind, flower, herb and whiffs of diesel. The palate is medium bodied in style, with plentiful kiwi, watermelon and citrus flavors. A lovely sense of acidity stitches it all together. It's thoroughly delightful.

name: Eternal 2019 Eternal Perspective Grenache Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Intriguing aromas of lime zest, slate, green papaya, charcoal and whole lemon are followed by medium-bodied flavors backed by lemony acidity. The balance is spot on.

name: Epiphany 2019 Rodney's Vineyard Grenache Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Very light in the glass, this canned wine starts with aromas of honeysuckle and lime sorbet, with a slight bit of reduction. Bright and fresh flavors of guava and orange flesh show on the sip, yet there remains a lingering funk, likely from the can.

name: Engine House 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: This full-bodied wine tastes lean and refreshing as mild herb and citrus flavors ride a crisp texture. It's good, straightforward sipping.

name: Elk Cove 2019 Estate Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Adam Campbell's Pinot Gris is one of Oregon's best, year in and year out. A lush and vivid mix of grapefruit, lemon curd and candied orange peel, this new vintage both surprises and delights. Such complexity is rare in a grape mostly centered around apple and pear flavors—both are here, but in a back seat role. This unique mix is deliciously irresistible.

name: Elk Cove 2019 Estate Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: This well-defined wine mixes fresh flavors of apple, Asian pear and a suggestion of honeydew melon. It fits nicely between the fleshy fruit of Pinot Gris and the broader palate of most Chardonnays. This is confined, crisp and focused, a good choice for appetizers and lighter entrées.

name: Eleven Eleven 2019 Beckstoffer Melrose Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Rutherford)
review: Inviting aromas of wet stone, honeysuckle and grass are met by a wealth of grapefruit, lime and more stone on the palate, offering a lovely combination that impresses from start to lengthy finish. Elements of lemongrass add to an intriguing experience.

name: El Lugar 2019 Blanc Greengate Ranch & Vineyard Pinot Noir (Edna Valley)
review: Winemaker Coby Parker-Garcia began exploring white Pinot Noir years ago, and this is his best vintage yet. Broad aromas of melon, nectarine and lemon blossom are fresh and crisp, while the palate is wrapped in a stony tension and lively acidity, delivering apple, pear and melon flavors.

name: Eight Bells 2019 Boushey Vineyard Chardonnay (Yakima Valley)
review: The fruit for this wine comes from a single block of the vineyard and is a mixture in equal parts of four different clones: Wente, 95, 96 and 548. With 84% fermented and aged in stainless, it carries vibrant aromas of freshly cut pear, green apple, flower and wet stone. Lively, lemony acidity brightens the textured palate. It's delicious on its own but will shine at the dinner table. Pair it with seared scallops with a lemon beurre blanc.

name: Ehlers Estate 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (St. Helena)
review: Robustly oak-driven on the nose, this is sourced from two blocks of the producer's estate, retaining exotic floral aromatics. Full and fleshy on the palate, with a silky smoothness, it has nuanced flavors of melon, pineapple and lemon, ending fresh.

name: Efeste 2019 Adrienne Chenin Blanc (Yakima Valley)
review: This is a variety change for this wine. Coming from vines planted in 1970, the aromas are light, with notes of herb, honey and mineral. The palate shows an appealing sense of precision, detail and balance. The bright acidity of the variety shines throughout.

name: Efeste 2019 Feral Evergreen Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Ancient Lakes)
review: This wine starts out quite funky, so much so that it is off-putting. Beneath that are pleasing aromas of wet slate, pink grapefruit and herb. Sleek, tart, puckering pink grapefruit flavors follow. The palate is delightful. The aromas straighten but take significant time to do so.

name: Eberle 2019 Côtes-du-Rôbles Blanc White (Paso Robles)
review: Strong aromas of banana, lemon peel and pineapple cream are ripe but clean on the nose of this blend of 43% Grenache Blanc, 43% Roussanne and 14% Viognier. There's just enough tension on the sip, which cuts into the creamy mouthfeel and rich flavors of pineapple and sea salt.

name: Eberle 2019 Eberle Estate Vineyard Chardonnay (Paso Robles)
review: Apple-cider and pear-juice aromas make for a fruity nose on this bottling. There's a brief wet stone flavor on the palate, where crisp yellow-apple slices arise and then grow into riper pineapple toward the finish.

name: Eberle 2019 Viognier (Paso Robles)
review: Tight apple-blossom, candied jasmine and tart apple-slice aromas show on the nose of this bottling from the Mill Road and Four Lanterns vineyards. There's a pleasant ashy tone on the palate, where peach, melon and bubblegum flavors converge.

name: Dutton-Goldfield 2019 Dutton Ranch Shop Block Pinot Blanc (Green Valley)
review: Stainless-fermented, this perennially beautiful white is bursting in fruity, floral aromas and flavors of fresh apricot, peach and fig. Crushed rock, oyster shell and wet stone contribute a minerality that intrigues and adds complex composure, with high-toned acidity integrated throughout.

name: Dutton Estate 2019 Kylie's Cuvée Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Tank fermented, this white is steely, stony and fresh, a nicely structured celebration of pear, melon and apple blossom. Textured, it is medium bodied and balanced in acidity and fruitiness.

name: Dusty Nabor 2019 The Florist Viognier (Ballard Canyon)
review: Chiseled stone, light sandalwood, a pinch of chamomile and dried mango show on the nose of this bottling. The palate is loaded with yellow-peach and ripe cantaloupe flavors, yet those elements are deftly cut by a streak of crushed stone and tense acidity.

name: Durant 2019 Southview Pinot Gris (Dundee Hills)
review: This is a well-balanced, meticulous take on this variety, all stainless fermented and finished crisp and clean. Aromas and flavors favor lovage, fennel and cilantro, with crunchy pear fruit filling out the midpalate. It's a most refreshing style, unencumbered by barrel flavors.

name: Dunham 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This wine is still settling in, with reductive notes up front. Beyond that are notes of banana, pear, citrus and herb. Passion fruit and citrus flavors follow with a strong acidic streak. It's hard to get a sense of where it's going at present.

name: Duck Pond 2019 Chardonnay (Willamette Valley)
review: An easy-drinking style, this features fresh apple, pear and white melon fruit, lively but light. A gentle hint of toast as the flavors fade indicates some short time in oak prior to bottling. Drink this over the next couple of years.

name: Duck Pond 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Quite light, to the point of losing varietal specificity, this seems less than optimally ripened. There are highlights of white radish along with green fruits and cucumber. It's a bit of a disappointment following some excellent recent vintages.

name: Dry Creek Vineyard 2019 Fumé Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: Stainless-steel fermented and inspired by the wines of the Loire Valley, this is a grassy, herbal white with enduring freshness and wide appeal. Value priced, it offers lovely, integrated flavors of pineapple and guava that lead to a finish of lemon candy.

name: Dry Creek Vineyard 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Dry Creek Valley)
review: A perennial bright star, this white opens on tones of lemon rind, stone and lychee. Textured and lively in acidity, it has complexity and approachability in equal measure.

name: Dressage 2019 Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: Mandarin flesh and pith meet with ripe apple slices on the nose of this bottling. The wine lands with a distinct sweetness on the palate, where ripe pineapple flavors show.

name: Dragonette 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Happy Canyon of Santa Barbara)
review: Crisp pear peels, orange blossom and a sharper tone of citrus show on the nose of this appellation blend. The palate is tightly wound with fresh acidity, which frames the rounded hints of pear, melon and tropical fruits.

name: Dovecote 2019 Thompson Vineyard Estate Grenache Blanc (Alisos Canyon)
review: Very light in the glass, this bottling offers white flower and honey aromas alongside a mineral-driven edge. There is a chalky structure on the sip, where Key lime sorbet and ripe nectarine flavors align.

name: Dovecote 2019 Quail Valley Vineyard Viognier (Los Olivos District)
review: Steely aromas of cold metal meet with a light hint of peach on the tightly wound nose of this bottling. That taut sense carries to the palate, which also offers more generous flavors of stewed pome fruit.

name: Domaine Divio 2019 Beurot Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: The unusual moniker, Winemaker Bruno Corneaux explains, is the traditional Burgundian name for Pinot Gris. Fermented in neutral oak and left on the lees until bottling, this is a smooth, supple wine, with lovely flavors of cut pear and apple cobbler. The texture and lingering fruit flavors suggest drinking this while young, though it should age well through 2025.

name: Dogwood & Thistle 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino Ridge)
review: This rather light-bodied wine shows a strong dried garlic aroma, followed by onion and dried herb flavors.

name: Doffo 2019 Concrete Fermented Viognier (Temecula Valley)
review: This concrete-fermented Viognier is easily one of the best white wines to emerge from this region in recent years. Clean and crisp aromas of Asian pear flesh, lime zest, honeysuckle and chalk lead into a tight palate of wet gravel, ash, apple blossom and more pear flesh. The texture lingers long into the finish.

name: Dobbes Family Estate 2019 Sundown Vineyard Viognier (Rogue Valley)
review: This spicy showcase sports varied botanical aromas and flavor highlights, and the fruit seems anchored in lemon rind—not a criticism, but a firm stylistic approach that may help determine the optimal food matches. There are also suggestions of jicama and white melon, and the length and crisp definition provide a good finish.

name: Division 2019 La Frontière Sauvignon Blanc (Oregon)
review: This is part of the Division-Villages portfolio. It was sourced from two vineyards—Redford-Wetle in the Eola-Amity Hills, and Allegre in the Columbia Gorge—and fermented sur lie in a mix of puncheons, barrels and stainless. Lovely scents of clover and honeysuckle lead into a lively wine with a lush mix of apple, pear and melon fruits. There's a dash of barrel toast and just enough residual sugar-acid tension to elevate and lengthen the finish.

name: Diatom 2019 Bar-M Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)
review: Greg Brewer's wines are always intriguing and sometimes challenging, often recasting what one grape can be. This bottling from a vineyard in the Los Alamos Valley leads with dried lime, wet wool and citrus pith aromas. The palate is laser-focused on citrus peel and pith and yet somehow also earthy, reflective of damp sand.

name: Devison 2019 Evergreen Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Ancient Lakes)
review: Winemaker Peter Devison has made a name for others for two decades with best-in-the-state white wines. His inaugural eponymous Sauvignon Blanc makes a name for him. The aromas are arresting, with notes of crushed rock, white grapefruit, citrus, dried herb and saline. The palate brings tart, puckering, electric acidity that commands attention. But there is much more here—texture, layering, purity, depth and length. An acid lover's delight, it's flat-out delicious and boundlessly ambitious, succeeding on every level. Enjoy it with oysters on the half shell for a sublime experience.

name: Delta 2019 Chenin Blanc (California)
review: This is a fun and fruity white wine at a remarkably fair price, abundant in aromas of honeysuckle and honey. Sleek in texture and high in acidity, it winds together layers of fig, blood orange and pear.

name: Delta 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Savory and crisp with a salty, briny characteristic of oyster shell, this bright, cheerful and well-made white is a celebration of Ruby Red grapefruit and melon. Crisp and high toned, it rewards in lasting freshness and is a steal at the price.

name: DeLille 2019 Chaleur Blanc 25th Vintage White (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: With 69% Sauvignon Blanc and 31% Sémillon, this wine is still in its shell at present. It offers aromas of toast, nut oil, tropical fruit and lemon zest that need time to get going. The palate is all about sophistication, showing a pleasing sense of depth and polish, along with vibrant acidity. It has all the stuffing to excel in the cellar. Best from 2024–2029, with plenty of life beyond that.

name: DeLille 2019 Roussanne (Red Mountain)
review: The aromas are intoxicating, with notes of apricot, sea shell and herb showing complexity. The flavors display texture, depth, purity, balance and length. The acid balance in particular is exquisite. It's an accomplishment for this variety and for white wines in the state. It is delicious now but has a long life ahead of it. Best from 2022–2026.

name: Debts and Lessons 2019 Zabala Vineyard Riesling (Monterey County)
review: Crisp aromas of lime pith and a hint of grass are tightly wound on the nose of this off-dry Riesling that should even enthuse those who prefer bone-dry. There is a light sweetness on the palate, where pear, apple and peach tones meet with a touch of petrol.

name: Dashe Cellars 2019 McFadden Farm Dry Riesling (Potter Valley)
review: This vibrant wine exudes tropical-fruit aromas of banana and mango, and tangy flavors of green apple and spiced apple. It is medium to full bodied and nicely mouthfilling.

name: Dashe Cellars 2019 Les Enfant Terrible Concrete Cuvée Chenin Blanc (Clarksburg)
review: This attractive, technically light-bodied wine fermented in a concrete egg is rich in ripe fruit flavors and has a mouthcoating texture, but stays nicely balanced thanks to underlying acidity.

name: Dark Horse 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: Nicely concentrated, crisp fruit flavors give this medium-bodied wine a lot to like. Tangy white-peach and Pippin apple flavors are lightly accented by lemon, adding to the vibrant mouthfeel.

name: Dark Horse 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Crisp and tangy, this well-balanced, grapefruit-scented wine offers vibrant lemon and kiwi flavors on a lively texture. It's light-bodied but quite flavorful.

name: Daou 2019 Chardonnay (Paso Robles)
review: Broadly tropical aromas of plumeria, mango and guava are clean and ripe on the nose of this bottling. There's just enough tension on the palate to frame the tropical show of jasmine, tuberose and ripe papaya fruits.

name: Daou 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Crisp aromas of peach, cut grass and chiseled rock make for a refreshing nose. There is firm tension to the palate, where a ripe peach element is balanced by a light green-pepper accent.

name: Dancing Crow 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: Lively fruit flavors and light accents of baking spices give this light-bodied wine an attractive profile, as crisp acidity livens the mouthfeel in an appetizing way.

name: Damsel 2019 Boushey Vineyard Marsanne (Washington)
review: This young release wine is the first white to come from the winery, and it's a variety few are working with in Washington, with this vineyard the home to most plantings. The aromas bring notes of mascarpone, lemon curd and lees. The flavors are broad but fresh in feel. It's equal parts intrigue and delivery—a promising start.

name: Cymbal 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Pear, citrus, kiwi and pineapple aromas are followed by full, fleshy tropical-fruit flavors, with plenty of acidity backing them up. There's hang time on the finish. It's an enchanting look at the variety's fuller bodied side and will have a long life ahead of it. Best after 2022.

name: Cuvaison 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Los Carneros)
review: Lengthy and intense, this estate-grown white is fermented entirely in stainless steel and aged similarly for another six months. Kiwi, honeydew and grapefruit combine around a stony texture of grip and viscosity.

name: Croma Vera 2019 Spanish Springs Vineyard Albariño (San Luis Obispo County)
review: Pear peels, apple flesh and a squeeze of lime make for a crisp and fresh nose on this bottling. The tense palate combines more pome fruit with citrus pith, climaxing in a grippy finish.

name: Croma Vera 2019 Albariño (Edna Valley)
review: Crushed stone, salted apple peels, a touch of peach and a hint of pasilla chile show on the nose of this bottling. There's a grippy and grassy tone to the palate, where dried apple and peach flavors ride a tense structure.

name: Croft Vineyards 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This top-tier vineyard does exceptionally well with its Sauvignon Blancs, and applies similar practices to this Pinot Gris. Spicy, leesy and loaded with citrus, apple and pear, it's a crisp, refreshing wine with a foundation of wet stones. Just a hint of residual sugar helps to round off the acids, but it's quite dry.

name: Croft Vineyards 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: Croft supplies Sauvignon Blanc grapes to a number of Oregon wineries, and has a sure hand with its own version. The newest vintage is young, pungent, penetrating and grassy. It touches on pea-vine, thistle, grapefruit and apple scents and flavors, and will benefit from another year or two of bottle age and/or aggressive aeration.

name: Crū 2019 Unoaked Chardonnay (Arroyo Seco)
review: The nose on this unoaked Chardonnay is very Sauvignon Blanc-like, featuring tonic-like aromas of lemongrass, cut grass, wild lime and jasmine oil. The jasmine element is strong on the sip too, where Thai basil and spearmint flavors also show. Pair with spicy and fresh Asian food.

name: Crū 2019 Albariño (Edna Valley)
review: Aromatic hits of apple blossom, bubblegum, melon and tuberose show on the nose of this bottling. There is compelling tension and sizzling acidity to the sip, which is fresh although very ripe in tropical flavors of papaya, mango and plumeria.

name: Crū 2019 French Camp Vineyard Viognier (Paso Robles Highlands District)
review: Broad aromas of guava, melon, plumeria and jasmine show on the nose of this bottling. The real reward is the incredibly lively palate, where fresh, zesty acidity delivers clean flavors of pear rind and guava.

name: Coyote Canyon 2019 Coyote Canyon Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Aromas of red apple, herb and tin lead to just off-dry flavors. It brings enjoyment, but the variety seems hard to find.

name: Coyote Canyon 2019 Concrete fermented Albariño (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: The aromas pop, with notes of honeydew and apricot. The palate is richly flavorful and brings a vibrant sense of acidity that piques the interest.

name: Coyote Canyon 2019 Coyote Canyon Vineyard Albariño (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Effusive aromas of mineral, red apple skin, tropical fruit and melon are followed by dry flavors that bring some weight with them. It's a sure-fire crowd-pleaser.

name: Covenant 2019 Red C Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: This well-balanced medium-bodied wine fermented in steel barrels with native yeast is alive with tangy citrus aromas and flavors that are shaded by hints of riper melon and cream notes. It is crisp, clean and refreshing.

name: Covenant 2019 Mensch Roussanne (Lodi)
review: This Rhône-style white is distinctive and well balanced, and will help cure Chardonnay boredom. Made with native yeast in neutral barrels, it offers enticing, toasty aromas and tasty but subdued apricot and peach-skin flavors that leave a tangy finish.

name: Cória 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Showing definite sweetness on the palate, this skirts the border of off dry. Honey, ripe pears, peaches and a streak of vanilla complete a full-bodied, well-rounded and fruit-forward style. It would be interesting to try this with a slice of peach pie or pear tart.

name: Cooper Mountain 2019 Tocai Friulano (Willamette Valley)
review: A relative of Sauvignon Blanc, this unusual grape has lovely floral highlights and plenty of tart citrus. The crunchy apple fruit is paired with tangy tangerine, and it adds up to a refreshing, acid-driven alternative to the more standard Willamette Valley whites.

name: Conveyance 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This is fresh, clean and fruit forward, with flavors of pear and apple. The texture is spot on, keeping the fruit lively across the palate, and the wine finishes with a hint of wet stone.

name: Convergence Zone 2019 Sunbreak Upland Vineyard Chenin Blanc (Snipes Mountain)
review: The aromas are bright and fresh, with notes of citrus zest, lemon balm and tangerine. The palate is sleek and juicy, with passion fruit flavors and bright acidity. There's a lot to like.

name: Comartin 2019 Private Reserve Chardonnay (Monterey)
review: Light aromas of honeydew rind, white melon and a hint of guava show on the nose of this bottling. It leans tropical on the palate, where mango, guava and papaya flavors are strong yet clean and crisp.

name: Coeur de Terre 2019 Pinot Gris (McMinnville)
review: This is a striking wine that cuts down through the palate with a lively mix of citrus, herb and wet grass. There's a whiff of sawdust, and somehow it all jells into a tasty wine centered on crisp apple fruit, with highlights of white radish.

name: Clos Pegase 2019 Mitsuko's Vineyard Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (Carneros)
review: This grassy, herbal white is effusive in pea shoot and legume, with a creaminess to the palate. It develops into layers of fresh peach and lime on the palate that contrast the wine's herbal sensations well.

name: Clos du Val 2019 Estate Sauvignon Blanc (Yountville)
review: Fresh and fruity with depth and complexity, this brilliant wine is bursting in grapefruit, tangerine and pineapple, with an herbal undercurrent of lemongrass. A lasting note of peach lingers from the midpalate onward, accentuated by the bright, lasting acidity.

name: Clockwise 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: Crisp and mineral-driven, this is a floral white, with fresh acidity throughout, that is light and bright in style. Green-apple, Meyer lemon and mango flavors combine effortlessly and in balance.

name: Cline 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (North Coast)
review: This deceptively full-bodied wine offers light petrol and earth aromas followed by peach-skin and peach-flesh flavors on a juicy, appetizing texture. It's lively, flavorful and different.

name: Cline 2019 Viognier (North Coast)
review: This rich, soft and ripe-tasting wine folds peach, butter and almond flavors into a smooth texture and full body. It gives a nice sense of breadth and fullness for such a well-priced offering.

name: Cline 2019 Farmhouse White (California)
review: A tinge of pink in the color and distinctive chalk and peach-skin aromas lead to dry and fairly subtle flavors of minerals, peaches and apricots in this earthy and medium-bodied wine.

name: Cliff Lede 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This delightfully complex, structured white blends in 12% Sémillon and 3% Sauvignon Vert, fermenting in a majority of French oak, as well as stainless-steel tank and concrete egg. Seamless and supple, it explodes in lemon meringue, tangerine and a subtle hint of brioche, long on the finish in crushed rock and stone.

name: Clif Family 2019 Rte Blanc Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Entirely varietal, this white is aged in a majority of stainless steel with 16% neutral French oak and made from organically farmed grapes. A sweetness of peach and honeysuckle hits the nose in alluring ways, leading to a dry, balanced and fruity palate of green apple and lemon meringue.

name: Cliche 2019 Hybrid Pinot Grigio (Lodi)
review: This wine is clean and lean, offering light herb and crisp apple flavors on a light body. It provides good, simple refreshment.

name: Clementine Carter 2019 Grenache Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
review: Fresh lemon peel, crisp apple, honeysuckle and a steely mineral tone show on the nose of this bottling. The wine lands rather rich and ripe on the sip, offering Gravenstein apple and toasty flavors.

name: Claiborne & Churchill 2019 Chardonnay (Edna Valley)
review: Very fresh and tightly wound aromas of crisp apple and pear drizzled with lime juice make for a brisk entry to the nose of this bottling. It's impressively lively on the palate, where that citrus sharpness cuts into the guava and tropical notes.

name: CK Mondavi and Family 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: This soft, fruity, medium-bodied wine is nicely packed with peach, lemon and apple flavors. It offers a smooth mouthfeel and a touch of butter on the finish.

name: City Limits 2019 Virginia Street Roussanne (Yakima Valley)
review: It is exceedingly rare to see a bottle of this variety from the state at this price. Intriguing aromas of pear, apricot, citrus and seashell are followed by full-bodied, textured flavors.

name: Circle B 2019 Grenache Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Peach, honeysuckle, a squeeze of lime and a pinch of beach sand show on the nose of this bottling. There's a fresh sizzle of acidity and firm grip across the palate, where pear, melon and lime balm flavors align.

name: Chuparosa 2019 Kohill Vineyard Semillon (Ramona Valley)
review: Sémillon is a tough grape to work with solo, and this wine, which is golden in the glass, smells of oxidized apple juice, orange rind and dried mango. There's an oily quality to the palate, which is flat in texture and showing similar notes as the nose.

name: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2019 Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas intoxicate, with notes of white peach, lime, jasmine and flower. Off-dry leaning sweeter stone fruit and Nestea Lemon Iced Tea flavors follow, with a strong sense of acidity threading through it. The balance and intensity are exquisite. It's everything one could want from this variety.

name: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2019 Cold Creek Vineyard Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are charming, with notes of lime leaf, white peach and wet rock. Just off-dry lemon iced tea flavors follow. It's a lovely expression of the variety.

name: Château Bianca 2019 Riesling (Van Duzer Corridor)
review: This has the appealing sweet-tart puckery mouthfeel and flavor of past vintages. It's a bright, fresh wine, with off-dry flavors of ripe apple. The balance between fruit and acid is spot on, and it's a good match to fruit-based desserts.

name: Château Bianca 2019 Gewürztraminer (Van Duzer Corridor)
review: This is a pleasant, quaffable white wine, slightly off-dry, but it almost completely lacks varietal specificity. Light flavors of clean apple and pear fruit are combined with moderate acidity and a touch of residual sugar. But in a blind tasting it would be hard to identify this as Gewürztraminer.

name: Charles Krug 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This is a stainless-steel fermented white made in large quantities that hits the mark in its candied perfume of peach and apricot. Lively and crisp, it retains a wealth of high-toned acidity and brightness throughout.

name: Charles B. Mitchell 2019 Estate Reserve Fumé Blanc (Fair Play)
review: This tangy, lemony and light-bodied wine seems rather lean at first sip yet it offers dabs of vanilla and nutmeg along with good acidity and refreshment on the palate.

name: Charles B. Mitchell 2019 Estate Reserve Semillon-Sauvignon Blanc (Fair Play)
review: A lively snap-pea aroma is followed by nicely rich herb, honey and melon flavors in this full-bodied, complex and lightly earthy-tasting wine.

name: Chamisal 2019 Stainless Chardonnay (Central Coast)
review: Years ago, this stainless bottling was one of the first to establish the now common style. It offers aromas of poached pear and guava peel, then finds a tense structure on the palate where warmer tones of papaya, cherimoya, pineapple and honeysuckle combine.

name: Chacewater 2019 Windrem Vineyard Organic Grapes Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)
review: Herb and spice flavors complement each other in this medium-bodied and crisp-textured wine. Light, leafy aromas lead to light vanilla and toast accents.

name: Cavatappi 2019 Pinot Grigio (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are light, with notes of freshly cut yellow apple and pear followed by sleek, pleasing, fruit forward flavors that bring a bright sense of acidity. Acid and freshness are the drivers. Pair it with scallop ceviche with generous amounts of lime.

name: Castoro Cellars 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Light yellow-grapefruit rind and lime-pith aromas meet with a delicate hint of grass on the nose of this bottling. It's very straightforward on the palate, offering light pear and clean lime-peel flavors.

name: Cass 2019 Mr. Blanc White (Paso Robles Geneseo District)
review: Hints of honey and blanched almonds meet with honeysuckle on the soft and smooth nose of this blend of 56% Roussanne, 24% Viognier and 20% Marsanne. Ripe flavors of guava and orange blossom show on the palate, with a bit more honey.

name: Cass 2019 Roussanne (Paso Robles)
review: Ripe aromas of pear, tuberose and lily are very tropical, yet cut with a twist of lime. The palate is driven by ripe pear and guava flavors, highlighted by a decent acidity.

name: Cass 2019 Viognier (Paso Robles)
review: Broad aromas of melon, peach, baked apple and tropical guava flesh show on the nose of this bottling. There is a flash of sizzling acidity to the sip, before the ripe pear and peach flavors return.

name: CASA Wines 2019 Chardonnay (Petaluma Gap)
review: Fleshy, rounded and influenced by oak, this approachable white is enjoyable fruit-forward and richly nuanced in brioche and butterscotch. It represents great value for the quality, vintage and appellation.

name: Cardella 2019 Giuliana's Vineyard Chenin Blanc (California)
review: Lots of apple and honey flavors and a nice nip of apple-skin astringency give great appeal and balance to this medium-bodied, barrel-fermented wine.

name: Canvasback 2019 Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: This wine still hasn't fully settled in, showing reduction initially. Behind it are aromas of lime and wet slate. The palate brings mouthwatering, electric acidity to the supremely flavorful lemon-lime flavors. Decant if drinking in the immediate term.

name: Canoe Ridge 2019 The Expedition Pinot Gris (Horse Heaven Hills)
review: Pear aromas are out front, followed by notes of apple and pineapple. Dry tropical and stone fruit flavors follow. It delivers broad appeal.

name: Cannonball 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: Pungent herbal aromas and lively, vivid, leafy flavors make this medium-bodied wine refreshing and assertive. It's tight and lean and tailored for pairing with seafood.

name: Camins 2 Dreams 2019 Spear Vineyards Grüner Veltliner (Sta. Rita Hills)
review: Superfresh aromas of orange peel, tangerine flesh and a hint of grapefruit show on the nose of this bottling. There is a broad sense of nectarine and ripe orange on the palate, but the tension is driven by grapefruit peel into the finish.

name: Caliza 2019 Kissin' Cousins White (Paso Robles)
review: This blend of 48% Viognier, 30% Grenache Blanc and 22% Roussanne begins with aromas of Meyer lemon peel, light brine, white flowers and drying honeysuckle. The palate combines lemon-peel, tangerine-rind and yellow-apple flavors, with a lingering sense of paraffin that stretches into the finish.

name: Caliza 2019 End of the Day Albariño (Paso Robles)
review: Warm aromas of honey-drizzled, pan-seared apples on brioche make for a very inviting entry to this bottling. The palate leans into the mineral side of the grape, showing ash and wet concrete flavors as well as lime pith and apple flesh.

name: Cairdeas 2019 Marsanne (Yakima Valley)
review: Blended with 16% Roussanne from Lawrence Vineyard with the rest of the fruit coming from famed Boushey, this wine was fermented in concrete and then aged in stainless steel and new French oak. The aromas are brooding out of the gate, with notes of lemon balm, almond and crushed rock. The palate is medium bodied and seamless in feel. It brings a whole lot of acidity for this variety.

name: Cairdeas 2019 Nellie Mae Lawrence Vineyard White (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Viognier makes up two-thirds of this wine, with the rest Roussanne. Bright aromas of lemon zest, mineral and ripe peach are followed by textured, almost viscous feeling full-bodied fruit flavors. Melon notes linger on the finish.

name: Cairdeas 2019 Picardan (Lake Chelan)
review: This is the first bottling in the state of this variety. The aromas draw you into the glass, with notes of lime zest, whole tangerine, lemon and schist. Puckering, sleek, high-acid lemon and lime flavors follow.

name: Cadre 2019 Band of Stones Grüner Veltliner (Edna Valley)
review: When his pioneering wine family sold off its large brands, John Niven started his own small project, and the early results are stunning. Best is this Gruner Veltliner, which begins with clean and sharp aromas of lime skin, white pepper and green apple peels. The palate is tense and green in pome and citrus fruits, with a touch of briny kelp that coasts into the acid-driven finish.

name: Cadre 2019 Sea Queen Albariño (Edna Valley)
review: Aromas of peach blossom, apple blossom and pineapple guava show on the nose of this  refreshing bottling. It's zesty and lively on the youthful palate, where pineapple and guava flavors are cut by lime peel and grapefruit zest, with a hint of spicy ginger toward the finish.

name: Cadre 2019 Stone Blossom Sauvignon Blanc (Edna Valley)
review: Lemongrass, lime-sorbet, wet grass, light bell-pepper and lily aromas are reminiscent of walking through a springtime meadow on the nose of this bottling by John Niven, whose family pioneered the Edna Valley. The palate is zesty with green grass, shiso chiffonade and minced jalapeño flavors, with a lingering white-pepper elements into the acid-sizzled finish.

name: Buttonwood Farm 2019 Zingy Sauvignon Blanc (Los Olivos District)
review: Juicy aromas of ripe apple and bubblegum show on the nose of this always zesty bottling. There's a firm grip to the sip, where easygoing flavors of pear and lime slide toward the structured finish.

name: Buttonwood 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: This entry-level Sauvignon Blanc clicks all the varietal buttons. Clean and light aromas of lychee, passion fruit, wet rocks and cut grass lead into a palate that sizzles with acidity and shows flavors of ash and light melon. The texture remains taut into the finish.

name: Buttonwood 2019 Grenache Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Asian pear and grassy aromas show on the fresh nose of this brisk bottling. There's a firm tension and bright acidity on the sip, where pink-citrus and tight peach flavors make for an edgy experience.

name: Bryn Mawr Vineyards 2019 David's Block Pinot Blanc (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: Quite dry and tart, this is a wine for acid lovers. It opens with light scents of white flowers and flavors of tart tangerines. The theme continues with hints of botanical herbs and a squeeze of lime. The concentration and balance belie a wine with alcohol under 12%.

name: Bryn Mawr Vineyards 2019 Estate Riesling (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: Estate grown and medium dry (as the back label indicates), this is a complex weave of lemon juice, curd, dried apricot and orange peel. It tilts toward tartness, though rounded out just a bit with a dollop of residual sugar. With good balance and plenty of acid, it can age indefinitely, but should be in a prime drinking window for the next six to year years.

name: Brooks 2019 Amycas White (Willamette Valley)
review: This unusual blend is 29% Pinot Blanc, 27% Muscat, 25% Riesling, 11% Pinot Gris and 8% Gewürztraminer. Despite the low percentage, it's the Gewurz that stands out aromatically, and brings those flavors of floral baby powder into the body of the wine. It's not overpowering, however, and the nifty mix of other white grapes fleshes it out with apple, peach, pear and a dash of lemon oil.

name: Brooks 2019 Brooks Estate Vineyard Pinot Gris (Eola-Amity Hills)
review: Old vine, estate-grown fruit is the star here, with Asian pear and a touch of white radish front and center. It's crisp through the finish, dry but not at all sour, with a finish of slightly honeyed apples.

name: Brooks 2019 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: In an excellent vintage for all the Brooks white wines, this Pinot Blanc is a particular success. The house style favors stainless steel, high acidity whites, which brings up the complex aromatics of citrus blossom and rind. The texture, minerality and flinty persistence provide a dense underpinning to a lingering finish.

name: Broken Earth 2019 Limited Release Fiano (Paso Robles)
review: This bottling begins with clean, pleasant and yet strong lines of peach, apple blossom and lime peel on the nose. There's undeniable ripeness of guava and other tropical fruits on the palate, but the texture is chalky and the acidity is fresh.

name: Brigadoon 2019 Pinot Blanc (Willamette Valley)
review: Light and fresh, though not quite fully ripened, this pleasant wine conjures up impressions of celery, watercress, white radish and green apple. It's an elegant wine, with a finishing dash of cinnamon spice.

name: Brigadoon 2019 Dry Riesling (Willamette Valley)
review: This will have plenty of appeal for those who enjoy a high-acid style. The searing tartness is somewhat mitigated by 8 g/l of residual sugar, just enough to round things off a bit. Dried apples, oranges, grapefruit and lemon-rind flavors give this exceptional density and complexity through a long finish.

name: Brigadoon 2019 Off-dry Riesling (Willamette Valley)
review: A perfect example of the off-dry style, this brings scents and flavors of honeysuckle and honeydew melon, moving into a midpalate with sweet nectarine and Cosmic Crisp apple fruit. It's high in acid but 18 g/l of residual sugar provides a delicious sweet-tart balance. Drink now or age it for up to a decade.

name: Brian Carter 2019 Oriana White (Yakima Valley)
