review: This unique blend is 49% Viognier, 41% Roussanne and 10% Riesling. Ripe peach and honeysuckle aromas are out front. Lightly sweet-seeming stone fruit and pear flavors follow, with enough acidity to tie it all together.

name: Brevity 2019 Pinot Grigio (Santa Barbara County)
review: Very light in the glass, this crisp and clean bottling begins with aromas of soft red apple, fat white-gardenia flower buds and a light tone of nectarine. The palate is both tight and expressive, as a firm texture delivers flavors of Asian pear, grippy tangerine skin and a touch of honeysuckle.

name: Brecon Estate 2019 True Acacia Head Albariño (Central Coast)
review: Bright and fresh aromas of limeade and ripe honeydew are cut by a metallic minerality on the nose of this bottling. It's very taut on the sip, where the ripe honeysuckle and melon flavors are leveled by a lime-balm element.

name: Brecon Estate 2019 Albariño (Central Coast)
review: Warm aromas of peach, nectarine and light pineapple are cut by lime balm on the nose of this bottling. The wine lands broadly on the palate, with peach and apricot flavors, then grows extremely tight and grippy toward the finish.

name: Bravium 2019 Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)
review: This is a versatile, well-balanced, well-made wine. It's medium to full bodied and offers tempting cream and vanilla aromas, mouthfilling pear and vanilla flavors and a rich texture supported by good acidity.

name: Brassfield 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (High Valley)
review: Great acidity and vibrant citrus and fig flavors give this medium-bodied wine a lot to like. It is very well balanced, cleansing the palate and inviting another sip each time.

name: Brassfield 2019 High Serenity Ranch Pinot Gris (High Valley)
review: A charming floral aroma and abundant honeydew-melon flavors give this medium-bodied wine plenty to like. A nicely rounded texture is braced by subtle acidity for a clean mouthfeel.

name: Brady Vineyard 2019 Chardonnay (Paso Robles)
review: Ripe white peach, daffodil, apple blossom, poached pear and plumeria aromas show on the clean and full nose of this bottling. Those floral and white fruit flavors feature strongly on the sip too, but the racy acidity and a linear tension keeps everything in check, finishing on a hint of coconut oil.

name: Bota Box 2019 Chardonnay (California)
review: Light herb and spiced pear flavors give this medium-bodied wine a straightforward appeal. It's smooth in texture and should please a crowd.

name: Bota Box 2019 Pinot Grigio (California)
review: This wine has more flavor than many of its competitors, boasting bright peach and ripe melon in the aroma and satisfying white peach on the palate. It's medium bodied and mouthfilling.

name: Bota Box 2019 Nighthawk Gold Sauvignon Blanc (California)
review: This herb- and veggie-laced wine cannot be accused of being bland. Vivid snap-pea aromas and dried herb flavors join a core of honeydew melon while good acidity makes it tangy.

name: Bonny Doon 2019 Beeswax Vineyard Picpoul (Arroyo Seco)
review: Very mellow on the nose, this bottling offers brisk touches of wet cement, apple flesh, white flower and pomelo rind. Dried apple and lime-peel flavors show on the palate, which is mellow in texture.

name: Bonny Doon 2019 Le Cigare Blanc White (Central Coast)
review: Very light in the glass, this blend of 46% Grenache Blanc, 34% Vermentino and 20% Clairette Blanche offers honeydew, lime pith, wet gravel and a hint of lanolin on the nose. The palate is full of flavors, offering sliced pear and lime peel set upon a delicate texture.

name: Bodega de Edgar 2019 Albariño (Paso Robles)
review: Very precise aromas of lime peel and white flower meet with a chalky, even ashy edge on the nose of this bottling. It's bone dry on the sip and clings to the palate with flavors of lemon and grapefruit pith.

name: Boatique 2019 Sauvignon Blanc
review: This excellent wine is bright and fresh, gently acidic and showing citrus and fresh herb flavors. It's medium bodied, clean and refreshing, just what most Sauvignon is supposed to be.

name: Bliss 2019 Estate Bottled Sauvignon Blanc (Mendocino)
review: Crisp and lively, this wonderfully fruity wine lifts bright apple and citrus flavors with good acidity for a refreshing, tangy expression. Perfect for lunches and appetizers.

name: Black Stallion 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: This wine is textured and fleshy, supple on the palate with a full-bodied, opulent style. Peach, apricot and vanilla mesh well to offer fruit-forward flavor and approachability.

name: Bevela 2019 White Lotus Camp 4 Vineyard Grenache Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)
review: Very light in the glass, this bottling begins with crisp aromas of Alpine water alongside hints of red apple and honeysuckle. There's a compelling almond-cream flavor on the palate, where passion fruit and apple-blossom elements make for a refreshing sip.

name: Bernardus 2019 Griva Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Arroyo Seco)
review: Lime-skin, freshly cut Anjou pear and a hint of stone aromas show on the nose of this single-vineyard expression. Granny Smith apple and guava-peel flavors converge on the palate, with hints of cut grass and wet rocks as well.

name: Benziger 2019 Paradiso De Maria Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma Mountain)
review: Lemony, grainy and freshly wrapped in lime salt, this is a sophisticated and lengthy white wine, with body and grace. A steely finish caps off and underscores the lively acidity beneath.

name: Benom 2019 Contrast Chardonnay-Sauvignon Blanc (Paso Robles)
review: Though from the Old World, Frenchmen-turned-Californians Arnaud and Guillaume Fabre buck tradition with this blend of 64% Chardonnay and 36% Sauvignon Blanc, aged in 30% new French oak. The result is aromas of crushed red apple, peach and green chile followed by a palate that's both round with guava and apple and yet brisk with grass, lime pith and a cement-like grip.

name: Bella Grace 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Amador County)
review: Bright aromas of bananas and bubble gum make this fruity, medium-bodied wine an outlier. It's a gushing, effusive style of Sauvignon.

name: Battle Creek 2019 Unconditional Blanc Pinot Noir (Oregon)
review: Fruit forward aromas of tangerine, tropical fruit and pear lead to lightly smoky pear flavors. It's interesting but doesn't seem entirely settled.

name: Battle Creek 2019 Winemaker's White Blend White (Willamette Valley)
review: The aromas pull you into the glass, with notes of wet stone, satsuma and dried rose petal. It drinks off dry, with a pleasing sense of tension between the stone fruit and citrus flavors and a compelling mouthfeel. A flavorful finish caps it off.

name: Bartholomew Estate 2019 Estate Grown Marsanne-Roussanne (Sonoma Valley)
review: Blending 70% Marsanne with 30% Roussanne, barrel fermenting it together and aging it further in French oak, this lush white has a reductive quality on the nose, along a whiff of petrol. Floral, fruity and cohesive on the palate, it is made in a robust, fleshy and appealing style.

name: Barra of Mendocino 2019 Estate Grown Chardonnay (Mendocino)
review: Very smooth and mouthfilling, this lushly textured wine made from organic grapes is packed with buttered popcorn and baking-spice aromas and piles of poached pears on baked apple flavors. For all that richness it doesn't overdo the oak aspects, and comes out nicely balanced on the finish.

name: Bannister 2019 Scheurebe (Alexander Valley)
review: Dry, floral and earthy, this rare white found more commonly in Germany is unusual, with strong aromas of geranium and stone. Given 24-hours of skin contact, it has a relatively dark color and tannin profile, ending crisply in ample acidity and racy grapefruit.

name: Balverne 2019 Estate Grown Sauvignon Blanc (Chalk Hill)
review: Grassy, herbal and legume-like, this 100% varietal white delves into a focused, fresh middle led by juicy peach and lemongrass. The acidity is well balanced and keeps it enduringly refreshing.

name: Balletto 2019 Pinot Gris (Russian River Valley)
review: From estate-grown fruit, this is a stony, slatey white wine enveloped in flavors of apricot and a lightness of baking spice. Low in oak, it is juicy, fleshy and fully integrated.

name: Balletto 2019 Teresa's Unoaked Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)
review: Estate-grown fruit presents as light in color and floral on the nose, with a fleshy flavorful palate of peach, green apple and pear. Long-lasting freshness and focus support its steely, light-bodied frame.

name: Balboa 2019 Grüner Veltliner (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: Pear, cut red apple and herb aromas lead to lively pear flavors that bring texture. The acidity keeps the interest high.

name: Badger Hound 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)
review: A stainless-steel fermented and aged white, this is steely and citrus-driven in grapefruit, Meyer lemon and melon. Sleek and crisp, it continues the brightness theme in a finishing touch of tangerine.

name: B.R. Cohn 2019 Silver Label Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: This is a tank-fermented white that is crisp and buzzy in acidity, the flavors focused and fresh in apple, fig and honeydew melon. A taste of fennel gives it an earthy finish.

name: Avalon 2019 Flint & Steel Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)
review: Tasting of lemon candy and peach, this wine has subtle accents of baking spice and vanilla. Light and bright, it offers a lush, fleshy texture that will widely appeal.

name: Aspen Peak 2019 Riesling (Colorado)
review: Ripe peach, clementine and pear aromas are mellow on the nose. On the off-dry palate, Valencia orange, peach and Key lime pie filling meet saline minerality. Cheerful acidity keeps this sunny and boisterous through the lingering, juicy peach and mandarin finish.

name: Aspen Peak 2019 Viognier (Grand Valley)
review: On the nose, aromas of lemons, grass clippings and lime peels are dipped in Calamansi juice. Juicy on the palate and full of vibrant energy, bright, ripe and sunny tones of apple and Mandarin orange are highlighted by a delicate bouquet of white flowers. The acidity is charming and well integrated. A dry finish lingers long and juicy, pushing flavors of white peach and clementine.

name: Ashan 2019 Barrel Fermented Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))
review: The aromas are pleasing, with notes of ripe, cut pineapple, fig and almond. Rich pineapple and other tropical-fruit flavors follow, bringing density and balance. It shows the variety's riper, fuller side.

name: Arietta 2019 Sauvignon Blanc-Sémillon (Napa Valley)
review: A blend of 74% Sauvignon Blanc and 26% Sémillon, this superb white is robust and complex, with a sophisticated structure and lively underlying acidity. Hints of nutmeg, honey and flint meet a serious palate of tension and length.

name: Arianna 2019 Silvaspoons Vineyard Torrontés (Lodi)
review: Butter dominates the aromas and flavors of this full-bodied oak-influenced wine. Scents of buttered toast, vanilla and almonds lead to nutty meaty flavors that fill out a rich texture.

name: Aquilini 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Red Mountain)
review: The aromas are light, with notes of herb, citrus and whiffs of tropical fruit. Full-bodied guava, kiwi and pineapple flavors lead to a warm finish.

name: Apolloni 2019 Estate Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Ripe pear fruit, drizzled with a squeeze of lemon, incorporates a subtle grainy, fleshy mouthfeel. It's clean and straightforward, a solid example of classic Oregon Pinot Gris. Drink over the next two vintages while it's at its freshest.

name: Aperture 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: Balanced between crisp and complex tropical fruit, citrus and dried herb, this barrel- fermented white is well-defined on the palate. While deft in approach, it still has weight and breadth as well as a lingering spice note on the finish.

name: Aperture 2019 Chenin Blanc (Clarksburg)
review: This exciting wine is crisp and tangy, full of vivid fruit flavors like kiwi, lime and green apple. Good acidity gives it a lively, appetizing mouthfeel.

name: Antiquum Farm 2019 Alium Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: One of three radically different Pinot Gris from Antiquum Farm, this was fermented on the skins for a full month, and is approaching orange wine status. A sunset hue of orange and cherry, it tastes of blood oranges, both flesh and rind. The extended maceration does tend to flatten the flavors a bit, so drink this over the next year.

name: Antiquum Farm 2019 Aurosa Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: One of three radically different Pinot Gris from Antiquum Farm, this was cold-soaked for 72 hours prior to pressing, yielding a coppery gold wine with intense aromas of hibiscus, rose hip tea and chamomile. Flavors follow along, edging close to light tropical with a hint of papaya. The skin phenolics linger and put a tight frame around the finish.

name: Antiquum Farm 2019 Daisy Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This breaks new ground for Oregon's signature white grape. It's rich and leesy, fragrant and full, a bonanza of apple, pear and melon fruits. There's a lingering trail of honeysuckle, and a lush minerality that underscores the freshness. A stunning wine.

name: Anne Amie 2019 Two Estates Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: This complex, nicely detailed wine opens with aromas of flower, citrus and pear, leading into a leesy, textural palate with a mix of white peach and pear. A small percentage was fermented in neutral wood, which seems to have nicely rounded out the acids.

name: Anne Amie 2019 Estate Müller-Thurgau (Yamhill-Carlton)
review: Rare indeed is Willamette Valley Müller-Thurgau, and these vines date from the late 1970s. But this is more than history in a bottle; it's a thoroughly delicious and refreshing wine, leesy and mineral-driven, with tightly wound core flavors of citrus skin, apple and pear.

name: Andis 2019 Painted Fields Cuvée Blanc White (Sierra Foothills)
review: Crisp and citrusy, this medium-bodied wine is mostly a blend of Vermentino and Grenache Blanc. It leads with good acidity and bright lemon, lime and grapefruit notes. The texture is tangy and palate cleansing.

name: Andis 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sierra Foothills)
review: Ripe, rounded and full bodied, this wine offers ripe apple and pear flavors that are especially mouthfilling. The addition of 15% Sémillon may account for that. A tangy foundation of acidity keeps it well balanced.

name: Ancient Peaks 2019 Chardonnay (Santa Margarita Ranch)
review: This is a fresh and bright style of Chardonnay, reminiscent of a Riesling. Aromas of lime peel, coconut oil and strong jasmine lead into a very floral palate of white flowers and white-fleshed fruits. The structure is quite racy and taut.

name: Ampelos 2019 Phi Viognier (Santa Barbara County)
review: Made 100% varietal, the grapes stainless-steel fermented and aged, this wine is earthy in jasmine and geranium, the texture viscous and thick. Pineapple and pear flavors round out the experience.

name: Amici 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa County)
review: With 3% Sémillon, this is tart, textured and cohesive, densely layered in lime and lychee, with an underlying earthiness of dried herb. Medium bodied, it has a subtle lushness that appeals.

name: Amador Cellars 2019 Breitmeyer Vineyards Fiano (Amador County)
review: This medium-bodied and lively textured wine offers floral aromas and tantalizing peach flavors that are nicely concentrated and deep. Aged on the lees, it shows there's more to Amador than big reds.

name: Amador Cellars 2019 Estate Sauvignon Blanc (Shenandoah Valley (CA))
review: This medium-bodied wine tastes fresh and juicy, blending citrus and herb flavors that are nicely lifted by crisp acidity.

name: Alma de Cattleya 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Sonoma County)
review: Intriguing aromas of brine and nori wrap meet with grapefruit peel on the sea-inflected nose of this bottling. Wet stone and grass flavors join with guava on the complex palate.

name: Alexana 2019 Terroir Series Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: A solid, fruity wine, this mixes crisp apple and white-peach flavors. It continues along that path and into a finish that displays some phenolic bite and skin tannins.

name: Alexana 2019 Revana Vineyard Riesling (Dundee Hills)
review: Dry and balanced, this is a fruit-driven wine with good aging potential. Tart apple, lemon, pineapple and peach flavors come together in a well-integrated whole, with snappy acids and excellent concentration.

name: Akash 2019 Sauvignon Blanc (Temecula Valley)
review: A bit shy on the nose at first, this bottling eventually allow peach and guava aromas to arise alongside a light hint of wood spice. There's a fine grip to the palette, where ripe peach, butter and toasty oak flavors converge.

name: Airlie 2019 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)
review: Despite being pressed quickly off the skins, the wine shows a light tawny straw color. It's tightly-focused, with penetrating flavors of dried apples, straw and citrus. The palate is layered and textured, with good length and clarity. The wine went through no malolactic and was done entirely in stainless steel.
